title: [epic] Focus-redirect hot-path cache
description: From PR #4574 review (holomush-x1lwf.1, important). Dispatcher.maybeRedirectForFocus reads connection focus via sessionStore.GetConnection (uncached Postgres round-trip) on EVERY dispatch of the redirect-candidate verbs pose/say/ooc/emit — the highest-frequency command class — because the focus read is how it determines scene-vs-grid. Pre-PR, Dispatch was DB-free for these verbs. session.Store has no cache-aside layer. Add a per-connection focus cache (or read focus from in-memory session state) so the hot path doesn't hit Postgres per pose. Design input needed on the cache invalidation vs SetConnectionFocus.
acceptance_criteria: pose/say/ooc/emit dispatch no longer performs an uncached DB round-trip per invocation; focus reads are cached/in-memory with correct invalidation on focus change.
notes:
grounding/codegraph: focus-redirect hot path — maybeRedirectForFocus(dispatcher.go:371)→ConnectionFocusKind(focus_reader.go:42)→store.GetConnection = uncached PG PK SELECT per pose/say/ooc/emit
grounding/rg: FocusKey write paths — ALL three coordinator writes (SetConnectionFocus set_connection_focus.go:91, AutoFocusOnJoin auto_focus_on_join.go:149, RestoreConnectionFocus restore_connection_focus.go:58) funnel through Store.UpdateSessionConnection; sole SQL UPDATE SET focus_key at session_store.go:1206; AddConnection INSERT can carry initial focus (:558). Bead's 'invalidate at SetConnectionFocus' undercounts paths.
grounding/rg: single-core is a DOCUMENTED INVARIANT (session-liveness spec:67-69 → docs/plans/2026-04-07-cursor-lock-finding-1-closure.md); multi-core cross-replica invalidation is explicit non-goal reserved for INV-CLUSTER. In-process cache correct today.
decision: Design A (caching session.Store decorator, FocusKind-scoped) over Design B (SessionStreamRegistry — transport-lifetime-scoped, unseeded, needs DB fallback) and Design C (reuse session Info — not connection-scoped, rejected).
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-07-focus-hotpath-cache-design.md
design-review round 1 NOT READY: (C1) evict-before-write is a cache-aside populate-stale race — GetConnection is plain SELECT, concurrent miss repopulates pre-write value post-evict; fix = generation-guarded populate + invalidate-after-write. (C2) Store.Delete (session CASCADE, server.go:513/577 quit/boot) clears focus outside the 5-method set — reframe: delete-path miss = bounded leak not stale-serve, so correctness-evict only on UpdateSessionConnection + LRU backstop. (I1) DeleteByCharacter evict unimplementable (Info has no conn IDs). (I2) INV-SCENE-68 miscited — only INV-SCENE-67 is fail-closed sibling.
Spec revised (round 2): correctness-evict only on UpdateSessionConnection; class-B removals (incl Store.Delete CASCADE) best-effort + LRU-cap backstop; generation-guarded populate closes the cache-aside race; invalidate-after-successful-write; INV-SCENE-67 (not 68) cited; INV-SCENE-69 reworded to no-stale-serve.
design-review READY (round 2). All 4 round-1 findings verified closed (generation-guard fix provably correct; class-A/B split grounded). 3 non-blocking advisories folded into spec Open-Questions #4-6: hot-path Mutex contention (benchmark; consider clock-LRU/RWMutex), DeleteByCharacter FindByCharacter hop, connection-ID-spoofing residual (pre-existing). Reinforcement: ValidateSessionOwnership fails closed pre-dispatch (session_ownership.go:34-89). NEXT: writing-plans.
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-07-focus-hotpath-cache.md
grounding/probe-paths (plan): verified all file paths + signatures — session.ConnectionCounts (session.go:285), NewSessionConnectionMutator (:167), mocks.MockStore EXPECT() for Update/Remove/List/Delete, observability engineFailures pattern (server.go:47), sub_grpc.go:230/371 + harness.go:314/451 wiring points, test/integration/scenes sibling focus tests (focus_routed_input_test.go = Task5 API model). Note: harness sessionStoreInst is concrete *PostgresSessionStore (:=) → plan Step redeclares as session.Store for decorator reassignment.
plan-review round 1 NOT READY: (C1) Task2 reader tests call unexported beginLoad/commitLoad but focus_reader_test.go is package command_test → won't compile; fix = new internal package command test file. (I1) INV-SCENE-69 scope must be 'INV-SCENE' not 'SCENE' (cmd/inv-render hard-fails on unknown scope). (I2-minor) add legacy: [] to match template. Wiring analysis + all other paths/sigs verified correct.
plan-review READY (round 2). Both round-1 findings fixed (internal package command test file for reader; scope: INV-SCENE + legacy: []); minor stale filename refs in File Structure + Task2 Files header corrected. Wiring + cache logic + all paths re-confirmed. NEXT: capture-adrs → plan-to-beads (6 tasks → epic).
ADRs: holomush-sfxte (UpdateSessionConnection chokepoint), holomush-cfqxp (Design A store decorator over B/C), holomush-sz877 (generation-guarded populate). Rendered to docs/adr/, converted frontmatter→H1+SPDX, README index updated.
plan-to-beads: materialized epic + 6 children (wm0fi.1 ConnectionFocusCache → .2 reader+metrics / .3 decorator → .4 wiring → .5 integration test → .6 INV-SCENE-69 binding); TDD dep chain T1→{T2,T3}→T4→T5→T6; 3 ADRs (sfxte/cfqxp/sz877) linked via 'related'. First ready = wm0fi.1.
Docs PR: https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/4592 (spec + plan + 3 ADRs + INV-SCENE-69 pending). pr-prep fast lane green (exit 0). Implementation tracked in wm0fi.1-.6.

title: ListPublishedScenes, GetPublicSceneArchive, DownloadPublicSceneArchive skip BeginServiceDispatch
description: The plan (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-web-portal-scenes.md Task 11 Step 3) specifies the per-RPC flow as: resolvePlayerSession -> IsGuest -> ownedCharacter -> BeginServiceDispatch(verified char actor)+defer release -> delegate. The three public-archive RPCs (ListPublishedScenes, GetPublicSceneArchive, DownloadPublicSceneArchive) correctly skip ownedCharacter (no acting character needed), but they also skip BeginServiceDispatch entirely -- they call the plugin SceneService client on the undecorated ctx. This means the plugin call context is NOT stamped with an actor identity, and the audit/dispatch machinery (whatever BeginServiceDispatch sets up) is bypassed for these calls. The plan text does not explicitly exempt these three RPCs from BeginServiceDispatch. Either the plan needs updating to clarify the exemption (with rationale: no character actor -> no dispatch stamp), or BeginServiceDispatch needs a no-actor variant for anonymous plugin calls so the audit trail is consistent. File: internal/grpc/sceneaccess_service.go:332-345, 350-368, 372-388.

title: docs-only PRs skip adr-doctor (lint:adr) — ADR-template violations reach main unguarded
description:
Goal: close the process gap where docs-only PRs land non-conformant ADRs on main.

Root cause: the CI docs-skip lane (ci-docs-skip.yaml) and pr-prep:docs fast lane provide a PASS status for the 'Lint' required check WITHOUT running the real lint task — confirmed: PR #4262's Lint check passed in 4s (skip-stub). 'lint:adr' (scripts/adr-doctor.sh) is part of the full `task lint` only, so a docs-only PR never runs it. #4262 thereby merged 4 crypto ADRs (holomush-c3kyv/edqh1/g3d4l/wfh42) missing ## Rationale and using ## Options Considered instead of ## Alternatives Considered. The violation only surfaced on the next code-touching PR (#4263) whose full Lint ran adr-doctor → red. Every code PR off that main was blocked until the ADRs were fixed (done in #4263).

Fix options to evaluate: run lint:adr (and other docs-relevant linters) in the docs-skip lane / pr-prep:docs; or make adr-doctor a separate always-run check not gated by docs-skip; or have the docs lane run the ADR subset.

Out of scope: the immediate ADR fixes (already bundled into #4263).

title: web: authed terminal route not responsive on mobile — TopBar header overflows, disconnect-screen text clips, terminal pane collapses to a narrow strip
description:
Goal: make the authed /terminal route usable on mobile-width viewports (the web client is a PWA, so mobile is a first-class surface). Currently the primary mobile entry point is effectively unusable. Symptom (observed on iOS, Vivaldi, ~390px viewport, game.holomush.dev, tip-of-main, 2026-05-24; two screenshots): 1. Disconnected state: TopBar header tabs cramped/overlapping, "disconnected" conn-pill text truncated; the "Connection lost. Click Reconnect or refresh the page." body text overflows the viewport and is CLIPPED AT THE LEFT EDGE (renders as "onnection lost" / "efresh the page"). 2. Connected state: the terminal column (with the command input + Composer hints) is squeezed into a ~20% left strip; the remaining ~70-80% on the right is an empty dark pane. Root cause (class: authed terminal route was built desktop-first; mobile breakpoints exist only partially and do not cover the page-level layout): - Disconnect/login screen: web/src/routes/(authed)/terminal/+page.svelte:677-687 renders

inside a flex column with align-items:center. The .error rule (:736) has no max-width / padding-inline / word-wrap, so the

takes max-content (single-line) width, exceeds the mobile viewport, and overflows-clips at x=0. - Connected layout: +page.svelte:689-711 uses .terminal-layout flex-direction:row with Resizable.PaneGroup direction="horizontal". sidebarDefaultPct is derived by pctFromPx (+page.svelte:99-103 = (px / cw) * 100) from a fixed sidebar pixel width. On a narrow container that percentage balloons (~280-320px / ~390px ≈ 72-82%), so the terminal pane defaultSize={100 - sidebarDefaultPct} collapses to ~18-28%. Meanwhile Sidebar.svelte (@media max-width:767px → .sidebar{width:0}) and Rail.svelte (same) zero out their CONTENT but NOT the wrapping Resizable.Pane, so the sidebar pane still claims its large percentage → the empty right pane. - TopBar: web/src/lib/components/TopBar.svelte header (:156-185) is a single non-wrapping flex row (.left logo+breadcrumb char@loc#id, .right kbd-hint+conn-pill+icon buttons). The only mobile rule is @media (min-width:768px){.kbd-hint{display:inline-flex}} (hides kbd-hint below 768). No flex-wrap, no breadcrumb truncation/ellipsis, no overflow handling → header overflows and clips on narrow viewports. Blast radius: entire mobile/narrow-viewport web experience for the authed terminal route (both the disconnect screen and the live terminal). 100% reproducible at mobile widths. Desktop unaffected; telnet unaffected. Not a correctness/security/data issue, but renders a primary product surface unusable. Candidate fix directions (NOT decided here — the mobile layout strategy is a design call, hence design-needed): A. Add @media (max-width:767px) handling at the page level: collapse the horizontal PaneGroup to a single terminal column on mobile and move the sidebar into the existing mobile Sheet/drawer pattern (web/CLAUDE.md references shadcn Sheet) rather than an inline pane. B. Clamp sidebarDefaultPct so the terminal pane can never collapse below a min width on narrow containers (pctFromPx +page.svelte:99-103), and force the sidebar pane to 0/hidden in concert with the existing Sidebar/Rail width:0 rules. C. TopBar: add flex-wrap or a mobile breakpoint that truncates/ellipsizes the breadcrumb (location name + #id) and/or hides lower-priority chrome below 768px. D. Disconnect screen .error (+page.svelte:736): add max-width / padding-inline / text-align:center / word-wrap so the message wraps within the viewport. (A/B address the connected-layout symptom; C the header; D the disconnect text. A single responsive pass likely touches all.) Files touched (candidates): web/src/routes/(authed)/terminal/+page.svelte (.terminal-layout, .login-screen/.error, PaneGroup, pctFromPx/sidebarDefaultPct); web/src/lib/components/TopBar.svelte (header/.left/.right/.breadcrumb); web/src/lib/components/sidebar/Sidebar.svelte and web/src/lib/components/terminal/Rail.svelte (existing @media max-width:767px rules — coordinate pane sizing with content collapse). Out of scope: telnet client rendering; desktop layout; any non-/terminal authed routes (login/register/characters) unless the same shared TopBar overflow manifests there (verify separately).
acceptance_criteria: On a ≤767px viewport (e.g. 390px iOS): (1) TopBar header fits without horizontal overflow or clipped text (breadcrumb truncates/ellipsizes or wraps; conn-pill fully visible); (2) the disconnect "Connection lost…" screen text wraps within the viewport with no left/right clipping; (3) the connected terminal column fills the available width (no narrow left strip, no large empty pane) with the sidebar collapsed/drawered; (4) command input + Composer hints are usable. Verify: cd web && pnpm dev, load /terminal in a 390px-wide responsive viewport (or add a Playwright mobile-viewport e2e asserting terminal column width ≥ ~90% of viewport and no horizontal page overflow).

title: TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_LockAcquisitionOrder_NoDeadlock hangs ~10min on gfo6-drain branch
description:
During PR #4198 autofix verification (task test:int -- ./internal/store/ -run TestMigrate), TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_LockAcquisitionOrder_NoDeadlock failed (unknown — likely panic or hang). Test took 602.923s (10+ minutes) suggesting deadlock detection or infinite wait.

Not caused by gfo6 autofix: the autofix only touched migrations (000040/000041 DO-block guards), approval/repo.go boundary semantics, and checkpoint.go TTL precision. None of these touch session_store.go or session connection code.

Likely candidates:
- Pre-existing on main, surfaced by the long-running gfo6-drain branch testing  
- Recent merge to main (#4189-#4194 cluster) introduced lock ordering regression
- Resource contention with other parallel test runs

Reproduce: task test:int -- ./internal/store/ -run TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_LockAcquisitionOrder_NoDeadlock

Per feedback_no_flakes_investigate: investigate, don't re-run. Read the test + identify the lock order vs application's actual lock order.

title: bootstrap_seed_secrets.py: multi-line paste corrupts SSH key via terminal bracketed-paste escapes
description:
**Goal:** scripts/bootstrap_seed_secrets.py validates DIGITALOCEAN_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY by writing the collected value to a temp file and running 'ssh-keygen -y -f'. When the operator PASTES the key into the multi-line prompt (collect_with_confirm allow_multiline=True, line 159-172), modern terminals in bracketed-paste mode wrap the pasted block in ESC[200~ ... ESC[201~ escape sequences. input() captures these into the first/last lines, corrupting the key's armor. Result: 'ssh-keygen -y failed: error in libcrypto: unsupported' even though the SAME key validates fine via 'ssh-keygen -y -f <file>' directly. Confirmed 2026-05-23: operator's DO key works standalone, fails when pasted (from 1Password) into the script. Env: OpenSSH 10.3p1 + OpenSSL 3.6.2, macOS.

**Fix (3 parts):**
1. (primary) Let the SSH private key be supplied by FILE PATH — read the file directly, sidestepping paste entirely. Recommended path in the prompt; blank falls back to paste.
2. (hardening) Strip CSI/bracketed-paste escape sequences (regex \x1b\[[0-9;?]*[ -/]*[@-~]) from each line in the multi-line reader, so any pasted multi-line secret survives.
3. (safety) In validate_do_ssh_private_key, write private_key.rstrip(newline)+newline to the temp file so the key always ends with exactly one trailing newline.

**Files touched:**
- Modify: scripts/bootstrap_seed_secrets.py (add re import; collect_with_confirm multiline strip; SSH-key call site file-path option; validate temp-file trailing newline)

**Not under the activation epic** (it's a tooling bug in a script, not sandbox-activation sequencing) — standalone, theme:deployment. Blocks operator task holomush-hryj.4 (seed-secrets dry-run) until fixed.

## Required Skills
dev-flow:requesting-code-review,jj:jujutsu
acceptance_criteria: Operator can supply DIGITALOCEAN_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY via a file path (recommended) OR paste; ssh-keygen -y validation passes for a key that validates fine standalone. Bracketed-paste escape sequences no longer corrupt multi-line input.
notes: Fix PR (branch holomush-9s4wv-seedsecrets-paste): _ANSI_CSI strip in multiline reader + trailing-newline normalize in validate. +4 regression tests; 25 pass; ruff clean. IMMEDIATE OPERATOR WORKAROUND (no merge needed): enter the key FILE PATH at the 'Path to private key file' prompt instead of pasting — the file-path branch already exists (line 256).

title: test: design proper D11 canonical lock-order test (current test is shape-insufficient)
description:
CodeRabbit feedback on PR #4191 round 6 thread internal/store/session_store_integration_test.go:177:

> 'TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_LockAcquisitionOrder_NoDeadlock does not actually prove the lock-order invariant. Both goroutine paths call the same UpdateSessionConnection(session, different-connection) shape, so this still passes even if the implementation locks session_connections before sessions. The test validates concurrent updates complete, not D11 canonical order.'

The critique is correct: two callers using the SAME lock order (whatever it is) won't deadlock with each other. To actually pin canonical (sessions FIRST, then session_connections), the test needs an adversary that takes locks in the OPPOSITE order:

1. Goroutine A: BEGIN; SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id=$S FOR UPDATE; sleep 100ms; COMMIT.
2. Goroutine B (adversary, raw SQL): BEGIN; SELECT * FROM session_connections WHERE id=$C FOR UPDATE; (sleep so M can race in); SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id=$S FOR UPDATE; COMMIT.
3. Goroutine M (impl under test): UpdateSessionConnection($S, $C, m).

If impl is canonical (S→SC): M blocks on A. When A commits, S free. M takes S, wants SC (held by B). B wants S (held by M). DEADLOCK. Postgres kills one (40P01 SQLSTATE).

If impl is reverse (SC→S): M blocks on B for SC. B finishes its A-blocked S acquisition, releases SC. M gets SC, takes S (free). NO deadlock.

Test asserts: deadlock error fires for either M or B (positive evidence of canonical order). If both complete without deadlock, FAIL.

Design risks: flake from timing (M may not race in at the right moment to interleave). Mitigation: run multiple iterations; require at least N deadlock observations across M iterations.

Alternative: use pg_locks system catalog introspection inside a probe transaction to assert M acquires sessions lock BEFORE session_connections lock. More deterministic, more invasive.

Defer to dedicated session because the design needs deliberation; the existing test still catches deadlock-from-canonical-callers-among-themselves, which is one valid invariant just not THE D11 invariant.

title: F-E12 chain-verification E2E flake: operator_read_completed audit row times out under load (10s Eventually too tight)
description:
## Symptom

`cmd/holomush/admin_read_stream_e2e_test.go::scenarioFE12ChainVerification` (line 2087) intermittently fails at line 2113:

```
[FAILED] Timed out after 10.001s.
F-E12: operator_read_completed row MUST be projected
Expected
    <int>: 0
to equal
    <int>: 1
```

Observed 2026-05-22 ~20:30 EDT during `task pr-prep` against a workspace whose only changes were unrelated Lua emit-table key renames (holomush-fz9h migration). Re-running `task test:int` immediately afterward with the SAME code passed cleanly: 1865 tests in 137s, including F-E12.

## Why this is a flake, not a regression

The triggering change (fz9h: `stream =` → `subject =` in plugins/ and `internal/plugin/lua/host_test.go`) is:
- Lexical key rename in Lua emit tables; semantics unchanged
- No effect on production code paths (test fixture changes are test-only)
- No effect on `cmd/holomush` E2E, audit projection, admin auth, or read-stream paths

Second pr-prep-equivalent run on same working copy passed clean. Definitionally a flake.

## Mechanism (grounded)

The test performs:

1. Seed 5 events (line 2088-2091)
2. Call `RunAdminReadStream` (line 2093-2098)
3. Assert ReadStarted/ReadFinished + TerminatedBy=CLIENT_EOF (line 2100-2103)
4. `Eventually` poll for `operator_read` audit row (line 2107-2110, 10s/200ms) — **passed both runs**
5. `Eventually` poll for `operator_read_completed` audit row (line 2111-2114, 10s/200ms) — **timed out on first run**
6. `chain.VerifyScope` (line 2116-2121)

The failure is specifically on the second poll. The completed-row audit event is emitted at session teardown after CLIENT_EOF. Under contention from concurrent integration suites (the full pr-prep lane runs ~28 suites in parallel — `internal/eventbus/crypto/dek` took 61s in the passing run, `internal/store` 132s), the audit projector queue can apparently exceed the 10s window between event publish and PG INSERT visibility.

## Related but distinct

- **`holomush-1nl7`** (P1, open) — `TestProjectionDrainsPublishedMessageToAuditTable` AwaitDrained cold-start race in unit-test path. Same general class (audit projection timing), distinct test + distinct failure mode ("no rows in result set" vs. `Eventually` timeout) + distinct root cause (consumer-sync window vs. projector-queue-under-load).

## Fix options

**A. Loosen the `Eventually` window** to 30s for the second poll, keep 10s for the first. Cheapest. Tradeoff: hides genuine projection-latency regressions behind a wider window, and 30s in a CI lane is real wall time.

**B. Switch the projection poll to a deterministic sync signal.** Wait for a specific projection-acked notification (e.g., a NotifyAudit channel the projector writes after INSERT commit) instead of polling `events_audit` row count. Removes the wall-clock dependency entirely. Requires a small projection-side hook + thread it into the test env (`adminAuthEnv`).

**C. Serialize the heavy crypto/store suites.** Lower wall-clock contention. Tradeoff: pr-prep wall time grows; doesn't fix the underlying race.

Recommend **B** — same shape as the fix the `1nl7` plan proposes for the cold-start race (anchor on actual progress signal rather than time). The two fixes could share a small helper API on `internal/eventbus/audit/Subsystem` if 1nl7 has landed first.

## Acceptance

1. Run F-E12 100/100 via `go test -count=100 -race -tags=integration -run 'scenarioFE12' ./cmd/holomush/...` under `task test:int`-equivalent load (or parallel with other heavy suites) — passes every time.
2. The fix MUST also keep F-E12's chain-verification semantics: both audit rows present + `VerifyScope` succeeds.
3. If fix is shape B (projection-sync signal), the same helper applies to the first poll (`operator_read` start row) and to other `operatorReadAuditCount` Eventually-poll callsites in the test file (line 872, 876, 1923, 2108, 2112).

## Files of interest

- `cmd/holomush/admin_read_stream_e2e_test.go:2087-2122` — the failing scenario
- `cmd/holomush/admin_read_stream_e2e_test.go:584-620` — `operatorReadAuditCount` helper
- `internal/eventbus/audit/projection.go` — projector that writes `events_audit` rows
- `internal/eventbus/audit/subsystem.go::AwaitDrained` — sibling sync primitive (subject of `holomush-1nl7`)

## Out of scope

- The underlying `AwaitDrained` race in unit-test path (covered by `holomush-1nl7`)
- Tuning audit-projection batch size or write-path latency in production (production doesn't poll for visibility; tests do)

title: QueryStreamHistory has same off-by-precision bug as Subscribe (iwzt.15); fixing it surfaces +page.svelte snapshot-apply-after-backfill UI race
description:
## Context

iwzt.15 (Subscribe filter-at-delivery, see PR landing this bead) fixed the off-by-precision bug at internal/grpc/server.go::dispatchDelivery by truncating the scope floor to microseconds before comparison, matching the publisher's µs truncation per INV-P7-16. The same bug exists at internal/grpc/query_stream_history.go:241 (`scopeFloor := streamScopeFloor(info, req.Stream)` — no .Truncate(time.Microsecond)).

The fix was attempted in the iwzt.15 PR but reverted because it deterministically broke web/e2e/terminal.spec.ts:136 (presence list shows self and other connections). The presence test PASSED with just the Subscribe-path fix but FAILED when QueryStreamHistory's floor was also µs-truncated — across 2 consecutive full pr-prep runs, panel showed 2 unrelated character names (different each run, from earlier tests in the suite) and the test's own characters were missing.

## Root cause hypothesis

QueryStreamHistory drives the terminal's backfill loop. With ns-precision floor, events emitted in the same µs as LocationArrivedAt are excluded from backfill (silently — wrong, but the bug). With µs-truncated floor, those same-µs events are included → backfill is slightly heavier.

Backfill runs BEFORE snapshot-apply in web/src/routes/(authed)/terminal/+page.svelte:386-451 (finally block). When backfill is heavier under accumulated-prior-test-session load, snapshot-apply is delayed past the test's 10s timeout, AND something about backfill-event-replay corrupts the presence panel rendering (the test's own characters are absent while 2 prior-test characters are present, which is suspicious — that suggests presence is being populated by backfilled arrive events rather than from the snapshot, and only the LAST 2 events make it before the assertion times out).

## Acceptance

1. internal/grpc/query_stream_history.go::QueryStreamHistory: `scopeFloor := streamScopeFloor(info, req.Stream).Truncate(time.Microsecond)` — symmetric with the Subscribe-path fix.
2. Add unit test TestQueryStreamHistoryScopeFloorTruncatedToMicrosecond mirroring the Subscribe-path test (see TestDispatchDeliveryForwardsEventTruncatedWithinSameMicrosecondAsFloor in internal/grpc/subscribe_loop_test.go).
3. Investigate and fix the +page.svelte UI race so the presence panel is populated from the SNAPSHOT, not from backfill replay. Snapshot-apply MUST be the source of truth for the panel; backfill arrive/leave events should only UPDATE the panel additively (already what mirrorMovementPresence does on paper, but the failure suggests something about backfill ordering corrupts this). Likely fix: apply snapshot BEFORE backfill loop, not after; backfill-time arrive events then idempotently re-confirm what's already in the panel.
4. web/e2e/terminal.spec.ts:136 passes under full pr-prep lane with both precision fixes active.
5. Add a higher-level pr-prep tripwire test that asserts the presence panel for a session that connects late shows snapshot entries even when many prior sessions are at the same location.

## Out of scope

- Session cleanup between e2e tests (separate concern; sessions persist across tests by design)
- Backfill performance tuning (separate concern)

## Why P1 not P0

The Subscribe-path fix is sufficient to make Tier 2 work correctly for steady-state event flow. QueryStreamHistory's off-by-precision bug only surfaces for events emitted within the same µs as session-create / move / focus-join — a narrow window. iwzt.15 closes the load-bearing privacy gate; this bead closes the symmetric pull-side gate plus the UI brittleness it exposes.

title: TestProjectionDrainsPublishedMessageToAuditTable flake — AwaitDrained cold-start race
description:
Goal: Eliminate the cold-start race in internal/eventbus/audit AwaitDrained that causes intermittent integration-test failures on PRs.

Symptom: TestProjectionDrainsPublishedMessageToAuditTable fails with 'no rows in result set' on projection_test.go:159 after sub.AwaitDrained returns. Observed today on PR #4088 (otlptracegrpc bump, head d54fe0d09e) and PR #4130 (npm batch, head 8e9f5e3e) — same parent commit on main (#4049). Main's own CI passed integration for #4049 just minutes earlier, so this is timing-dependent.

Root cause: internal/eventbus/audit/projection.go:300-311 awaitDrained polls consumer.Info() and returns when NumPending==0 && NumAckPending==0. When AwaitDrained is called immediately after publishTestMessage, the consumer's view can briefly report 0/0 before the consumer has synced with the stream and observed the just-published message. The first poll returns 'drained' (false positive); the test then queries events_audit before the projection's INSERT has run.

Likely trigger: nats-server v2.14.0 bump (#4126, 18:17Z today) may have widened the consumer-sync window enough to surface a long-latent race.

Files touched: internal/eventbus/audit/projection.go (awaitDrained), internal/eventbus/audit/subsystem.go (Subsystem.AwaitDrained wrapper signature stays the same).

Out of scope: production projection behavior (this is a test helper); plugin-owned subject skip path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. TestProjectionDrainsPublishedMessageToAuditTable passes 100/100 with -count=100 under task test:int.
2. TestProjectionResumesAfterRestart still passes (must not break the durable-resume case where AckFloor is already at LastSeq with no new publishes).
3. All other AwaitDrained call sites in projection_test.go pass without change.
4. The fix references the actual stream's LastSeq (or equivalent progress signal) rather than just consumer pending counts, so the wait condition is anchored to actual progress.

title: Comprehensive features/usage/admin/operator/player documentation under site/docs/
description:
## Goal

Audit, consolidate, and complete the user-facing documentation under `site/docs/` — the public docs site rendered via zensical. Four audiences, four-ish current subdirs:

- `site/docs/guide/` — players and game designers
- `site/docs/operating/` — admins/server runners
- `site/docs/extending/` — plugin developers
- `site/docs/contributing/` — codebase contributors
- `site/docs/reference/` — auto-generated API + event refs

Each subdir is incomplete to varying degrees and inconsistent in style/depth.

## Scope

### Audit

Survey `site/docs/**/*.md` and produce per-audience inventories:

**Players** (`guide/`):
- What commands exist? What does each do?
- Character creation, login, character switching
- Movement, communication (say, pose, whisper, page, channels), looking, examining
- Scene system: create, join, leave, set, end
- Quit, logout, multi-tab behavior
- What happens on page reload / WiFi drop (link to lifecycle doc)
- Game-state expectations: what persists, what doesn't

**Game designers** (`guide/`):
- Content authoring: locations, exits, objects, characters
- Plugin commands available for designers
- Setting plugins (configuration-only)
- Scene templates / RP conventions

**Admins/operators** (`operating/`):
- Server config (every env var + flag, what it does, default value)
- Deployment (currently has sandbox-operations.md, sandbox-restore.md)
- Database migrations
- Plugin loading + admin (`plugin list`, `plugin info`)
- Session lifecycle from an operator standpoint (link to lifecycle doc)
- Monitoring: where to look for problems
- Backup + recovery (sandbox-restore.md exists)
- Audit log access
- Player administration: reset password, kick, ban (admin commands)
- Reaper / TTL tuning
- Capacity planning + scale considerations

**Plugin developers** (`extending/`):
- Plugin types (lua, binary, setting) — when to use which
- Manifest schema (current schema at `schemas/plugin.schema.json`)
- Lifecycle: Init, requires/provides, storage
- Event emission patterns + history_scope (link to iwzt history-scope spec)
- ABAC integration
- Testing harnesses for plugins

**Contributors** (`contributing/`):
- PR guide (current: pr-guide.md, pr-prep.md)
- Sessions / jj workspaces (current: sessions.md)
- Database migrations guide (current: database-migrations.md)
- Testing conventions (link to .claude/rules/testing.md)
- ABAC: how to add a policy, how to add a resource type
- Adding event types
- Adding commands

### Consolidate

For each audience, produce:
- An index page that orients the audience
- Sub-pages organized by task
- Cross-links to neighboring audiences ("if you're an admin and want to plug in a custom plugin, see extending/")

### Test the docs

- Each command reference has a verified example.
- Each config flag has its default + valid range documented.
- Each migration explains what it changes + rollback semantics.

## Why P1

The first thing a player, admin, or plugin author needs is a coherent on-ramp. Today we have islands — sandbox-operations.md is excellent for sandbox.holomush.dev but a self-host operator finds little. Players get no command reference. Plugin authors can read CLAUDE.md fragments but no clean tutorial.

This is what makes HoloMUSH a project people can adopt rather than just contribute to.

## Acceptance

- `task docs:build` succeeds; `task docs:serve` shows the new structure.
- Each audience subdir has a populated index with ≥ 5 substantive sub-pages.
- Every command in the player guide actually exists in current code (verified by an integration test or build-time check).
- Every config option in the operating guide has a corresponding hit in `internal/...` config code.
- A new player can join a sandbox server, find the player guide, and learn `say` / `pose` / `look` / `go` / `who` in < 5 minutes.
- A new self-host operator can find deployment docs and a list of every env var.
- A new plugin author can build a hello-world plugin from the extending docs alone.

## Companion bead

This is the player/admin/operator-facing leg. The contributor/maintainer-facing leg is `holomush-k7qy` (system design documentation). The two cross-link extensively.

## Out of scope

- Implementation work — pure docs.
- Migration of doc tooling (still zensical).

## Source

Identified during the holomush-lj2r investigation on 2026-05-17. The session lifecycle doc gap surfaced a broader question: do contributors, admins, plugin authors, and players have a coherent doc set today? Audit revealed: not really.
notes:
Note (2026-05-17): holomush-bgxg (session lifecycle reference doc — operator + contributor audience) is a natural first child of this epic. Companion epic: holomush-k7qy (system design docs). Both should cross-link extensively.
RE-SCOPED (2026-05-29 docs audit). theme:docs-platform (SP1 Astro Starlight migration, SP2 Diátaxis IA, SP5 editorial pass) substantially fulfilled this epic. Content moved site/docs/ → site/src/content/docs/. Re-verified: operating/, extending/, contributing/, reference/ audiences are above threshold. REMAINING GAP: guide/ has only 4 substantive pages (fd site/src/content/docs/guide -e md: the-world, building, connecting, commands) vs acceptance ≥5. Narrowed scope = add ≥1 substantive player guide page (e.g. scene-system walkthrough, character-switching how-to, or link-drop/multi-tab lifecycle) under site/src/content/docs/guide/. All other per-audience acceptance items met. Stale path in title (site/docs/) → site/src/content/docs/.

title: Comprehensive system design documentation: consolidate + complete
description:
## Goal

Audit, consolidate, and complete the HoloMUSH system design documentation. Today the architecture-relevant material is scattered across:

- `docs/superpowers/specs/` (AI-skill-generated specs — many one-shot, no cross-referencing)
- `docs/specs/` (older, hand-authored design docs)
- `docs/plans/` and `docs/superpowers/plans/` (implementation plans, not architecture)
- `docs/adr/` (architecture decision records)
- `docs/roadmap.md` (strategic themes)
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-01-18-holomush-roadmap-design.md` (system overview)
- `docs/repository-audit/` (point-in-time architectural snapshots)
- `.claude/rules/` (project conventions; partly architectural)
- Scattered package-level READMEs and CLAUDE.md files

A contributor or maintainer joining the project today has no single canonical entry point into 'what is the system, how do its pieces fit, what are the boundaries'.

## Scope

### Audit

Survey all of the above directories and produce an inventory:

- Each doc's purpose, audience, current accuracy (verify against code at `path:line`)
- Overlaps and contradictions across docs
- Gaps (architecturally-relevant topics with no doc)
- Stale specs (design landed; implementation diverged; or design rejected; or spec superseded)
- Mark each as: KEEP / ARCHIVE / MERGE-INTO-X / REWRITE

### Consolidate

Produce a canonical system-design hierarchy. Recommended structure (subject to plan-stage refinement):

- `docs/architecture/overview.md` — single-page "what is HoloMUSH" entry point; C4 context diagram
- `docs/architecture/containers.md` — C4 container view (core server, gateway, web, telnet, plugins, bus, DB)
- `docs/architecture/components/` — one doc per major component (eventbus, session, world, plugin, access, auth, focus, ...) with: purpose, key interfaces, invariants, neighbors
- `docs/architecture/invariants.md` — cross-system invariants enumerated by ID (I-PRIV-*, I-S5, etc.)
- `docs/architecture/lifecycles/` — session lifecycle (holomush-bgxg), plugin lifecycle, character lifecycle, scene lifecycle, event lifecycle
- `docs/architecture/streams-and-subjects.md` — NATS subject taxonomy, legacy stream-name format, history scopes
- `docs/architecture/glossary.md` — terminology (location vs. room, character vs. player, etc.)

ADRs stay in `docs/adr/`. Implementation plans stay in `docs/plans/`. Superpowers specs stay where they are but get linked-from / marked-superseded as appropriate.

### Cross-reference

Each architecture doc links to:
- The ADRs that shaped it
- The specs that drove its current implementation
- The package-level CLAUDE.md or README that says "see architecture doc"
- The relevant invariants

### Diagrams

Use mermaid for: C4 context, C4 container, component-level sequence diagrams for the load-bearing flows (Subscribe, QueryStreamHistory, MoveCharacter, SelectCharacter reattach, plugin Init, eventbus dispatch).

## Why P1

Today's investigation of holomush-lj2r took hours partly because the session-lifecycle invariants are spread across 4+ specs with no canonical source of truth. A new contributor (human or AI) onboarding to non-trivial work in the eventbus / session / privacy area has no architectural starting point.

This is the substrate documentation that makes every subsequent epic faster.

## Acceptance

- Inventory document produced; every existing design doc has a verdict.
- Canonical hierarchy populated; each major component has a doc.
- C4 context + container diagrams render cleanly via `task docs:build`.
- Cross-reference web links validated (no broken links to ADRs, specs, code paths).
- Glossary covers every term in `.claude/rules/terminology.md` and any other ambiguous terms surfaced during the audit.
- A new contributor can start at `docs/architecture/overview.md` and navigate to any other architectural detail in ≤ 3 clicks.

## Companion bead

This is the contributor/maintainer-facing leg. The player/admin/operator-facing leg is filed separately. The two should cross-link extensively.

## Out of scope

- Plugin developer documentation (separate epic — `site/docs/extending/`).
- API reference (auto-generated under `site/docs/reference/`).
- This epic does NOT touch implementation — pure docs.

## Source

Identified during the holomush-lj2r investigation on 2026-05-17. The privacy spec's reattach semantics conflicted with the unified session lifecycle; with no consolidated architecture doc, the conflict went undetected through 6 PRs and broke main CI for several hours.
notes: Note (2026-05-17): holomush-bgxg (session lifecycle diagram + reference doc) is a natural first child of this epic when materialized via plan-to-beads. Companion epic: holomush-rm9g (user-facing docs). Both should cross-link extensively.

title: iwzt amended-spec privacy tests: ReattachWithinTTL + TTLExpiryFreshFloor
description:
## Goal

Implement the two integration tests that the holomush-iwzt history-scope privacy spec §8 calls out as the iwzt-spec amendment landed in PR #4050 (holomush-lj2r):

- `TestPrivacy_ReattachWithinTTLPreservesFloor` — character A connects at T0 in location L (LocationArrivedAt=T0), third party emits events at T1, A's transport drops at T2 (status=Detached), third party emits events at T3, A's `Subscribe.ReattachCAS` at T4 (within TTL); A's subsequent history query for `location:L` returns events with timestamps in [T0, T4] — INCLUDING the T1 and T3 events. The session row stayed alive through the disconnect; floor preserved at T0.

- `TestPrivacy_TTLExpiryEndsSessionFreshFloor` — character A connects at T0, A's transport drops at T1, no reattach for TTL+1, reaper deletes the row at T2; A logs in again at T3 → fresh `SelectCharacter` creates a new session with `LocationArrivedAt=T3`; events with timestamps in [T0, T3) are NOT visible to the new session.

These are the two-sided assertion of the spec §2 / §5 / I-PRIV-3 amendment: reattach within TTL preserves the floor (events during disconnect ARE visible); reattach after TTL expiry is a new session (events during absence are NOT visible).

## Scope

- Tests live in `test/integration/privacy/privacy_test.go` next to the I-PRIV-7 placeholder.
- Use `privacytest` harness (iwzt.6); may require filling in stub helpers (`DetachTransport`, `ExpireSession`, `QueryStreamHistory`).
- Both tests reference the spec §8 case names verbatim so the meta-test in spec §8 can find them.

## Acceptance

- `task test:int -- -run TestPrivacy_(ReattachWithinTTLPreservesFloor|TTLExpiryEndsSessionFreshFloor) ./test/integration/privacy/...` passes.
- Both tests fail if the spec §2 / §5 / I-PRIV-3 amendment is reverted (i.e., they're load-bearing assertions of the corrected rule).

## Dependencies

- PR #4050 merged (holomush-lj2r — the spec amendment + implementation revert).
- Possibly fills in privacytest harness stubs that iwzt.6 left as TODO panics (`DetachTransport`, `ExpireSession`, `QueryStreamHistory`).
- Could be subsumed by holomush-izk0 (comprehensive test matrix) if a single PR lands both. Otherwise this is a tight, focused PR.

## Source

Named explicitly in `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-17-history-scope-privacy-design.md` §8 (post-2026-05-18 amendment via PR #4050 / holomush-lj2r).

title: Comprehensive session-lifecycle Ginkgo test matrix
description:
## Goal

Stand up the empty `test/integration/session/session_persistence_suite_test.go` suite with an explicit matrix of cases covering every session-lifecycle transition × identity-type (regular character / guest) × transport (web / telnet). Today the suite has 107 lines of bootstrap and zero Describe/It blocks.

## Scope

The matrix below MUST be fully covered. Each cell either gets a Ginkgo `It` block in the new suite or is explicitly marked "covered elsewhere" with a pointer to the existing test.

| Transition | Web guest | Web regular char | Telnet | Multi-session |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Fresh `SelectCharacter` → Active | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | n/a |
| Reattach within TTL (SelectCharacter path) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Reattach within TTL (Subscribe.ReattachCAS) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Detach (drop all connections) → status=Detached + TTL | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | n/a |
| Reaper sweep at TTL expiry → row deleted | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | n/a |
| Post-TTL re-login → fresh session (new LocationArrivedAt) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | n/a |
| `quit` command → row deleted | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | n/a |
| Explicit logout → row deleted | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Admin boot → row deleted | n/a | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Character move while attached → LocationArrivedAt advances | n/a | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Tmux-style telnet reattach (same playerSession, new connection) | n/a | n/a | ✓ | n/a |
| WiFi blip (transport-level drop + reconnect, no logout) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

## Anti-redundancy

`test/integration/auth/multi_tab_test.go` already covers a lot of the multi-session column (two-tab guest, same-character-two-tabs, two-characters-one-player, logout cascade, Subscribe post-logout). The new suite MUST NOT duplicate — instead, the matrix table above can cite `multi_tab_test.go` for those cells.

## Acceptance

- `task test:int -- ./test/integration/session/...` runs ≥ 15 specs.
- Each row in the matrix above has at least one passing spec or an explicit `covered elsewhere` pointer.
- Spec IDs from the iwzt history-scope-privacy spec (e.g., I-PRIV-3) are asserted by-ID in the relevant specs.
- The two named privacy tests from spec §8 (`TestPrivacy_ReattachWithinTTLPreservesFloor` + `TestPrivacy_TTLExpiryEndsSessionFreshFloor`) live here.

## Dependencies

- privacytest harness from iwzt.6 (already on main, with stub helpers — some may need filling in: `DetachTransport`, `ReattachTransport`, `ExpireSession`, `QueryStreamHistory`).
- Note: harness fill-ins for `MoveTo`, `WaitForEvent`, `QueryStreamHistory` are TODO panics today (per iwzt.6 worker report). Filling them in is part of this task's scope OR a precursor bead.

## Source

Discovered while investigating holomush-lj2r on 2026-05-17. The lj2r flake would have been caught pre-merge by a "reattach within TTL preserves floor" integration test.

title: Session lifecycle diagram + site reference doc
description:
## Goal

Produce a canonical session-lifecycle reference: a mermaid state diagram + a `site/docs/reference/session-lifecycle.md` document consumable by **both** contributors writing code that touches session state AND admins/game runners who need to know what `quit` vs `logout` vs TTL expiry mean for player experience.

## Scope

### Diagram (mermaid stateDiagram-v2)

Cover all of:

- `Active` ↔ `Idle` (per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-session-lifecycle-as-events-design.md; verify this transition even exists in current code — it may be vestigial)
- `Active` → `Detached` (transport disconnect; expires_at=now+TTL, default 1800s) — `internal/grpc/server.go::Disconnect:1299-1318`
- `Detached` → `Active` (reattach within TTL) — `auth_handlers.go::SelectCharacter` reattach branch + `server.go::Subscribe` ReattachCAS branch
- `Detached` → reaped (TTL expiry, row deleted) — `internal/session/reaper.go`
- `Active` → row-deleted via quit / logout / boot / kicked / evicted
- Multi-session: same character, multiple sessions, each with independent state

Show explicit transition labels (which code site fires the transition, which event is emitted, what the cause string is).

### Site doc structure

`site/docs/reference/session-lifecycle.md` — the canonical reference. Two audiences:

**For admins/game runners ("what does this mean for my players?"):**
- When does a session end vs. detach? (table)
- What's the TTL? (default 1800s; configurable per session via `info.TTLSeconds`)
- What happens to player scrollback across reload / WiFi blip / tmux reattach? (answer: preserved within TTL — link to iwzt history-scope privacy)
- What happens when a guest disconnects? (currently: same as regular char, holomush-hfvc is the future change)
- What's the difference between `quit`, `logout`, admin boot, and reaper sweep? (cause table)
- Operator knobs: TTL, reaper interval

**For contributors ("what's the contract when I touch session code?"):**
- The session row is the unit of continuity (link iwzt history-scope spec §2)
- LocationArrivedAt advances on session-create + character-move only (link spec §5)
- Don't bump the floor on transport reattach (link spec §5 rows 2 + 3)
- Multi-session isolation (link 2026-04-25-multi-tab-session-isolation-design)
- Cause taxonomy: quit / logout / guest_end / kicked / reaped / evicted

Cross-link from `site/docs/operating/` and `site/docs/contributing/` indexes.

### Mermaid in the spec too

Also embed the diagram in `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-session-lifecycle-as-events-design.md` so the spec carries its own authoritative picture.

## Acceptance

- `site/docs/reference/session-lifecycle.md` ships with the mermaid diagram + the two-audience sections.
- Linked from `site/docs/operating/` and `site/docs/contributing/` indexes.
- Spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-session-lifecycle-as-events-design.md` embeds the same diagram.
- `task docs:build` succeeds; mermaid renders correctly on the local docs server.

## Source

Discovered while investigating holomush-lj2r (E2E flake / floor-reset-on-reattach issue) on 2026-05-17. The lifecycle has subtle behaviors scattered across 4+ spec docs; new contributors and admins need a single canonical reference.
notes: Note (2026-05-17): This task is naturally the first child of either holomush-k7qy (system design docs epic) or holomush-rm9g (user docs epic) when those epics are materialized via plan-to-beads. Per the bd epic-blocks-only-epic rule, no dep edge was created between this task and those epics — but conceptually bgxg ships the session-lifecycle subset of both efforts.

title: Wire active session-consumer GC on quit (DeleteConsumer subscriber)
description:
Audit reference: docs/repository-audit/2026-05-13/design-alignment-review.md §1.2

## Problem
JetStream spec §4 "Session GC" lines 577-583 requires an internal subscriber to `events.session.*.lifecycle` that calls `js.DeleteConsumer("session_<id>")` immediately on quit. Only the passive `InactiveThreshold = 24h` path exists.

Verification: `rg -n "DeleteConsumer.*session" internal/` returns no production hits. The only mention is a comment at internal/eventbus/subscriber.go:158 pointing forward to "the session-lifecycle listener in F5."

## Impact
Operational debt — consumer state and filestore pressure linger after every quit. Especially bad on rapid-reconnect telnet flows.

## Fix
Implement the subscriber:
- ~30 LOC + test
- Listen on `events.<game>.session.*.lifecycle`
- On `session_ended`, call `js.DeleteConsumer("session_<id>")` (best-effort)
- The passive 24h threshold remains as the safety net

## Why P1
Spec-mandated, not implemented. Operational debt that grows with traffic. Small fix, real win.

title: Move plugin-domain payload structs out of internal/core/event.go
description:
Audit references:
- docs/repository-audit/2026-05-13/design-alignment-review.md §1.1
- docs/repository-audit/2026-05-13/layer-review.md §F-1.1

## Problem
JetStream design spec §1b lines 200-222 mandates: "The plugin-domain payload structs at internal/core/event.go:74-143 ... MUST also move out of internal/core/."

Current state: internal/core/event.go:68-139 still defines:
- LocationStatePayload, LocationStateInfo, LocationStateExit, LocationStateChar
- ExitUpdatePayload
- PagePayload, WhisperPayload, WhisperNoticePayload, OOCPayload, PemitPayload

Direct host code still constructs these — internal/grpc/location_follow.go:219 builds core.LocationStatePayload{}.

This is the single largest unfixed drift per the audit, and directly contradicts the CLAUDE.md "Plugin Boundary" invariant.

## Fix
Move each payload struct to the owning plugin package:
- communication payloads (PagePayload, WhisperPayload, WhisperNoticePayload, OOCPayload, PemitPayload) → plugins/core-communication/events.go (already exists)
- location/exit state payloads (LocationStatePayload, ExitUpdatePayload, related) → probably stay near the gateway translator OR move to a plugins/core-locations/ package per JetStream §1d

Update callers:
- internal/grpc/location_follow.go:219
- internal/web/translate_test.go:227,273,302,320,334,353
- internal/grpc/dispatcher_test.go:77
- internal/grpc/pipeline_rendering_test.go:257

## Why P1
Spec mandate + boundary-rule violation called out by TWO independent audit reports. Mechanical move (struct definitions + import updates + tests) but spans multiple packages.

## Alternative
If a decision exists to retain some of these host-side, amend JetStream spec §1b with an ADR explaining why. The audit notes this would be the second-best outcome.

description:

title: [E5.8.1] Align auth spec with implementation — schema/expiry/migration-number drift
description:
Re-filed from holomush-odos (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-dwk).

## Current State

The auth spec (`docs/specs/2026-01-25-auth-identity-design.md`) was written as a design document before implementation. Implementation diverged in several areas (appropriately), but the spec was not updated.

## Gaps Found

**1. Web Session Schema (lines 73-89):**
- Spec: `character_id NOT NULL` → Implementation: nullable (character selected after login)
- Spec: `token_signature` column → Implementation: `token_hash`
- Spec: `last_active_at` column → Implementation: `last_seen_at`
- Missing: `user_agent` and `ip_address` columns added in implementation

**2. Session Expiry (line 154):**
- Spec: "30-day expiry, refresh on activity"
- Implementation: 24-hour expiry (`SessionTokenExpiry = 24 * time.Hour`)

**3. Migration Numbers:**
- Spec references: 000009, 000010, 000011
- Actual: consolidated into 000001_baseline.up.sql

**Note:** HMAC token design discrepancy (lines 146-155) tracked separately — see audit-finding bead for cri1 (HMAC design ADR).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Web session schema in spec matches `internal/auth/session.go:WebSession` struct
- [ ] Session expiry duration corrected to 24 hours
- [ ] Migration numbering updated (consolidated baseline)
- [ ] Column names match implementation (token_hash, last_seen_at)
- [ ] Cross-reference the HMAC ADR once it's filed

title: [E5.8.2] Document HMAC token design decision (implementation diverged from spec)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-cri1 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-dwk).

## Current State

The auth spec describes a signed token design using HMAC-SHA256:
```
token = base64(payload) + "." + base64(hmac_sha256(payload, secret))
payload = {player_id, character_id, issued_at, expires_at}
```

The implementation uses a simpler approach:
- Generate 32 random bytes via crypto/rand
- Store SHA256(token) in database
- Send plaintext token to client
- Validate by hashing received token and looking up in DB

## Questions to Document (ADR)

1. **Why was HMAC not implemented?**
   - Deliberate security decision? Performance? Simplicity?
2. **Security implications:**
   - Current approach requires DB lookup for every validation
   - HMAC approach is stateless (no DB lookup)
   - Current approach allows server-side session invalidation
   - HMAC approach would require token blacklist for invalidation
3. **Should the spec be updated or the implementation changed?**

## Acceptance
- ADR filed at docs/adr/ documenting the chosen design and reasoning
- Spec docs/specs/2026-01-25-auth-identity-design.md updated to match (related to holomush-odos)

title: E5.7.1 Telnet connect observability gap — refreshCharacterList errors swallowed
description:
Re-filed from holomush-snq5 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-dwk).

Verified 2026-05-16: internal/telnet/gateway_handler.go refreshCharacterList at lines 779-782: when ListCharacters fails, sends "Failed to refresh character list." to client but no slog call. Operators blind to this failure.

Note: refreshCharacterList runs on every QUIT path, so failures here mask DB issues that affect every disconnect.

Original observation: auth_handler.go:119-130 swallows errors without logging when character listing fails after successful login.

Fix: add slog.ErrorContext (or errutil.LogError) with operation/session_id/error context so operators have visibility.

title: XSS: ansi_up output used with @html in AnsiRenderer.svelte — input not pre-escaped
Fix resolver/provider entity ID format mismatch (blocks ABAC integration tests)
description:
## Goal

Fix the entity ID format mismatch between `Resolver` and providers. The `Resolver` strips the entity type prefix before passing to providers (e.g., `character:01ABC` becomes `01ABC`), but real providers (`CharacterProvider`, `LocationProvider`) re-parse expecting the full `type:id` format.

**Symptom:** Unit tests pass because they use mocks that accept any format. Integration tests (T30 full-pipeline) expose this mismatch and fail.

**Status:** Re-filed 2026-05-15 as top-level standalone (previously `holomush-5k1.601`, child of now-closed Epic 7 epic `holomush-5k1`).

## Design reference

- Original bead: `holomush-5k1.601` (filed 2026-03-16, post-PR-#114 integration testing).
- Audit reports referencing this: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1316-5k1-batch19.md` (flagged as live post-ship bug); `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1349-phase77-impl-tasks.md` (T30 blocker).
- Code references: `internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go:323` passes `entityRef` through `safeResolve` to `ResolveSubject`/`ResolveResource`; `CharacterProvider.resolve()` calls `access.ParseEntityRef()` internally — design tension here.

## Plan reference

Two options:

- **Option A:** Pass full `type:id` from resolver to providers. Providers continue to use `access.ParseEntityRef()`.
- **Option B:** Make providers accept bare IDs (resolver strips prefix; providers don't re-parse).

The integration test failure pattern needs to be reproduced first to confirm which layer breaks. Then pick the option that doesn't require changing the AttributeProvider interface contract widely.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- The T30 full-pipeline integration test (`test/integration/access/evaluation_test.go`) passes end-to-end with real providers (not mocks).
- A new unit-level test pins the resolver↔provider format contract (whichever option wins).
- Existing unit tests using mocks still pass.

## Verification steps

1. `task test:int -- ./test/integration/access/...` green.
2. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/...` green.
3. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go` (entity ref handling)
- `internal/access/policy/attribute/character.go`, `location.go`, etc. (provider re-parse logic — if Option B)
- Test files

## Dependencies

- Blocks: integration test work that depends on full-pipeline ABAC eval (T30 — now tracked as separate top-level bead per 2026-05-15 audit consolidation).

## Out of scope

- Re-shaping the AttributeProvider interface broadly.
- Other resolver/provider concerns (separate beads if found).

title: ABAC integration test fixtures missing engine wiring
description:
## Goal

Wire an ABAC engine into the plugin integration test fixtures. Currently `test/integration/plugin/integration_test.go:50` and `communication_integration_test.go:46` both call `hostfunc.New(nil)` with no engine — meaning the hostfunc→ABAC enforcement path is never exercised at the integration level. A regression that disconnects hostfunc from the engine would pass all integration tests undetected.

**Note:** distinct from `holomush-jsna` (unit-level engine-error branch coverage in `checkKVAccess`). This bead is about integration-level wiring of the enforcement path.

## Design reference

- Originally filed as `holomush-gqjb.11`. Promoted 2026-05-15.
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1106-gqjb-children.md`.

## Plan reference

Direct fix:

1. Pass a real ABAC engine (or a `policytest.AllowAllEngine` with explicit deny rules) into `hostfunc.New` in both integration fixtures.
2. Add at least one assertion that an unauthorized plugin operation is denied at integration level.
3. Consider extracting a shared `WireABACForIntegration(t)` helper if multiple fixtures need it.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- Both integration tests now construct `hostfunc.New(realEngine)` (or `policytest`-wired engine).
- Existing tests still pass with the wired engine.
- A new integration assertion verifies an unauthorized op is denied.

## Verification steps

1. `task test:int -- ./test/integration/plugin/...` green.
2. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `test/integration/plugin/integration_test.go:50`
- `test/integration/plugin/communication_integration_test.go:46`

## Dependencies

- None.

## Out of scope

- Unit-level KV checkKVAccess engine-error coverage (covered by `holomush-jsna`).

title: ABAC engine: SESSION_INVALID PolicyID should use deny: prefix, not infra:
description:
## Goal

Fix `internal/access/policy/engine.go` Evaluate Step 2 — SESSION_INVALID errors produce a decision with PolicyID `"infra:session-invalid"`. `IsInfraFailure()` checks `strings.HasPrefix(policyID, "infra:")`, so SESSION_INVALID is incorrectly classified as an infrastructure failure. This propagates spurious error logs and failure metrics to every call site (`world/service.go::checkAccess`, `command/access.go::CheckCapability`, plugin host).

**Status note:** Originally filed as `holomush-wfza.21`. Sub-fix `holomush-wfza.26` was created and CLOSED but the code change NEVER LANDED — bead-auditor caught this on 2026-04-26 and again on 2026-05-15 as a FALSE-FIX pattern. The session expiry is still classified as infra failure in current code.

## Design reference

- Originally filed as `holomush-wfza.21` (with fix-bead `wfza.26`). Promoted 2026-05-15 due to false-fix pattern.
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-supplement-wfza.md`.
- Cross-link: `holomush-eulm.9` was the call-site test for the reclassification, also previously deferred to wfza.21.

## Plan reference

Direct fix (this time, actually land the change):

1. Change `engine.go:197` from `"infra:session-invalid"` to `"deny:session-invalid"` (or chosen `deny:` prefix variant).
2. Update test `engine_test.go:261` assertion.
3. Verify every call site's `IsInfraFailure()` branch no longer fires for session-invalid (audit log shows DENY, not infra failure; no `observability.RecordEngineFailure` call).
4. **Verify the merge actually contains the change** before closing this bead — do not repeat the wfza.21→wfza.26 false-fix.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- New unit test asserts `Evaluate()` returns a decision with PolicyID starting with `"deny:"` (not `"infra:"`) for SESSION_INVALID.
- Existing tests that previously expected `infra:session-invalid` updated to expect `deny:session-invalid`.
- An integration test exercising session-expiry → access-check → audit-log verifies the audit row records this as a normal deny, not as a backend error.
- Add a regression test that fails if the PolicyID ever regresses to `infra:`.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/...` green.
2. `rg '"infra:session-invalid"' internal/` returns ZERO hits.
3. `task test:int` covering session-expiry flows green.
4. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/engine.go:197`
- `internal/access/policy/engine_test.go:261` (and any other assertions)
- Tests exercising `world/service.go::checkAccess`, `command/access.go::CheckCapability`

## Dependencies

- None. Direct fix.

## Out of scope

- Other `IsInfraFailure` classification gaps (separate concern).

title: ABAC Phase 7.7 integration & unit test gaps
description:
## Goal

Close 5 specific test-coverage gaps in the Phase 7.7 ABAC implementation surfaced by the 2026-05-15 audit. Each gap is a missing test that the spec required.

## Design reference

- Originally filed as 5 separate beads under `holomush-fxa9`: `fxa9.27`, `fxa9.31`, `fxa9.33`, `fxa9.35`, `fxa9.44`.
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1019-fxa9-pr114.md`.

## Plan reference

Five scoped test additions:

### 1. Circuit-breaker call-window pruning (`fxa9.27`)

- Code: `circuit_breaker.go:184` — `pruneOldCalls` exists but is untested directly.
- Test: verify that old calls outside the window are evicted before the trip decision is made; assert the eviction happens at expected boundary.

### 2. `evalAccess` integration helper error swallowing (`fxa9.31`)

- Code: `test/integration/access/access_suite_test.go:199-201` — `evalAccess` calls `env.engine.Evaluate(...)` then `Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())` and returns decision regardless.
- Fix: either propagate the error to the test, or only assert NoError when test explicitly expects success. Audit found the helper hides engine failures.

### 3. `DegradedMode` idempotency + concurrent entry (`fxa9.33`)

- Code: `engine.go:44` — `EnterDegradedMode` uses `CompareAndSwap` for idempotency.
- Test: assert repeated calls are idempotent (only first transitions); assert concurrent entries from N goroutines all land in degraded state with degraded-count incremented exactly once.

### 4. Cache-reload-then-evaluate / stale-cache deny (`fxa9.35`)

- Code: integration tests don't exercise `Cache.Invalidate()` → `Evaluate()` sequencing.
- Test: integration test that invalidates the cache, then evaluates; assert correct refresh behavior + stale-cache deny semantics.

### 5. Task-30 scenarios (`fxa9.44`)

- Code: `test/integration/access/` has canary/concurrent_engine/seed_policies but Task-30 plan required: property_visibility, re-entrance, cache-invalidation-then-evaluate, audit modes.
- Test: add the 4 missing scenarios; partial overlap with .35 above for cache-invalidation.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- Each gap closed by a named test in the appropriate file.
- All existing tests still pass.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/attribute/...` green.
2. `task test:int -- ./test/integration/access/...` green.
3. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/attribute/circuit_breaker_test.go` (window pruning)
- `internal/access/policy/engine_test.go` (DegradedMode idempotency)
- `test/integration/access/access_suite_test.go` (evalAccess helper fix)
- `test/integration/access/cache_invalidation_test.go` (new — .35 + .44 partial)
- `test/integration/access/property_visibility_test.go` (new — .44)
- `test/integration/access/audit_modes_test.go` (new — .44)
- `test/integration/access/reentrance_test.go` (new — .44)

## Dependencies

- None. Test-only additions.

## Out of scope

- Production-code changes (production bugs are tracked in separate consolidated bead).
- Missing features (separate consolidated bead).

title: ABAC Phase 7.7 missing features (fair-share timeout, debug endpoint)
description:
## Goal

Implement two features from the Phase 7.7 spec that didn't land in PR #114 and surfaced during the 2026-05-15 audit.

## Design reference

- Originally filed as 2 separate beads under `holomush-fxa9`: `fxa9.47` (fair-share timeout), `fxa9.42` (debug endpoint).
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1019-fxa9-pr114.md`.

## Plan reference

### 1. Fair-share per-provider timeout (from `fxa9.47`)

- Issue: `resolver.go` has no per-provider context deadline computation. Circuit breaker is wired (skip/record), but a slow provider with an open circuit could still consume the budget reserved for healthy providers.
- Fix: compute per-provider `context.WithTimeout` deadlines that exclude circuit-opened providers from the fair-share denominator.
- Spec impact: re-read Phase 7.7 spec to confirm the exact fair-share algorithm intended.

### 2. `/debug/abac/providers` debug endpoint (from `fxa9.42`)

- Issue: Task 34 SHOULD'd a debug endpoint listing each provider's name, health, circuit state, last-error timestamp, recent latency percentiles. Not implemented.
- Fix: add HTTP handler at `/debug/abac/providers` on the observability server. JSON response. Gated by debug auth.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- Fair-share: new test asserts that one slow provider doesn't deplete the budget of healthy providers; circuit-opened providers don't get counted in the timeout split.
- Debug endpoint: new test asserts the endpoint returns the expected shape; integration test asserts it's exposed when debug enabled.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/...` green.
2. `task test:int` covering observability + ABAC integration green.
3. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/resolver.go` (fair-share timeout)
- `internal/observability/server.go` or `internal/access/policy/debug.go` (new endpoint)
- Corresponding `_test.go` files

## Dependencies

- None.

## Out of scope

- Production bugs from same audit (separate consolidated bead).
- Test gaps from same audit (separate consolidated bead).

title: ABAC Phase 7.7 production bug cluster (resolver re-entrance, circuit breaker, schema validation)
description:
## Goal

Fix 4 defensive-code bugs surfaced during the 2026-05-15 audit of PR #114 (ABAC Phase 7.7). Each is a discrete fix in a different code path; they're bundled because they share the same review context, the same testing harness, and similar shape (defensive-code holes).

## Design reference

- Originally filed as 4 separate beads under `holomush-fxa9` (Phase 7.7 review): `fxa9.4`, `fxa9.32`, `fxa9.34`, `fxa9.45`.
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1019-fxa9-pr114.md`.

## Plan reference

Four targeted fixes (likely 4 separate commits, possibly 1-4 PRs depending on review preference):

### 1. Re-entrance guard panic vs error (from `fxa9.4`)

- Code: `internal/access/policy/resolver.go:154-155` — `if isInResolution(ctx) { panic("resolver re-entrance detected...") }`.
- Issue: panic crashes the process if `Resolve` is called outside provider scope.
- Fix: return `oops.Code("RESOLVER_REENTRANCE").Errorf(...)` instead of panic. Update callers to handle the error.

### 2. `CircuitBreakerConfig` zero-value validation (from `fxa9.32`)

- Code: `internal/access/policy/attribute/circuit_breaker.go:35-52` — `Validate()` exists but `NewCircuitBreaker(name, CircuitBreakerConfig{}, nil)` constructs with zero-value config without calling `Validate()`.
- Issue: callers using `DefaultCircuitBreakerConfig()` are safe; others can create unvalidated configs.
- Fix: have `NewCircuitBreaker` call `Validate()` on the passed config; return error if invalid.

### 3. `AttributeSchema.Replace/Remove` validation bypass (from `fxa9.34`)

- Code: `internal/access/policy/types/types.go:409-417` — `Replace()` and `Remove()` are exported public methods on `*AttributeSchema` with no validation.
- Issue: callers holding `*AttributeSchema` pointer (via `SchemaRegistry.Schema()`) can bypass all schema validation.
- Fix: either make these methods unexported (only called from `SchemaRegistry` which already validates), or have them call the registry's validation logic before mutating.

### 4. Schema evolution: namespace removal rejection (from `fxa9.45`)

- Code: `internal/access/policy/schema_evolution.go:188-196` — `UpdateNamespace` logs `slog.Warn` on referenced-attribute removal but does NOT persist a `needs_review` flag on the affected policy rows.
- Issue: namespace removal correctly rejects when policies reference attributes, but attribute removal only warns; affected policies aren't flagged.
- Fix: persist `needs_review` (or analogous) flag on affected policy rows when attribute removal would impact them.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- New test per fix verifies the bug's specific failure mode.
- All existing tests still pass.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/...` green.
2. `task test:int` covering schema-evolution + circuit-breaker paths green.
3. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/resolver.go:154-155` + caller-side error handling
- `internal/access/policy/attribute/circuit_breaker.go:35-52`
- `internal/access/policy/types/types.go:409-417`
- `internal/access/policy/schema_evolution.go:188-196` + policy store row schema for `needs_review`
- Corresponding `_test.go` files

## Dependencies

- None. Each sub-fix is independent.

## Out of scope

- Process-global degradedCount (separate bead — `holomush-p305`).
- Corrupted permit policy comment lies (separate bead — `holomush-aefb`).
- Test gaps and feature gaps from same audit (other consolidated beads).

title: Plugin manager + hostfunc error-path test gaps (lifecycle + access)
description:
## Goal

Add tests for two untested error paths in the plugin host that were surfaced during the 2026-05-15 audit of PR #106 (T29 capability.Enforcer removal). Both are about exercising error branches that the audit found were genuinely uncovered.

## Design reference

- Originally filed as 2 separate beads under `holomush-g8o9`: `g8o9.7` (Manager WithPolicyInstaller lifecycle paths) + `g8o9.9` (KV checkKVAccess engine-error path).

## Plan reference

### 1. Plugin manager install-failure rollback (`g8o9.7`)

- Gap: no test in `manager_routing_test.go` or `manager_test.go` wires `installFn` to return an error. Three specific assertions missing: (a) `installFn` returns error → `loadPlugin` propagates; (b) plugin not in `ListPlugins()` after failure; (c) `host.Unload` was called as rollback.
- Also: Close with loaded plugins never asserts `RemovePluginPolicies` was invoked.

### 2. KV checkKVAccess engine-error branch (`g8o9.9`)

- Gap: `functions_test.go` has tests for deny, nil engine, allow — but none uses `policytest.NewErrorEngine` to exercise the `err != nil` branch.
- The analogous path in `get_command_help` is already covered (per resolved `g8o9.8`) — the pattern is established.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- `internal/plugin/manager_routing_test.go` has install-failure tests covering propagation + rollback + listing absence.
- `internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions_test.go` has `TestKVGet/Set/DeleteEngineErrorReturnsCheckFailed` using `policytest.NewErrorEngine`.
- All existing tests still pass.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/plugin/...` green.
2. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

## Dependencies
