title: core-channels: banned/kicked member keeps receiving live channel events until reconnect (no stream eviction on moderation)
description: CodeRabbit review of PR #4595 (2026-07-10, thread on 01-SECURITY.md:80). BanFromChannel/KickFromChannel remove the membership row and gate future access, but an already-connected banned/kicked member's LIVE channel stream is NOT torn down — they keep receiving channel_say/pose events until they reconnect (which re-runs session-establishment membership resolution). The membership-gated live-delivery guarantee is therefore eventually-consistent, not immediate. Fix: on ban/kick, remove the target character's channel stream from the live loop (RemoveStream via the stream registry / focus coordinator) so delivery stops at moderation time, not next reconnect. Grounded: plugins/core-channels moderation RPCs (BanFromChannel/KickFromChannel) + the SessionStreamRegistry/StreamSender path. Phase 1 (Channels). Related to the pluginauthz fence beads.

title: Lua ambient session-stream hostfuncs bypass the stream-subscribe fence
description: Found in the v1.0-milestone ship ABAC review (2026-07-09, non-blocking #4, Medium; PRE-EXISTING — not introduced by Phase 1/2). The shared stream-subscribe fence AuthorizePluginStreamContribution (internal/plugin/pluginauthz/streamsubscribe.go:56) guards the served host.v1 handler and the session-establishment merge, but the ambient Lua hostfuncs add_session_stream/remove_session_stream (internal/plugin/hostfunc/stdlib_streams.go) pass the raw client stream string to SessionStreamRegistry.AddStream with NO fence/ABAC — a third path the fence doc's 'BOTH paths' framing (streamsubscribe.go:29-38) understates. A Lua plugin could subscribe any session to any stream. core-channels is BINARY and uses the fenced client, so it neither introduces nor depends on this path. Fix: route the ambient Lua hostfunc path through the same AuthorizePluginStreamContribution fence (relative-only, forbidden system/audit/crypto, owned-emit-domain); update the streamsubscribe.go doc from 'BOTH' to 'all three paths'.

title: core-channels: ban/kick does not immediately evict live delivery (T-01-20, moderation gap)
description: gsd-security-auditor Phase-1 finding T-01-20 (medium, non-blocking under block_on=high). The live-delivery removal on moderation has only ONE of two arms. Arm 1 (present): a banned character is excluded from QuerySessionStreams on reconnect (main.go:84-85,130-138). Arm 2 (MISSING): the plan's 'kick/ban triggers RemoveStream' is not implemented — BanMember/KickMember (service_rpcs.go:132-155,161-184) only mutate membership + emit a notice; unsubscribeLive/RemoveStream fires only on self-LeaveChannel (service.go:462). Residual: a currently-connected banned/kicked character keeps receiving live channel_say until it reconnects (bounded, not unbounded). Fix: on BanMember/KickMember, evict the target's active session stream host-side (session->stream removal keyed on target character id, mid-session RemoveStream) — OR amend the T-01-20 mitigation text to state live-delivery removal is reconnect-bounded by design. Documented in .planning/phases/01-channels-subsystem/01-SECURITY.md.

title: Lua add_session_stream hostfunc bypasses AuthorizePluginStreamContribution fence
description: Discovered during phase-1 plan 01-02 execution. The ambient Lua hostfunc holomush.add_session_stream (internal/plugin/lua/stdlib_streams.go) is a pre-existing path that does NOT pass through the new unified AuthorizePluginStreamContribution / AuthorizeStreamSubscribe fence that binary plugins go through (internal/plugin/pluginauthz/streamsubscribe.go). This is a plugin-runtime-symmetry concern (a Lua path reaching a capability without the same policy chokepoint as binary = potential privilege gradient). Related to holomush-l6std. Fix: route the Lua add_session_stream path through the same shared fence, or confirm/document it is already fenced elsewhere. Wants abac-reviewer/crypto-reviewer attention on the symmetry.
notes: abac-reviewer (2026-07-08) concurs and adds detail: this is the THIRD stream-contribution path (establishment + served host.v1 + Lua ambient), and internal/plugin/pluginauthz/streamsubscribe.go:28-38 doc OVERCLAIMS coverage ('both paths') — there are three. Unfenced site: internal/plugin/hostfunc/stdlib_streams.go:42-94 -> AddStream forwards raw client string into the session subscribe filter (stream_registry.go:179-181), bypassing forbidden+owned-namespace fences; a Lua plugin could subscribe a session to events.<game>.system.rekey.* / audit / foreign-game. Metadata exposure (sensitive payloads stay AuthGuard/DEK-gated). Fix: route ambient add/remove_session_stream through AuthorizePluginStreamContribution (or gate behind a declared capability) AND narrow the doc comment. Channels (binary) uses the fenced host.v1 client — does not widen the hole.

title: core-channels: target-character ABAC binding is best-effort (defense-in-depth gap, WR-01)
description: Phase-1 code review (01-REVIEW.md) WR-01. In plugins/core-channels/service.go:283-286 the target-character identity is bound only by actorMismatch, which no-ops when actor metadata is absent. ListChannels/WhoInChannel/QueryChannelHistory query directly on req.character_id with no independent ABAC binding on the target. The telnet command path is SAFE (host-vouched actor). The gap is latent: any future metadata-less typed-RPC/BFF channel path could enumerate a victim's memberships/roster/history. Mirrors the same pattern in plugins/core-scenes/service.go — so a fix likely spans both. MUST be weighed by the abac-reviewer gate before any BFF channel surface ships. Fix: bind an independent ABAC decision on the target character, or require host-vouched actor metadata (fail closed when absent).

title: crypto reminder path-fallback misses manifest-only crypto.emits edits
description: remind-pre-action-review.sh's changed_paths crypto grep (the path-based fallback for the crypto-reviewer handoff gate) matches internal/eventbus code + crypto migration paths but NOT plugins/*/plugin.yaml. So a generic handoff prompt ("push this branch") whose diff adds/changes crypto.emits ONLY in a plugin manifest skips the crypto-reviewer reminder — the prompt-clause only catches literal 'crypto.emits' TEXT the user typed, not the manifest content. CodeRabbit (PR #4593) proposed matching any (^|/)plugin.yaml$, but that over-triggers (fires the crypto gate on every manifest edit, not just crypto.emits ones). Precise fix: content-aware detection (grep the changed plugin.yaml for a crypto.emits block) before adding to the crypto reminder path set. Deferred from the jj->git migration PR to keep scope focused.
acceptance_criteria: A handoff prompt whose diff adds crypto.emits to a plugin.yaml triggers the crypto-reviewer reminder; a plugin.yaml edit WITHOUT crypto.emits does NOT falsely trigger it.

title: Add real-store test for workspaceStore.isFocusReady flip-after-await ordering (T7 race fix)
description: From PR #4574 review (holomush-x1lwf.7, important). The actual sub-100ms race fix — workspaceStore.select() setting focusReadyBySceneId[id]=false then true after awaiting setSceneFocus — has no test against the REAL store (SceneComposer.svelte.test.ts mocks the whole store; workspaceStore.test.ts was untouched). Add a workspaceStore-level test asserting isFocusReady is false immediately after select() sets selectedSceneId and true only after setSceneFocus resolves.
acceptance_criteria: A real-store test verifies the false->true flip ordering around the setSceneFocus await in select().

title: SceneComposer stuck disabled when setSceneFocus rejects — surface focus-set failure + retry affordance
description: From g1qcw.7 code-review (Medium). The focus-before-send gate sets focusReadyBySceneId[sceneId]=false in workspaceStore.select() (web/src/lib/scenes/workspaceStore.svelte.ts:~164) and true only after 'await setSceneFocus(altSessionId, connectionId, sceneId)' resolves (:~176). The await is unwrapped, and callers invoke select() fire-and-forget (web/src/lib/components/scenes/ScenesShell.svelte:~159-160). If setSceneFocus REJECTS (transient RPC failure), focusReadyBySceneId[sceneId] stays false forever: the scene shows as selected (selectedSceneId already set) but the Pose/Say/OOC buttons + Cmd-Enter stay permanently disabled with NO error surfaced. The disable polarity is CORRECT/fail-closed (a raw pose without a confirmed server focus would misroute), and re-selecting the scene retries — but the failure is invisible and recovery is guess-work.
acceptance_criteria: A setSceneFocus rejection surfaces a visible error on the composer and offers a retry (or auto-retries), so a stuck-false focusReady is recoverable without the user guessing to re-click. The gate stays fail-closed (no send while focus unconfirmed).

title: render-adr emits YAML frontmatter title that fails repo markdown lint
description:
## Goal
render-adr (_adr_render.py in the dev-flow plugin, lines 126/144-146) now emits a YAML `---\ntitle: ...\n---` frontmatter block and omits the body `# <TITLE>` H1. Combined with the license-eye SPDX comment that sits ABOVE it (lines 1-4), rumdl parses the `---` at line 6 as a horizontal rule + setext heading, failing MD041/MD065/MD003/MD022. All 186 committed docs/adr/*.md use the H1 form and pass; the frontmatter output does not.

## Repro
capture-adrs on holomush-kk1ot rendered holomush-2hhq2 + holomush-byqph with frontmatter; task pr-prep (fast lane, lint:markdown) failed with 8 rumdl issues in the 2 files. Hand-converted both to H1 to land the PR.

## Fix direction
Either render-adr emits an H1 title (matching the 186 existing ADRs) and no frontmatter, OR it places frontmatter at line 1 (incompatible with the SPDX-comment-first license-eye convention), OR the repo rumdl config is taught to accept SPDX-then-frontmatter. H1 is the established, lint-clean convention.

## Out of scope
The comm-content ADR content itself (correct; only the header format was wrong).

title: Acquire a project-tuned Svelte + shadcn UI dev skill (encode web/CLAUDE.md design-system conventions)
description:
## Goal

Close the gap in our Svelte UI dev tooling. We have generic Svelte-5 skills
(`svelte-code-writer`, `svelte-core-bestpractices`) and the generic
`shadcn-svelte` plugin skill, but NONE encode HoloMUSH's own web-UI design-system
conventions. Evaluate whether to (a) adopt/find a better external skill,
(b) extend an existing skill, or (c) author a project-local web-UI skill — then
execute the chosen path so agents writing `.svelte` code are steered onto the
design system by default.

## Why now (grounding)

Surfaced by `holomush-5rh.31`: `web/src/lib/components/scenes/SceneBoardRow.svelte`
was written with raw `<button>`/`<a>` elements + hand-rolled Tailwind — Join uses
a hardcoded `bg-[var(--brand-cyan-deep)]` fill that reads as "disabled" — bypassing
the shadcn `Button` component the project mandates. `rg` for
`shadcn|Button variant|design system|web/CLAUDE` across `.agents/skills/` returns
ZERO matches: the existing svelte skills are generic Svelte-5 guidance (runes,
reactivity, events) and would not steer an agent toward the project's design
system. The same design-system-bypass theme recurs in `5rh.32` (composer
string-builds `scene pose` instead of typed/focus-routed input).

## Surfaces to consider

- `.agents/skills/svelte-code-writer/`, `.agents/skills/svelte-core-bestpractices/`
  — existing generic skills; candidates to extend.
- `shadcn-svelte` plugin skill — generic component manager; evaluate adequacy and
  whether it triggers on component authoring.
- `web/CLAUDE.md` — the conventions any good skill MUST encode: shadcn `Button`
  over raw `<button>`, `cn()` for conditional classes, `--color-*` / `--mush-*`
  tokens, Svelte-5 runes, no raw `<button>` with hardcoded brand fills.

## Out of scope

- Fixing `5rh.31` itself (its own bead; this bead is the tooling that prevents the
  class, not the instance).
- Rewriting existing non-conforming components (a separate cleanup effort if the
  evaluation warrants it).
acceptance_criteria: MUST deliver a recorded decision on whether to (a) adopt an external skill, (b) extend svelte-code-writer/svelte-core-bestpractices, or (c) author a project-local web-UI skill, with rationale. The chosen/authored skill MUST encode web/CLAUDE.md conventions (shadcn Button variants over raw <button>; cn() for conditional classes; --color-*/--mush-* tokens; Svelte-5 runes; no hardcoded brand hex/fills) and MUST trigger when creating/editing .svelte files. SHOULD demonstrably catch the 5rh.31-class defect (a raw <button> with a hardcoded bg-[var(--brand-*)] fill where a shadcn Button variant belongs).
notes: Validate by dry-running the resulting skill against web/src/lib/components/scenes/SceneBoardRow.svelte (pre-fix): it SHOULD flag the raw Watch/Join <button>s + hardcoded bg-[var(--brand-cyan-deep)] and recommend shadcn Button variants. Confirm the skill description references the web/CLAUDE.md design-system rules. rg -l 'shadcn|Button|web/CLAUDE' over the chosen skill dir should be non-empty. Related (surfaced-by): holomush-5rh.31; same theme: holomush-5rh.32.

title: Document bd prime operator-side de-dup (T1a, RD4)
description: Goal: DOCUMENT ONLY (no repo change). The repo .claude/settings.json already registers bd prime exactly once per event (portable single source); the observed doubling is operator/plugin config outside the repo. Record the operator-side ~/.claude cleanup on this epic. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-03-token-cost-latency-optimization.md#task-6 (verbatim). Files: none repo-tracked. Out of scope: editing .claude/settings.json.
acceptance_criteria: Operator-side cleanup documented on holomush-wagqb; a fresh session shows a single Beads Workflow Context block after the operator applies it. NO repo change; MUST NOT edit .claude/settings.json; not mechanically closeable by a subagent (needs operator action + fresh-session check).
notes:
RD4 confirmed via read-only checks in workspace token-opt-exec (2026-07-03):
- repo .claude/settings.json: exactly 2 `bd prime` command registrations (SessionStart :69, PreCompact :35) — one per event, already single-source. Line 150 is an allowlist entry, not a hook. NO repo edit warranted.
- operator ~/.claude/settings.json: 1 `bd prime` registration at PreCompact (:176) — this is the external duplicate causing the doubling (repo PreCompact + operator PreCompact both fire).

Operator-side action (NOT done by this session — requires the operator's own machine):
1. Remove the duplicate PreCompact `bd prime` from ~/.claude/settings.json (repo already provides it portably; keeping the global copy is the operator's discretion for non-holomush projects).
2. If a fresh session still shows two SessionStart "Beads Workflow Context" blocks, the second is the beads-plugin's own SessionStart prime injector (beads@beads-marketplace) — operator disables whichever of {repo SessionStart bd prime, plugin injector} is redundant on their machine. Do NOT remove the repo's SessionStart registration — it's the portable guarantee for other contributors.
3. Verify: a fresh Claude Code session shows the "Beads Workflow Context" block exactly once.

Status: documented, no repo change made (correct per RD4/T1a). Awaiting operator to apply + verify on their own ~/.claude/settings.json. Label blocked:operator retained — not mechanically closeable by a subagent.

title: [E9.6] Forum Integration - Scene Requests, Scheduling
description:
Originally [E9.6] under the Scenes & RP epic (holomush-5rh); lifted out as a standalone item 2026-07-03 — fully gated on the Forums epic (holomush-djj), which has no design yet, so it no longer belongs to 5rh's active scope.

Integrate scenes with the forum system for requests and scheduling.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Scene request forum category
- [ ] Request template (participants, theme, timing)
- [ ] Scene scheduling with calendar integration
- [ ] Notification when scheduled scene starts
- [ ] Link forum thread to scene instance
- [ ] Post scene summary to thread when ended
- [ ] Scene interest/RSVP functionality
- [ ] Timezone-aware scheduling
- [ ] Reminder notifications
- [ ] Integration tests for forum linkage
notes:
Substrate-contract spec §4.3 says forums is OUT of theme-wide SDK scope. Reparenting decision deferred to djj brainstorm.

Recreated via create/delete from holomush-5rh.9 (deleted 2026-07-03) to fully detach from epic holomush-5rh's ID namespace, same cleanup as the templates lift-out (holomush-x4n1r). Historical dep (closed, not recreated as a live edge): depended on holomush-5rh.8.20. Likely candidate for reparenting under holomush-djj (Forums epic) once that gets a design.

title: Publish-vote web: optional polish deferred from PR #4564 review
description: Suggestion-severity findings deferred from the PR #4564 review gate (importants were all fixed in commit lwvyuykv). Optional polish, none blocking: (o5urv.3/.9) tighten ScenePublishPanel characterId/isOwner props to required now that the sole caller always supplies them — OR keep defaults as a deliberate safety net (design call); (o5urv.4) castInFlight is redundant with pendingVote!==null — intentional per spec §5, revisit only if it drifts; (o5urv.10) isActive/isPending vote-button predicate could collapse to a single derived comparison; (o5urv.11) publishStore's attempt-scoping guard is triplicated across 3 getters — extract a helper; (o5urv.7) no test exercises the controlErr/role=alert error surface on a rejected vote/withdraw; (o5urv.8) no integration test confirms SceneContextRail wires characterId/isOwner into the mounted ScenePublishPanel.

title: Add unit coverage for grpc Client.UpdateScene wrapper
description: Deferred suggestion from PR #4538 review (rak6z.4). internal/grpc/client.go Client.UpdateScene (thin delegate + oops RPC_FAILED wrap) has 0% direct coverage. Add a small client_test.go case mirroring the CreateScene/ResumeScene wrapper tests: success delegates+returns resp; downstream error -> oops.Code RPC_FAILED. Low priority (behavior is exercised indirectly via facade/BFF tests).

title: Add per-item max_len to tags repeated field in UpdateScene/WebUpdateScene protos
description: Deferred suggestion from PR #4538 review (rak6z.7). tags repeated field in sceneaccess.UpdateSceneRequest and web.WebUpdateSceneRequest carries only repeated.max_items=32 with no per-item string.max_len, unlike title(200)/description(4096). 32 arbitrarily-long tag strings can reach Postgres via buildSceneUpdate raw passthrough (content_warnings has a taxonomy allowlist; tags do not). Add (buf.validate.field).repeated.items.string.max_len to tags in both protos + regenerate stubs + task lint:proto. Reopens proto-bead scope, hence deferred out of the settings slice.

title: pnpm check: input-group-addon.svelte type error (Property 'focus' does not exist on type 'Element')
description: Pre-existing svelte-check error on main, surfaced during epic holomush-5rh.24 drain. `cd web && pnpm check` reports 1 ERROR at web/src/lib/components/ui/input-group/input-group-addon.svelte:47:68 — 'Property focus does not exist on type Element'. The file is a shadcn-svelte 'nova' component imported by the merged membership-slice PR #4531 (qmsxwtlz); the error predates and is independent of the settings-slice work. Fix: narrow the element type before calling .focus() (e.g. cast to HTMLElement / guard 'focus' in el). Not a merge blocker today (web typecheck evidently non-gating — #4531 merged with it), but pnpm check is red branch-wide until fixed. Verify with 'cd web && pnpm check' (expect 0 errors after).

title: Register scene actor-binding cross-check as a named invariant + bind across all 9 SceneService handlers
description: The defense-in-depth actor-identity cross-check (reject PermissionDenied when character-kind advisory actor metadata != req.CharacterId, before ABAC/store mutation) now guards all 9 acting-character SceneService handlers: WatchScene + LeaveScene/InviteToScene/KickFromScene/TransferOwnership (PR #4531) + JoinScene/EndScene/PauseScene/ResumeScene/UpdateScene (PR #4532, holomush-fgly4). It is a durable security guarantee with no entry in docs/architecture/invariants.yaml and no // Verifies: binding. Precedent: INV-SCENE-61 binds WatchScene gate-order via // Verifies on service_watch_test.go. Goal: allocate an INV-ACCESS-N (or INV-SCENE-N) id, add the registry entry, annotate the genuinely-asserting tests (service_membership_actor_binding_test.go, service_state_mutation_actor_binding_test.go, the WatchScene mismatch test) with // Verifies, set binding:bound + asserted_by, regenerate invariants.md (go run ./cmd/inv-render), and run the meta-tests. Out of scope of holomush-fgly4 because it spans the #4531 handlers too. Source: PR #4532 review finding holomush-xn3hd.5.

title: Pin buf binary version consistently across local + CI to prevent masked proto-format Lint failures
description:
CI pins the buf binary to 1.67.0 in .github/workflows/ci.yaml (Install buf step), but local dev/agent environments install newer buf (e.g. 1.71.0 via Homebrew). buf format rules changed between these versions: 1.67.0 requires a blank line between adjacent message blocks that 1.71.0 does not. Result: protos formatted with a newer local buf pass 'task lint:proto' / 'buf format --diff' locally but FAIL the required CI Lint check. This silently bit PR #4531 (web.proto), and the failure was further masked by two workflows both emitting a check-run named 'Lint' (gh pr checks dedupes by name and showed the passing one).

Goal: make local buf == CI buf so 'task pr-prep' catches proto-format issues before push. Options: (a) provision buf via a version-pinned mechanism the dev environment shares with CI (mise/asdf .tool-versions, or a vendored/downloaded pinned binary invoked by the Taskfile), or (b) bump the CI pin to a current buf and reformat the tree once. Also consider deduping the two 'Lint' check-run names so gh pr checks/--watch can't mask a failing required check (see memory fbebc771).

Files: .github/workflows/ci.yaml (BUF_VERSION), Taskfile.yml (lint:proto/fmt buf invocation), possibly a new .tool-versions/mise.toml.
acceptance_criteria: Local buf invoked by task matches the CI-pinned version (or both updated in lockstep); a proto formatted on a fresh checkout passes both local 'task lint:proto' and CI Lint; no duplicate 'Lint' check-run masks a required-check failure in gh pr checks.

title: CI: cache e2e web toolchain (pnpm) + verify Namespace profile cache toggles
description:
SCOPE CORRECTED after investigation (parent holomush-d5ra2 / PR #4525). The original title says "Docker layer cache" but that is the WRONG lever — see finding. Real target: cache the e2e WEB toolchain + verify Namespace runner-profile cache toggles.

## Finding
The e2e image Dockerfile is RUNTIME-ONLY: it COPYs a pre-built `holomush` binary + plugins into alpine:3.23.4 + `apk add ca-certificates tzdata wget`. So Docker LAYER caching is near-zero value — only the apk layer is reusable; `COPY holomush` invalidates on every commit, busting all later layers. The dominant e2e setup cost is UPSTREAM of `docker build`, in Taskfile steps run by docker:build:cover deps + web:embed: web:install (pnpm), web:generate (buf generate, pulls BSR plugins), web:build (SvelteKit/vite), plugin:build-all, and the CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux -tags realweb -cover Go build.

## Current caching
Only nscloud-cache-action with `cache: go` on every job (ci.yaml). The WEB toolchain (pnpm store, node_modules, SvelteKit/vite cache, buf module cache) is COMPLETELY uncached.

## Levers (highest value first)
1. Add `cache: pnpm` to the e2e + build jobs — Namespace nscloud-cache-action supports pnpm natively. Optionally cache the SvelteKit/vite build cache + buf module cache via the action's `path:` input. This is the real win and "the Namespace cache we can leverage."
2. Verify Namespace runner-profile cache toggles (Namespace dashboard, NOT in-repo) on namespace-profile-linux-amd64-4x8: 'Container images' (caches alpine/postgres/playwright image pulls+unpacks — transparent), 'Toolchain downloads', 'Action downloads'. Namespace Remote Builders are enabled by default with built-in build caching; confirm they're active for our profile. These are transparent — no workflow change once enabled.
3. Docker layer cache itself: DEPRIORITIZED given the runtime-only Dockerfile. If ever pursued: buildx --cache-to/from=type=registry, or Namespace "Local Docker caching" on the profile; expected payoff ~seconds.

Out of scope: the host-only test-path plugin build (landed in PR #4525).

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: Lever #1 (pnpm store cache on e2e + build jobs) implemented in PR #4526: https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/4526. Remaining in this bead: #2 verify Namespace runner-profile cache toggles (dashboard), and optionally cache buf module dir + SvelteKit/vite via the action's path: input.

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: PR #4526 MERGED — pnpm store cache (lever #1) now live on e2e + build jobs (dedicated 'Cache pnpm store' step after Install pnpm; the initial combined step failed because nscloud-cache-action execs pnpm before corepack installs it). CI green: E2E 4m6s. Remaining in this bead: lever #2 verify Namespace runner-profile cache toggles (dashboard) + optional buf/vite path: cache.

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: pnpm lever (#1) DONE: PR #4526 added a dedicated 'Cache pnpm store' step (after Install pnpm) to e2e+build jobs; PR #4527 aligned ci.yaml's corepack pnpm pin (11.0.3 -> 11.9.0) with web/package.json packageManager. Both merged. REMAINING (the reason this stays open): lever #2 — verify Namespace runner-profile cache toggles (Container images / Toolchain downloads / Action downloads) are enabled on namespace-profile-linux-amd64-* in the Namespace dashboard (transparent, no workflow change); and optional buf module + SvelteKit/vite caches via the action's path: input. Docker layer cache (original title) remains DEPRIORITIZED — the e2e Dockerfile is runtime-only.

title: Integration: hoist per-It full-stack Start to BeforeAll (privacy/presence/scenes)
description:
Goal: Cut integration wall-clock by hoisting per-It full-stack setup to per-suite — the largest remaining code-side win from the test-speed review (parent holomush-d5ra2 / PR #4525).

## Cost model (from the review)
Postgres is ALREADY optimal — one container per test binary (sync.Once) + per-test DB cloned from a migrated template (test/testutil/postgres.go). DO NOT touch it. The unshared, repeated costs are:
- (a) Embedded NATS boot+teardown PER Start — eventbustest.New(t) boots a fresh in-process JetStream server every call (internal/eventbus/eventbustest/embedded.go), with full drain -> shutdown -> WaitForShutdown on teardown (internal/eventbus/subsystem.go).
- (b) Plugin tree copy + binary subprocess spawn PER It in scene suites — startPlugins copyTree's the whole plugins/ + build/plugins/ into a t.TempDir then Manager.LoadAll spawns each binary plugin over go-plugin gRPC (internal/testsupport/integrationtest/plugins.go; harness.go Start ~264, eventbustest.New ~341).

## Costly call sites (Start in BeforeEach — fix these)
- test/integration/privacy/privacy_test.go — 18 It, ~8 Start in BeforeEach (lines 64,139,248,397,475,578,668,758)
- test/integration/presence — 6 It, per-spec Start in BeforeEach
- test/integration/scenes — ~29 It, per-Describe BeforeEach -> Start(WithInTreePlugins(), WithPluginCrypto()) — COSTLIEST (plugin subprocess spawn + DEK/AuthGuard wiring every test)
- test/integration/plugincrypto — per-spec Start(WithInTreePlugins, WithPluginCrypto)

## Already-good pattern to copy (BeforeSuite/BeforeAll, one stack per suite)
world (world_suite_test.go), plugin (plugin_suite_test.go), auth, store, wholesystem/{abac,census}_test.go.

## Why this NEEDS a plan (not a blanket sed)
Today the per-test DB IS the isolation (fresh template-cloned DB per test). A shared per-suite stack means one DB + one bus shared across specs, so each It must either namespace its own entities or the suite be read-only-after-BeforeAll setup. Suites that mutate shared world/scene state can't trivially share — per-suite assessment required. Also share the embedded NATS bus per suite (mirror the Postgres optimization).

## Sequencing / follow-on
After per-spec stacks are removed, the heavy suites could switch to the `ginkgo -p` runner for intra-package parallelism (the Serial/Ordered decorators already exist) — but ONLY after the shared-stack change; currently test:int runs via `go test`, so Ginkgo spec-level parallelism is inert.

## Also audit (possible tier demotion)
internal/access/.../evaluation_test.go + concurrent_engine_test.go — if they exercise only the in-memory engine, move to the unit tier (out of the integration suite entirely).

Out of scope: Postgres container strategy (already optimal — do not touch); the host-only plugin build (landed in PR #4525).

title: CommunicationLine.svelte channel-prefix branches for say and pose have no test coverage
seam-guard.test.ts describe name overclaims --mush-* positive assertion for PoseCard and CommunicationRenderer
description:
web/src/lib/comm/seam-guard.test.ts, header comment (line 8) and describe block name (line 9).

The file header says: "SEAM-2: message renderers MUST color via --mush-* tokens, never brand colors."
The describe block is named: "SEAM-2 message renderers use --mush-* not brand colors"

However, tests 1 and 2 in this block only assert the NEGATIVE:
  - 'PoseCard uses no --brand-* color': not.toContain('--brand-')
  - 'CommunicationRenderer uses no --brand-* color': not.toContain('--brand-')

Only test 3 ('the shared primitive uses --mush- tokens') checks a positive --mush-* presence, and only for CommunicationLine.svelte — not for PoseCard.svelte or CommunicationRenderer.svelte themselves.

The describe name and header comment claim that PoseCard and CommunicationRenderer 'use --mush-* tokens', but the tests only verify they don't use --brand-* colors. A PoseCard or CommunicationRenderer that uses hardcoded hex or --sl-* tokens would pass tests 1-2 while violating the stated contract.

Suggested fix: rename the describe block to 'SEAM-2 message renderers use no --brand-* colors' (matching what the first two tests actually assert) and update the header comment to 'message renderers MUST NOT use --brand-* tokens'. Alternatively, add positive --mush-* assertions for PoseCard and CommunicationRenderer to bring the tests in line with the existing description.

title: PoseCard.svelte contentWarning branch not exercised by any test
cog-release.bats setup git commit fails under global commit.gpgsign=true (op-ssh-sign/1Password unreachable in non-interactive runs)
description:
Goal: make scripts/tests/cog-release.bats robust to a runner whose global git config sets commit.gpgsign=true with an SSH/1Password signer.

Symptom: all 9 cog-release.bats tests fail in pr-prep (task test:bats) when run in a non-interactive/detached context (e.g. an agent session). setup() at scripts/tests/cog-release.bats:21 does 'git commit -q -m "chore: seed"' which inherits the global commit.gpgsign=true + gpg.ssh.program=op-ssh-sign; op-ssh-sign cannot reach the 1Password agent → 'error: 1Password: failed to fill whole buffer' → 'fatal: failed to write commit object' → status 128 → setup fails for every test.

Root cause: the test's temp repo sets user.email/user.name but NOT commit.gpgsign false, so it inherits the runner's global signing config. CI passes only because CI runners lack commit.gpgsign=true.

Fix: in cog-release.bats setup(), add 'git config commit.gpgsign false' (and/or 'git config tag.gpgsign false') to the temp repo so the suite is hermetic w.r.t. the runner's global git config. Mirror in any other bats suite that does real git commits.

Workaround used 2026-06-26: ran pr-prep with process-scoped 'GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=commit.gpgsign GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=false' — non-destructive, overrides global for that run only.

title: scene composer ignores leading pose/say/OOC sigils — sends raw text, leaking ':' / '"' into content
description:
Goal: Make the web scene composer recognize MUSH-style leading sigils so typing `:waves` poses and `"hi` says — routing to the right verb and stripping the sigil — matching telnet muscle-memory and keeping sigil chars out of stored scene content.

Symptom (local dev, tip of main 19822b1, version=dev, 2026-06-25):
  In a scene, typing `:tests` and submitting produced an entry whose content is the literal `:tests` (colon retained). The composer exposes explicit Pose/Say/OOC buttons but does NO leading-sigil detection, and Cmd/Ctrl+Enter always submits as pose regardless of content. The user clearly expects MUSH-style detection (the open draft in the screenshot is `"This should be a 'say'"`).

Root cause: web/src/lib/components/scenes/SceneComposer.svelte
  - send(verb) (lines 55-73) sends `scene ${verb} ${draftText.trim()}` verbatim — no inspection of a leading sigil, no stripping.
  - handleKeydown (lines 90-95) hardcodes send("pose") on Cmd/Ctrl+Enter, so the keyboard path can never produce a say/ooc and never honors a typed sigil.
  Writes go through the command path (design D4), so `scene pose :tests` reaches handleEmit (plugins/core-scenes/commands.go:518) which stores the colon as content; the sigil is not interpreted server-side either.

Blast radius: every web scene pose/say/ooc submission; 100% reproducible. A workaround exists (use the explicit buttons, avoid typing sigils), so not data-loss — but content is corrupted for any user with telnet muscle-memory.

Candidate fix directions (NOT decided here):
  A. Detect the leading sigil in the composer, route to verb + strip before send (`:`->pose, `;`->semipose/no-space pose, `"`->say; pick an OOC sigil or keep OOC button-only). Make Cmd+Enter honor the detected verb. Buttons remain an explicit override.
  B. Interpret sigils server-side so telnet and web share one parser — but telnet sigil handling lives in the command dispatcher, not the plugin, so this likely belongs at the composer/dispatch layer.
  Decide: the exact sigil vocabulary, and whether buttons override detection.

Files touched (candidates): web/src/lib/components/scenes/SceneComposer.svelte:55-73,90-95

Out of scope: center-pane rendering of say/pose/ooc — separate bead (related).
acceptance_criteria: Typing `:waves` + submit (button or Cmd+Enter) emits a pose with content "waves" (no colon). Typing a leading-quote say emits a say with content stripped of the sigil. Cmd+Enter honors the detected verb instead of always posing. Explicit Pose/Say/OOC buttons still work. Vitest unit test covers sigil->verb routing + stripping. Verify: cd web && pnpm check && pnpm test; manual: pose `:x` renders as an action, not literal `:x`.
notes:
PARITY REFERENCE (confirmed by user via /terminal screenshots, 2026-06-25): the web TERMINAL already does this correctly — typing `:tests` in the terminal command input shows a POSE chip, and the location pose renders as `Foob tests`. The scene composer must reach the same behavior ("scene handling should be the same for say/pose/ooc").

Reference implementation to reuse, NOT reinvent:
  - web/src/lib/components/terminal/composerChip.ts::resolveComposerChip — command-list-driven sigil detection. SIGILS = {`"`, `:`, `;`}; resolves against the SERVER-SOURCED command/alias set (state.aliases / state.names), explicitly NOT hardcoded prefixes.
  - web/src/lib/components/terminal/ModeChip.svelte — renders the POSE/SAY/OOC chip.
  - web/src/lib/components/terminal/CommandInput.svelte:43 wires it: `chip = resolveComposerChip(text, $commandList)`.
  - Server alias expansion: internal/command/alias.go (prefix aliases `:`→pose, `;`→pose-no-space, `"`→say), tested in internal/command/alias_test.go ("colon prefix expands to pose"). NOTE: this expansion fires on TOP-LEVEL commands only.

DESIGN CONSTRAINT: any chip/recognition added here MUST respect the recognized-command-chip invariants (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-recognized-command-chip-design.md — INV-4 server-sourced recognition, INV-7 speech-mode chips, INV-6 graceful incompleteness). Do NOT hardcode prefixes.

ARCHITECTURAL NUANCE for the fix: SceneComposer wraps input as `scene pose <text>`. The server alias expansion only fires on top-level commands, so `scene pose :tests` will NOT strip the colon server-side (handleEmit stores it verbatim). So the fix is either (A) client-side: detect+strip the sigil in SceneComposer before wrapping the correct `scene <verb>`, reusing resolveComposerChip for recognition; or (B) server-side: pre-parse leading sigils in the scene content args. Decide — but the chip/affordance reuse (resolveComposerChip + ModeChip) is the clear shared piece.
RE-SCOPED (2026-06-25): NOT fixed by client-side sigil stripping. Root cause is architectural: top-level pose/say/ooc do not consult Connection.FocusKey (v2 §3.2 designed it; unimplemented for top-level; web focus UX = Phase 9). Correct fix = focus-routed scene input: (1) server — top-level pose honors connection focus → routes to focused scene; (2) web — composer drops 'scene pose' wrapping, sends raw ':bows'/'pose bows', chip becomes pure preview. Interim: composer buttons work; typing a leading sigil is unsupported (documented), no band-aid strip. New brainstorm/spec to follow.
Fixed by holomush-g1qcw (focus-routed scene input). This bug stays OPEN until that lands; interim is buttons-work / sigil-typing-unsupported.

title: Flaky E2E recurrence: scenes.spec.ts 'second tab does not disturb terminal stream' — CI-budget branch may not apply
description:
Goal: investigate why web/e2e/scenes.spec.ts:396 'Scenes workspace (E9.5) > workspace open in a second tab does not disturb the terminal stream' still flakes.

Recurrence: failed once on PR #4518 (run 28071136901, job 83105919987), then PASSED on a no-change rerun. #4518 is a web-CSS-only diff (SceneListItem subtext color/size) that is causally incapable of affecting terminal websocket event delivery — so this is pure load variance, same signature the in-test comments document (passed on #4492, flaked twice on #4493's Go-only diff). The real invariant is deterministically covered green by Go integration tests (INV-SCENE-23 multi_connection_visibility_test.go, TestSendToConnection_TargetsOneConnectionOnly).

Prior flake beads holomush-mwmzt and holomush-hjk6x (same test) were CLOSED as fixed via the 30s CI token2 budget + focusBurstDone WebGetScene drain — but the flake persists.

CONCRETE LEAD (investigate first): the failure stack showed 'Timeout 10000ms exceeded' on the token2 poll at scenes.spec.ts:475, even though that call passes `process.env.CI ? 30000 : 10000`. A 10000ms timeout implies process.env.CI was falsy in the Playwright test process — i.e. the CI-scaled 30s budget never applied in the E2E job. Verify whether the E2E runner propagates CI/env into the test process (playwright.config + the task test:e2e harness); if not, the intended CI headroom is dead and the test always runs on the tighter 10s ceiling.

Files: web/e2e/scenes.spec.ts (token2 wait ~L474-481, waitForOutputMatching helper ~L40-70), web/playwright.config.* / the test:e2e task env.
Out of scope: PR #4518 (already green on rerun); not caused by it.
acceptance_criteria: Root-caused why the CI 30s budget did/didn't apply; either the env propagation is fixed so token2 gets its intended CI ceiling, or the test is quarantined per test/quarantine.yaml against an open bead with a mitigation plan. Test no longer flakes across N consecutive E2E runs.
notes: Recurrence on PR #4565 (holomush-5rh.24.41.7, run 28589486297, job 84769187072): failed at scenes.spec.ts:467 'workspace open in a second tab does not disturb the terminal stream', same 10000ms token2 timeout signature. PR #4565's diff is web/e2e/scene-publish-vote.spec.ts (new, isolated file) + a data-testid attribute on ScenePublishPanel.svelte (no logic change) — causally incapable of affecting this test. Parent commit main@8bbdffb8 has a clean E2E Test pass. Re-running the CI job; not investigating the root cause further here (out of scope for .41.7) but this is a second data point supporting the CI-budget-env-propagation lead already filed.

title: warm-light theme: accent/accent-foreground pairing fails WCAG AA (2.70:1) app-wide
description:
Goal: warm-light's accent (#f57c00) + accent.foreground (#ffffff) yields 2.70:1 contrast, below WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text. This is a palette-token defect affecting EVERY bg-accent text-accent-foreground surface app-wide (selected scene rows, shadcn default buttons, etc.), not just the scene sidebar. warm-light muted.foreground (#6b6055) on accent is 2.26:1 (also sub-AA).

Discovered while fixing holomush-5rh.26 (scene-sidebar selected-row subtext contrast). That component fix conditions the subtext on accent-foreground, which restores legibility in default-light (5.00:1) and dark themes, but CANNOT rescue warm-light because the accent/accent-foreground token pairing is itself sub-AA — even the title (full accent-foreground) is only 2.70:1.

Files touched: web/src/lib/theme/warm-light.json (darken accent and/or adjust accent.foreground). Consider whether warm-dark needs the same audit.
Out of scope: default-light (already AA), the SceneListItem component (fixed in holomush-5rh.26).
acceptance_criteria: warm-light accent/accent-foreground contrast >= 4.5:1; a theme-contrast unit test asserts every light theme's accent/accent-foreground pairing meets WCAG AA; visual check of a selected scene row + a default button in warm-light.

title: Web surface for 1:1 direct messages (page/whisper)
description:
Goal: a web control expression for 1:1 direct messages, so a web-only player can send and receive pages/whispers without the terminal. Per the web-portals principle (holomush-sz0h3: web is a superset of telnet).

Current state (verified 2026-06-19): page and whisper exist ONLY as commands (plugins/core-communication: 'page', 'whisper'); there is NO web GUI surface. bd holomush-jq34t is a backend sender-echo unify (page/whisper/pemit), not a web surface.

Scope: a web DM surface (conversation list + thread view + compose) for point-to-point messages. Reads via Web* BFF RPC(s) over the comms substrate; writes ride the EXISTING command path (HandleCommand 'page <target>=<msg>' / 'whisper ...') per the web-portals principle and the scenes-portal D4 precedent (writes ride the command path, no new write RPCs).

Out of scope: 1:N group channels (Epic 10, holomush-0sc); notifications (separate — aqq.6/djj.6/5rh.19); the backend echo-unify (holomush-jq34t).

Design first (dev-flow:brainstorming) before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A web-only player can list their 1:1 conversations, open a thread, and send a page/whisper entirely from the web GUI (no terminal). task pr-prep passes.

title: Principle: the web is a superset of telnet — every gameplay subsystem has a web control expression
description:
PRINCIPLE (product directive; NOT a registry invariant — see below): the HoloMUSH web client MUST be a superset of telnet functionality. A player MUST be able to play completely through the web; telnet — or a raw command line — is never REQUIRED.

BOUNDARY (what 'web control expression' means):
- On-grid real-time play (movement, room say/pose/look, exits, presence) is served first-class by the WEB TERMINAL (/terminal) and/or telnet. The embedded web terminal is a complete, legitimate answer for grid play — no GUI button is required for 'go north'. The web terminal is first-class, not a fallback.
- Beyond on-grid play, a defined set of SUBSYSTEMS each get a dedicated rich web GUI ('control expression') above and beyond the terminal: scenes (private/public); DMs (1:1 page/whisper and 1:N channels); forums; wiki / help / lore / game-setting docs; character profiles / sheets / directory / roster; character and player settings/preferences; and administration.

RATIONALE (why this is written down): the principle was implicit and never a checkable artifact, so it was silently violated. Web create-scene was an EXPLICIT Phase-9 acceptance criterion (bead holomush-5rh.18: 'Web client can list, CREATE, join, and view scenes') that the E9.5 portal redesign (5rh.8) dropped with no recorded decision — yielding a shipped design that contradicts itself (E9.5 makes a 'scenes-only player with no terminal session' first-class, yet gives them no way to ORIGINATE a scene). Capturing the principle is the fix for the CLASS of bug: every future web-surface design MUST consult this directive so scope is not silently re-narrowed.

NOT A REGISTRY INVARIANT (decided 2026-06-19, choice 'a'): 'web superset-of telnet' universally is a directional product principle, not a test-pinnable system guarantee — most subsystem web surfaces are UNBUILT (missing features, not regressions), and .claude/rules/invariants.md rejects non-testable documentary notes as invariants. The testable unit is PER-SUBSYSTEM ('a SHIPPED web subsystem surface MUST be self-sufficient — its core ops never require telnet'); such a narrow invariant MAY be minted later, bound by a per-surface E2E (e.g. scenes create+manage with no telnet), once the first surface is genuinely telnet-free.

TRACKING: theme:web-portals (docs/roadmap.md, Active themes). Cross-cuts Epic 8 (qve: wiki/characters/admin/char-CRUD/offline) and the per-subsystem epics' web views (scenes 5rh, channels 0sc, forums djj). Implemented as a cross-cutting LABEL, not a new epic, because web surfaces are split across both organizing schemes (Epic 8 owns the non-subsystem portals; each subsystem epic owns its own web view), so no single epic owns 'the web as a complete surface.'

title: Bring service-kind capability injection under the resolver grant set (consistency with brokered RequiredCapabilities)
description: Code-reviewer non-blocking finding on PR #4446 regression fix (commit wwytpovr). host.go passes raw RequiredServiceNames() to Register→InjectRequired WITHOUT grant-filtering, whereas RequiredCapabilities() is filtered via grantedSubset before RegisterHostCaps. So service-kind capability modules (Go-native, e.g. cap_alias) are injected on manifest-declaration + registry-presence, not the resolver's per-plugin grant subset. NOT exploitable today (only read-only AuditService registered in production; double-gated by DAG manifest validation) + this is restored pre-cutover behavior. KNOWN-deferred consolidation (epic lesson eykuh.2.12: 'consolidation DEFERRED to sub-specs 3-5'). Standalone follow-up: fold service-kind injection under the same grantedSubset gate. Also: stale cap_property/cap_session/cap_world_query modules linger unregistered (latent footgun) — covered by flgpj.

title: describe me / describe here broken: no host-side 'character' entity mutator
description: Discovered while adding core-objects behavioral coverage (PR #4446 / linmh.1). 'describe me <text>' emits a brokered property.SetProperty{entity_type=character, property=description} correctly, but the host registers NO character entity mutator — property.DefaultEntityMutatorRegistry wires only location + object, and descriptionDefinition.Set resolves property.SharedEntityMutatorRegistry() with no injection seam. So PropertyService fails closed and 'describe me' returns 'Unable to set description right now.' PRE-EXISTING (the old holomush.set_property('character',…) path hit the same missing mutator) — NOT a cutover regression. Fix: add a host-side character entity mutator + an update-character-description seam on the brokered world.mutation/property path (extend property.WorldMutator). Out of scope for #4446 (host-side internal/property).

title: TestPluginServerAdapterInitInjectsBothEventSinkAndFocusClient asserts non-nil injection only — no end-to-end sink exercised
description:
pkg/plugin/service_test.go:683-704

TestPluginServerAdapterInitInjectsBothEventSinkAndFocusClient uses startFocusServiceTestServer (which registers FocusService + StreamHistoryService but NOT EmitService) and then only asserts provider.sink != nil and provider.focusClient != nil (lines 703-704). The dualAwareProvider.Init is a no-op (line 679), so neither client is exercised. The EventSink injection test is vacuous for the emit path: the conn points at a server with no EmitService registered, so any actual Emit call would fail with codes.Unimplemented. The test does not demonstrate the sink works, only that it was assigned.

Contrast with TestPluginServerAdapterInitInjectsFocusClientIntoServiceProvider (line 644), which actually calls JoinFocus through the injected client and verifies the request reached the server.

Suggested fix: either (a) upgrade startFocusServiceTestServer to also register EmitService (e.g., use the testPluginHostServiceServer as the EmitService impl on the same server), or (b) add a separate test that exercises the injected sink's Emit call end-to-end — asserting the request reaches the EmitService stub — analogous to the focus-client injection test.

title: Remove inner-error leaks from non-Internal gRPC status returns in core-scenes
description: plugins/core-scenes/service.go formats an oops error onto the wire via two FailedPrecondition returns: mapTransitionError (service.go:1538, shared by End/Pause/Resume/Update) returns codes.FailedPrecondition with a trailing : %v on err, and JoinScene (service.go:895) does the same. Unlike the codes.Internal fix in holomush-2dsxm (opaque internal error), a FailedPrecondition SHOULD tell the client which precondition failed — so the fix maps each oops code to a SAFE static reason rather than collapsing to internal error. Surfaced by PR #4421 security review (holomush-2g34d findings 1 and 2). Out of scope for 2dsxm which targets codes.Internal only.
acceptance_criteria: No client-facing status return in core-scenes service.go interpolates an inner error via a format verb; FailedPrecondition returns a safe static reason per oops code; opacity tests added; consider broadening TestNoInternalErrorLeaksInSceneSource to all client-facing codes.
notes: Also harmonize logging in core-scenes service.go to errutil.LogErrorContext(ctx, msg, err, extraAttrs...) — the repo's canonical error logger (CLAUDE.md Error Handling), which preserves the oops code + context map that bare slog.WarnContext flattens. The 13 existing handler sites + the 2 added in PR #4421 (holomush-2dsxm) use slog.WarnContext(ctx, ..., "error", err); errutil.LogErrorContext takes extraAttrs so it carries subject_id/scene_id too — strictly better, no tradeoff. Correction to PR #4421 review: finding holomush-2g34d.6 was declined partly on the wrong premise that errutil couldn't carry handler fields; it can (extraAttrs ...any). grpc-errors.md canonical example updated to errutil.LogErrorContext via holomush-x1zol.

title: Audit non-Internal gRPC error leaks in core-scenes (FailedPrecondition %v on oops)
description: Goal: remove inner-error interpolation from non-Internal status returns in plugins/core-scenes/service.go. mapTransitionError (service.go:1538) returns status.Errorf(codes.FailedPrecondition, "scene transition forbidden: %v", err) — shared by End/Pause/Resume/Update — and JoinScene (service.go:895) returns status.Errorf(codes.FailedPrecondition, "scene cannot be joined in its current state: %v", err). The %v formats an oops error onto the wire. Unlike the codes.Internal fix (holomush-2dsxm, opaque 'internal error'), a FailedPrecondition SHOULD tell the client which precondition failed — so the fix is to map each oops code to a SAFE static reason, not collapse to 'internal error'. Surfaced by PR #4421 security review (holomush-2g34d.1/.2). Out of scope for 2dsxm (codes.Internal only); deferred here. Consider broadening TestNoInternalErrorLeaksInSceneSource to all client-facing codes once fixed.
acceptance_criteria: No status.Errorf(<any code>, ...%v..., err) on the wire in core-scenes service.go; FailedPrecondition returns a safe static reason per oops code; tests assert opacity.

title: cryptoActive can be false despite provisioned KEK when rekey wiring deps incomplete
description: Follow-up from holomush-5rh.8.29.12 branch review (non-blocking). buildRekeyWiring (cmd/holomush/crypto_rekey_wiring.go:123-134) returns rekeyWiring{} with Manager==nil when any of CheckpointRepo/PolicyHashSrc/SubjectResolver/SessionStore/RoleChecker/CoordHolder is nil even though KEKProvider!=nil; core.go:866-880 only WARNs. Since BOOT_KEK_REQUIRED guarantees a KEK but cryptoActiveFor gates on RekeyManager!=nil, an incompletely-wired deployment boots with a KEK yet cryptoActive=false (plaintext posture). Decide: make incomplete rekey wiring fatal too (symmetric with BOOT_KEK_REQUIRED), or document the warn-and-degrade as intentional. Pre-existing shape, not introduced by the epic.

title: Integration test: real core-communication page/whisper/pemit emit Sensitive=true & encrypt with crypto enabled
description: Deferred from PR #4395 review (finding holomush-7il0p.5). The crypto-correctness fix for core-communication is covered in layers (compose-match: real-manifest unit test corecomm_crypto_correct_test.go; parseEmitEvents reads sensitive: lua/host_test.go:196; encrypt-on-sensitive: test/integration/crypto/emit_test.go using a synthetic plugin; Lua claim present: brace-scoped source test). The remaining belt-and-suspenders gap is a single end-to-end integration test driving the REAL core-communication 'page' command (integrationtest harness + WithInTreePlugins + WithCryptoEnabled, two characters/sessions via Session.SendCommand) and asserting the resulting character.<id> event is Sensitive and persisted as ciphertext in events_audit. Mirror test/integration/crypto/emit_test.go but with the real plugin + a command, not a synthetic EmitIntent.
acceptance_criteria: An integration test loads core-communication via WithInTreePlugins with crypto enabled, sends a 'page' command between two characters, and asserts the emitted event has Sensitive=true and its audit row is encrypted (codec != identity).

title: rumdl tooling drift: brew installs 0.2.4 but CI pins v0.1.62 — local fast lane can't catch CI docs-lint failures
description:
The local fast lane (task pr-prep / fmt:check) uses the brew-installed rumdl (currently 0.2.4), but CI pins rumdl v0.1.62 via .github/actions/install-formatters/action.yml (RUMDL_VERSION=v0.1.62). The two versions disagree on blank-line rules (MD031/MD032 around fenced blocks/list items), so a docs file can pass local pr-prep and still fail the CI 'Lint docs' lane (rumdl fmt --check).

Hit on PR #4357 (holomush-hz0v4 docs): plan file passed local 0.2.4 but failed CI 0.1.62 (needed a blank line inside a fenced example block). Fixed by formatting with a downloaded 0.1.62 binary; verified both versions accept the result.

Options:
1. Bump the CI pin to 0.2.4 (RUMDL_VERSION) and refresh the SHA256 checksums in install-formatters/action.yml + the other workflows that install it (ci.yaml, ci-docs-skip.yaml, scripts-tests.yaml). Re-run rumdl fmt repo-wide if 0.2.4 reformats existing docs.
2. Pin local tooling to 0.1.62 (e.g., via mise/.tool-versions) so brew upgrades don't drift; add a version-assert to the fast lane.

Recommend (2) plus a fast-lane guard that warns when local rumdl --version != the CI pin, so the gate the developer runs matches the gate CI runs.

title: Meta-test walker descends into .claude/worktrees, causing spurious INV-FS-4 failures
description:
test/meta/focus_delta_gate_test.go's nonTestGoFilesContaining() does a bare filepath.WalkDir(repoRoot) with no exclusion for nested worktree/VCS directories. When a developer has a Claude Code worktree under .claude/worktrees/ (gitignored, not part of the committed tree), the walker descends into it and flags THAT worktree's older code, producing spurious failures.

Observed: TestFocusAdapterPairAssembledOnlyInHelper failed in the 'default' jj workspace because .claude/worktrees/practical-heyrovsky-8331e1/{cmd/holomush/sub_grpc.go,internal/grpc/stream_registry.go} matched NewStreamSenderAdapter( — neither in the allowed map. The main tree itself is clean (only stream_registry.go + focus_wiring.go match, both allowed).

Affected walkers/tests in test/meta/focus_delta_gate_test.go:
- nonTestGoFilesContaining (shared walker)
- TestSendToConnectionConfinedToFocusAndRegistry (INV-FS-1)
- TestFocusAdapterPairAssembledOnlyInHelper (INV-FS-4)

Fix: have the walker skip dot-prefixed VCS/worktree dirs (.git, .jj, .claude/worktrees, node_modules) via a fs.SkipDir on d.IsDir() && excluded(d.Name()). Audit other test/meta walkers for the same latent bug.

Out of scope: the stale .claude/worktrees/practical-heyrovsky-8331e1 worktree itself (abandoned May 8 cruft; can be removed separately).

title: Central invariant doc registry: catalog every INV-* invariant in one place
notes:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-invariant-registry-design.md
design-review round 1 NOT READY: migration count off by 2x, CRYPTO/CLUSTER boundary overlap, markdown-parsing fragility
design-review round 2 READY
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-31-invariant-registry-implementation.md
plan-review round 1 READY

title: Scope and apply INV-P8-N rename + ship Phase 8 invariant meta-test
description:
Deferred from PR #4353 review findings holomush-sl0ir.11 (INV-N namespace collision) and holomush-sl0ir.10 (spec §4 promises an INV-catalog completeness meta-test that does not ship).

CONTEXT / why deferred (investigation during address-findings):
- The Phase 8 spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-scenes-phase-8-board-content-warnings-design.md §4) defines bare INV-1..INV-12.
- Bare INV-N is NOT a Phase-8 anomaly: it is a per-spec LOCAL numbering convention used across dozens of unrelated repo files (the 'repo uses scoped prefixes like INV-P4-N' claim holds only for some specs).
- A mechanical INV-1→INV-P8-1 rename is UNSAFE: inside the same settings/scenes code files, INV-1 means at least three different things —
    * Phase 8 'board returns only open scenes' (spec §4),
    * Lua identity-safety 'NEVER from Lua args' (internal/plugin/hostfunc/stdlib_settings.go:52,78),
    * command-visibility-filter spec (internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions.go:73).
  Renaming blindly would corrupt the identity-safety and command-visibility references.

SCOPE (do this carefully, per-reference disambiguation):
1. Rename INV-N→INV-P8-N in self-contained Phase-8 docs where every INV-N is Phase 8's: the spec §4 catalog + its references, and ADRs holomush-74ib4, holomush-uvbyt, holomush-0blcz.
2. Rename ONLY the unambiguously-Phase-8 code/test comments (CW taxonomy fallback INV-5, CW union INV-6, settings-substrate isolation INV-11, runtime parity INV-8, host domain-ignorance INV-10). Leave INV-1/INV-2 identity-safety (stdlib_settings.go) and command-visibility (functions.go) untouched.
3. Ship test/meta/scenes_phase8_invariants_test.go asserting the INV-P8-1..INV-P8-12 catalog is complete and each is backed by a test (honoring spec §4; precedent: test/meta/scenes_phase6_invariants_test.go, plugin_config_invariants_test.go).

Acceptance: spec/ADRs use INV-P8-N; unambiguous Phase-8 code comments updated; identity-safety/command-visibility INV-N untouched; meta-test ships and passes; task lint green.
notes: Tied to holomush-ts4z (Central invariant doc registry) per controller decision: the INV-P8-N scheme MUST follow the repo-wide naming convention ts4z establishes, to avoid Phase 8 diverging into a naming island. ts4z's broader theme epic is holomush-s6wp.

title: Add parse-only mermaid lint to the docs lane (catch broken diagrams pre-merge)
description:
Goal: gate mermaid diagram SYNTAX in markdown docs before merge, so broken diagrams (e.g. a semicolon in a sequenceDiagram Note, or subgraph "Name [Title]" with a space-in-id) are caught locally + in CI instead of only surfacing as a render-error box when a human opens the file on GitHub.

Why now: nothing gates mermaid syntax pre-merge today. rumdl does not parse fenced-block content; the site uses @pasqal-io/starlight-client-mermaid which is a CLIENT/runtime renderer (renders in the browser; does NOT fail astro build). The surface is large and growing — 33 markdown files already contain ```mermaid blocks, and the project mandates mermaid for all diagrams (feedback_mermaid_diagrams). Concretely hit on PR #4348 (holomush-rsoe6 spec): the §4.2 sequence diagram failed to render because `;` is a statement separator in mermaid sequenceDiagram and the final Note over G,K parsed its second clause as a new statement.

CRITICAL placement requirement: the check MUST live in the docs lane (task pr-prep:docs + the ci-docs-skip docs checks), NOT only in the main Lint. Docs-only PRs no-op the main CI Lint (reference_docs_skip_lint_debt / ci-docs-skip.yaml), and docs-only PRs are exactly the ones that add/edit diagrams — a mermaid check only in main Lint would never run on the PRs that need it most.

Recommended shape (parse-only, not full render):
- A lint:mermaid task that extracts ```mermaid fenced blocks from markdown (docs/ + site/) and runs mermaid parse() on each — syntax validation, no rendering, no headless browser.
- Preferred impl: mermaid parse() API (needs a small jsdom shim), wired into BOTH the docs lane and main lint. Fast; catches the semicolon/subgraph class directly.
- Avoid: @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli (mmdc) full render — pulls puppeteer + ~300MB chromium, slow, CI-flaky. Overkill for syntax gating.
- Toolchain note: adds a node/JS step to an otherwise Go + rumdl + dprint pipeline; acceptable since the site is already bun/JS.
- Coverage caveat: confirm the chosen parser covers every diagram type the repo uses (flowchart, sequenceDiagram, stateDiagram-v2, ER, etc.); the standalone @mermaid-js/parser is newer and may not cover all — mermaid core parse() + jsdom is the safe bet.

Files touched (candidates): Taskfile.yaml (new lint:mermaid + wire into pr-prep:docs and the main lint aggregate), scripts/lint-mermaid.* (the extractor+parser), .github/workflows/ci-docs-skip.yaml (ensure the docs-skip path still runs lint:mermaid), site/package.json or a small tools dir for the JS dep.

Out of scope: full mmdc rendering/visual diffing; auto-fixing diagrams; linting mermaid semantics beyond parseability.
acceptance_criteria: A lint:mermaid task parses every ```mermaid block in docs/ + site/ markdown and exits non-zero on a syntax error; wired into task pr-prep:docs AND the main lint aggregate, and reachable on docs-only PRs (verify ci-docs-skip path runs it). Verified by introducing a deliberately-broken diagram (e.g. a semicolon in a sequenceDiagram Note) and confirming the task fails, then passes once fixed. No headless-browser dependency.
notes: Correction on impl approach: mmdc is ALREADY the de-facto local mermaid validator here — `bunx @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli -i <file.md> -o /tmp/out.md` (user-provided 2026-05-25, recorded in the feedback_mermaid_diagrams memory; bunx pulls it, no repo install; exits non-zero with parse error + line number). So the CI/docs-lane gate should likely REUSE mmdc-via-bunx for consistency with the existing local practice, rather than build a separate parse-only path. Re-evaluate the original "avoid mmdc / prefer parse-only" stance: parse-only is lighter per-PR (no chromium) but introduces a second tool; mmdc-via-bunx is proven + consistent but heavier/flakier in CI. Implementer chooses, but default to reusing mmdc unless CI weight/flakiness proves blocking. Either way: the value is automating a manual step that gets skipped in practice (this very PR #4348 shipped a broken diagram despite the documented manual validator).

title: Extend INV-ROPS-3 colon-stream scan to web/e2e Playwright specs (*.ts)
description: Goal: close the coverage gap that let holomush-rops's colon→dot migration ship a broken E2E. INV-ROPS-3 scans .go + .lua only; the Playwright web/e2e specs were outside scope (the plan trusted 'Task 6 + web vitest tests', but vitest != Playwright e2e). Result: web/e2e/terminal.spec.ts:389 kept a colon `location:${id}` sent to WebQueryStreamHistory, passed every gate, and failed E2E in CI on PR #4343 (fixed reactively in commit d8d016fd). Extend the scan to web/e2e specs (and consider backtick-template colon patterns, which the double-quote regex misses). Files: internal/test/invariants/colon_eradication_test.go. Out of scope: re-running the migration (done).
acceptance_criteria: INV-ROPS-3 (internal/test/invariants/colon_eradication_test.go) scans web/e2e/**/*.ts (excluding generated web/src/lib/connect/ stubs) for colon-style stream literals incl. backtick-template form (`location:${...}`); a reintroduced colon stream in an e2e spec fails the meta-test, not CI E2E.

title: Gateway OTLP relay endpoint for browser OpenTelemetry (same-origin, mirrors /api/sentry-relay)
description:
Goal: add a same-origin gateway endpoint (e.g. /api/otlp/v1/traces) that accepts browser OTLP-HTTP and forwards to the configured OTel collector, so the OTel-native browser tracer (web/src/lib/telemetry.ts) can be enabled in production without ad-blocker/CORS failures on an external ingest origin.

Why: holomush-yak8r root-caused that browser OTel spans cannot reliably reach a collector in prod because (a) the internal collector isn't browser-reachable and (b) browser POSTs to external OTLP/ingest origins are blocked by ad-blockers + CORS — the exact reason the Sentry browser SDK tunnels through the same-origin /api/sentry-relay (internal/web/sentry_relay.go). The OTel browser tracer needs the same same-origin relay treatment.

Plan reference: pattern after internal/web/sentry_relay.go (same-origin handler registered on the gateway mux in internal/web/server.go). Set PUBLIC_OTEL_ENDPOINT (release.yaml vars.PUBLIC_OTEL_ENDPOINT, already wired empty-default) to the relay's same-origin path once it exists.

Files touched (candidates): internal/web/ (new relay handler + server.go mux registration), web/src/lib/telemetry.ts (endpoint already env-driven), .github/workflows/release.yaml (set the var), docs in site/.../operating/how-to/sentry.md.

Out of scope: the OTel browser span enrichment (done in yak8r) and the live-Sentry verification (yak8r AC).
acceptance_criteria: A same-origin gateway endpoint accepts browser OTLP-HTTP trace POSTs and forwards them to the collector (validated by a Go test on the handler). With PUBLIC_OTEL_ENDPOINT pointed at the relay, a live browser session produces command.roundtrip spans that reach the collector and (via the collector's Sentry exporter) Sentry, with connection_id present.
notes:
Carries the residual acceptance of holomush-yak8r (closed; code merged in #4325): after this same-origin gateway OTLP relay lands and the operator sets vars.PUBLIC_OTEL_ENDPOINT + deploys, verify a live browser session's command.roundtrip span reaches Sentry (via the collector's Sentry exporter) with connection_id present. Also independently verify the @sentry/svelte path (PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN secret non-empty + deployed image post-dates the Sentry browser wiring) — that is the actual current cause of zero browser spans in Sentry per yak8r root-cause.
Code deliverable landed in PR #4329 (branch otlp-relay-2uogn): same-origin /api/otlp/v1/traces relay (internal/web/otlp_relay.go) forwarding browser OTLP/HTTP to the collector, gated on OTLP_RELAY_ENDPOINT. Mirrors /api/sentry-relay; forward target is fixed by server config (no open-forwarder/SSRF surface). Handler-level AC validated by Go tests (internal/web/otlp_relay_test.go). Compose/release.yaml/docs wired. code-reviewer READY, pr-prep fast lane pass.

RESIDUAL (kept open): live post-deploy verification — operator sets OTLP_RELAY_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:4318 + PUBLIC_OTEL_ENDPOINT=/api/otlp, deploys, confirms a browser command.roundtrip span reaches Sentry via the collector with connection_id present. Cannot be done in CI.

title: Design: full-system load/perf testing harness + SLOs
description: Brainstorming session: design a repeatable full-system load/performance testing harness for HoloMUSH (telnet + web clients → gateway → core gRPC → EventBus/JetStream → Postgres) plus end-to-end SLOs. Primary driver: establish SLOs and a reusable harness to validate them; capacity-ceiling, CI-regression, and soak are secondary uses building on the same harness.
notes:
grounding/probe: integrationtest harness — Start(t, opts...) boots real CoreServer+embedded NATS JetStream+Postgres testcontainer; StartOption pattern (WithInTreePlugins/WithRealABAC/WithPolicyEngine); in-process gRPC clients, NO gateway/telnet/web layer; plugin event emission not yet wired (shcyu spec in flight).
grounding/rg: existing perf primitives — 11 Go Benchmark funcs; scripts/check-benchmark-regression.sh gates internal/access/policy at 110% baseline; task soak:eventbus = nightly //go:build integration,soak, SOAK_DURATION override, asserts row-parity+goroutine-leak+RSS<50MB (spec §8 1k ev/s 5min). No benchstat tooling. Existing beads: holomush-p7lc (ABAC bench suite), holomush-7b9n (audit row times out under load).
grounding/rg: observability — request paths emit OTel traces (internal/eventbus/telemetry, internal/observability, internal/gatewaymetrics) but NO server-side latency histograms on say/broadcast paths. Harness measures client-perceived send→receive latency. docker/otel-collector present.
grounding/context7: /connectrpc/connect-go v1.19.2 — full-stack web driver consumes Subscribe via *connect.ServerStreamForClient; loop 'for stream.Receive() { stream.Msg() }'; measure broadcast latency at Receive. Telnet has no client lib — thin net.Dial line driver (ref internal/telnet/gateway_handler_test.go). k6/vegeta rejected: can't drive bespoke telnet protocol; Go-native generator fits the all-Go stack.
grounding/context7: k6 + xk6 re-evaluated (user challenge). k6/net/grpc has streaming Client+Stream; xk6 wraps raw TCP for telnet (exact fidelity). say->broadcast latency measurable via emit-timestamp-in-payload + custom Trend. k6 brings ramp/arrival-rate executors + thresholds-as-gates + native Grafana/Prom output + distributed load. HARD LIMIT: cannot attach to in-process integrationtest core tier (needs running server). FIDELITY NUANCE: web tier served by connect-go NewWebServiceHandler (all 3 protocols); browser uses Connect protocol, k6 uses gRPC protocol — different envelope path. Go connect-go client = exact Connect. Adopting k6 REORDERS tiers: full-stack primary, in-process optional. Earlier 'rejected k6' was wrong.
grounding/exa: xk6-connectrpc ALREADY EXISTS — github.com/bumberboy/xk6-connectrpc (Jan 2026), from k6 core issue #5193 (Connect RPC support, open/triage; maintainers suggested xk6 ext first). connect-go client speaks Connect protocol by default → xk6 ext importing connectrpc.com/connect drives EXACT browser wire path incl. streaming (conformance-proven). DECISION CONVERGED: k6 + custom binary [xk6-connectrpc (vet/fork) + xk6-telnet/raw-TCP] = exact fidelity BOTH protocols + k6 batteries + reuse .proto. Caveats: xk6-connectrpc young (vet server-streaming/proto-load/h2; be ready to fork+contribute); pin xk6 build in CI. In-process core tier now optional (Go bench + eventbus soak cover component level).
scenario CORRECTION (user): comms-heavy ~80% mix. say/pose 45% (room broadcast, hot-scene amplified), dm/whisper 35% (directed 1:1 + storage write if offline), move 8%, look 6%, channel 4%, history 2%. TWO distinct fanout classes both hot under comms skew → SLO split 1a say->broadcast p99<250ms (room), 1b dm/whisper->delivery p99<150ms (directed). GROUNDING: say/pose/look/move/who/channel exist; dm = event facet (internal/admin/readstream/filter.go); whisper/page player commands NOT yet implemented → scenario models directed path, gates whisper sub-action on command availability.
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-load-perf-testing-harness-design.md (committed; self-reviewed — 2 consistency fixes: SLO 1-6 steady vs 7-9 soak; client-measured vs server-observed split)
design-review round 1 NOT READY: critical findings — (1) my grounding missed core-communication LUA plugin: say/pose/emit/ooc/page/whisper all EXIST (plugins/core-communication/plugin.yaml + main.lua:186,285); (2) dm is NOT a command (event facet only); (3) channel does NOT exist (no core-channels plugin; future per theme:social-spaces). Plus 4 minor (nightly sequencing, clock-skew tolerance, fork pre-assumption, INV-LOAD-4 vagueness).
Spec revised: directed path = page/whisper (was dm/whisper); SLO 1b renamed; channel dropped from default mix → future variant gated on core-channels plugin; nightly-load 07:00 sequencing clarified; clock-skew ≤50ms tolerance added; §9 fork made contingent on P1 spike; INV-LOAD-4 made concrete (exit-masking meta-test). Re-running reviewer.

ADR captured: holomush-evggu (Use k6 + Custom xk6 Binary for Full-System Load Testing) → docs/adr/holomush-evggu-use-k6-custom-xk6-binary-full-system-load-testing.md. ADR-2 candidate (emit-timestamp latency measurement) reviewed and SKIPPED — kept as spec §4.1 detail.
