Plan ADR captured: holomush-2344h (Isolate xk6 extensions in a separate Go module). ADR-2 candidate (build-time verb validation) SKIPPED — already covered by INV-LOAD-6. Plan ADRs total: holomush-2344h.

title: proto comment: MUST NOT declare AttributeResolverService in provides — contradicted by test-abac-widget/plugin.yaml
description: The new proto comment (attribute.proto lines 15-18) states plugins MUST NOT declare holomush.plugin.v1.AttributeResolverService in their manifest provides: field. This is correct per plugin-manifest.md. However, plugins/test-abac-widget/plugin.yaml:14 still declares it in provides:, violating the MUST NOT. binary_plugin_test.go:151 explicitly notes this causes a load conflict. Either test-abac-widget needs its provides: entry removed, or the proto comment should acknowledge the legacy exception. As written, the MUST NOT is contradicted by an in-tree reference plugin, which will mislead plugin authors.
notes: Orchestrator-verified (drain iter 18): test-abac-widget/plugin.yaml:13-14 declares 'provides: [holomush.plugin.v1.AttributeResolverService]', which the host auto-registers — violating the MUST NOT in .claude/rules/plugin-manifest.md and causing SERVICE_ALREADY_REGISTERED (binary_plugin_test.go:151 works around it by isolating core-scenes). The attribute.proto comment (bead holomush-300ad.9) states the contract CORRECTLY; this is a test-fixture manifest bug, NOT a proto-doc bug. Test-infra scoped (test-abac-widget never loads in production), so P2 not P1. Fix: drop the provides line from test-abac-widget/plugin.yaml (resource_types:[widget] suffices; host auto-wires the resolver).

title: control.proto Shutdown: graceful field logged but not actioned
description: The graceful field in ShutdownRequest is logged by GRPCServer.Shutdown (internal/control/grpc_server.go::Shutdown) but does not alter shutdown behavior. Both graceful=true and graceful=false invoke shutdownFunc identically via go s.shutdownFunc(). The proto comment implies the field controls drain behavior, but the handler makes no distinction. Either the handler should branch on graceful (allow in-flight requests to complete vs hard stop), or the field should be documented as reserved/future-use.

title: plugin.proto: PluginHostService Log/KVGet/KVSet/KVDelete/AddSessionStream/RemoveSessionStream RPCs are declared but unserved
description:
Six PluginHostService RPCs in api/proto/holomush/plugin/v1/plugin.proto are declared in the proto but have NO server implementation and NO client caller in production code:

- Log
- KVGet
- KVSet
- KVDelete
- AddSessionStream
- RemoveSessionStream

The live binary-plugin host server (internal/plugin/goplugin/host_service.go::pluginHostServiceServer) embeds UnimplementedPluginHostServiceServer and only implements EmitEvent, JoinFocus, LeaveFocus, LeaveFocusByTarget, PresentFocus, QueryStreamHistory, DecryptOwnAuditRows, RequestEmitToken, SetConnectionFocus, AutoFocusOnJoin, IsAnyConnFocused, Evaluate (11 of 17). Calls to the six above therefore return codes.Unimplemented. No SDK client (pkg/plugin) wires them either.

Found during SP0 Task 17 (holomush-300ad.17) proto doc-comment authoring. Per SP0 protocol, schema was NOT changed; the proto comments document the current (unwired) reality. Decide: implement these host functions, or remove the vestigial RPCs+messages from the proto. Note the plugin-runtime-symmetry invariant — if KV/Log are intended host functions, they should exist for both binary and Lua runtimes.

title: cog-release.bats setup() depends on developer git signing config — fails non-interactive pr-prep
description:
scripts/tests/cog-release.bats setup() (lines 10-23) runs 'git init' + local user.email/user.name but does NOT disable signing, so the 'git commit -q -m "chore: seed"' at line 21 inherits the developer's global commit.gpgsign=true + gpg.ssh.program=op-ssh-sign (1Password). In any non-interactive shell where the 1Password agent isn't reachable (e.g. a background-launched 'task pr-prep'), signing fails ('1Password: failed to fill whole buffer' / 'fatal: failed to write commit object', status 128), failing tests 1-9 at setup and collapsing 'task test:bats' → pr-prep exit 201.

Impact: LOCAL non-interactive pr-prep only. CI (GitHub Actions) runs with a clean git config (no op-ssh-sign, no commit.gpgsign) so these tests pass there — NOT a CI blocker. But it blocks autonomous/background pr-prep runs (drain landing, etc.).

Fix: make the fixture hermetic — add 'git config commit.gpgsign false' and 'git config tag.gpgsign false' to setup() after the user.name/email lines. Discovered during the holomush-0f0f4 drain landing; worked around there via GIT_CONFIG_COUNT env override.

Acceptance: 'task test:bats' passes when run from a non-interactive background shell with the developer's global signing config active (no GIT_CONFIG override).
acceptance_criteria: scripts/tests/cog-release.bats setup() sets commit.gpgsign=false (+tag.gpgsign=false) on the throwaway repo; task test:bats passes in a non-interactive shell without any GIT_CONFIG env override

title: Migrate test/integration/crypto/readback_test.go to Ginkgo/Gomega
description: Migrate the read-back crypto integration suite from testify (func Test + assert/require) to the repo-standard Ginkgo/Gomega framework. Re-filed standalone from holomush-m7pxs.16 after the epic closed (feature merged via #4267). Suite passes under task test:int today; framework-consistency, not a correctness fix.

title: Wire scene creation + core-scenes command path into the integrationtest harness
description:
The integrationtest harness cannot exercise full-stack scene command-path tests: (1) Session.CreateScene is a stub that calls t.Fatalf (internal/testsupport/integrationtest/session.go:461 — 'scene creation RPC not yet wired'); it must invoke the real FocusCoordinator/scene service CreateScene. (2) The harness wires an EMPTY command registry (harness.go:214 — command.NewDispatcher(command.NewRegistry(), pe)) and loads no plugins, so SendCommand('scene ...') can't reach core-scenes handlers. Both are needed for any Ginkgo/Gomega E2E that drives 'scene <subcommand>' through the real stack.

Also CORRECT the stale 'iwzt-9' references in session.go:164/460/462/600 — they point at holomush-iwzt.9, a CLOSED, unrelated privacy integration-test task (epic E-IWZT 'History scope privacy'), not this harness work. Repoint those TODOs at this bead.

Out of scope: the scene business logic itself (separate beads).
acceptance_criteria: Session.CreateScene invokes the real CreateScene path (no t.Fatalf); integration tests can create a scene and drive its subcommands via SendCommand against a harness that loads core-scenes; the session.go iwzt-9 TODO comments are repointed at this bead.
notes: Unblocks holomush-w6bue (Tier-2 full-stack scene extend E2E, deferred from holomush-8kkv5 / PR #4266). Surfaced during PR #4266 when the deferred E2E was found to reference a non-existent/stale 'iwzt-9' blocker.

title: Consistency: scene subcommand handlers should tokenize args like the gate (Fields[0]+arity)
description: abac-reviewer (PR #4266 CR follow-up) noted a pre-existing parsing asymmetry: sceneResourceRef (the gate) now uses strings.Fields(args)[0], but handleEnd/handlePause/handleInfo use strings.TrimSpace(args). Not an auth bypass (ULID ids; divergent multi-token input fails downstream), but inconsistent. Migrate those handlers to the fields[0]+arity-check shape used by handleLeave. plugins/core-scenes/commands.go:151/191/249.

title: Polish: type-design + comment suggestions from PR #4266 review
description: Deferred non-blocking suggestions from PR #4266 review (epic holomush-p1tq2): .12 Input bare-string field-swap risk; .13 Decision allowed/reason coherence; .15 EngineProvider leaks *attribute.Resolver; .17 denied-by-engine vs no-engine ambiguity; .20 handleResume stale Phase2/3 comment; .22 splitResourceRef doc wording; .23 nil-host/nil-engine paths lack log. Low priority, no behavior/security impact.

title: host_service.go Evaluate: engine-nil and host-nil paths return error without log
description:
In internal/plugin/goplugin/host_service.go, the Evaluate handler (lines 574-590) has two early-return error paths that produce no log entry:

  1. s.host == nil (line 574-576): returns error with no log
  2. eng == nil / EVALUATE_ENGINE_UNCONFIGURED (line 586-590): returns error with no log

By contrast, the token-rejected path (lines 610-615) correctly calls slog.WarnContext before returning the error. The inconsistency means that misconfiguration (nil host or nil engine) is silent at the gRPC handler level.

These are structural misconfigurations that SHOULD produce at least a Warn-level log so operators can diagnose why plugin Evaluate calls are failing. The gRPC interceptor may or may not log the returned error depending on the interceptor stack configuration.

Suggested fix: add slog.ErrorContext or slog.WarnContext calls for the nil-host and nil-engine paths, consistent with the token-rejected pattern at line 611.

Note: the engine-nil path is guarded by a test (TestEvaluateEngineUnconfiguredFailsClosed) so the fail-closed behavior is verified; only the log visibility is missing.

title: splitResourceRef doc-comment says 'single colon' but rejects multi-colon IDs silently
handleResume doc-comment says 'Owner-only in Phase 2' but resume policy already admits any participant
EvaluateDecision and pluginauthz.Decision have no way to distinguish 'denied by engine' from 'no engine consulted'
description:
Files: pkg/plugin/evaluate_client.go lines 17-24; internal/plugin/pluginauthz/evaluate.go lines 91-96

Both EvaluateDecision (SDK-facing) and pluginauthz.Decision (internal) use Allowed=false + MatchedPolicy="" as the shape for three distinct cases: (a) engine returned a default-deny with no matching policy, (b) the entitlement check failed before the engine was consulted, and (c) an engine error occurred (though the error path also returns a non-nil error, so callers who check err first are safe).

For case (b) — entitlement rejection — the error is non-nil so the Decision value is irrelevant. But for case (a) vs a future allow-with-no-policy case, MatchedPolicy="" is ambiguous. More practically, GatedSubcommand.Run reads dec.Reason for UX copy (line 52-55 of gated_dispatch.go): if the engine returns EffectDefaultDeny with Reason="" (a valid engine response, tested in TestEvaluate_DefaultDenyOnUnmatchedPolicy), the fallback 'you are not permitted to do that' fires. This is safe today but the type does not communicate that Reason may legitimately be empty even on a valid deny decision.

Suggested fix: consider adding an explicit Source field to Decision (e.g. SourceEngine, SourceEntitlement, SourceError) so callers and log consumers can distinguish without pattern-matching on error codes or reason strings. This is lower priority than findings 1-4 but would make the type self-documenting for future consumers.

Ratings: Encapsulation 6, Invariant expression 5, Usefulness 6, Enforcement 6.

title: EngineProvider.AttributeResolver() leaks concrete *attribute.Resolver through a public interface
description:
File: internal/plugin/setup/subsystem.go lines 44-50

The EngineProvider interface now includes AttributeResolver() *attribute.Resolver, returning a concrete pointer to an internal implementation type. This couples every implementor and every consumer of the interface to the attribute package's internal Resolver struct — the interface can no longer be satisfied by a mock, a test double, or a future alternative implementation without importing (and depending on) the attribute package.

The ABACSubsystem.AttributeResolver() method (internal/access/setup/subsystem.go) returns the same *attribute.Resolver. The subsystem_test.go compile-time guard (var _ setup.EngineProvider = (*fakeEngineProvider)(nil)) confirms this is understood as an intentional tight coupling, but it makes the seam brittle: if the attribute.Resolver API changes shape, both sides of this interface break simultaneously.

Suggested fix: extract a minimal interface that exposes only the operations the plugin subsystem actually uses — RegisterProvider and UnregisterProvider — and return that interface from EngineProvider.AttributeResolver():

  type AttributeProviderRegistry interface {
    RegisterProvider(*plugins.PluginAttributeProvider) error
    UnregisterProvider(namespace string) bool
  }

EngineProvider.AttributeResolver() AttributeProviderRegistry

This keeps the concrete type internal to the ABAC subsystem and satisfies the Dependency Inversion principle for a public interface.

Ratings: Encapsulation 4, Invariant expression 6, Usefulness 7, Enforcement 7.

title: Decision struct permits allowed=true with denial-flavoured Reason (no coherence invariant)
description:
File: internal/plugin/pluginauthz/evaluate.go lines 91-96

pluginauthz.Decision is a plain struct with three exported mutable fields: Allowed bool, Reason string, MatchedPolicy string. Nothing prevents constructing Decision{Allowed: true, Reason: "access denied: policy X", MatchedPolicy: ""} — an incoherent state where the result claims allowed but the reason reads as a denial, and the matched policy is absent. The mirrored PluginHostServiceEvaluateResponse in the proto has the same shape and the same risk.

The zero-value Decision{} correctly encodes 'denied with no reason', which is the right default (fail-closed). But the allowed=true + denial-reason combination is a latent footgun for callers (e.g. GatedSubcommand.Run checks only dec.Allowed; a future caller that also reads dec.Reason for UX copy could surface confusing messages).

Suggested fix: make Decision a value type with a private constructor analogous to types.Decision (which uses types.NewDecision(effect, reason, policyID)). Export NewDecision(allowed bool, reason, matchedPolicy string) Decision so the invariant 'when allowed=false, Reason explains the denial; when allowed=true, Reason is advisory or empty' is encoded in one place. Alternatively, add a docstring stating which combinations are valid, and add a Validate() method for test assertions.

Ratings: Encapsulation 5, Invariant expression 4, Usefulness 8, Enforcement 4.

title: Input struct free-string fields allow subject/action/resource misassignment at construction
description:
File: internal/plugin/pluginauthz/evaluate.go lines 81-89

pluginauthz.Input has three adjacent bare-string fields: Subject, Action, and Resource. Because all three are untyped strings, a caller can trivially swap them at the struct literal (e.g. pass the action string as Subject, or the resource ref as Action) with no compile-time or construction-time feedback. The comment 'Subject is HOST-DERIVED and MUST NOT originate from plugin-supplied data (INV-1)' is enforced by convention and code review, not by the type system.

Suggested fix: introduce typed wrappers — e.g. type SubjectRef string, type ActionString string, type ResourceRef string — or a constructor function NewInput(...) that validates ordering. Even without named types, a NewInput(engine, auditor, pluginName, ownedTypes, subject, action, resource) constructor would surface argument-order mistakes at call sites (the compiler's positional error messages call out mismatches). The higher-value option is typed wrappers so the compiler rejects Input{Subject: someActionValue} outright.

Ratings: Encapsulation 7, Invariant expression 4, Usefulness 8, Enforcement 5. The validation inside Evaluate() catches empty strings, but field-swap bugs (non-empty, wrong-slot) are invisible to it.

title: Meta-test outOfScope check is a tautology — tests author-controlled list, not actual file content
description: test/integration/crypto/readback_test.go lines 700-705: TestINVRBInvariantCoverage asserts that outOfScope invariants (INV-RB-8, INV-RB-10) are not in the inScope slice — but inScope is a hardcoded constant in the same function. The check cannot fail unless the author puts INV-RB-8 in their own inScope list. It does NOT verify that INV-RB-8/10 are absent from actual test names in the test files. Additionally, fileReferencesInvariant (line 738) does a string.Contains search, meaning coverage is satisfied if the invariant appears in any comment — not just in an assertion or test function name. This means INV-RB-3 'passes' the meta-test purely due to comments, not an actual test. The meta-test provides weaker coverage guarantees than implied by its documentation.

title: TestReadbackWithoutReadbackFlagDenied inlines 100+ lines of setup duplicating buildReadbackEnv
description: test/integration/crypto/readback_test.go lines 461-608: TestReadbackWithoutReadbackFlagDenied re-implements the full DEK/KEK/audit/publisher/authguard wiring inline (~100 lines) rather than using buildReadbackEnv. The only meaningful difference is the noReadbackManifestLookup vs singlePluginReadbackLookup. buildReadbackEnv could accept a functional option or a manifest lookup parameter to avoid this duplication. As written, any change to the setup (e.g., new required field in authguard.New, new audit wiring) must be made in two places. Per YAGNI and project code style (SHOULD prefer simple, readable over clever), the inline setup is unnecessary complexity.

title: E2E: scene extend command-path via integrationtest (Ginkgo/Gomega)
description:
Full-stack E2E for the host Evaluate gate (deferred from holomush-8kkv5.10 Step 3). Drive 'scene extend' (admin allow / player deny) and 'scene end' non-participant deny THROUGH the real engine + core-scenes AttributeResolver, end-to-end. MUST use Ginkgo/Gomega, //go:build integration, task test:int.

BLOCKED ON HARNESS WORK (no dedicated bead yet; tracked by code TODOs): the internal/testsupport/integrationtest harness currently (a) wires an EMPTY command registry (harness.go:214 command.NewDispatcher(command.NewRegistry(), pe)) and loads NO plugins, and (b) Session.CreateScene is a t.Fatalf stub (session.go:462, 'TODO iwzt-9 — scene creation RPC not yet wired'). Both must be implemented before this E2E can run: load the core-scenes binary plugin + its command registry into the harness, and wire the scene-creation RPC.

The admin-gate SECURITY PROPERTY is already proven at Tier-1 by holomush-8kkv5.10 (internal/access/policy/plugin_evaluate_gate_test.go: real engine + real DSL, admin allow / player deny). This follow-up adds the full-stack command-path coverage the project standard expects, plus participant/owner resource-attribute assertions that need the loaded AttributeResolver.
notes: Real prerequisite is holomush-knnrj (harness scene-creation + core-scenes command-path support), NOT iwzt-9. The 'iwzt-9' in session.go TODOs is a stale ref to the CLOSED, unrelated holomush-iwzt.9 (History scope privacy). Re-pointed: this E2E is blocked-by holomush-knnrj.

title: ADR beads diverge from render-adr output — /adr update/supersede/render would corrupt legacy & retroactively-captured ADRs
description:
Discovered during holomush-g8ubx (upstream render-adr fix reconciliation). holomush's committed docs/adr/*.md files are NOT reproducible by the current dev-flow render-adr. Running /adr update|supersede|render (or a mass re-render) on these beads silently corrupts the committed files.

Concrete divergences (controlled /tmp re-render of 5 ADRs vs committed):
- SLUG: render-adr derives slug from bead title; committed slugs are hand-curated/shorter → re-render emits a NEW filename and orphans the original, breaking README index links. (~3/5 sampled diverged.)
- DATE: render-adr uses bead created_at; for the 17 migrated legacy ADRs created_at = 2026-05-14 migration date, and for retroactively-captured ADRs it is the filing date — neither is the true decision date preserved in the committed file (e.g. vi5e committed 2026-02-02).
- STATUS: render-adr maps bead status=open → 'Proposed'. holomush-ydti is committed 'Accepted' but its bead is still open → re-render downgrades a live Accepted ADR to Proposed.
- LOST CONTENT: render-adr strips the SPDX license header (emits markdownlint-disable/adr-render comments instead), drops the '**Originally:** ADR NNNN' legacy line, and emits Deciders '—' because beads lack adr_deciders metadata (committed says 'HoloMUSH Contributors').
- BODY: committed body text differs from the current bead description in places (committed/bead drift).

Note: adr-doctor.sh does NOT enforce render-based INV-A22 drift (counts/frontmatter/supersession-edges/meta-coverage only), so this divergence is currently invisible to lint — masking the risk until someone runs /adr on a legacy bead.
acceptance_criteria: Either (1) backfill decision beads so render-adr reproduces committed files faithfully — set true decision date, adr_deciders metadata, reconcile open-but-Accepted bead status, pin/curate slugs, port SPDX + Originally lines into the render template or bead body, sync body text; OR (2) explicitly scope which ADRs are render-adr-managed vs frozen-migrated, and gate /adr to refuse the frozen set. adr-doctor.sh SHOULD then gain a real render-drift check (INV-A22) so divergence cannot silently return.

title: INV-P5-7 pin is a happy-path test; atomicity observer-isolation is not verified
description:
The INV-P5-7 coverage pin was moved from the deleted TestUpdateSessionConnection_AtomicCommit (memstore_test.go) to TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_HappyPath (internal/store/session_store_integration_test.go:71-113).

INV-P5-7 requires: Connection.FocusKey + Info.PresentingFocus MUST be applied under one Store-lock acquisition with no external observer seeing torn state. The original pin explicitly tested that — it interleaved concurrent Get calls to verify no torn intermediate state was visible.

TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_HappyPath verifies only that both fields commit successfully in the success path (lines 104-112). It does NOT:
- Verify that a concurrent Get between the two writes cannot see a torn intermediate state
- Verify rollback on mutator error (i.e., if the mutator returns an error, neither field is committed)

The atomicity guarantee is mechanically correct (Postgres transaction + defer tx.Rollback), but the pin now documents INV-P5-7's operation-level atomicity contract with a weaker test than the original.

A stronger alternative already exists in the same file: TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_LockAcquisitionOrder_NoDeadlock (line 122) uses concurrent goroutines and would be a better peer. A genuine atomicity pin would be a test where the mutator returns an error and the test asserts that neither sessions.presenting_focus nor session_connections.focus_key was modified.

Suggested action: either (a) add TestPostgresUpdateSessionConnection_MutatorError_Rollback to session_store_integration_test.go and repoint the pin, or (b) document in the coverage note that Postgres transaction semantics provide the rollback guarantee and the happy-path test is intentionally sufficient for INV-P5-7's commit path (leaving the rollback path to implicit DB semantics). Option (b) would be an explicit decision rather than a silent gap.

title: Design: error-event seam — slog.Any+LogValuer idiom + handler-level CaptureException (evolves holomush-1wbzn)
description: Brainstorm/design the error-event seam, decomposed out of holomush-ow4ix (sloglint Tier C). Agreed direction (2026-05-24 session): KEEP oops (no migration — it implements slog.LogValuer); adopt the idiomatic slog.Any("error", err) logging pattern so oops renders its full structured context (code/context/tags/stacktrace) natively; RETIRE errutil.oopsAttrs and largely dissolve the errutil LogError/LogErrorContext wrappers. Move sentry.CaptureException to a HANDLER in internal/telemetry (errors.As-inspect ERROR records for the live oops error, capture with stacktrace) instead of a call-site wrapper — catches every error log uniformly, keeps sentry-go confined to internal/telemetry (better for INV-L1 than the errutil-call-site decision). This EVOLVES the merged 1wbzn ADR (CaptureException placement moves errutil->handler); use dev-flow:evolve-adr. Migrates the ~8 errutil.LogError sites here. Overlap note: error-log sites touched for ctx in the sloglint migration may be touched again here for slog.Any attr-shape — accept the double-touch, keep PRs focused.
notes: custom-funcs IS available via golangci-lint YAML (linters.settings.sloglint.custom-funcs) — re-verified context7 2026-05-24. So IF a thin errutil wrapper survives, sloglint CAN enforce ctx/attrs on it via custom-funcs (an option, not foreclosed). The agreed direction (slog.Any + LogValuer, dissolve wrapper) likely makes this moot, but record it as available.

title: Design: gateway-survival deploy strategy (don't restart gateway unless it actually changed)
description:
STATUS: future design work — needs a proper dev-flow:brainstorming session (not yet started). This bead captures the requirement + findings so a fresh session can resume cold.

== Requirement (from user, 2026-05-24) ==
A release MUST NOT restart the gateway process unless the gateway itself actually changed. The gateway holds ALL client TCP connections — telnet :4201 AND web/WS via cloudflared→gateway:8080 — so recreating it drops every connected user. A pause/restart of CORE is acceptable (core holds NATS + DB conns, no client sockets; client sockets live on the gateway and survive a core restart). Goal: don't kill web/telnet TCP unless absolutely needed (i.e., gateway code/web assets changed).

== Grounded findings (this session) ==
- Process split: cmd/holomush/gateway.go:96 'gateway = telnet/web servers' (binds telnet :4201 + web 8080); cmd/holomush/core.go:117 'core = engine, plugins' (no published client ports; gateway→core over gRPC --core-addr). Confirmed in compose.prod.yaml: ports 4201:4201 only on gateway (line ~115); caddy/cloudflared point at gateway:8080.
- ROOT BLOCKER: core and gateway are the SAME image at the SAME pin — compose.prod.yaml:55 and :93 both 'ghcr.io/holomush/holomush:${HOLOMUSH_VERSION}'. One Go binary, two subcommands. Every release bumps both → up -d recreates both → gateway TCP dropped.
- Restart semantics: gateway restart = hard-drop all telnet+web conns (sockets terminate on gateway). core restart = gateway sockets survive, backend briefly unavailable (commands fail); depth of impact depends on whether session state is persisted (event-sourced/Postgres) vs in-memory in core — NOT YET VERIFIED, confirm in brainstorm.
- A TCP conn cannot survive a process restart without socket handoff or a front proxy; so 'don't kill TCP' ⇒ 'don't restart the gateway process'.

== Options sketched (for the brainstorm to evaluate) ==
A. Minimal: fix the deploy so plain 'up -d' reliably recreates ONLY the changed service (it already has 'recreate-iff-changed' semantics; the bug is it no-op'd when the image DID change). Does NOT decouple gateway from core while they share an image — a code release still restarts both. = holomush-aocap.
B. Graceful gateway restart: connection draining + client auto-reconnect (web WS reconnects; telnet users still drop). Softens, doesn't eliminate.
C (recommended starting point): independent version pins on the SAME image — compose vars HOLOMUSH_CORE_VERSION / HOLOMUSH_GATEWAY_VERSION; release always bumps core, bumps gateway ONLY when gateway-affecting paths changed (web/, internal/web/, cmd/holomush/gateway*, proto, curated shared set). 'up -d' then recreates only core; gateway (unchanged tag) keeps running → TCP survives. Trade-offs to resolve: how to detect 'gateway changed' (path allowlist vs commit scope vs manual), brief core↔gateway gRPC version skew window + compat policy, default-to-restart-when-unsure.
D. Horizontal gateways + rolling behind an LB for zero-downtime web. Biggest lift; telnet single host port 4201 + single cloudflared tunnel both need rework.

== Relationship ==
Subsumes/overlaps holomush-aocap (deploy 'up -d' didn't recreate changed service; readiness-only health probe masked stale version). The chosen design should also (1) make recreate reliable and (2) add a health probe that asserts the RUNNING build version, not just readiness.

== Next step ==
Run dev-flow:brainstorming on this bead. Verify session-state persistence (core-restart safety) and the gateway-change detection mechanism early.
notes: Option A (minimal recreate fix = holomush-aocap) LANDED in PR #4260: deploy now reliably recreates the changed service (fixed the -T/heredoc bug that made up -d a no-op) + asserts the deployed image tag + polls readiness. This bead's remaining scope is unchanged: gateway-survival (Option C — independent core/gateway version pins on the shared image so releases recreate ONLY core, sparing telnet/web TCP). #4260 still recreates BOTH, so the connection-drop problem persists until this design ships.

title: Decide whether production should fail-closed when KEK provider is unavailable (currently silent fail-open)
description:
Goal: decide and document the PRODUCTION boot policy when the KEK provider cannot be constructed (HOLOMUSH_KEK_FILE / HOLOMUSH_KEK_PASSPHRASE absent or invalid). Today the behavior is unconditional silent fail-open, which is correct for dev but questionable for prod.

Background: cmd/holomush/core.go fail-opens across the board when kekProvider == nil:
  - admin TOTP RPCs (Authenticate / Approve / ResetTOTP) return errors (core.go:691-743)
  - Rekey admin RPCs fall back to Unimplemented (core.go:815)
  - AdminReadStream (operator-read) falls back to Unimplemented
  - history reader keeps nil-auth passthrough (core.go:820)
On a MISCONFIGURED production deploy (KEK env vars missing), the server boots "successfully" and silently disables TOTP-gated admin auth, dual-control, rekey, and operator-read — all security controls — with only WARN logs. An operator could run for a long time believing admin TOTP is enforced when it is not.

Current state: there is NO prod/dev mode flag in core config. The only prodMode concept is local to kek.NewEnvSource (internal/eventbus/crypto/kek/source_env.go:44-48), which rejects ENV-source in prod; the core server uses FILE-source, so that guard does not apply here.

Design questions to resolve:
  - Should production refuse to boot (fail-closed) when KEK is absent, rather than silently degrading?
  - If degradation is allowed, should it require an explicit opt-in (e.g., --allow-no-kek / config flag) so the operator consciously accepts disabled admin TOTP?
  - Does this need a first-class prod/dev mode signal in core config (none exists today)?
  - Which subset of the degraded RPCs is acceptable to disable silently vs MUST be fail-closed in prod?
This decision is ADR-worthy (trust posture of admin/crypto controls).

Files touched (candidates):
  - cmd/holomush/core.go:684-820 (the fail-open sites)
  - internal/config/ (potential new mode/flag)
  - relevant ADR under docs/adr/

Related: holomush-q825 (prod KEK wiring — appears already implemented), holomush-aub5 (Vault KMS substrate epic), the jxo8 event-payload-crypto epic line.

Out of scope: the log-hygiene fix for the dev WARN (holomush-iw804); implementing Vault (aub5).
acceptance_criteria:
An explicit, documented production boot policy for missing/invalid KEK is decided and recorded (ADR).
If fail-closed (or opt-in degradation) is chosen, it is implemented and covered by tests, and the dev path remains fail-open (or low-friction).
The policy disambiguates which degraded RPCs are acceptable-silent vs must-block in prod.

title: Enable sloglint (context: scope) + migrate bare slog/logger calls to *Context variants
description:
Goal: mechanically enforce the 'MUST use context-carrying slog variants' rule codified in CLAUDE.md (Structured Logging) and .claude/rules/logging.md.

Plan reference: companion to the OTel-native log-surfacing design (holomush-zgtqo). Trace correlation into the OTel/Sentry log pipeline only works when ctx is threaded into log calls.

Scope:
- Add 'sloglint' to .golangci.yaml linters.enable with settings { context: scope } (flags bare slog/logger calls ONLY when a context.Context is in scope — encodes the 'unless absolutely impossible' carve-out).
- Migrate offending call sites to *Context variants (slog.InfoContext, errutil.LogErrorContext, etc.). Blast radius at filing: ~321 bare slog.X( + ~368 logger.X(-style + ~296 already-Context. Likely phased by package to keep PRs reviewable.
- Where no ctx is in scope and none is plumbable (main/init/bare goroutines), leave bare — sloglint context:scope already permits these.

Out of scope: changing log levels/formats; the OTel LoggerProvider wiring (separate, in zgtqo).

title: Plugin does not react to character deletion — orphan participant + vote-roster rows accumulate
description:
Surfaced by Phase 6 FK audit (2026-05-24).

## Problem

The per-plugin Postgres role isolation (CLAUDE.md schema-isolation: plugin role has REVOKE ALL on public) means plugin-owned tables CANNOT FK to public.characters or public.players. As a result, plugin tables that reference character IDs cannot rely on Postgres CASCADE to clean up after character deletion. Two known affected tables:

- plugins/core-scenes/scene_participants (since 000003): rows where character_id references a now-deleted character orphan indefinitely.
- plugins/core-scenes/published_scene_votes (added in Phase 6 / holomush-5rh.20.1): vote-roster rows for deleted characters orphan; the resolution check counts them as 'pending' indefinitely until the vote window times out → ATTEMPT_FAILED with reason TIMEOUT. Owner retry re-snapshots the roster (which won't include the deleted character).

The host-side codebase uses ON DELETE CASCADE extensively (e.g., characters.player_id REFERENCES players(id) ON DELETE CASCADE per migration 000002; legacy scene_participants in 000001:160 cascades on both scene + character). So character deletion IS a real lifecycle event — the gap is just that plugin-side tables can't participate in the cascade.

## Phase 6 mitigations (in place, not new work)

- Vote-roster timeout pattern: a roster row referencing a deleted character ages out via vote_window timeout. Attempt fails, owner retries with fresh roster. Tolerable but not surgical.
- Single-membership inference (commands.go:828 resolveSingleSceneMembership): defensively reads ListScenesForCharacter, which filters by character ID. If the character is deleted, no rows return, so the helper degrades gracefully.

## Proposed solutions (out of scope for Phase 6)

Option A: 'character_deleted' lifecycle event. Add a host-emitted event when characters are deleted; plugins subscribe and clean up their references. Substrate work; affects all plugins with character refs.

Option B: Per-plugin GC sweep. Periodic background task (similar to publish_scheduler) that JOINs against an authoritative character ID list and removes orphans. Requires the plugin role to have read access to characters table (loosens role isolation) OR the host to expose a 'list-deleted-characters' RPC the plugin can poll.

Option C: Defer cleanup; treat orphans as a known limitation. Read paths gracefully degrade ('[deleted character]' rendering). Storage cost is small because character deletion is uncommon in production. Cheapest; matches current Phase 6 posture.

## Scope considerations

- Affects multiple plugins (not just core-scenes). Setting plugin substrate, not Phase 6.
- Should NOT block Phase 6 implementation — the timeout pattern handles it.
- Worth a brainstorm pass before implementation to pick A/B/C and define the contract.

## References

- Phase 6 FK audit on bead holomush-5rh.20 notes (2026-05-24)
- CLAUDE.md Plugin System section — schema isolation invariant
- internal/store/migrations/000001_baseline.up.sql:160 — host-side scene_participants CASCADE example
- plugins/core-scenes/migrations/000003_scene_participants_and_ops_events.up.sql — plugin-side scene_participants (no character_id FK)
- plugins/core-scenes/migrations/000008_scene_publication.up.sql (when shipped via holomush-5rh.20.1) — published_scene_votes (no character_id FK)

title: obs: vendor-neutral error/telemetry/metrics abstraction at every seam
description:
Generalize error capture, telemetry, and metrics emission behind a thin
vendor-neutral surface so the backend choice (Sentry, slog-only, future swap)
becomes a single-file decision instead of a sprinkle of sentry.CaptureException
calls across the codebase.

Goal
----
A single 'reporter' (or equivalent) abstraction that:
- Captures errors / panics at every seam (Connect handlers, plugin runner,
  HTTP/telnet edges, EventBus subscribe errors, ABAC audit failures, DB
  retry exhaustion, etc.) — without naming Sentry at the call site
- Emits matching spans + metrics at the same seams so even when no error
  backend is wired we still get OTel coverage in Tempo/Prometheus
- Has a no-op / stdlib-slog implementation so disabling Sentry doesn't
  silently break anything
- Lets implementers swap Sentry → another backend (Honeycomb errors,
  Rollbar, native Issues, anything) in one file

Why now
-------
The Sentry trial (holomush-0wun, PR #4197) proved the pipe works. If we
keep Sentry past trial, the next instinct is to call sentry.CaptureException
at handler edges. Doing that directly couples N files to one vendor;
doing it through a thin Reporter interface keeps that coupling at one
edge. Cheap to add now, expensive to retrofit later.

Scope
-----
Phase 1 — abstraction:
- internal/telemetry/reporter.go (or similar) with a Reporter interface
  covering CaptureError(ctx, err, attrs...), CaptureMessage(ctx, level,
  msg, attrs...), and optionally Breadcrumb(ctx, ...)
- Sentry-backed implementation (uses sentry-go directly; only file that
  imports sentry-go)
- Default implementation: OTel span error-recording + slog
- Package-level Reporter singleton wired by telemetry.Init based on
  whether SENTRY_DSN is set
- Tests: fake reporter records calls for assertion in unit tests

Phase 2 — seam wiring (likely each is its own child task):
- Connect server interceptor calls Reporter.CaptureError on non-OK
- Plugin runner panic recovery uses Reporter
- slog handler bridge: WARN+ -> Reporter
- Telnet accept-loop panic recovery
- EventBus subscriber error path
- ABAC engine internal errors (currently swallowed per holomush-3y6l)

Phase 3 — metrics standardization:
- Define naming convention (component.seam.outcome counter pattern)
- Catalog existing metrics (Prometheus + OTel) and rationalize names
- Hook coverage check so new seams don't ship without metrics

Out of scope (file as separate beads / child tasks)
---------------------------------------------------
- Actual implementation of seam wiring (each seam = its own child task)
- Session Replay / RUM on browser (separate vendor-specific work)
- Switching away from Sentry (the abstraction enables it, not this bead)

Relationship to existing work
-----------------------------
- Builds on holomush-0wun (Sentry trial — provides the first real
  Reporter consumer)
- Supersedes the 'follow-ups' list under holomush-0wun (those become
  child tasks of this epic once Phase 1 design lands)
- Closes the design gap behind holomush-ghm (comprehensive Prometheus
  metrics) and holomush-gkhn (event+session metrics) by giving them a
  naming convention to hang on
- Strengthens the partial fixes from holomush-3y6l, holomush-xlty,
  holomush-afs9, holomush-fu0r — those become 'use the new Reporter'
  follow-ups
- Likely warrants a theme:observability-seams label once Phase 1 design
  spawns 5+ child tasks (per CLAUDE.md Strategic Themes)

Skills
------
- dev-flow:brainstorming for the abstraction shape
- dev-flow:writing-plans for Phase 2 / 3 task graph
- dev-flow:test-driven-development for the Reporter contract

title: cleanup: backfill unit tests for Phase 5 Lua + gRPC client paths (codecov debt)
description:
codecov/patch failed on PR #4191 with 54.88% patch coverage (target 75%). The gap is 466 missing lines, concentrated in 3 files exercised via integration but not unit tests:

1. internal/plugin/hostfunc/stdlib_focus.go — 2.58% (113 missing lines). Lua bindings for 3 Phase 5 RPCs (SetConnectionFocus, AutoFocusOnJoin, IsAnyConnFocused). Need table-driven unit tests covering: ULID parsing failure paths, nil FocusOps fallback, response translation (proto bytes → Lua tables), and the FocusFailureReason enum→string mapping.

2. pkg/plugin/focus_client.go — 17.52% (80 missing). gRPC client wrappers (SetConnectionFocus, AutoFocusOnJoin, IsAnyConnFocused). Existing tests at lines 510-580 cover SetConnectionFocus 3 cases via real gRPC test server; extend to AutoFocusOnJoin + IsAnyConnFocused.

3. internal/plugin/lua/focus_ops_adapter.go — 0% (25 missing). Coordinator→FocusOps adapter. Add direct unit tests with mock Coordinator covering all 7 forwarded methods + AutoFocusOnJoin tuple translation.

Plus medium-coverage gaps in internal/store/session_store.go (57%), internal/plugin/goplugin/host_service.go (50%), internal/grpc/stream_registry.go (56%). These are exercised by integration tests but unit coverage is light.

Integration tests pin INV-P5-N correctness end-to-end. This bead is about unit-level depth, not correctness.

title: Plugin-installed policies bypass seed-coverage validator (holomush-xxel scope gap)
description:
## Background

holomush-xxel added `warnOnMissingSeedCoverage` at `internal/access/setup/seed_coverage.go` — at BuildABACStack construction, walks `policy.SeedPolicies()` and WARNs per namespace referenced by seeds but not registered as a provider.

## Gap

The validator covers ONLY the seed corpus. Plugin manifests can install additional policies at plugin-load time via `internal/plugin/policy_installer.go::PolicyInstaller.InstallPluginPolicies`. These are NOT scanned by xxel's validator.

A plugin that ships a manifest policy gating on `resource.kv.X` while no `kv` provider exists hits the SAME silent default-deny class g776 / xxel were written to surface — with no startup signal.

## Acceptance

1. Either:
   - Extend `warnOnMissingSeedCoverage` to ALSO scan plugin-installed policies. Tricky because they load AFTER BuildABACStack — would need to fire at plugin-load time per plugin, OR run a sweep after all plugins load.
   - Add per-plugin validation in PolicyInstaller.InstallPluginPolicies that walks the policies being installed and validates against the (then-current) resolver's registered namespaces. Symmetric pattern; per-plugin grain.
2. Test pinning: a plugin manifest with an invalid namespace reference produces a clear error at install time.

## Why P2 (not P1)

Plugin manifests are reviewed at install time; the bug class is less likely than the seed-corpus case (where bugs slip in via PR review of seed.go edits). Still worth fixing for parity.

## Related

- holomush-xxel: ships the seed-only validator
- holomush-g776: original bug instance
- holomush-72ou, holomush-k3ud: other provider gaps surfaced by xxel

title: Scenes: in-band signal when reconnect drops focus due to revoked membership
description:
Phase 5 §8 reconnect focus restoration: when a session's PresentingFocus points at a scene the character is no longer a member of (revoked while disconnected), the new connection silently falls back to grid + emits a structured warning log. There is no in-band signal to the player — their telnet just lands on grid without explanation, and subsequent expectations of scene #X events are silently violated.

## Scope

Render a one-shot message to the new connection's first frame when reconnect-fallback fires:

  > Your focus on scene #42 was lost while disconnected (you are no longer a member). Use 'scene list' to see your current scenes.

## Mechanism

Two paths to consider during brainstorming/spec:

1. Substrate emits a 'focus_restored_failed' event on the new connection's notifications:<character_id> stream; client renders the message. Pro: composes with existing event delivery. Con: requires a new event type + plugin schema.

2. Substrate stashes a transient 'first-frame banner' on the new Connection, rendered by the dispatcher before any other output. Pro: simple. Con: new render-injection mechanism.

3. Plugin-side: scene plugin observes reconnect via JoinFocus-with-restore signal and renders the message. Pro: keeps logic plugin-local. Con: requires substrate to surface 'this was a restoration attempt' on JoinFocus.

## Out of scope

Web client UI for the same case (Phase 9 territory).

## Predecessor

Phase 5 spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-21-scenes-phase-5-focus-model-and-multi-connection-visibility-design.md §8

title: Scenes: scene_idle_nudge background trigger implementation
description:
## Goal

Implement the background trigger for scene_idle_nudge events. Phase 4 (holomush-5rh.13) ships the wire shape (event type in crypto.emits + EmitTypeRegistrar registration + scenes.idle_nudge_threshold config column) but not the trigger. This bead lands the triggering logic — either a per-scene polling goroutine or a reactive on-pose hook that detects "next-up character has been idle beyond idle_nudge_threshold" and emits scene_idle_nudge to the scene's OOC stream.

## Plan reference

Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-scenes-phase-4-streams-and-pose-order-design.md §10.4 (wire shape), §13 (deferral); plan #task-10 (manifest entry).

## Files touched

plugins/core-scenes/main.go (timer / hook lifecycle), plugins/core-scenes/idle_nudge.go (new, trigger logic), tests.

## Out of scope

Manifest declaration + scene config column (shipped in Phase 4). UX rendering of nudges in telnet / web (separate phases).

## Acceptance

- Per-scene idle threshold respected (off by default; per-scene config).
- Nudge fires when next-up character has been idle > threshold.
- Emit is participant-visible (OOC stream) per spec §10.4.
- INV-P4-3 sensitivity:never preserved (no manifest change).
- Tests cover: nudge fires when threshold exceeded; no nudge when off; no double-nudge after pose.

## Required Skills
jj:jujutsu,dev-flow:test-driven-development

title: Remove unused Functions.RegisterWithEmitCapture API + tests (post jg9b.3 capture-pass narrowing)
description:
**Goal:** Remove dead-code `Functions.RegisterWithEmitCapture` API and its 3 internal tests after the INV-S5 capture-pass surface was narrowed in jg9b.3 / PR #4049.

**Context:** Before the narrowing (per CodeRabbit #3263117468 — 'Prevent side-effectful hostfunc execution during INV-S5 capture pass'), `internal/plugin/lua/host.go::Load` called `h.hostFuncs.RegisterWithEmitCapture(L, manifest.Name, reg, manifest.Requires...)` to install the FULL `holomush.*` surface (with the capturing `register_emit_type` variant) during the capture pass. The fix replaced that with the minimal `RegisterEmitTypeFuncs(L, mod, reg)` pattern, which installs ONLY `register_emit_type`.

After this change, `RegisterWithEmitCapture` has zero production callsites (verified via `rg --type go '\\.RegisterWithEmitCapture\\('`). The function and its 3 internal tests in `internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions_internal_test.go` (`TestRegisterWithEmitCapture_LuaCallsAccumulate`, `TestRegisterWithEmitCapture_DuplicateCallsIdempotent`, `TestRegisterWithEmitCapture_PreservesStandardNamespace`) are now dead code.

**Files to touch:**
- `internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions.go` — remove `RegisterWithEmitCapture` method
- `internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions_internal_test.go` — remove the 3 tests
- Any documentation referencing `RegisterWithEmitCapture` (mechanism spec §2.3, ADR `holomush-7h0c`, ADR `holomush-vie9`) — update to reflect that capture uses `RegisterEmitTypeFuncs` directly, not the `RegisterWithEmitCapture` wrapper.

**Out of scope:**
- Re-introducing a full-surface capture path. Per CodeRabbit feedback, the surface narrowing is intentional (prevents substrate KV/world side effects during Load that would persist past validator rejection).
- Doc changes that aren't directly about this API removal.

**Acceptance:**
- `rg --type go 'RegisterWithEmitCapture'` returns zero hits (across source + tests + docs).
- `task pr-prep` end-to-end green.
- A bd note links back to PR #4049's autofix round that triggered this cleanup.

**Dependencies:** Should land AFTER PR #4049 merges to main.

title: iwzt: I-PRIV-6 floor-preservation arm — assert staff sees only post-LocationArrivedAt events
description:
## Goal

Complete the I-PRIV-6 verification in `test/integration/privacy/privacy_test.go`. PR #4048 landed the gate-bypass arm only: staff with `read_unrestricted_history` bypasses the location hard-gate. The other half of I-PRIV-6 ("but NOT the temporal floor" — staff still sees only events from their own `LocationArrivedAt` forward) is not yet asserted.

## Why deferred from #4048

The full assertion requires emitting events with controlled timestamps across the staff session's `LocationArrivedAt` boundary:

1. Third party emits `event_pre` at locB before staff connects (before staff.LocationArrivedAt).
2. Staff connects in locA (LocationArrivedAt = T_staff).
3. Third party emits `event_post` at locB after T_staff.
4. Staff queries `location:locB` via allowAllPolicyEngine override.
5. Asserts: staff sees `event_post` but NOT `event_pre` (floor still applies even with gate bypassed).

The privacytest harness currently has no way to emit events:

- The CoreServer's command dispatcher is wired with `command.NewRegistry()` (empty), so `Session.SendCommand("say ...")` returns COMMAND_REJECTED.
- Direct emit helpers that bypass the command layer (e.g., `Server.EmitTestEvent(stream, eventType, payload, ts)`) don't exist on the harness.

Either is a non-trivial harness extension and out of scope for the iwzt.20 PR.

## Fix

Two options:

A. **Register a `say` command in the harness's dispatcher.** Smallest change if the core-communication plugin is reused; harness gains realistic event emission via SendCommand. Downside: pulls plugin code into the integration harness.

B. **Add a direct-emit helper to `Server` or `Session`.** E.g., `Server.EmitLocationSay(ctx, locationID, characterID, payload string, at time.Time) string` — bypasses the command layer, writes directly to the event store / publisher, returns the event ULID for assertion. More flexible but bigger surface.

(B) is cleaner because it gives test-controlled timestamps. (A) emits at `time.Now()` and would require sleeps for ordering.

Recommend (B) as part of the harness build-out tracked in `holomush-izk0` (comprehensive session-lifecycle test matrix).

## Acceptance

- New `It("floor-preservation arm: staff sees only post-LocationArrivedAt events")` in `test/integration/privacy/privacy_test.go` passes.
- Asserts both halves of I-PRIV-6:
  - err == nil (gate bypassed)
  - returned events do NOT include `event_pre` (pre-floor)
  - returned events include `event_post` (post-floor)
- The gate-bypass-only Describe in PR #4048 can then be merged into a single full I-PRIV-6 block.

## Source

CodeRabbit thread on PR #4048 on 2026-05-18: the test name "bypasses gate, not floor" implied both arms were verified but only the gate-bypass half was. Renamed to "(gate-bypass arm)" inline; floor arm filed here.

title: InactiveThreshold not validated against SessionTTL: events can be lost mid-disconnect
description:
## Symptom

The holomush-iwzt history-scope privacy spec (post-lj2r amendment) relies on the durable JetStream consumer's `InactiveThreshold` exceeding the session `TTL` so events emitted during a transport disconnect survive long enough to be delivered on reattach. Today this relationship is **unvalidated and unenforced**:

- `internal/eventbus/subscriber.go:38` — `DefaultSessionInactiveThreshold = 24 * time.Hour`
- `cmd/holomush/core.go:151` — `--session-ttl` default = `"30m"`
- `cmd/holomush/sub_grpc.go:320` — production `subscriber := s.cfg.EventBus.Subscriber()` called with no `SubscribeOption`s.

At default config the invariant holds *by accident* (24h » 30m). An operator who sets `--session-ttl=48h` (or any value > 24h) silently breaks it: the durable evicts at 24h, the session row persists until 48h, and events emitted in [24h, 48h] are lost on reattach.

No leak, no extra exposure — strict subset of in-flight events delivered. But the privacy invariant's central technical argument depends on the durable holding events for the whole TTL.

## Fix options

A. **Validate** in `parseSessionConfig`: reject configurations where the resolved `sessionTTL > inactiveThreshold`. Cheapest. Fails fast at startup.

B. **Auto-derive**: `subscriber.WithSessionInactiveThreshold(max(DefaultSessionInactiveThreshold, sessionTTL))` from the gRPC subsystem wiring. Self-correcting. No operator burden.

C. **Both**: validate the operator-supplied value if explicitly set, otherwise auto-derive from sessionTTL.

(C) is cleanest. (B) alone is the smallest change.

## Acceptance

- A test in `internal/eventbus/` or `cmd/holomush/` covers: `sessionTTL > inactiveThreshold` is either rejected at startup OR auto-corrected at wiring time.
- The spec amendment at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-17-history-scope-privacy-design.md` §5 (the InactiveThreshold paragraph after row 7) is updated to remove the "by configuration accident" caveat once enforced.

## Source

Code-reviewer finding on PR #4050 / holomush-lj2r — spec amendment claim about InactiveThreshold matching TTL is false at HEAD. Downgraded from BLOCKING to non-blocking by amending the spec to acknowledge the gap + tracking the fix here.

title: Disconnect path deletes guest sessions immediately, preventing page-reload reattach
description:
## Symptom

`internal/grpc/server.go::Disconnect:1258-1296` — when `totalCount == 0` (all connections dropped) AND `info.IsGuest == true`, the server immediately:
1. Emits a `leave` event
2. Emits `session_ended` (cause=guest_end)
3. Deletes the session row

This breaks page-reload reattach for guest sessions: the brief momentary connection drop during `page.reload()` is interpreted as a permanent disconnect, the session is destroyed, and the reload's SelectCharacter call has nothing to reattach to.

## Discovery context

iwzt.5 (PR #4043) initially stamped `session.Info.IsGuest = true` for guest sessions (correctly capturing the player.IsGuest flag at session creation). This activated the disconnect branch above and broke 6 E2E tests in `web/e2e/terminal.spec.ts` (session-survives-reload, reconnect-after-replay, page-reload-replays-prior-events, detach+reload-no-duplicates, command-history-persists, in-progress-input-persists).

iwzt.5 was reworked to:
- Drop the `IsGuest=true` stamping in `SelectCharacter` (`internal/grpc/auth_handlers.go`).
- Switch `streamScopeFloor` (`internal/grpc/scope_floor.go`) to use `!GuestCharacterCreatedAt.IsZero()` as the guest-overlay signal instead of `info.IsGuest`.

That preserves the I-PRIV-2 guest temporal floor (which only needs the timestamp, not the bool flag) while keeping `info.IsGuest` false so the disconnect path doesn't fire the delete branch. The `session.Info.IsGuest` field is now effectively dead-code in the persistent store.

## Fix

Redesign the guest disconnect path so guests can detach + reattach within a TTL window (probably shorter than registered-user TTL — e.g., 60-120 seconds is enough for a page reload). Then re-enable `IsGuest=true` stamping in `SelectCharacter` and revert `streamScopeFloor` to use `info.IsGuest` again.

### Candidate shapes

- Add a `guestTTLSeconds` config (default 60s); use the existing non-guest detach branch with a shorter TTL.
- Reaper handles guest cleanup after TTL expiry (it already handles non-guest detach cleanup).
- New tests: page-reload-after-N-seconds detach succeeds; page-reload-after-2*N seconds creates a new session.

## Acceptance

- All 6 currently-passing `terminal.spec.ts` reload tests stay green after re-enabling `IsGuest=true` and detach-with-TTL.
- New E2E test: guest sessions are deleted by the reaper after the guest TTL elapses with no connections.
- streamScopeFloor uses `info.IsGuest` again (revert iwzt.5's workaround); session.Info.IsGuest is read by the disconnect path again.

## Source

PR #4043 (iwzt.5) — both the `IsGuest` field activation that exposed the bug and the workaround that papered over it pending this fix.

title: Merge NewX + NewXWithLogger constructor pairs via functional options
description:
Audit reference: docs/repository-audit/2026-05-13/humanization-review.md §2.1 (Quick Wins #4-6)

## Problem
Three constructor pairs each define two functions with 30-line copy-pasted `if dep == nil` nil-check blocks:

| Location | Pair |
|----------|------|
| internal/auth/auth_service.go:51-99 | NewAuthService + NewAuthServiceWithLogger |
| internal/auth/reset_service.go:27-85 | NewPasswordResetService + NewPasswordResetServiceWithLogger |
| pkg/holo/emit.go:50 | NewEmitter + NewEmitterWithLogger |

The `…WithLogger` variant exists only to avoid adding a 5th parameter to the constructor.

## Fix
Replace each pair with a single constructor taking functional options:

```go
type AuthOption func(*authConfig)
func WithLogger(l *slog.Logger) AuthOption { ... }

func NewAuthService(players Players, ..., opts ...AuthOption) (*Service, error)
```

The `ServiceOption` pattern is already used elsewhere in the same files — this just consolidates.

Could also adopt a `ServiceConfig` struct pattern matching `world.ServiceConfig` precedent (audit's preferred direction per §3.4).

## Estimated impact
~200 LOC eliminated across three services.

## Why P2
Quality improvement; not blocking anything. Reasonable single-PR scope.

title: Strategic theme: social-spaces — substrate-and-uses cluster (scenes → channels → forums → discord)
description:
## Decision

Frame scenes, channels, forums, and discord as a single strategic cluster ("social-spaces") sharing one substrate stack (JetStream + focus coordinator + AccessPolicyEngine + plugin focus client). Track via `theme:social-spaces` bd label and a roadmap section in `docs/roadmap.md`.

## Context

By 2026-05-16, the platform's foundational pivots had landed (JetStream cutover PR #252, plugin architecture rework PR #192, ABAC PRs #88+, session consolidation PRs #123/#139, crypto Phases 1-5+7). The remaining open work has a recognizable shape: 4 product surfaces (scenes, channels, forums, discord) that each consume the same substrate.

Without a strategic frame, these epics get treated as independent. With a shared frame, sequencing decisions become coherent — e.g., "do scenes Phase 4 first because it's the reference implementation; channels learns from it; forums brainstorms in parallel because no code dep; discord is last because it depends on channels."

## Why a bd label + markdown doc instead of GitHub Projects

For a solo-developer workflow, the cost of double-entry (bd ↔ GH Projects sync) exceeds the benefit of a visual board. bd labels give cross-epic queryability in one command; the markdown doc carries the "why" alongside the code. Revisit GH Projects when team grows or external roadmap visibility becomes a real need.

## Tagged beads (initial 19)

Epics: 5rh (scenes), 0sc (channels), djj (forums), aqq (discord)
Scenes children: 5rh.8, 5rh.9, 5rh.13-19, ac50, cb4x
Channels: 0sc.12, 72sj
Cross-cutting: mjy3 (object_examine sensitivity), aqq.5 (Discord OAuth)

## Sequencing rationale

1. Scenes Phase 4-6 first (5rh.13/.14/.15) — reference implementation
2. Channels in parallel where unblocked (0sc.12 in_progress)
3. Forums brainstorm in parallel (no code dep; design phase)
4. Discord last (depends on channels + OAuth substrate)

## Out of scope for this decision

- Whether to formally cut a v0.1 release tag (epic a3a7 substrate is done; tagging is separate)
- Web portal scope for each surface (deferred to use-level)
- GitHub Projects adoption (deferred until team coordination need)

## References

- docs/roadmap.md (the strategic narrative)
- docs/specs/2026-04-06-scenes-and-rp-design-v2.md (scenes architecture)
- docs/specs/2026-04-03-channels-architecture.md (channels architecture)

title: Scenes: scene log + scene export commands + export renderers
description:
Re-filed from holomush-5rh.7 (2026-05-16 audit reframe).

## Substrate already shipped
- scene_log table (plugins/core-scenes/migrations/000004) — PR #267 (2026-04-25)
- PluginAuditService.AuditEvent in plugins/core-scenes/audit.go

## What this bead tracks
1. `scene log` command — show recent log entries for a scene the caller is a participant in
2. `scene export` command — dump full scene log in a chosen format
3. Export renderers — at minimum: plain text, markdown. Stretch: HTML, JSON

## Dependencies
- Blocked by holomush-5rh.13 (Phase 4: scene IC event emission). Without IC events being captured, the scene log only contains ops events (join/leave/state changes), not poses/dialogue.
- Vote state machine (publish gating) is separate work tracked in holomush-5rh.15 (Phase 6).

## Acceptance

- Renderer functions producing valid output
- Tests with mixed ops + IC events in the log
- ABAC gate: must be participant or scene-owner OR have admin.scene.log_export capability

title: PluginProvider.SetRegistry race — use atomic.Pointer for plugin registry
description:
Re-filed from holomush-59zu.11 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-59zu PR #107).

internal/access/policy/attribute/plugin_provider.go:31-33: plain field write `p.registry = r` with no mutex or atomic.Pointer. ResolveSubject reads `p.registry` at line 44 without sync.

The ABAC subsystem's Start() in subsystem.go:95 launches the poller goroutine, returns, and then the plugin subsystem later calls SetRegistry. The poller's immediate first poll can fan out into ResolveSubject before SetRegistry completes. Race detector will flag.

Already flagged in 2026-04-26 audit as "REAL OPEN BUG" — persists.

Acceptance: change `registry PluginRegistry` to `registry atomic.Pointer[PluginRegistry]` with `Load()`/`Store()`. Confirm `-race` clean under integration test stress.

title: Test ErrSessionEnded teardown failure paths (HandleDisconnect/sessionStore.Delete errors)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-5ayg.13 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-5ayg PR #139).

server.go:428-446 — failure paths log slog.WarnContext and continue. TestDispatcher_HandleCommand_Quit covers the happy path only. No test injects:
- failing engine.HandleDisconnect in the errors.Is(dispatchErr, command.ErrSessionEnded) branch
- failing sessionStore.Delete after EndSession

The "retain session row for reap" path (server.go:434-442) — triggered when EndSession fails — is real operational behavior with no test coverage.

Acceptance: add tests covering these failure injection scenarios; assert the retain-for-reap fallback behavior.

title: Implement boot command (admin: boot player/guest with reason)

title: HandleCommand returns generic 'session not found' for all store errors — masks DB failures
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.25 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

server.go:337-349: all ValidateSessionOwnership errors collapse to `Error: "session not found"` regardless of whether it's a DB timeout, connection failure, or genuine missing session.

Operators investigating "session not found" errors will have no signal that the underlying problem is DB infrastructure.

Acceptance: distinguish session-not-found (return 404-equivalent) from infra errors (return 500-equivalent with structured logging).

title: Create test/integration/access/location_equivalence_test.go — Phase 7.7 migration validation
description:

title: Wire EVENTS_AUDIT_DLQ for audit projection MaxDeliver exhaustion
description:
Re-filed from holomush-1tvn.17 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-1tvn JetStream + PG audit projection).

**Operational gap**: poison messages that exhaust MaxDeliver (10 attempts) are silently dropped from the audit cold tier today — no operator signal, no recovery path. Violates the "PG audit is the durable source of truth for cold reads" invariant.

Verified 2026-05-16:
- internal/eventbus/audit/projection.go:74 — "Phase A TODO: wire a DLQ"
- internal/eventbus/audit/subsystem.go:59 — "TODO (Phase B): wire a DLQ"
- No EVENTS_AUDIT_DLQ stream definition exists in the codebase

Acceptance:
- Define EVENTS_AUDIT_DLQ JetStream stream
- Route MaxDeliver-exhausted messages to it
- Add operator alert/metric (e.g., events_audit_dlq_depth)
- Document recovery procedure in operating docs
- Integration test: poison message → ends up in DLQ + metric increments

title: Reconsider object_examine sensitivity: 'never' will silently reject future sensitive emits (security implication)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-k18g.4 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r).

## Audit-deepened priority

Original priority was P3. Auditor flagged this as a latent security risk; promoted to P2 with aspect:security label.

## Problem

Task 7 classified all core-objects:object_* events as sensitivity: never. Code reviewer flagged that object_examine in particular is a leak vector — Lua examine handlers at plugins/core-objects/main.lua:190-225 already render o.description, c.description, loc.description into command output.

If a future task emits object_examine as a real bus event carrying the description payload, the 'never' contract (per crypto_manifest.go:12-13: 'emit-time attempts to set Sensitive=true MUST be rejected') would block it from being marked sensitive.

## Audit verification (2026-05-16)

plugins/core-objects/plugin.yaml:170 — sensitivity: never. internal/plugin/sensitivity_fence.go:25-31 enforces INV-6 EVENT_SENSITIVITY_NOT_DECLARED: 'manifest=never + claim=true → REJECT'. Real security implication: any future emit code marking object_examine sensitive will be silently rejected unless reclassified FIRST.

## Recommendation

Today no object_examine events are emitted on the bus, so the contract is theoretical. But before any emit code is added in a later phase, reclassify to 'may' (per-event runtime decision) and update the description to mention the payload-vs-metadata distinction.

Same potential applies to object_use (use payloads sometimes carry private state) — consider in this work.

Refs: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-phase1-manifest-grammar.md (Task 7 review)

title: Phase 2 follow-up: systemd-credential KEKSource
description:
Re-filed from holomush-e49r.2 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r Event Payload Cryptography).

Per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-event-payload-crypto-phase2-substrate-design.md Decision 3, Phase 2 deferred this implementation. Add a KEKSource implementation that reads from $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/<name> per systemd LoadCredentialEncrypted= service unit directive.

Acceptance:
- internal/eventbus/crypto/kek/source_systemd_credential.go implements kek.KEKSource
- Name() returns 'local-aead/systemd-credential'
- Linux integration test that reads a valid credential
- Returns CRYPTO_*_READ_ONLY on Persist (matches EnvSource pattern)

Reason for deferral: Linux-only test path; not blocking Phase 3.

Master spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §5.2 Topology 2.

Verified 2026-05-16: `git ls-tree origin/main internal/eventbus/crypto/kek/` shows no source_systemd_credential.go.

title: Phase 2 follow-up: keyring-backed KEKSource (go-keyring)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-e49r.1 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r Event Payload Cryptography).

Per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-event-payload-crypto-phase2-substrate-design.md Decision 3, Phase 2 deferred this implementation. Add a KEKSource implementation backed by the OS keyring (go-keyring; macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, Windows Credential Manager).

Acceptance:

- Name() returns 'local-aead/keyring'

Reason for deferral: cgo on macOS expands the test matrix; not blocking Phase 3.

Verified 2026-05-16: `git ls-tree origin/main internal/eventbus/crypto/kek/` shows only source_env.go + source_file.go; source_keyring.go absent.

title: E5.7.2 Silent hash upgrade failure — Argon2idHasher.NeedsUpgrade() never called in auth flow
description:
Re-filed from holomush-qsoi (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-dwk).

## Original problem statement
auth_service.go:140-145 silently ignores hash upgrade errors (bcrypt to argon2id). Should log at WARN level for observability.

## Audit-deepened finding
Verified 2026-05-16: `NeedsUpgrade()` exists on `Argon2idHasher` but is NEVER called in `ValidateCredentials` or `AuthenticatePlayer`. The bcrypt→argon2id auto-upgrade described in ADR holomush-4x7x is not implemented.

## Security implication
Any legacy bcrypt hashes (imported from another MUSH, or pre-argon2id migration data) will fail auth with `AUTH_INVALID_HASH` rather than transparently upgrading to argon2id on first successful login. Players with bcrypt hashes will be permanently unable to log in.

Note: as of 2026-05-16 this is a latent risk (no deployed users); becomes a real bug if/when import flows are added or if any bcrypt hashes exist in the DB.

## Acceptance
- Call `NeedsUpgrade(hash)` after successful `Verify(password, hash)` in `Argon2idHasher.Hash` upgrade path
- On true, generate new argon2id hash and persist via `PlayerRepository.UpdatePasswordHash`
- Log at INFO with player_id (no hash material)
- Test: bcrypt hash + correct password → successful login + DB now holds argon2id hash

title: Auth: Add test for password reset token_hash collision (UNIQUE constraint violation)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-r88a (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-dwk).

Integration tests for PasswordResetRepository.Create() don't test duplicate token_hash insertion. Add test that verifies the UNIQUE constraint violation is properly detected and surfaced as a structured error for duplicate token hashes.

Verified 2026-05-16: internal/auth/postgres/reset_repo_test.go covers Create/GetByPlayer/GetByTokenHash/Delete/DeleteByPlayer/DeleteExpired/ConsumeByTokenHash but no test verifies the token_hash UNIQUE constraint violation on Create. Genuine coverage gap.

title: Focus substrate architecture documentation — concept doc + plugin API guides
description:

Recommend writing AFTER oy6e.10 (core-scenes adoption) and oy6e.11 (core-channels) re-files land so docs can reference real plugin usage patterns.

title: Create core-channels plugin — use AddSessionStream(LIVE_ONLY) + QueryStreamHistory
description:
Re-filed from holomush-oy6e.11 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-oy6e Server-Owned Focus Substrate).

Create core-channels plugin (does not yet exist in plugins/) to use AddSessionStream(LIVE_ONLY) + QueryStreamHistory for the channel join experience. Spec: Section 4.6.

Substrate ready: QueryStreamHistory RPC shipped in core.proto, handler at internal/grpc/query_stream_history.go, plugin-side via PluginHostService.

Current state: plugins/core-channels does not exist. Channels Epic is holomush-0sc (12 children, 0 closed) — this bead is the substrate-aware plugin adoption work distinct from the channels epic's domain design.

title: Missing TestGuestAuthenticator_ReturnsIsGuest unit test specified in plan
description:
Re-filed from holomush-ycrv.19 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-ycrv).

The implementation plan (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-17-session-lifecycle-hooks.md, Task 5 Step 5.1) explicitly requires adding TestGuestAuthenticator_ReturnsIsGuest to internal/telnet/guest_auth_test.go. The diff shows guest_auth_test.go was modified (comment tweaks to existing tests) but the new TestGuestAuthenticator_ReturnsIsGuest test was not added.

The test should verify:
  result, err := auth.Authenticate(context.Background(), "guest", "")
  require.NoError(t, err)
  assert.True(t, result.IsGuest)

This is a spec criterion: 'guest sessions are ended via EndSession on disconnect' — the IsGuest flag is the mechanism, and the test directly validates the flag is set. The implementation sets IsGuest=true correctly in internal/telnet/guest_auth.go, but the test coverage is missing.

Verified 2026-05-16: internal/telnet/guest_auth_test.go covers StartLocation, GenerateName, ReleaseGuest, ActiveCount — but not the IsGuest flag path through Authenticate.

Also subsumes holomush-ycrv.6 (closed as duplicate during audit).

title: Add Reason field to DisconnectRequest — leave events currently hardcoded to 'quit'
description:
Re-filed from holomush-ycrv.3 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-ycrv).

In internal/grpc/server.go Disconnect, s.engine.HandleDisconnect is called with hardcoded reason 'quit' regardless of whether the disconnect was voluntary, a timeout, or a network drop. The spec acknowledges this deferral ('Differentiating between voluntary quit and involuntary disconnect ... deferred to follow-up work').

This means 'leave' events in the event store are ambiguous — a timeout-evicted guest and a player who typed 'quit' both produce identical events. Plugins and analytics that consume leave events cannot distinguish these cases.

Verified 2026-05-16: internal/grpc/server.go:1254,1313 still calls HandleDisconnect with literals "quit" and "phased out". No Reason field on DisconnectRequest.

Suggested fix: Add a Reason field to DisconnectRequest in the proto and pass it through HandleDisconnect. Define an enum of disconnect reasons (USER_QUIT, IDLE_TIMEOUT, NETWORK_DROP, ADMIN_BOOT, etc.).

title: arrive event log lacks session_id correlation field + uses raw slog instead of errutil.LogError
description:
Re-filed from holomush-ycrv.2 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-ycrv).

Related: holomush-0hag.34 covers the broader slog→errutil.LogError migration across the codebase. This bead is a specific site-and-field concern that may not be auto-swept by the general migration.

In internal/grpc/server.go Authenticate, HandleConnect is called after sessionStore.Set succeeds. When the arrive-event append fails (e.g. DB unavailable), the error is logged at Warn and the response is still returned as a success. This is the intended best-effort design (documented in the spec and the inline comment).

However, the log entry uses slog.WarnContext rather than errutil.LogError, so the structured oops context (operation, wrapped cause) is not fully surfaced. The warn message 'arrive event failed' does not include the session_id, which would help correlate the warning to the session that was just created.

Location: internal/grpc/server.go ~line 1611-1618 in the diff.

Suggested improvement (not a correctness bug, but a logging quality issue):
  Add 'session_id', sessionID.String() to the WarnContext call so the event can be correlated to the session in log queries. Consider using errutil.LogError at Warn level for consistent oops unwrapping.

title: T30: Integration tests for full ABAC flow (Phase 7.7)
description:
## Goal

Land the Phase 7.7 T30 acceptance criteria: end-to-end integration tests for the full ABAC evaluation pipeline using real providers (not mocks), real PostgreSQL, and seeded policies. Verify every layer of the engine works together: subject prefix parsing → attribute resolution → policy filtering → DSL evaluation → deny-overrides → audit.

**Status:** Re-filed 2026-05-15 as top-level standalone (previously `holomush-5k1.600.1`, child of now-closed Phase 7.7 epic `holomush-5k1.600`).

## Design reference

- Original bead: `holomush-5k1.600.1` (filed 2026-02-11, Phase 7.7 plan task).
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1349-phase77-impl-tasks.md` — confirms scaffolding landed (`test/integration/access/evaluation_test.go` exists with 9 of 17 spec scenarios active, 8 pending; `seed_policies_test.go` 132 lines).
- Spec: Phase 7.7 plan acceptance criteria for T30.

## Plan reference

The integration test harness is in place. Remaining work:

1. Activate the 8 pending spec scenarios in `test/integration/access/evaluation_test.go`.
2. Fix the entity-ID format mismatch (blocker — tracked separately) that causes real providers to fail.
3. Add canary tests covering: subject prefix parsing, attribute resolution, policy filtering, DSL evaluation, deny-overrides, audit writes.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- All 17 spec scenarios pass in `evaluation_test.go`.
- Canary tests cover the full engine pipeline with real components.
- Tests run reliably under `task test:int`.

## Verification steps

1. `task test:int -- ./test/integration/access/...` green.
2. `task pr-prep` green (full lane).

## Files touched

- `test/integration/access/evaluation_test.go` (activate pending scenarios)
- `test/integration/access/seed_policies_test.go` (extend if needed)
- Possibly new test files

## Dependencies

- **BLOCKED on:** the resolver/provider entity ID format mismatch bug (separate top-level bead — see related work for the ID).

## Out of scope

- Production-code changes to the resolver (separate bead).
- Unit tests for individual components (already covered).

title: Attribute resolver panic-recovery log uses Error not ErrorContext (distributed-trace gap)
description:
## Goal

Fix `internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go:375-380` — when a provider panics during attribute resolution, the recovery code calls `r.logger.Error("provider panicked during resolution", ...)` instead of `r.logger.ErrorContext(ctx, ...)`. The log emission drops the `ctx`, breaking distributed-trace correlation for the panic event.

**Status note:** Originally filed as `holomush-phq3.13`. Marked Closed via in-bead comment claiming "panic recovery now uses oops with full context (namespace, resolve_type, entity_id)." That claim is partially true — the **returned error value** uses `oops.With(...)` correctly — but the **log emission** still lacks `ctx`. Audited 2026-05-15 as a FALSE-FIX (third such case this session, after wfza.21 and wfza.62).

## Design reference

- Originally filed as `holomush-phq3.13`. Promoted 2026-05-15 due to false-fix pattern.
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1259-phq3-batch1.md`.

## Plan reference

Direct fix:

1. Change `r.logger.Error(...)` at `resolver.go:375` to `r.logger.ErrorContext(ctx, ...)`.
2. Verify the `safeResolveEnvironment` panic-recovery site has the same fix (if it exists).
3. Add a test that asserts trace context is preserved across panic recovery (or at minimum, that the log call uses ErrorContext).

## TDD acceptance criteria

- `rg "logger\.Error\(" internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go` returns ZERO hits (or only hits where ctx is genuinely unavailable).
- Existing panic-recovery tests still pass.
- New test asserts trace-context propagation across the panic path (if testable).

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/attribute/...` green.
2. Manual: inspect that the log call is `ErrorContext(ctx, ...)`.
3. `task pr-prep` green.
4. **Verify the change actually lands** — do not repeat the wfza.21/.62/phq3.13 false-fix pattern.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/attribute/resolver.go:375` (and potentially `safeResolveEnvironment` panic-recovery path)
- Test file under `internal/access/policy/attribute/`

## Dependencies

- None.

## Out of scope

- Provider-level error-context logging granularity (covered by wfza.22, already closed in its own scope).
- Other Error vs ErrorContext audits elsewhere (file separately if found).

title: Phase 7.6 ABAC + KV polish (test gaps, dead code, design comments)
description:
## Goal

Consolidated small cleanup + test-coverage items from the 2026-05-15 audit of PR #88 (`wfza`) and PR #106 (`txf0`). Each is small but together they form one PR's worth of polish on the ABAC + KV substrate.
