- notifications:<character_id> stream emission when scene IC events occur for non-focused members
- 'scene mute <id>' / 'scene unmute <id>' commands
- Notification preferences (per-scene mute, digest vs real-time, channels)
- Telnet edge cases: multi-character on same connection, connection recovery (rejoin scenes on reconnect)
- Idle timeout defaults (game-wide config, per-scene override)
- Idle nudge implementation (4.4 — optional, off by default)
- Final integration test pass across all phases
- Documentation polish

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Notification events delivered to non-focused members
- [ ] Per-scene mute works
- [ ] Telnet reconnection restores scene memberships and focus state
- [ ] Idle timeout transitions active → paused
- [ ] All Phase 10 features have integration tests
- [ ] task pr-prep passes
notes: Brainstorm paused 2026-06-19 (defer until 5rh.22 lands). Scope decision: Phase 10 notification/mute surface INCLUDES a web notification-preferences/mute UI (option 2), which is a 4-layer slice (SceneAccessService facade -> WebMuteScene/prefs BFF RPC on web.proto -> internal/web/scene_handlers.go -> web/src/lib/scenes/*) touching the SAME files 5rh.22 (web create-scene slice) is rewriting. Build on 5rh.22's facade->BFF->client pattern, don't race it. Dependency added: 5rh.19 depends-on 5rh.22. Grounding for pickup: (1) scene mute/unmute does NOT exist yet; (2) idle-timeout infra PARTLY exists (idle_timeout_secs column in store.go, scene_idle_nudge event type in plugin.yaml/main.go) -- wire defaults+transitions, don't rebuild; (3) notification DELIVERY already server-side via subscription router and web already ships passive unread badges, so only a prefs/mute CONTROL needs new web code; (4) telnet edge cases (multi-char/reconnection) + idle defaults are spec section 11 'needs deeper design'; notification streams = spec section 3.3.

title: Plugin developer docs for commands[].aliases field
description: Add 'Declaring command aliases' section to site/docs/extending/plugin-guide.md with YAML example, validation rules, cross-plugin collision behavior, and override semantics. Add aliases: example to binary-plugins.md. From PR #194 review (HIGH-5).

title: Unify plugin alias pgxpool with shared database pool
description: Plugin subsystem creates its own pgxpool via pgxpool.New(DatabaseConnStr) at internal/plugin/setup/subsystem.go:227. This bypasses the shared instrumented pool (no otelpgx tracer) and doubles connection footprint. Should accept store.DatabaseSubsystem or PoolProvider via PluginSubsystemConfig. From PR #194 review (HIGH-3).

title: Plugin manifest alias format validation
description: Add ValidateManifestAliasName to reject control chars, whitespace, and enforce length limits on aliases from plugin manifests. Currently CommandSpec.Validate only rejects empty strings and duplicates. From PR #194 review (HIGH-1). Not in the original PR #194 spec scope. Permit punctuation aliases (legitimate core aliases). Max length e.g. 20 chars. Reject unicode control characters.

title: Review pgx pool and PostgreSQL connection settings for plugin architecture
description: With per-plugin roles, each plugin gets its own pgx pool. Review and configure: (1) Server pgxpool settings (max conns, min conns, idle timeout, health check period). (2) Plugin pgxpool settings — plugins should use smaller pools since they're narrower workloads. (3) PostgreSQL max_connections in compose deployment — needs headroom for server + N plugin pools. (4) Connection mode — avoid session-mode connections where possible; prefer transaction-mode pooling patterns (short-lived connections, no SET/PREPARE across transactions). (5) Whether PgBouncer or similar should sit between plugins and Postgres. Audit current defaults and propose explicit configuration.

title: Review polymorphic command capability declarations
description:
Polymorphic commands like describe (targets characters, locations, objects) and examine (reads characters, locations, objects) declare a single resource type (object) for pre-flight. This causes issues:

1. describe declares write:object, but seed:builder-object-write requires builder role. Non-builders can't pass Layer 2 for 'describe me' (which writes to a character, not an object).

2. The capability model uses AND logic — all declared capabilities must pass. Polymorphic commands that target different resource types can't express OR ('can write to characters OR objects').

3. CodeRabbit flagged this: pre-flight capabilities don't match the commands' actual target types.

Options to explore:
- OR-logic capabilities (any of these must pass)  
- Separate commands per target type (describe-me vs describe)
- Remove pre-flight from polymorphic commands (handler ABAC only)
- Broader resource type that covers all entity types

Discussion context: PR #182 code review, session 2026-04-04.
notes: Sean's insight: it may be as simple as the ABAC model/policies treating 'object' as the supertype of locations, characters, and exits — so write:object would match any entity type at the policy level.

title: Build ABAC performance benchmark suite
description: Create a comprehensive benchmark suite for the ABAC policy engine covering Evaluate, CanPerformAction, attribute resolution, and condition evaluation. Should measure: single-policy eval, multi-policy with deny-overrides, condition AST walking, attribute bag resolution, cache snapshot overhead. Results feed into optimization decisions (e.g., caching compiled condition metadata like ReferencesResourceAttrs). Currently only have basic benchmarks in internal/access/policy/.

title: Rich text support for game messages (markdown + emoji)
description:
Game messages (say, pose, emit, ooc, channel, page, whisper) currently render as plain text with only URL auto-linking. Need markdown rendering and emoji support in the terminal message pipeline.

## Current State
- MarkdownContent.svelte exists but only used for landing page CMS content
- Terminal renderers use escapeHtml() + linkUrls() — plain text only
- No emoji processing anywhere in message pipeline
- Telnet gateway passes raw text

## Why
- Channel bridging (Epic 12) requires markdown + emoji — Discord/Slack messages arrive with formatting
- Players expect basic formatting in modern chat
- Without this, bridged messages render as raw markup

## Scope
- Web client: Add markdown rendering to CommunicationRenderer and other terminal renderers (reuse marked + DOMPurify pattern from MarkdownContent.svelte)
- Emoji: Add emoji shortcode support (:thumbsup: → 👍) — consider gemoji or similar
- Telnet: Strip or convert markdown to ANSI formatting (bold → ANSI bold, etc.)
- Security: All markdown MUST be sanitized with DOMPurify (already in deps)
- Consider: subset of markdown only? (bold, italic, links, emoji — no headers, code blocks, images in chat context)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Say/pose/emit/ooc messages render markdown in web terminal
- [ ] Emoji shortcodes render as Unicode emoji in web terminal
- [ ] Telnet strips or converts markdown gracefully
- [ ] DOMPurify sanitization on all rendered content
- [ ] No XSS possible through message content
- [ ] Existing plain text messages render unchanged

title: Centralize E2E test helper for register + create character + enter game
description: The register → character select → create character → enter terminal flow is duplicated in auth.spec.ts (3x), admin.spec.ts (1x), and character-switcher.spec.ts (1x). Extract a shared helper into e2e/helpers/fixtures.ts. This would have prevented the shadcn checkbox selector fix from needing updates in 4 separate files.

title: Disaster recovery: recreate droplet from backup
description: Step-by-step guide to recreate a HoloMUSH deployment on a fresh droplet from a backup. Cover: new droplet with cloud-init, restore /opt/holomush/ from backup/snapshot, update DNS, verify. Follow-on from backup guide (holomush-q426).

title: Backup and restore guide for production deployments
description: Document backup strategy for /opt/holomush/ (postgres data, certs, config). Cover tar-based backups, DO volume snapshots, scheduled backups via cron, and restore-to-new-host procedure. Follow-on from the deployment guide (holomush-a3a7.6).

title: Drop HTTP/1.1: gateway requires HTTP/2 for all connections
description:
## Decision

The web gateway requires HTTP/2 for all client connections. HTTP/1.1 is not supported. Non-TLS mode uses h2c (HTTP/2 cleartext) so both dev and production operate over HTTP/2.

## Rationale

Server-streaming (event subscriptions) depends on HTTP/2 PING/PONG keepalive for dead connection detection. With HTTP/1.1, dead connections are only detected after TCP retransmit timeout (~1-2 minutes), causing session leaks and stale grid_present state. HTTP/2 server pings (ReadIdleTimeout + PingTimeout) detect dead peers in ~45 seconds.

All target clients support HTTP/2:
- Modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge): native HTTP/2
- SvelteKit dev proxy (Vite): HTTP/2 via h2c
- ConnectRPC clients: HTTP/2 native
- Playwright E2E tests: Chromium with HTTP/2

## Alternatives Considered

- **Application-level heartbeat only**: Heartbeat writes to dead HTTP/1.1 connections succeed because TCP write buffers absorb small frames. Detection still takes ~1-2 minutes waiting for kernel retransmit timeout. Does not solve the fundamental problem.
- **Dual HTTP/1.1 + HTTP/2 support**: Adds complexity for a protocol no target client needs. Session cleanup behavior would differ by protocol version, creating subtle bugs.
- **WebSocket for streaming instead of server-streaming RPC**: Large architectural change, abandons ConnectRPC server-streaming, requires separate connection management. Disproportionate to the problem.

## Affects

- holomush-kfjs (HTTP/1.1 dead connection detection)
- internal/web/server.go (h2c handler wrapping)
- Site docs: operating guide needs HTTP/2 requirement noted
- compose.yaml: no changes needed (internal Docker networking supports h2c)

title: Communication event type extensibility for channels, forums, and plugin-defined verbs
description:
Current communication event types are hardcoded constants: say, pose, page, whisper. Each verb is its own EventType. This doesn't scale to channels, forums, telepathy, texting, or other plugin-defined communication methods.

The proto already uses string for event type (not an enum), so the wire format is extensible. But the Go constants and client rendering logic assume a closed set.

Design questions:
1. Should communication events use a single type (e.g., 'communication') with a verb/channel field, or keep discrete types?
2. How do plugins register new communication verbs that clients know how to render?
3. How do channels fit — is a channel message a 'say' on a channel stream, or a 'channel_message' type?
4. How does the web client render event types it doesn't recognize?

This affects: event.go constants, web client event rendering, plugin event emission, the events reference docs.

Current types from code: say, pose, arrive, leave, system, move, object_create, object_destroy, object_use, object_examine, object_give, page, whisper, command_response, location_state, exit_update.
notes: Plugin verb registration (section 4 of spec) depends on holomush-cv2k (plugin parity). The verb registry, proto restructure, and rendering migration (sections 1-3, 5) can ship independently. Channel infrastructure (section 2) can ship as Go handlers initially.

title: Claude Code skill for authoring HoloMUSH plugins
description:
Create a superpowers skill that guides plugin development including ABAC policy creation and debugging.

The skill should:
1. Scaffold plugin structure (plugin.yaml + main.lua or main.go)
2. Generate ABAC policies based on what the plugin needs to do — ask what host functions it will call, then produce the minimal policy set
3. Validate policy DSL syntax against the parser (internal/access/policy/dsl)
4. Debug access denied errors — given a log line, explain what policy is missing and generate the fix
5. Generate test scaffolding for the plugin
6. Lint the manifest against the plugin.schema.json

The skill should reference:
- extending/access-control.md for progressive examples
- reference/access-control.md for the full DSL spec
- extending/plugin-guide.md for manifest structure
- extending/getting-started.md for the quickstart flow

This would make plugin development significantly faster — instead of reading docs and guessing at policies, developers get an interactive workflow that produces correct manifests.

title: Add event and session Prometheus metrics
description:
Current metrics cover commands, connections, and engine failures but are missing key observability signals:

Missing metrics needed:
- holomush_events_total (counter, labels: type, stream) — events appended to store
- holomush_sessions_active (gauge) — current active sessions
- holomush_sessions_detached (gauge) — current detached sessions
- holomush_session_duration_seconds (histogram) — session lifetime
- holomush_event_delivery_latency_seconds (histogram) — LISTEN/NOTIFY to client send
- holomush_subscribe_streams_active (gauge) — active LISTEN subscriptions

Also: ABAC metrics exist (policy evaluations, degraded mode, provider errors, circuit breaker) but are registered via promauto on the default registry rather than the custom observability.Server registry. They show up at /metrics but aren't documented or consistently registered.
notes:
[2026-04-26 audit] METRIC REQUEST STILL VALID — names need rescoping.

Bead requests Prometheus metrics including 'holomush_events_total (counter, labels: type, stream) — events appended to store'. Post-JetStream cutover (PR #252, 2026-04-21):
- 'events appended to store' phrasing is stale — events go to JetStream stream, not a PG events table (table dropped in F6)
- The underlying observability need (count of events emitted, by type/stream) is still valid

Current state: only one event-related metric reference found via rg (plugins/core-scenes/metrics.go). The eventbus subsystem may already have native NATS metrics exposed (per memory: M7 'OTEL header propagation + Prom NATS exporter wiring' shipped) — overlap should be checked before adding more.

Triage decision: rewrite metric names to reflect JetStream model (e.g., publish-rate, subscribe-lag, audit-lag) and verify what M7's Prom NATS exporter already exposes before duplicating.

title: Background database sync to S3/GCS/Azure Blob Storage
description:
Operators need a built-in option for continuous or scheduled backup of PostgreSQL data to object storage. Should support any S3-compatible endpoint (AWS S3, GCS, Azure Blob, MinIO, Backblaze B2, etc.).

Scope:
- WAL archiving or logical replication to object storage
- Configurable via config.yaml (endpoint, bucket, credentials, schedule)
- Support for S3-compatible APIs as the common denominator
- Point-in-time recovery from object storage backups
- Status reporting via metrics endpoint (last successful sync, lag)

This replaces the current 'run pg_dump manually' guidance in operating/database.md with a first-class operator feature. Should integrate with the existing operations/monitoring story.

title: Unified help system: in-game help command + website command reference
description:
Commands already have HelpText (markdown) in their CommandEntryConfig. The plugin host function system can query the command registry. But there is no 'help' command registered, and the website command reference (guide/commands.md) is hand-written separately.

Need:
1. A 'help' core command that lists available commands and shows HelpText for a specific command (help, help say, etc.)
2. A generation pipeline that produces guide/commands.md (or a reference page) from the same HelpText data, so the website and in-game help are always in sync
3. A requirement in the plugin manifest schema that plugins MUST provide Help and HelpText for any commands they register
4. The plugin guide should document this requirement

The HelpText markdown is already written for all core commands in register.go. The infrastructure (CommandEntryConfig.Help, .HelpText, .Usage) is already there. This is about wiring it up end-to-end.

title: Policy attribute validation at compile time
description: The ABAC policy compiler should validate attribute paths against the registered schema during compilation. When a policy references principal.character.foo, the compiler should verify that 'foo' exists in the character namespace schema and that the operator is compatible with the attribute type (e.g., == on a StringList should warn/error, 'in' is correct). This catches stale attribute references (like the role→roles rename) and type mismatches before runtime. Discovered when engine_test.go had 20+ references to the old 'role' attribute that passed because test mocks were self-consistent but didn't reflect production.

title: Content storage — interface-backed blob storage for uploads
description: Abstracted content storage behind a ContentStore interface supporting character portraits, wiki images, file uploads. Implementations: PostgreSQL large objects (default, zero-config), S3/blob storage (for scale), filesystem (for dev). Interface: Put(ctx, key, reader) / Get(ctx, key) / Delete(ctx, key) / Exists(ctx, key). Metadata in DB (content type, size, owner), blobs in backend. Needed by: character portraits, wiki pages, file uploads.

title: Custom theme support — operator-defined color schemes
description: Allow operators to define custom themes beyond default-dark/default-light. Themes are JSON files following the existing ThemeColors schema. Need: theme discovery (scan directory or DB), theme selection UI in player preferences, theme preview, documentation for theme authors. Currently the theme system works but is hardcoded to two built-in themes.

title: Update site docs: terminal UI, glossary, architecture invariants
description: The site docs (site/docs/) need a pass to cover recent work: (1) Terminal UI feature documentation for operators and contributors, (2) Glossary/terminology page — location not room, session vs connection, presence, grid present, etc. (3) Gateway boundary invariant (protocol translation only, no direct service access). (4) Event channel system (TERMINAL/STATE/BOTH) for developers. (5) Theme system documentation for operators (dark/light, custom themes).

title: Recalibrate benchmark baselines (BenchmarkAttributeResolution, BenchmarkSinglePolicyEvaluation)
description: Benchmark regression check has stale baselines from an earlier runner. BenchmarkAttributeResolution baseline is 100μs but actual is ~138μs. BenchmarkSinglePolicyEvaluation same. These cause false positive CI failures on every PR. Update .benchmarks/baseline.txt with current runner performance.

title: [E8] Character creation + management UI
description: Design and implement character creation flow (new characters), character management (rename, description), and integration with roster system (Epic holomush-gloh). Includes web UI for creating characters, selecting from roster, and managing existing characters. Depends on the two-phase login flow from sub-spec 2a which provides the character picker.

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: theme:web-portals reconciliation (2026-06-19): SCOPE = owner-facing character create / edit / manage (the WRITE surface) + character-LEVEL settings/preferences (per-character config). Distinct from holomush-qve.9 (public profiles/sheets/directory — the VIEW surface) and holomush-w7t5 (PLAYER-level preferences, server-side). Character-level settings folds HERE (no separate bead). Cross-link qve.9.

title: Add strict audit mode to EngineConfig for compliance deployments
description: Engine.Evaluate silently drops audit failures (4 call sites log+metric). Add StrictAudit mode to EngineConfig that returns audit errors to callers for compliance-required deployments. See PR #88 finding holomush-eulm.2.

title: Add integration tests for transactional cascade deletion
description: Spec (05-storage-audit.md:146) requires integration tests for each entity deletion path verifying same-transaction behavior. Current coverage is unit/mocked only (service_test.go). Need DB-backed integration tests for DeleteLocation, DeleteObject, DeleteCharacter that verify parent+property deletion occurs atomically.

title: Epic: Character Rostering & Transfer
description:
Character rostering (releasing to unclaimed pool) and transfer between players.

**Goal:** Enable characters to be released to a roster and claimed by other players.

**Key Features:**
- Players can release (roster) their own characters
- Rostered characters have NULL player_id
- Claiming a rostered character requires admin approval
- Direct player-to-player transfer (admin-mediated or request/accept flow)

**Design Discussion (from Epic 5):**
- player_id nullable to support rostered state
- Self-service roster release in future
- Admin approval required for claims
- Possible roster table for metadata (notes, restrictions, available_at)

**Depends on:** Epic 5 (Auth & Identity) for nullable player_id

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Players can release characters to roster
- [ ] Admins can approve/deny roster claims
- [ ] Character ownership transfers cleanly
- [ ] Audit trail for ownership changes

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: theme:web-portals note (2026-06-19): this epic owns the ROSTER/TRANSFER MECHANIC (release to pool, admin-approved claim, player-to-player transfer). Its WEB control expression — an 'available characters / claim' page — is a web-portals slice and is DISTINCT from holomush-qve.9's profile directory (a read view). When gloh's web view is beaded, label it theme:web-portals. Not labeling the whole epic (it's mostly backend mechanic).

title: [E12.5] Status Sync - Online/Offline Presence
description:
Implement presence synchronization between Discord and HoloMUSH.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Game online status reflected in Discord (custom status or role)
- [ ] Discord presence optionally shown in-game who list
- [ ] Character name displayed in Discord status when playing
- [ ] Idle/AFK status synced bidirectionally
- [ ] Players can opt out of presence sync
- [ ] Presence updates debounced to avoid rate limits
- [ ] Graceful degradation if Discord API unavailable
- [ ] Unit tests cover presence state machine
- [ ] Integration tests verify sync accuracy

title: [E12.4] Notifications - Mentions and DMs Forwarded
description:
Implement notification forwarding between Discord and HoloMUSH.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Game mentions (@playername) forward to linked Discord account as DM
- [ ] Discord @mentions in bridged channels forward to player in-game
- [ ] Players can configure notification preferences (on/off, quiet hours)
- [ ] Page/tell commands optionally forward to Discord DM
- [ ] Notification rate limiting prevents spam
- [ ] Offline players receive notifications when they return (configurable)
- [ ] Rich notification content (room name, context)
- [ ] Unit tests cover notification routing
- [ ] Integration tests verify DM delivery (mocked)

title: [E11.4] Notifications - New replies
description:
Implement notification system for forum activity.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Subscribe/unsubscribe to threads
- [ ] New reply notifications (web push and/or in-game)
- [ ] Mention notifications (@username)
- [ ] Notification preferences UI
- [ ] Batch digest option (immediate vs daily)
- [ ] Mark as read functionality
- [ ] Integration with existing notification infrastructure
- [ ] Test notification delivery

title: [E11.5] In-game Integration - forum recent command
description:
Implement in-game commands for forum integration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] +forum/recent - show recent posts summary
- [ ] +forum/read <thread> - read thread in-game
- [ ] +forum/post <board> - quick post from game
- [ ] Link character to forum account
- [ ] Respect board permissions from game side

description:
Build the admin portal section.

## Scope
- Server statistics dashboard
- Player management (list, search, actions)
- Character management
- Ban/mute management
- Audit log viewer

## Acceptance Criteria

Build the character portal section.

## Scope

This is the VIEW surface. Distinct from: holomush-qve.15 (owner create/edit/manage + character-level settings — the WRITE surface) and holomush-gloh (Character Rostering & Transfer — the claim/release/transfer MECHANIC). Cross-link all three; the 'roster' directory here is the read view, gloh owns the claim/transfer actions.

title: [E8.6] Portal: Wiki - Help pages, lore, documentation
description:
Build the wiki/documentation portal section.

## Scope
- Wiki page rendering (Markdown support)
- Help command documentation
- Lore/world documentation
- Search functionality
- Navigation and categorization

- Version history

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Help topics indexed and searchable
- [ ] Lore pages with rich formatting
- [ ] Full-text search across wiki
