title: [E8.5] Offline support - Command queue, event cache, reconnect sync
description:
Implement offline-first PWA capabilities.

## Scope
- Command queue for offline input
- Event cache in IndexedDB
- Reconnection sync (replay missed events)
- Optimistic UI updates
- Conflict resolution strategy
- Background sync when online
- Cache management (size limits, eviction)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Commands queued when offline
- [ ] Queued commands sent on reconnect
- [ ] Events cached in IndexedDB
- [ ] Event cache survives page reload
- [ ] Reconnect replays missed events correctly
- [ ] No duplicate events on reconnect
- [ ] Optimistic updates for user commands
- [ ] Cache size limits enforced
- [ ] Old events evicted properly
- [ ] Background sync works
- [ ] Offline indicator in UI
- [ ] Unit tests for queue logic
- [ ] Integration tests for sync
notes: Strategic question (2026-05-16 qve audit): the original framing (IndexedDB event cache + command queue + background sync) may be substantially superseded by the JetStream server-side replay architecture (streamBackfill.ts already implements reconnect+backfill at web/src/lib/backfill/). Before building, decide between: (a) close as superseded — JetStream replay covers the operational need; (b) keep IndexedDB approach for true offline-first (intermittent network, mobile/PWA install); (c) hybrid (small IndexedDB cache as warm restore + JetStream for cold). The decision is product-strategic, not technical. Consider in conjunction with holomush-jxwr (replay_complete UI signal — same supersession question).

title: [E10.5] Channel History - Logging, Replay on Join
description:
Implement channel message history and replay functionality.

## Scope
- Persist channel messages to event store
- Replay recent messages on channel join
- Channel history command for scrollback
- Configurable history retention

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Channel messages stored in event store
- [ ] New joiners see last N messages (configurable, default 20)
- [ ] @channel history <name> [count] shows recent messages
- [ ] History respects join timestamp (no messages before join)
- [ ] Admin channels may have extended/unlimited history
- [ ] History pruning for old messages (configurable retention)
- [ ] Unit tests for history storage/retrieval
- [ ] Integration tests for replay on join
- [ ] Performance tests for large history
- [ ] >80% test coverage

title: [E10.4] Moderation - Mute, Ban, Ops
description:
Implement channel moderation tools for operators.

## Scope
- Channel operator (op) role with moderation powers
- Mute: temporarily silence a member
- Ban: remove and prevent rejoining
- Op management: grant/revoke op status

## Commands
- @channel op <player> <channel> - Grant op status
- @channel deop <player> <channel> - Revoke op status
- @channel mute <player> <channel> [duration] - Mute player
- @channel unmute <player> <channel> - Unmute player
- @channel ban <player> <channel> - Ban from channel
- @channel unban <player> <channel> - Remove ban

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ops can mute, ban, unmute, unban members
- [ ] Owner can grant/revoke op status
- [ ] Muted players cannot send messages
- [ ] Banned players cannot rejoin
- [ ] Moderation events emitted (Mute, Ban, Op changes)
- [ ] Staff can override moderation as needed
- [ ] Mute duration with auto-expire (optional)
- [ ] Unit tests for each moderation action
- [ ] Integration tests for permission boundaries
- [ ] >80% test coverage

title: [E10.3] Channel Types - Public, Private, Admin
description:
Implement channel type-specific behavior and access control.

## Scope
- Public channels: visible to all, joinable by all
- Private channels: invite-only, hidden from list
- Admin channels: staff-only, hidden from players

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Public channels appear in @channel list for all players
- [ ] Private channels only visible to members
- [ ] Private channels require invite to join
- [ ] Admin channels only visible/joinable by staff
- [ ] ABAC policies enforce channel type restrictions
- [ ] @channel invite <player> <channel> for private channels
- [ ] Channel creation respects type permissions
- [ ] Unit tests for each channel type
- [ ] Integration tests for access control
- [ ] >80% test coverage

title: [E10.2] Channel Commands - join/leave/list/say
description:
Implement basic channel commands for player interaction.

## Scope
- @channel command with subcommands
- Join/leave channel functionality
- List available channels
- Say to channel (message broadcast)

## Commands
- @channel list - Show available channels
- @channel join <name> - Join a channel
- @channel leave <name> - Leave a channel
- @channel <name>=<message> - Send message to channel

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] @channel list shows public channels and joined channels
- [ ] @channel join adds membership with proper permissions
- [ ] @channel leave removes membership (unless owner)
- [ ] Channel messages broadcast to all members
- [ ] Channel events emitted (ChannelJoin, ChannelLeave, ChannelMessage)
- [ ] Error handling for invalid channels, permissions
- [ ] Unit tests for each command
- [ ] Integration tests for command flow
- [ ] >80% test coverage

title: [E10.1] Channel Schema - Name, Type, Membership
description:
Implement channel database schema and core data types.

## Scope
- Channel table with name, type, description, owner
- ChannelMembership table with role (owner, op, member, banned)
- Channel types enum (public, private, admin)
- Go types in internal/core/channel.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database migration for channels and channel_memberships tables
- [ ] Channel struct with all required fields
- [ ] ChannelMembership struct with role enum
- [ ] ChannelType enum (Public, Private, Admin)
- [ ] ChannelStore interface defined
- [ ] PostgreSQL ChannelStore implementation
- [ ] Unit tests for channel types and validation
- [ ] Integration tests for ChannelStore CRUD operations
- [ ] >80% test coverage

title: [E10] Implementation Plan: Channels
description:
Create detailed implementation plan for the channels system.

## Scope
- Break down design into implementable tasks
- Define test strategy for each component
- Estimate effort and identify risks
- Plan migration/rollout strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implementation phases documented with clear deliverables
- [ ] Test plan covering unit, integration, and E2E tests
- [ ] Dependencies on other epics identified
- [ ] File/package structure planned
- [ ] API versioning strategy if needed

title: Epic 10: Channels
description:
In-game communication channels with membership and moderation.

**Goal:** Flexible channel system for community communication.

**Channel Types:**
- Public: Open to all
- Private: Invite only
- Admin: Staff only

**Phases:**
- 10.1: Channel schema
- 10.2: Channel commands
- 10.3: Channel types
- 10.4: Moderation
- 10.5: Channel history

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Channel name, type, membership stored
- [ ] channel join/leave/list/say commands work
- [ ] Public, private, admin channel types work
- [ ] Mute, ban, ops moderation works
- [ ] Logging and replay on join works

title: Epic 9: Scenes & RP
description:
Scene management for narrative play, visibility isolation, logging.

**Goal:** Enable structured roleplay with scene isolation.

**Visibility Model:**
Location: 'The Tavern'
├── Real-time (no scene) ← new arrivals land here
├── Scene #42 'Secret Meeting' ← isolated
└── Scene #87 'Bar Fight' ← isolated

**Scene Metadata:**
- Visibility: public, private, unlisted
- Tags: combat, mature, social, plot, custom

**Phases:**
- 9.1: Scene schema
- 9.1b: Scene metadata
- 9.2: Visibility isolation
- 9.3: Scene commands
- 9.4: Scene logging
- 9.5: Web portal: Scenes
- 9.6: Forum integration

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Create/join/leave scenes works
- [ ] Tags and visibility (public/private/unlisted) work
- [ ] Scene participants only see each other
- [ ] scene create/join/leave/end commands work
- [ ] Events captured and exportable
- [ ] Web portal shows active scenes and logs
- [ ] Scene requests and scheduling via forums
notes:
## 2026-04-06: Architecture rework — v2 spec

The original phase plan (9.1-9.6) is obsolete due to the plugin architecture
rework that landed in PRs #192/#194/#195. The product design (D1-D10) is
unchanged but the implementation architecture is fundamentally different:
scenes are now defined and served by a binary plugin (core-scenes), not by
the server core.

**v2 design spec:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-06-scenes-and-rp-design-v2.md
**v1 spec (preserved as reference):** available via
  jj file show -r scene-system docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-04-scenes-and-rp-design.md
**Reference branch with original work:** scene-system bookmark in jj

**New 10-phase implementation plan (vertical slices):**

1. Foundation slice — scaffold, scene create/info, ABAC resolver, per-resource policy
2. Lifecycle — end/pause/resume + state machine
3. Membership — join/leave/invite/kick + scene_participants table
4. Streams + pose order
5. Focus model (multi-connection visibility)
6. Logs + vote + hard privacy boundary
7. Templates
8. Scene board + content warnings
9. Web client (chat view)
10. Notifications + telnet edge cases + polish

**Out of scope (separate epic):** Forum view (cross-cutting for scenes + OOC comms)

**Forum-related acceptance criteria from v1 description** ("Forum integration",
"Scene requests and scheduling via forums") move to the future Forum epic.

Phase beads created as children of this epic with blocks dependencies.

title: Epic 8: Web Client
description:
SvelteKit PWA with terminal, offline support, and portal features.

**Goal:** Modern web client with offline-first design.

**Offline Behavior:**
- Online: Real-time WebSocket events
- Offline: Commands queued, cached content available
- Reconnect: Queue flushed, missed events replayed

**Phases:**
- 8.1: Project scaffold
- 8.2: WebSocket transport
- 8.3: Terminal UI
- 8.4: Auth flows
- 8.5: Offline support
- 8.6: Portal: Wiki
- 8.7: Portal: Characters
- 8.8: Portal: Admin

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SvelteKit + PWA setup works
- [ ] WebSocket connects and receives events
- [ ] Terminal with input, scrollback, ANSI rendering
- [ ] Login and character select flows work
- [ ] Command queue, event cache, reconnect sync work
- [ ] Wiki/help pages available
- [ ] Public character profiles/sheets work
- [ ] Admin stats and player management work

title: Document the rumdl tooling-dir exclusions (.planning/.claude/.beads/.serena) in docs/CLAUDE.md
description: Loose end from PR #4595 (merged 2026-07-10). The Taskfile excludes .planning/.claude/.beads/.serena from rumdl (lint:markdown/fmt:markdown/fmt:check:markdown, commit dde5ab07a), but docs/CLAUDE.md:29 still states unconditionally 'All markdown files MUST pass rumdl validation. The project uses .rumdl.toml for configuration…'. Two inaccuracies: (1) there is NO root .rumdl.toml — the root invocation is CLI --exclude-flag-driven (only site/.rumdl.toml exists); (2) the guideline omits the tooling-dir carve-outs. CodeRabbit flagged this (PR #4595 deferred-items.md thread). Fix: update docs/CLAUDE.md 'Markdown Standards' to state that tooling/working-state dirs (.git, .claude, .beads, .serena, .planning) and site/ (own config) are excluded from the root rumdl run, so published-docs + code markdown stay linted while GSD/tooling artifacts do not. Needs a small new branch/PR (main is protected; the milestone branch is merged).

title: Scene mute/notify defense-in-depth: tighten MuteScene + ListCharacterScenes to require vouched metadata
description: Found in the v1.0-milestone ship ABAC review (2026-07-09, non-blocking #1/#2/#3, Low; optional defense-in-depth, NOT reachable in production). (#1) MuteScene uses the advisory mismatchedActingCharacter (fail-open-on-absent) because it is ABAC-gated + carries a host-trusted req.character_id; if metadata were ever absent AND req.character_id differed from the host-derived dispatch subject, a participant could persist a mute keyed on another character's id (service.go:1074 keys on req.character_id; the mute ABAC gate at :955 evaluates the dispatch-token subject). Not co-occurrable in production (host.go:1268-1269 stamps WithOutgoingActorMetadata + sets req.character_id=dispatch actor; facade BeginServiceDispatch as verified char). (#2) ListCharacterScenes mute read-back (service.go:665-704) trusts req.character_id with no self-scope guard (facade overrides with verified owned char per INV-SCENE-63; command path stamps from session). (#3) notify-pref self-writes have no ABAC audit trail (prefs are not access-control state). Optional hardening: switch MuteScene to callerNotVouchedAsCharacter (fail-closed) and add a self-scope guard on the ListCharacterScenes mute read-back once all callers guarantee vouched metadata. Deferred per WR-02 split-guard decision (holomush-gl751).

title: core-channels: InviteToChannel records invitee (not inviter) as ops-journal actor (WR-02)
description: Phase-1 code review (01-REVIEW.md) WR-02. plugins/core-channels/service_rpcs.go:89 -> plugins/core-channels/store.go:255: the moderation ops-journal (channel_ops_events) records the TARGET character as the actor for an invite, not the inviter. Loses moderation accountability in the durable audit trail. Fix: thread the acting subject (inviter) into the ops-event actor field for InviteToChannel (and audit the other moderation verbs for the same).

title: core-channels: minor polish (prune graceful shutdown, rate-limiter GC, js_seq, subject facet strictness)
description: Phase-1 code review info findings (01-REVIEW.md IN-01..IN-04). (IN-03) plugins/core-channels/main.go:321-330 prune goroutine cancel func discarded — no graceful shutdown on plugin unload. (IN-04) plugins/core-channels/service.go:118-162 per-player create rate-limiter map grows unbounded across distinct players — add TTL/GC eviction. (IN-01) migrations/000002_create_channel_log.up.sql:27 channel_log.js_seq column declared but never populated — wire it or drop it. (IN-02) plugins/core-channels/audit.go:444-461 parseChannelSubject accepts trailing facets rather than rejecting (benign, no leak) — tighten. None blocking; polish batch.

title: Web-BFF/typed-RPC channel create: trusted owner-player-id for per-player rate limiting
description: Phase-1 core-channels CreateChannel's 5/hr per-player limiter keys on the command-path host-vouched CommandRequest.PlayerID (threaded via a trusted dispatch-context binding stamped by the command layer). The typed-RPC / web-BFF create path (out of scope for Phase 1, which is telnet-only) has no host-vouched player id in the typed service context, so it must reuse the seam via BeginServiceDispatch's ownerPlayerID before it can enforce the limiter. Documented as a deferred seam in .planning/phases/01-channels-subsystem/01-05-PLAN.md (review finding R2-C, codex round 2). Surfaced by gsd-plan-checker.

title: Consolidate seed-disable-migration test Describes + harness CreateScene visibility option
description: From PR #4586 review (holomush-y4auh.4/.5, suggestion severity, deferred). (1) internal/store/migrate_integration_test.go now has two ~95%-identical Describe blocks (Migration000047/000048 seed-disable + operator-CAS-guard specs) — extract a parameterized describeSeedDisableMigration helper if a third seed retirement ever lands, or opportunistically sooner. (2) test/integration/scenes/scene_info_read_access_test.go carries local createPrivateScene/commandText helpers that near-duplicate Session.CreateScene (hardcodes visibility=open — add a visibility option/variant to the harness helper) and a payload-unmarshal idiom inlined in two sibling files — hoist to the harness/package level.

title: render-adr YAML frontmatter output breaks task lint (license-eye header lands above frontmatter; repo ADRs use H1 style)
description: The dev-flow plugin's render-adr script (version 8de289837130) emits ADRs with YAML 'title:' frontmatter and no H1. In this repo, task fmt (license-eye) prepends the HTML license comment at byte 0, which invalidates the frontmatter and trips rumdl MD041/MD065/MD003/MD022 in task lint:markdown. All pre-existing docs/adr/*.md use the H1-title format and pass. Hit on holomush-pbp9j (rendered during holomush-uprtc); worked around by hand-converting the rendered file to H1 style — meaning a future /adr render or /adr update of ANY bead re-emits frontmatter and re-breaks lint. Fix options: teach render-adr an H1 mode (or repo-level config), OR configure license-eye to insert below frontmatter, OR have the ADR task pipeline run the conversion. Decide and make render idempotently lint-clean.

title: Validate FocusRedirect.FocusKind against known kinds in CollectFocusRedirects (defense-in-depth)
description: From octopus review on PR #4574 (holomush-g1qcw focus-routed scene input). CollectFocusRedirects (internal/plugin/manager.go ~L841) validates target-command existence and duplicate verb/kind entries, but does NOT validate that FocusRedirect.FocusKind is a known value (currently only 'scene'). The manifest JSON schema already enforces enum=scene at load, so the normal path is covered — this is defense-in-depth: a hand-crafted/schema-bypassing manifest with an unknown kind would produce a silently-inert redirect. Add a knownFocusKinds set + FOCUS_REDIRECT_UNKNOWN_KIND fail-closed check in CollectFocusRedirects, mirroring the existing unknown-target/duplicate guards. Files: internal/plugin/manager.go (CollectFocusRedirects) + a table test. Out of scope: any dispatcher change.

title: Focus-redirect polish: dedup storeFocusReader, typed FocusRedirectTable key, blank-verb test + error-code assertions
description: From PR #4574 review suggestions. (x1lwf.2) storeFocusReader duplicates get_connection_focus.go's CONNECTION_NOT_FOUND handling — centralize in internal/session. (x1lwf.11) FocusRedirectTable.Target/Redirects take plain string for a semantically-session.FocusKind key — consider typing it. (x1lwf.8) validateFocusRedirects blank-verb-in-non-empty-list branch is untested + focus_reader tests use assert.Contains instead of errutil.AssertErrorCode.
acceptance_criteria: The three polish items are addressed or explicitly declined with rationale.

title: integrationtest.Session.SendCommand omits connection_id — focus-dependent specs must use SendCommandOnConnection
description: test/integration harness footgun (g1qcw.6 code-review, Low): Session.SendCommand (internal/testsupport/integrationtest/session.go) builds HandleCommandRequest without ConnectionId, so the dispatcher's per-connection focus redirect (maybeRedirectForFocus no-ops on zero connID) never fires through it. g1qcw.6 added SendCommandOnConnection as the additive fix, but a future author writing a focus-dependent spec may reach for the obvious SendCommand and get a silently non-redirecting dispatch (routing assertion passes/fails for the wrong reason). Currently mitigated only by a doc comment. Consider auditing SendCommand callers and making connection-threading the default (or renaming for clarity) once safe.
acceptance_criteria: Either SendCommand threads connection_id by default (after a caller audit confirms no existing spec relies on its absence), or the two methods are named/documented so the footgun is structurally hard to hit.

title: Integration harness: refresh command AliasCache from alias_seeder
description: Surfaced by holomush-kk1ot.11 integration spec. The integrationtest harness (internal/testsupport/integrationtest) loads plugin manifests via WithInTreePlugins, and alias_seeder writes command aliases to a persisted alias repo, but the harness's in-memory command dispatcher AliasCache is never reloaded from those rows. Result: top-level alias-invoked commands (e.g. ';waves'/':waves' for pose) 404 as 'Unknown command' in integration tests even though the alias table works in production (unit-covered in internal/command/alias_test.go, resolve_test.go). content_contract_test.go worked around this by driving the embedded-sigil form ('pose ;waves'), which exercises the same comm.ParsePose branch. Fixing the harness to refresh the AliasCache would let integration specs drive real alias invocations.
acceptance_criteria: The integrationtest harness refreshes the dispatcher AliasCache from the alias_seeder's persisted rows after plugin load, so an integration test can drive a top-level alias command (e.g. ';waves') and have it dispatch to the aliased command. A regression test in test/integration drives an alias invocation successfully.

title: Scene emit/no_space live-render convergence (web + telnet)
description:
Follow-up surfaced by code-review of holomush-kk1ot.7 (Slice 1 scene emit migration). After migrating core-scenes handleEmit to CommunicationContent builders:

1. scene_emit is now actorless (comm.Emit sets no actor_id/actor_display_name). The archived/export/snapshot path already renders EntryKindEmit content-only with no speaker (publish_render.go:32,61), but the LIVE renderers still label emit events: telnet formatCommunication falls back to truncateActorID(ev.GetActorId()) (gateway_handler.go:1160-1163) and web translate.go falls back to actor_id (translate.go:90-96). Live emit rendering should converge to content-only (drop the actor label for emit-class events) to match the archived form.

2. no_space is now carried on scene pose payloads (from InvokedAs ';'), but the web scene view does not yet render it (the ';' semipose no-space form). Converge web scene rendering to honor no_space.

Both were explicitly deferred as 'rendering convergence' out of scope for kk1ot Slice 1. Relates to holomush-g1qcw (focus-routed scene input).
acceptance_criteria: Live telnet + web emit-class scene events render content-only (no speaker label), matching publish_render's archived form. Web scene view renders no_space (';' semipose) without the separating space. Tests cover both.

title: Add a regression guard that the 'releases' sidebar topic stays registered in astro.config.mjs
description: PR #4542 review (xlx7q.3): no test asserts the 'releases' starlightSidebarTopics entry remains; a future edit could orphan release pages silently. Add a bats/check-docs assertion.

title: Extract shared actorBinding helper for the ~10 duplicated SceneService guard blocks
description: After PR #4532, plugins/core-scenes/service.go has ~10 structurally identical inline actor-binding guard blocks (WatchScene + 4 membership + JoinScene/EndScene/PauseScene/ResumeScene/UpdateScene). Only the slog op-name string and the PermissionDenied message vary per copy. A private helper (e.g. actorBindingError(ctx, op, sceneID, reqCharacterID, deniedMsg string) error) would collapse each site to a 3-line guard call and remove the 'a future security patch misses N of 10 copies' regression class. Inline duplication was the correct call for the #4531/#4532 security diffs (surgical, consistent with merged precedent); this tracks paying the debt down deliberately, touching all ~10 sites in one refactor. Source: PR #4532 review findings holomush-xn3hd.7 (simplify) + .8 cluster.

title: CI: cache build/plugins artifacts across jobs
description: plugin:build-all rebuilds binary plugins from scratch in every CI job (integration, e2e, build). Persist build/plugins/ via nscloud cache keyed on the plugin source fingerprint (plugins/**, pkg/plugin/**, pkg/proto/**, go.mod/go.sum, scripts/build-plugins.sh) so jobs reuse compiled plugins. Follow-up from holomush-d5ra2 / PR #4525 (test-speed review Tier 1 #2). Out of scope: the host-only test-path build (already landed).

title: Polish: web communication seam non-blocking review nits (PR #4524)
description:
Non-blocking suggestions from PR #4524 comprehensive review (dev-flow code/type/comment analyzers). Not gating; captured for later.

1. commLine.ts exported decls (CommKind/CommLine/CommEvent/commEventToLine/logEntryToLine) use // comments; codebase convention (web/src/lib/scenes/types.ts) uses /** */ TSDoc for exported declarations. Convert for IDE tooltips + consistency.
2. CommunicationLine.svelte does not expose a 'class' prop; web/CLAUDE.md says custom components MUST expose one. Pre-existing gap shared with CommunicationRenderer (the renderer group doesn't expose it) — low priority, align the group together if pursued.
3. CommLine could be a discriminated union per kind (say/pose/ooc/emit) to make oocStyle/oocPrefix required on ooc and a compile error on other kinds. Nice-to-have; current flat interface is pragmatic for a 4-kind UI model and is fully tested. Zero runtime cost if pursued.

Source: PR #4524 review reports under .claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/reports/2026-06-25-*.md and the review agents' output.

title: Scenes web: unify SceneBoardRow status dot onto sceneStateDotClass helper
description:
Follow-up to holomush-5rh.27, which consolidated the center-header and SceneContextRail status dots onto a shared helper web/src/lib/scenes/stateStyle.ts (sceneStateDotClass). A third, divergent copy of scene state->dot-color logic remains in web/src/lib/components/scenes/SceneBoardRow.svelte (stateColor() at lines 34-36): it uses a different palette (active=bg-green-500 vs helper's bg-emerald-500; paused=bg-yellow-400 vs bg-amber-400) and omits the published state. So the board-list 'active' dot is a visibly different green from the rail and center header.

Goal: point SceneBoardRow at sceneStateDotClass so all scene status dots share one palette.
Files touched: web/src/lib/components/scenes/SceneBoardRow.svelte (remove stateColor(), import + use sceneStateDotClass).
Out of scope: dot SIZE differences (board uses size-2.5) are intentional and may stay; this is about COLOR consistency only. Confirm the board's larger green is intended to match emerald-500 before changing.

Source: code-reviewer finding on holomush-5rh.27 (report at .claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/reports/2026-06-23-2156-holomush-5rh-27-scene-status-dot.md).
acceptance_criteria: SceneBoardRow's active/paused/ended status dots use the same Tailwind color classes as sceneStateDotClass (bg-emerald-500 / bg-amber-400 / bg-muted-foreground); stateColor() is removed; existing board tests still pass.

title: Bump @bufbuild/protoc-gen-es + bufbuild/es codegen plugin to 2.12.1, regenerate web stubs
description: Clear the existing generator/runtime drift: @bufbuild/protoc-gen-es latest is 2.12.1 but web/package.json + web/buf.gen.yaml pin 2.12.0. Bump both pins to 2.12.1 and regenerate the committed web/src/lib/connect/**/*_pb.ts stubs via task web:generate so main matches the pin. This is the manual codegen step that the holomush-ai3o5 Renovate 'buf codegen' group (automerge:false) is designed to require on each bump. Files: web/package.json, web/buf.gen.yaml, web/pnpm-lock.yaml, web/src/lib/connect/**/*_pb.ts.
acceptance_criteria: web/package.json @bufbuild/protoc-gen-es = 2.12.1; web/buf.gen.yaml bufbuild/es = :v2.12.1; pnpm-lock updated; _pb.ts stubs regenerated at 2.12.1; task pr-prep fast lane passes.

title: jj amend force-push to a PR branch does not re-trigger path-filtered ci.yaml (close+reopen to re-fire)
description:
Observed twice this session (PRs #4489, #4492): after `jj git push -b <branch>` of an AMENDED commit (sideways bookmark move / force-update), GitHub fired the CodeQL/Analyze workflows but did NOT create a run for .github/workflows/ci.yaml (the workflow carrying the required Test/Integration Test/E2E/Lint/Build gates, triggered on pull_request with paths-ignore). Result: required gates absent on the new head → PR stuck (mergeStateStatus oscillates BLOCKED/UNSTABLE) with no CI running.

Reliable re-trigger: `gh pr close <n> -R holomush/holomush && gh pr reopen <n> -R holomush/holomush` fires pull_request:reopened → ci.yaml runs. Confirmed working both times. A plain re-push of the same bookmark ('Nothing changed') does NOT help.

Likely cause: GitHub's synchronize-event delivery for jj force-updates not matching ci.yaml's pull_request trigger the way the first push (opened) did. The CodeQL 'dynamic' workflow is unaffected (separate scheduling path). Investigate whether ci.yaml needs an explicit `types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]` or whether this is a jj-ref-update quirk. Low priority — close+reopen is a clean workaround — but document it (consider a note in the jj/landing-the-plane workflow docs).

title: Flaky E2E: session-security.spec.ts '11th login evicts the oldest session' (timing)
description: web/e2e/session-security.spec.ts:134 'Session Security (bd-urbq) › 11th login evicts the oldest session' failed once in CI E2E (run 27891959644, PR #4484, 2026-06-21); 92/94 specs passed, only this one failed. Timing/ordering-sensitive (11 sequential browser logins → async oldest-session eviction → assertion). UNRELATED to PR #4484 (scene name resolution; all scenes.spec.ts E2E specs PASSED). Not quarantined. Candidate to make the eviction-await condition-based (poll for the evicted session's 401 rather than a fixed wait) or quarantine citing this bead if it recurs. Out of scope for #4484. Sibling flake observed same run: holomush-o7k0p (crypto invalidation Test).

title: Register terminal scene-command guest-deny as a named invariant
description: abac-reviewer (holomush-5rh.23) flagged that the fail-closed terminal guest-deny gate (core-scenes execute-scene-commands: principal.character.is_guest == false) is a durable, test-pinned guarantee currently living only in plugin.yaml prose. It is the terminal-path parallel of INV-SCENE-64 (web scene-access facade guest denial). Register a new INV-SCENE-N (or INV-ACCESS-N) entry in docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, annotate the verifying test (test/integration/plugin/binary_plugin_test.go 'denies a guest's scene command execution') with // Verifies: INV-..., and regenerate via 'go run ./cmd/inv-render'. Consult .claude/rules/invariants.md. Out of scope for 5rh.23 (non-blocking; pending tolerated).
notes: Deferral target for PR #4481 review finding holomush-pt5zx.5 (tests-aspect, important): register the terminal scene-command guest-deny as INV-SCENE-65 and bind it to test/integration/plugin/binary_plugin_test.go's 'denies a guest's scene command execution' via // Verifies: INV-SCENE-65, then regenerate invariants.md (go run ./cmd/inv-render).

title: Scenes web theme: light mode lacks the pop/brightness of dark mode
description: Design/polish: across the scenes UI the light theme feels flat and washed-out compared to dark mode — less contrast and visual pop on cards, borders, and accents. Review the light-palette --color-* / --brand-* tokens for sufficient contrast and visual hierarchy. Broad theming-polish item, not a single-element bug. Out of scope: dark theme.
acceptance_criteria: Light theme has comparable visual hierarchy and contrast to dark mode across the scenes surfaces (design review).

title: fix(compose): dev otel-collector unreachable from gateway under dev:obs — add collector to frontend network
description:
Goal: fix the dev-compose network mismatch that makes the gateway's OTLP exporter spam 'rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = name resolver error: produced zero addresses' every ~10s under 'task dev:obs', and that silently drops the gateway's own server-side spans from Jaeger/Grafana.

Root cause (confirmed 2026-06-19): in compose.yaml the gateway exports to http://otel-collector:4317 (line 68) but is attached to the 'frontend' network only (line 82); otel-collector is attached to 'backend' only (line 96). They share no network, so the gateway's gRPC OTLP exporter cannot DNS-resolve 'otel-collector' → zero addresses. core is on BOTH networks (lines 52-53), which is why only gateway-1 errors and core-1 is silent. Verified empirically on the running stack: core resolves otel-collector (172.23.0.4); gateway cannot. Longstanding, not a regression (recent compose.yaml commits were Docker-digest bumps only).

Fix: add 'frontend' to the otel-collector service's networks in compose.yaml so it is on [backend, frontend], mirroring compose.prod.yaml:187-188 (collector on both networks; comment: 'must be reachable from the gateway's network'). Preferred over putting the gateway on 'backend' (the comment at compose.yaml:72-73 implies that) because it keeps the gateway off the DB/backend network per the gateway-boundary separation, fixes the gateway's own-span export, and also unblocks the optional OTLP browser-relay path. Refresh that now-misleading comment in the same change.

Files touched: compose.yaml (otel-collector networks; gateway OTLP_RELAY comment).

Out of scope: compose.prod.yaml (already correct); the OTLP relay feature itself; web/browser tracing (unaffected — posts to localhost:4318).
acceptance_criteria: compose.yaml otel-collector is on [backend, frontend] matching compose.prod.yaml. Under 'task dev:obs', gateway-1 no longer emits 'produced zero addresses' telemetry errors (validated live by connecting the collector to the frontend network). task pr-prep passes.

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: PR opened: https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/4466 (branch fix-otel-net). Fix = otel-collector on [backend, frontend] mirroring compose.prod.yaml. Validated live (0 'produced zero addresses' after gateway restart with collector reachable); pr-prep pass; code-reviewer READY. Stays in_progress until squash-merge; close on merge.

title: Decide whether plugins with stream.history may read private scene .ic stream metadata
description:
## Question
seed:plugin-stream-read (holomush-xakba) permits a declared plugin to read ANY non-forbidden stream's history, including events.<gid>.scene.<id>.ic. Scene .ic plaintext is DEK-protected (a non-member plugin cannot decrypt SENSITIVE payloads), and .ic membership privacy is enforced by sessionHasMembership (INV-17), NOT ABAC. So a non-participant plugin can still read scene .ic event METADATA (ids, timestamps, types) via QueryStreamHistory.

## Pre-existing, not introduced by xakba
The coarse stream.history capability had this exposure before (on the host.v1 path; noted as a Medium during holomush-kplrr review). xakba added the instance-level gate + forbids for system/audit/crypto namespaces but intentionally did NOT add a scene-membership condition (plugins have no character/session membership context).

## Decide
Whether plugin scene-IC metadata exposure is acceptable (working-as-designed: plugins are server-side trusted code) or should be gated (e.g. a scene-IC forbid for plugins, or a membership-aware condition). Flagged by abac-reviewer + code-reviewer on the xakba diff.

## Reference
seed:plugin-stream-read (internal/access/policy/seed.go); INV-17 scene membership; holomush-xakba; holomush-kplrr.

title: Route stub service-method @param/@return refs through the collision-aware classNamer
description:
Goal: make Lua stub service-method @param/@return references collision-aware, closing the latent gap left by holomush-t4tye.

Context: holomush-t4tye made message-FIELD references collision-aware (luaType threads namer.className). But service-method @param/@return references are rendered by a separate path: luastub.go template uses the hardcoded ClassPrefix "holomush.msg." + the bare short RequestGoType/ResponseGoType (main.go goTypeName). That path does NOT consult classNamer.

Impact (latent): if a host.v1 RPC request/response message ever becomes a disambiguated collider (e.g. holomush.plugin.v1.DecryptOwnAuditR{Request,Response} becoming transitively reachable alongside the host.v1 same-named pair — host.v1 audit.proto already imports plugin.v1), its @class decl would be holomush.msg.plugin.host.v1.Foo (via classNamer) while audit.<Method>'s @return stays holomush.msg.Foo — a dangling reference. Guarded today by TestRenderedStubIsStructurallyValid (asserts every holomush.msg.* ref is declared), so it fails loudly rather than shipping broken.

Fix direction: carry the namer-resolved class name onto methodData (instead of bare Go type + hardcoded prefix), or thread the classNamer into renderLuaStub so the template resolves param/return classes the same way field types are. Non-colliders must keep holomush.msg.<Short> (byte-identical output today).

Source: code-reviewer finding on holomush-t4tye.

title: Binary forged-dispatch-rejection: whole-system e2e once a scope-eligible binary capability exists
description: Deferred from PR #4451 review (holomush-rwaak.1). The binary plugin->host forgery defense is split across packages (pkg/plugin ferryDispatch strips plugin-forged outgoing; goplugin/host.go AttachOutgoing overwrites with host-vouched; server StampInterceptor reconstructs). Each leg is unit-tested, but no single end-to-end test spans ferry+server like the Lua TestPluginForgedDispatchMetadataIsIgnored — because the ferry (pkg/plugin) and server (internal/plugin/goplugin) live in different packages and the path is latent (BinaryDefaultSet omits the only scope-eligible capability; binary Host.WorldMutator()==nil). When a scope-eligible capability gains a real binary consumer, add a whole-system integration test asserting a plugin-forged x-holomush-dispatch is rejected end-to-end (SCOPE_NO_DISPATCH).

title: Brokered focus proto uses bytes ULID fields — awkward for Lua callers (no luabridge ULID-bytes helper)
description: Observed during PR #4446 focus_join fixture migration. The brokered focus service (_G['focus'].AutoFocusOnJoin etc.) takes proto 'bytes' ULID fields (16-byte binary; focusServer.bytesToULID rejects other lengths), while world.query uses string ULIDs. luabridge has no Lua-side ULID encode/decode helper, so the focus_join fixture had to embed a pure-Lua Crockford-base32 decoder. If brokered focus is intended for production Lua plugin use, add a shared luabridge ULID-bytes helper (or make focus accept string ULIDs). Standalone follow-up, not blocking #4446.

title: Delete orphaned capability *Fn helpers + cap modules from hostfunc after atomic cutover (holomush-eykuh.4.9)
description: Follow-up to the atomic cutover (holomush-eykuh.4.9). After capability registration was stripped from hostfunc.Register, the *Fn helper bodies (kvGetFn/queryLocationFn/createObjectFn/evaluateFn/getSettingFn etc.) + cap_property.go/cap_world_query.go modules remain compiled but unreachable in production (only export_test.go's RegisterCapabilityFuncsForTest references some). They pull an internal/access import + carry pre-existing bare slog.Warn on dead paths. Cleanup: delete orphaned helpers/modules + the export_test shim. Filed STANDALONE (not under epic eykuh.4) — deferred polish, not part of the cutover deliverable. Source: code-reviewer non-blocking finding on 4.9.

title: Forcible session disconnect: gateway-observed eviction backing for SessionAdmin.DisconnectSession
description: Goal: give SessionAdmin.DisconnectSession(ctx,sessionID,reason) a real production backing so a trusted plugin (session.admin capability) can forcibly disconnect a session. Deferred from decision holomush-t019a: today disconnect has ZERO callers and no core-side mechanism, so the brokered sessionAdminServer.Disconnect fail-closes (Unimplemented). Constraint: forcible connection teardown is structurally a GATEWAY concern (.claude/rules/gateway-boundary.md) — core cannot reach into a live connection. Likely shape: core writes an eviction signal the gateway observes (a session-state 'evicted' flag the gateway polls/observes on its next interaction, or a disconnect event on a reserved subject the gateway subscribes to and acts on by closing the connection). Must be wired symmetrically for both Lua and binary runtimes through the shared backing (plugin-runtime-symmetry). Out of scope: building it speculatively — pull this in only when a forcible-disconnect feature is actually needed. Refs: holomush-t019a (decision), holomush-eykuh.4.2 (broadcast backing), docs/adr/holomush-t019a-*.md.
acceptance_criteria: SessionAdmin.DisconnectSession is backed by a production mechanism that causes the gateway to close the named session's connection with the given reason; both runtimes reach the backing through the shared sessionAdminServer; disconnect no longer fail-closes Unimplemented; unit + integration coverage; respects gateway-boundary.md (no core->connection direct coupling).

title: Harden luabridge.pushBridgeError against fmt-wrapped status errors (latent opacity leak)
description: Discovered via holomush-eykuh.4.4. pushBridgeError (internal/plugin/luabridge/marshal.go:318) returns status.Convert(err).Message() to Lua. For a fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", secret, statusErr) input, grpc-go v1.81.1 status.FromError finds the wrapped status via errors.As but sets the resulting .Message() to err.Error() (the FULL outer string incl. the secret) — so such an input leaks the outer text to Lua. LATENT today: all current call sites (pluginsvc.go:110,114; bindings_gen.go) pass RAW gRPC client/status errors, never fmt-wrapped, so no active leak. Guard test TestPushBridgeErrorFmtErrfWrappingLeaksFinding pins the current behavior so a grpc-go change is caught. Hardening options (defense-in-depth): (a) pushBridgeError uses status.Code(err) + a fixed opaque message for codes.Internal rather than passing .Message() through; or (b) a lint/convention forbidding fmt.Errorf-wrapping of status errors before pushBridgeError. Low priority — backstopped by hostcap servers already sanitizing to 'internal error' at their boundary.

title: Add wholesystem census negative arm: under-declaring fixture binary plugin must fail load (INV-PLUGIN-54)
description: Follow-up from PR #4436 review (finding holomush-5aq3t.6, spec §3.6). The census currently asserts the POSITIVE (correctly-declared plugins load). Add a NEGATIVE arm: a fixture binary plugin that consumes a host capability without declaring it MUST fail Init→load→census. Requires a small test-fixture plugin; defense-in-depth proving the guard actually rejects, not just accepts.

title: Add unit test: multi-*Aware provider with partial capability declaration (INV-PLUGIN-54)
description: Follow-up from PR #4436 review (finding holomush-5aq3t.4). Add a pkg/plugin/capability_declaration_test.go case for a provider implementing TWO non-exempt *Aware interfaces where ONE capability is undeclared — confirms validateDeclaredCapabilities flags the undeclared one across the multi-requirement loop. Low-risk (loop logic already handles it); strengthens coverage of the fail-closed gate.

title: host_capability_servers_test.go: broker registration test checks 9 services but omits WorldService, PropertyService, SessionService which are in proto but not registered
description:
internal/plugin/goplugin/host_capability_servers_test.go:17-37

TestHostBrokerServerServesFocusService verifies the 9 services registered on the broker (Focus, Emit, Eval, Settings, StreamHistory, StreamSubscription, Audit, CommandRegistry, KV) and asserts PluginHostService is gone. This is correct and complete for the services that ARE registered.

However, world.proto, property.proto, and session.proto exist in api/proto/holomush/plugin/host/v1/ (confirmed by ls output). If WorldService, PropertyService, or SessionService are intentionally NOT registered (they are 'deferred to sub-spec 3' like kvServer), the test should assert NotContains for them — symmetrically to the PluginHostService deletion assertion — so that a future accidental registration is caught at the test level.

This is a completeness gap, not a regression: the test does not falsely pass, but it also does not pin the intentional omission of these three services. Without the assertion, a PR that mistakenly starts registering WorldService would pass this test.

Severity: suggestion. The kvServer is already stub-registered (Unimplemented) and the test does check for KVService, so the pattern is set. The omission of the three new services is only notable because their .proto files exist in the same directory.

title: Plugin dependency model polish: typed UnsatisfiedDep.Reason, RequiresDisplay zero-Kind format, VERSION optional consistency, fail-fast CHANGELOG/upgrade-guide
description: Deferred suggestions from PR #4426 review (x28e9.2/.4/.8/.16): (.2) UnsatisfiedDep.Reason is a stringly-typed enum — consider a typed Reason kind for compile-time safety; (.4) Manifest.RequiresDisplay produces malformed ':name' for a zero-Kind Dependency (cosmetic; zero-Kind is now reported UNKNOWN_DEPENDENCY_KIND by the resolver); (.8) VERSION_UNSATISFIED is optional-gated while MISDECLARED is unconditional — document/confirm the intended optional semantics matrix; (.16) the fail-fast boot policy (INV-PLUGIN-43) is a behavioral breaking change for plugin authors with previously-tolerated phantom requires — add a CHANGELOG entry / upgrade guide. None block the foundation PR.
acceptance_criteria: Each sub-item addressed or explicitly closed with rationale.
notes: Octopus review (PR #4426, dependency.go versionSatisfies): add a diagnostic when semver parse of a version/constraint fails, to distinguish a malformed manifest version from a genuine constraint mismatch (currently both return false / fail-closed). Pure helper — no ctx/logger in scope; fold into this polish pass. Thread: PRRT_kwDOQ7tZvM6I6PQe.

title: Bind INV-PLUGIN-43 cycle sub-clause with a boot-level cycle test
description: Follow-up from holomush-oeb4d.7 (PR for epic oeb4d). INV-PLUGIN-43 ('unsatisfied non-optional dep OR a dependency cycle MUST fail the boot') is bound, but only its unsatisfied-required clause is genuinely asserted (manager_internal_test.go::TestResolveLoadOrderFailsFastOnUnsatisfiedRequired). The CYCLE sub-clause rests on the resolver's res.Cycles mechanism (dependency.go Kahn) with no boot-level assertion. Add a test feeding a 2-plugin mutual-service cycle through Manager.resolveLoadOrder asserting PLUGIN_DEPENDENCY_UNSATISFIED (or the cycle-specific code), annotate it // Verifies: INV-PLUGIN-43, and add it to 43's asserted_by. Standalone (not under oeb4d) so the foundation epic closes per its 7-task plan.
acceptance_criteria: A boot-path test asserts a dependency cycle fails resolveLoadOrder; // Verifies: INV-PLUGIN-43 added; meta-tests green.

title: world/grpc_server.go: 4 InvalidArgument returns %v a ULID parse error
description: internal/world/grpc_server.go GetLocation/etc (lines ~40/58/76/97) return status.Errorf(codes.InvalidArgument, "invalid location_id: %v", err) where err is a ulid.ParseStrict error on client input. Low risk (describes the client's malformed ID, not internals) but per .claude/rules/grpc-errors.md strict form, prefer a static message (e.g. status.Errorf(codes.InvalidArgument, "invalid location_id")). Surfaced by the post-#4421 codebase leak audit (only remaining non-Internal %v sites outside core-scenes/mcqs9).

title: Integration-tier PG fault-injection coverage for dek Store/CheckpointRepo + core-scenes DB-error branches
description: Follow-up from holomush-psr40. After adding unit-tier negative tests, combined coverage reached dek 76.6% and core-scenes 77.5% -- short of the 80% target. Independent review (psr40 reviewer) confirmed the residual gap is genuinely un-unit-testable: internal/eventbus/crypto/dek Store (store.go:68) and CheckpointRepo (checkpoint.go:174) wrap a concrete *pgxpool.Pool with NO interface seam, so their DB-error branches (store.go SelectAllParticipants/selectByBindingID, checkpoint.go ListFiltered, rekey-phase PG-error paths) need a real Postgres returning controlled errors. core-scenes has analogous DB-error branches in service.go/audit.go plus main.go/metrics.go bootstrap wiring (uncoverable). Reach 80% via EITHER (a) integration-tier Postgres fault injection (testcontainer + forced constraint/connection errors) for these branches, OR (b) introduce a narrow query-interface seam on Store/CheckpointRepo so DB errors are unit-mockable -- note (b) touches crypto-sensitive code and MUST go through crypto-reviewer. Crypto criticality: P3 but the fail-closed paths matter.
acceptance_criteria: dek and core-scenes combined coverage >=80%; the previously-enumerated Store/CheckpointRepo/service DB-error branches exercised with asserted fail-closed behavior; crypto-reviewer run if a production seam is added

title: policy CreateBatch emits no oops code on constraint failure
description: Found during holomush-ltv9j negative-path test review. internal/access/policy/store postgres.go:407: PolicyStore.CreateBatch wraps a DB constraint failure with oops.In("policy_store") but no .Code() — unlike Create (POLICY_CREATE_FAILED) and Update. Callers can't assert a specific code on batch-insert constraint violations; the negative-path test (negative_path_integration_test.go:135) is forced to bare Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred()). Add a named code (e.g. POLICY_CREATE_FAILED or POLICY_BATCH_CREATE_FAILED) so the failure is assertable and translatable at the boundary. Out of scope: behavior beyond the error wrapping.
acceptance_criteria: CreateBatch constraint-failure path wraps with a named oops code; the ltv9j negative-path test asserts that code via errutil.AssertErrorCode / oops.AsOops().Code()

title: page/whisper/pemit: sender echo is direct command output, recipient is an event — unify so both flow through the event stream
description:
DESIGN SMELL surfaced during holomush-5rh.8.29 (sensitive-event crypto go-live). core-communication's page/whisper/pemit emit the RECIPIENT's copy as a sensitive event on character.<recipientID> (encrypted, DEK-protected), but the SENDER's copy ('You paged Bob: …') is returned as the command's direct text `output` (plugins/core-communication/main.lua:275-277 ok_events(events, formatted_for_sender)). So the two halves of one private message travel different channels: recipient via encrypted event stream, sender via plaintext command response.

WHY THIS IS WORTH FIXING LATER: (1) Asymmetry — the sender's view bypasses the event/audit/crypto path entirely, so there is no sender-side record/history of sent pages, no multi-connection sync of your own outbox, and the sender's copy is never encrypted. (2) If page/whisper instead emitted the sender's copy as an outbound event to character.<senderID>, it would gain encryption + audit + history 'for free' AND the holomush-5rh.8.29 genesis-seeding rule (seed the owner of each character.<id> stream an event is emitted to) would AUTOMATICALLY seed the sender as a DEK participant on their own stream — no new seeding logic.

OUT OF SCOPE for .8.29 (recipient-only seeding is correct for current behavior; seeding the sender on the recipient's context would be a real over-grant letting them decrypt all of the recipient's private messages). This bead is the 'room to do the right thing later'.

ACCEPTANCE (when picked up): page/whisper/pemit emit a sender-copy event to character.<senderID> (or a shared conversation context) so the sender's view rides the encrypted event stream; verify the .8.29 genesis rule seeds the sender automatically; confirm no double-render on the sender's client.

title: E9.5 landing review follow-ups: stale client.ts comment + is_guest seed note
description:
Two Low/non-blocking findings from the branch-level reviewers on the E9.5 web-portal-scenes landing PR (epic holomush-5rh.8):

1. web/src/lib/scenes/client.ts:30 — stale self-referential comment says participants/observers are 'populated once .8.25 lands', but .8.25 (momtolrk) IS in the landing. Update to describe current behavior. (code-reviewer)

2. internal/access/policy/attribute/player.go is_guest attribute (.8.13) is plumbed end-to-end but no seed/plugin.yaml consumes it yet (rg is_guest in seed.go/plugin.yaml = 0 hits). Guest rejection is enforced in facade code (INV-SCENE-64). Forward-looking note for future seed authors: gate on has_is_guest before trusting is_guest (omit-don't-sentinel fail-safe). (abac-reviewer)

Out of scope: neither blocks the landing; both reviewers returned READY.

title: Stale Status headers on implemented specs mislead readers/analyzers — refresh with completion pointers
description:
Goal: Make spec Status headers trustworthy so readers (human or automated) can tell shipped designs from live drafts without re-grounding in code.

Symptom (observed 2026-06-07, main@c9506394c): an external codebase analysis read design-doc prose at face value and reported four already-resolved issues as live concerns: (1) "dead actor-metadata injection" from 2026-04-25-plugin-actor-claim-authentication-design.md §1.1 (Status: DRAFT, but G7 shipped — actor stamped pre-Deliver at internal/plugin/subscriber.go:117 and internal/command/dispatcher.go:326; bead ec22.1 CLOSED); (2) "LegacyID elimination in progress" from 2026-05-04-legacy-id-elimination-design.md (Status: READY, but elimination complete + guarded by INV-PLUGIN-15 meta-test internal/eventbus/no_legacy_id_grep_test.go); (3) flaky telnet quit test from 2026-04-18-session-lifecycle-as-events-design.md line 12 (Status: Draft; cited test file test/integration/telnet/e2e_test.go deleted entirely).

Root cause: spec Status fields are write-once. ~53 of 116 docs/superpowers/specs/ files (plus docs/specs/) carry a Status header, overwhelmingly stuck at Draft/DRAFT/READY regardless of implementation state. No workflow step flips them on completion.

Blast radius: docs-only — no functional impact, but recurring misdirection cost for any reader, reviewer, or automated analyzer grounding in spec prose.

Candidate fix directions (NOT decided here):
A. Targeted: update only the specs that demonstrably mislead (the 3 exemplars first), Status -> Implemented (date) with one-line pointer to landed state/guard tests. Low effort, leaves the class unsolved.
B. Class sweep: audit all Status-carrying specs against bd/code, flip shipped ones to Implemented with pointers. Needs per-spec grounding; could partition by epic.
C. Process fix: add a completion-workflow step (e.g. landing-the-plane checklist item or plan-to-beads epilogue) that flips the origin spec Status when its epic closes — prevents recurrence.
A+C or B+C are compatible.

Files touched (exemplars): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-plugin-actor-claim-authentication-design.md (header table), docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-04-legacy-id-elimination-design.md (## Status), docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-session-lifecycle-as-events-design.md (header + line 12 framing).

Out of scope: archiving/consolidating stale plan files — that is holomush-ec22.22.
acceptance_criteria: The 3 exemplar specs carry an accurate Status with a dated completion note and a pointer to the landed code or guard test; a decision is recorded (in this bead or a follow-up) on whether to do the class-wide sweep (B) and/or the process fix (C); task lint:docs / markdown lint green on touched files.

title: test/meta INV-SCENE-41 (TestFocusAdapterPairAssembledOnlyInHelper) scans sibling .claude/worktrees checkouts
description: focus_delta_gate_test.go:18 uses filepath.WalkDir(root, ...) from the repo root, which descends into .claude/worktrees/<sibling-workspace>/ checkouts present in the default workspace. INV-SCENE-41 (and likely sibling meta-tests using the same walk) then flags NewStreamSenderAdapter usages in OTHER branches' code (e.g. .claude/worktrees/practical-heyrovsky-8331e1/cmd/holomush/sub_grpc.go), failing task test / pr-prep locally in the default workspace even when the current branch is clean. CI (clean checkout, no .claude/worktrees) is unaffected. Fix: exclude .claude/worktrees/ (and any nested workspace checkout) from the meta-test filesystem walk, or root the walk at git-tracked files of the current workspace. Found while landing holomush-r0kup.

title: Meta-test: every in-tree sensitivity:always Lua plugin must claim sensitive=true at emit sites
description:
## Problem

holomush-50zqs fixed core-communication's page/whisper/pemit to claim sensitive=true, but the acceptance criterion 'no other sensitivity:always Lua plugin remains non-conformant' is currently verified MANUALLY (core-communication is the only Lua always-plugin today). A future Lua plugin declaring crypto.emits sensitivity:always could silently omit the per-event sensitive claim and, depending on its wire-type convention, either ship plaintext or fail-closed at emit — re-introducing the 50zqs class of bug.

## Suggested fix

Add a meta-test (precedent: internal/plugin/lua/plugins_emit_subject_key_test.go which walks all in-tree Lua plugins) that, for every in-tree Lua plugin manifest declaring a crypto.emits entry with sensitivity:always, asserts the corresponding emit site(s) in main.lua set sensitive=true. Source-scan is acceptable (matches existing precedent) or, better, drive each via the VM. Pairs with the binary-plugin equivalent if one is wanted (core-scenes already covered by commands_emit_test.go).

## Out of scope
- The wire-type convention question (holomush-aneim).
acceptance_criteria: A meta-test fails CI if any in-tree Lua plugin declares a sensitivity:always crypto.emits entry whose emit site does not claim sensitive=true.

title: Register INV-ACCESS for omit-optional-attrs and bind scene resolver location tests
description: Surfaced by PR #4394 review (holomush-6ffk1.2). The abac-providers.md omit-optional-attrs / has_X witness guarantee (ADR holomush-ti1b) is a cross-cutting fail-safe invariant but has no INV-ACCESS-N registry entry. Add the entry to docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, annotate the scene resolver location tests (plugins/core-scenes/resolver_test.go) + StreamProvider tests with // Verifies:, regenerate invariants.md. Minting an invariant is a deliberate design-time act per .claude/rules/invariants.md, out of scope for the fail-open code fix.

title: Re-bind INV-PRIVACY-6 (floor-preservation arm) + INV-PRIVACY-7 (custom history_scope) when test gaps close
description: Goal: both were reverted from bound to pending during the 2026-06-06 audit (false-green / partial). INV-PRIVACY-6: only the staff-override gate-bypass arm is tested; the floor-preservation arm (staff still bounded by their own LocationArrivedAt) needs the integrationtest harness to emit timestamp-controlled events across a session boundary — add that capability + the arm's test, then re-bind. INV-PRIVACY-7: the only test is a Skip() placeholder (no plugin declares history_scope: custom); when a custom-scope plugin lands, replace the Skip with a real assertion and re-bind. Files: test/integration/privacy/privacy_test.go, internal/testsupport/integrationtest/harness.go, docs/architecture/invariants.yaml. Out of scope: changing the invariant statements.

title: Audit cleanup: reclassify/remove registry entries that fail the invariant bar
description: Goal: the 2026-06-06 audit (against the .claude/rules/invariants.md bar + new in/out ruling) found entries that are not durable guarantees. Remove or reclassify: self-admitted non-invariants INV-CRYPTO-70 ('operational property, not a security invariant'), INV-PLUGIN-14 ('documentary; verified by spec review'), INV-CRYPTO-96 (phase-implementation note), INV-SESSION-4 (migration-completeness bookkeeping). Reword enforcement-conflated summaries (INV-EVENTBUS-19/20/21/22, INV-PLUGIN-15) to state the guarantee, not the test that enforces it. Audit the test-infrastructure-fidelity cluster (INV-ACCESS-1..4, INV-SCENE-51..55, INV-TELEMETRY-1..8, INV-SESSION-2) against the new in/out ruling: keep genuine test-fidelity guarantees (reworded to the property), drop epic-acceptance criteria ('no t.Fatalf', 'adds zero production code', 'E6 acceptance'). Files: docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, docs/architecture/invariants.md (regenerate). Out of scope: the binding backfill (separate beads).

title: Backfill INV-PLUGIN verification bindings (39 pending)
description: Goal: Backfill // Verifies: INV-<SCOPE>-N annotations for the verifying tests of the pending invariants in this scope, flipping each docs/architecture/invariants.yaml entry from binding: pending to bound with a populated asserted_by, then regenerate invariants.md (go run ./cmd/inv-render) and confirm test/meta/invariant_registry_test.go passes. Map each invariant to its verifying test via the scope's origin_spec + existing test suites. Where an invariant genuinely has NO verifying test, that is a real coverage gap — file a sub-bug rather than fabricating a binding. Sibling of holomush-hz0v4.11 (crypto slice); same ratchet-down established by the binding:pending decision (hz0v4.10). Out of scope: changing the meta-test (already tolerates pending), re-cataloging, adding new invariants. Scope: INV-PLUGIN (39 pending; 0 bound). Plan: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-invariant-registry-design.md. Files touched: docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, docs/architecture/invariants.md, internal/plugin/**/*_test.go, pkg/plugin/**/*_test.go.
