Where an invariant genuinely has NO verifying test, that is a real coverage gap — file a sub-bug rather than fabricating a binding. Sibling of holomush-hz0v4.11 (crypto slice); same ratchet-down established by the binding:pending decision (hz0v4.10). Out of scope: changing the meta-test (already tolerates pending), re-cataloging, adding new invariants. Scopes: INV-STORE(8), INV-TELEMETRY(8), INV-ACCESS(8), INV-SESSION(4), INV-COMMAND(3), INV-CLUSTER(2 residual), INV-PRESENCE(1 residual) — 34 pending total. The larger scopes (SCENE/PLUGIN/EVENTBUS) and CRYPTO have their own beads. Plan: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-invariant-registry-design.md. Files touched: docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, docs/architecture/invariants.md, per-scope *_test.go.

title: Backfill INV-SCENE verification bindings (60 pending)
description: Goal: Backfill // Verifies: INV-<SCOPE>-N annotations for the verifying tests of the pending invariants in this scope, flipping each docs/architecture/invariants.yaml entry from binding: pending to bound with a populated asserted_by, then regenerate invariants.md (go run ./cmd/inv-render) and confirm test/meta/invariant_registry_test.go passes. Map each invariant to its verifying test via the scope's origin_spec + existing test suites. Where an invariant genuinely has NO verifying test, that is a real coverage gap — file a sub-bug rather than fabricating a binding. Sibling of holomush-hz0v4.11 (crypto slice); same ratchet-down established by the binding:pending decision (hz0v4.10). Out of scope: changing the meta-test (already tolerates pending), re-cataloging, adding new invariants. Scope: INV-SCENE (60 pending; 0 bound). Plan: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-invariant-registry-design.md. Files touched: docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, docs/architecture/invariants.md, plugins/core-scenes/**/*_test.go, test/integration/{scene,privacy}/*_test.go.
notes: INV-SCENE-11 bound incidentally by holomush-8m01u (PR #4575, merged 2026-07-06): the re-enabled non-participant scene-write denial spec (test/integration/scenes/focus_routed_input_test.go, WithRealABAC) now carries // Verifies: INV-SCENE-11; registry flipped pending→bound. One fewer INV-SCENE to backfill under this epic — do not re-attempt.

title: Migrate INV-DOCS scope (docs-IA / docs-quality invariants)
description: Deferred future scope-migration, acknowledged at .14.17 epic close-out. INV-DOCS is status:pending with 0 entries; covers the docs-IA / docs-quality invariants in scripts/check-docs-ia.sh (INV-1/2/3/5/6/9), check-docs-quality.sh (INV-1/2/4/7) + their scripts/tests/*.bats. Migrate to INV-DOCS-1..N via closed-world inv-migrate when prioritized. Does NOT block .14. Note: these live in scripts/ (CI tooling) — confirm scope boundary vs the exempt CI-tooling local numbering before migrating.

title: Migrate INV-BRANDING scope (site brand-token invariants)
description: Deferred future scope-migration, acknowledged at .14.17 epic close-out. INV-BRANDING is status:pending with 0 entries; owns site/src/styles/custom.css carrying INV-1/3/5/6/7 brand-token invariants (origin .claude/rules/branding.md — amber-cursor-only, token-housing, glyph-outline, light/dark lockups, asset-integrity). Migrate to INV-BRANDING-1..N via closed-world inv-migrate when prioritized. Does NOT block .14 (the migration epic covered cross-cutting + crypto families; branding is a separate per-scope pass). Letter-prefixed/local web tokens stay exempt.

title: Flaky: session_store_integration_test.go context-deadline (10s ctx too tight for testcontainer under -race/parallel CI load)
description: Surfaced on unrelated PR #4384 (.14.25, cmd/inv-migrate-only) — Integration Test failed TWICE then passed on a 3rd (slower, 8m29s) re-run with 'Oops: context deadline exceeded' at internal/store/session_store_integration_test.go:36 (runMigrations(ctx,pool,46)) and :54 (s.Set). Cause: the test's 10s context.WithTimeout covers testcontainer Postgres readiness + 46 migrations + ops, and with t.Parallel() + -race under loaded Namespace runners that window is exceeded. Main is green on this test → environmental timing flake, not a regression. Fix: widen the per-test timeout (or derive it from a shared, more generous integration budget), and/or drop t.Parallel for container-heavy session-store cases; consider a readiness wait separate from the op-deadline. NOT quarantined. Second timing flake to red-herring a PR this week (cf. the invalidation rate-limiter flake bead). If CI integration flakiness persists, consider a broader pass on per-test deadlines under -race.

title: Backfill // Verifies: INV-CRYPTO-N bindings for pending crypto invariants
description: Goal: add real // Verifies: INV-CRYPTO-<N> annotations to the verifying tests for the ~43 crypto invariants currently marked binding: pending in docs/architecture/invariants.yaml, flipping each to binding: bound (or removing the pending marker) with a populated asserted_by, until zero crypto pending entries remain. Map each invariant to its verifying test using the crypto master spec's Section 2 verification paths + the spec's Phase 3a enforcing-test table + the pre-existing crypto test suite under test/integration/crypto/ and internal/eventbus/crypto/. Where an invariant genuinely has NO test, that is a real coverage gap — file a sub-bug rather than fabricating a binding. This is the crypto slice of the ratchet-down established by the binding:pending decision; later scopes (SCENE/PRIVACY/etc.) get their own backfill beads as they are cataloged. Out of scope: changing the meta-test (already tolerates pending), re-cataloging.
notes: Progress 2026-06-05: 6 INV-CRYPTO entries now bound (INV-CRYPTO-7,9,10,11,12 free-flips from existing // Verifies annotations; INV-CRYPTO-28 annotated at both clause sites — see PR closing holomush-0sh1k). 109 INV-CRYPTO entries remain binding: pending. Sibling per-scope backfill beads filed for SCENE/PLUGIN/EVENTBUS/long-tail.

title: Test/annotation: I-LIVE-5 single-source-of-liveness has only positive-path coverage
description: Backstop review of epic holomush-rsoe6: the I-LIVE-5 annotation (server_liveness_test.go) rides on TestRecomputeSessionLivenessDetachesOnZeroConnections, which proves recompute DOES the transition but not that it is the ONLY writer ('only sanctioned writers transition liveness state'). The invariant is structurally un-asserted; the meta-gate gives false confidence. Either add a 'no other writer' audit (e.g. assert RefreshConnection/AddConnection don't independently flip status/grid_present) OR scope-down the I-LIVE-5 annotation's claim in a comment. The DRY-by-construction comment at server.go:1603-1605 is the real guarantee today. Parent epic: holomush-rsoe6.

title: Web gateway: bound the per-stream 'seen' event-dedup set (slow leak)
description: Backstop review of epic holomush-rsoe6: internal/web/handler.go:~244 keeps a session-lifetime 'seen' map growing one entry per distinct forwarded event ID for the life of the stream — a slow leak bounded only by stream lifetime, already self-documented with a proposed recent-id-ring follow-up. Telnet uses a single lastEventID (gateway_handler.go:88) which suffices because core acks after Send (≤1 in-flight frame replays on reconnect). Replace web's unbounded set with a small bounded recent-id window using the same reasoning. Parent epic: holomush-rsoe6.

title: Polish: telnet ceiling wiring + 2 doc-comment precision fixes (rsoe6 review)
description: Non-blocking cosmetic findings from the epic holomush-rsoe6 pre-PR backstop review: (1) internal/telnet/gateway_handler.go:717 reconnect ceiling = LeaseRefreshInterval*8, but cmd/holomush/gateway.go builds telnet.Limits{} WITHOUT LeaseRefreshInterval, so BOTH the ceiling and refresh cadence run on fallback defaults in production (correct today via zero-guards, but fragile cross-commit coupling between .8 and .11). Populate LeaseRefreshInterval: telnet.DefaultLimits.LeaseRefreshInterval in gateway.go OR derive the ceiling from an explicit constant; add an I-SURV-5 parity note. (2) internal/grpc/server.go:~1600 doc says 'Disconnect, the lease sweep (Task 5), and AddConnection all call it [recomputeSessionLiveness]' — the sweep actually calls the sibling recomputeAfterSweep; tighten the call-graph wording. Parent epic: holomush-rsoe6.

title: Test gap: telnet I-SURV-4 ceiling-exceed exit (transport-symmetry vs web)
description: Backstop review of epic holomush-rsoe6 (pre-PR) found the telnet reconnect ceiling-exceed branch (internal/telnet/gateway_handler.go:758 — time.Now().After(deadline) → return nil → picker + best-effort Disconnect) has NO dedicated test, while the web equivalent is covered (internal/web/handler_test.go:1221 TestStreamEventsReconnectCeilingIsPerOutageNotStreamLifetime). The bijection meta-test's I-SURV-4 binding is web-only; this is the I-SURV-5 (transport symmetry) thin spot. Add a telnet test driving a permanently-failing resubscribe past the per-outage ceiling, asserting the client is released to the picker + Disconnect issued (not infinite spin). Parent epic: holomush-rsoe6.

title: Test gap: I-LIVE-3 grid_present=false with a live non-grid (comms_hub) connection
description: Backstop review of epic holomush-rsoe6 found recomputeSessionLiveness (internal/grpc/server.go:~1651-1660) sets grid_present=false when connections exist but none are terminal/telnet (e.g. comms_hub-only) — but every unit test uses ClientType=terminal, so the grid-conns-vs-total-conns split (the whole point of the branch) is never exercised. A regression collapsing gridConns back to totalCount would pass the suite. Add a server_liveness_test.go case: a session with only a non-grid connection asserts status=active, grid_present=false. Parent epic: holomush-rsoe6.

title: E2E: presence ghost-clear (kill-transport) + core-restart reconnect (rsoe6 T15 c/d follow-up)
description:
Deferred sub-specs from holomush-rsoe6 Task 15 (presence-liveness E2E). The landed bead .15 covers (a) two-browser drop/no-ghost and (b) reload-keeps-present-once. Descoped here:
(c) kill-transport -> ghost clears within lease+sweep: needs the E2E core/gateway to run with a SHORT session-lease-ttl + session-reaper-interval AND a configurable gateway heartbeat (currently hardcoded 15s at internal/web/handler.go). Shortening globally in compose.e2e.yaml risks sweeping live connections in other e2e specs; needs a scoped per-spec config or a gateway heartbeat flag + boot-grace coordination.
(d) restart-core-mid-session -> RECONNECTING then gap-free resume: Playwright runs against a compose-managed server with no per-test restart handle; needs a test that spawns its own holomush core child process (separate port, globalSetup DSN) to kill+respawn.
Note: restart-survival is already covered server-side by the Go integration boot-grace test (holomush-rsoe6.14). This bead is the E2E-tier complement. Parent epic: holomush-rsoe6 (kept standalone so the epic drain can complete; this is genuinely deferred infra work).

title: GetScene/EndScene leak inner error into gRPC status (grpc-errors.md)
description: Found during iokti.12 review. plugins/core-scenes/service.go GetScene (~line 370) returns status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "failed to get scene: %v", err) and EndScene (~line 439) does the same — the %v substitution drops the internal error text (store/query internals) onto the wire, violating .claude/rules/grpc-errors.md ('never use status.Errorf(codes.Internal, ...: %v, err) in a gRPC handler when err is internal'). Fix: log the inner error via slog.*Context and return a generic status.Error(codes.Internal, "internal error") — the pattern the new ListScenes handler (service.go:399-403) already uses correctly. Audit other core-scenes handlers for the same %v-into-Internal leak while here.

title: Update claude-code-hooks-design.md: file utilities are nudges, not blocks
description: The design plan docs/plans/2026-02-05-claude-code-hooks-design.md (around lines 75-85) still describes the enforce-task-runner.sh hook as blocking cat/head/tail/find/grep and suggests stale targets (e.g. 'Grep tool'). Pre-existing drift: grep was softened earlier, and PR #4349 softened cat/head/tail/find from hard blocks (exit 2) to nudges. Update the doc's behavior table to mark grep/cat/head/tail/find as nudges with the current suggested targets (rg / mcp__probe__search_code / Read / Glob). Authoritative rule .claude/rules/search-tools.md is already correct; this is the design-plan doc only. Surfaced by code-reviewer on PR #4349.

title: Composer command chip: tier-3 player-alias resolution (deferred from holomush-2zjio)
description: Goal: resolve per-player dynamic aliases (tier 3) in the composer command chip. holomush-2zjio shipped tiers 1+2 (system/manifest + sigil prefixes, static, fetched once per session) and left a resolution seam in web/src/lib/components/terminal/composerChip.ts. Tier 3 is dynamic (created/deleted at runtime per character) so a cached client map goes stale; the design's intended approach is a server-side per-input ResolveInput RPC behind the resolveComposerChip helper. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-recognized-command-chip-design.md (Non-goals). Out of scope: changing tiers 1+2 behavior.

title: Reject cross-gameID qualified stream subjects at read/subscribe entry (defense-in-depth)
description: Goal: harden the stream read/subscribe entry against client-supplied fully-qualified subjects carrying a FOREIGN gameID. Surfaced by the rops.5 abac-reviewer (commit uktm). eventbus.Qualify is idempotent on already-'events.'-prefixed input, so a client can submit events.<OTHERGAME>.location.<id> / events.<OTHERGAME>.character.<id>, bypassing the server's currentGameID() injection. MOOT today (single-game-per-server: currentGameID single-valued; per-instance gates compare info.CharacterID/LocationID and ignore the embedded gameID; the JetStream fetch filters the exact subject so a foreign-game subject returns zero rows). Becomes LOAD-BEARING under a future multi-game / shared-JetStream pivot, where same-ID collisions across games could permit a sibling-game read. Files: internal/grpc/query_stream_history.go (read entry, after eventbus.Qualify ~:110), internal/grpc/server.go (Subscribe filter qualify, post-rops.6). Out of scope: the multi-game architecture itself.
acceptance_criteria: Read entry + Subscribe filter qualify reject a qualified subject whose parts[1] != currentGameID() with codes.InvalidArgument; regression tests feed events.OTHERGAME.location.<not-mine> -> denied and events.OTHERGAME.character.<ownCharID> -> empty/denied; unit tests green.

title: Guard fetchCommandList against stale-session clobber in CommandInput
description: code-reviewer finding on holomush-2zjio.13. CommandInput.svelte's session $effect fires fetchCommandList(sessionId) fire-and-forget with no captured-guard, unlike the sibling history fetch which guards 'if (captured !== sessionId) return' on resolve/reject. If session A's WebListCommands RPC resolves AFTER session B's $effect fires, A's seedCommandList clobbers B's command list, so the composer chip resolves against the wrong character's commands until B re-triggers. Severity Low: cosmetic only (chip is a non-authoritative composer affordance; does NOT gate submission or authorization); window is one RPC round-trip; session changes are user-driven (character switch), not high-frequency. The plan (2026-05-29-recognized-command-chip.md:1530-1534) prescribed the unguarded call despite its 'mirroring the stale-session guard' comment — plan gap inherited by the code. Fix: give fetchCommandList a captured-guard — accept an isStale:()=>boolean predicate OR return the fetched sessionId and let the caller seed only when captured===sessionId (touches web/src/lib/stores/commandListStore.ts + CommandInput.svelte). Add a vitest covering the out-of-order-resolution race.

title: Add gitignore for web/.claude/agent-memory so reviewer reports don't get committed
description: Root .claude/agent-memory/ is gitignored (code-reviewer persisted reports stay untracked), but web/.claude/agent-memory/ has no equivalent ignore rule. During holomush-2zjio.12 a code-reviewer report written under web/.claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/reports/ was snapshotted by jj and swept into the feature commit; it had to be manually moved out. Fix: add web/.claude/agent-memory/ (or .claude/agent-memory/ globally) to the appropriate .gitignore so web-dir review artifacts are untracked like the root ones. Low priority hygiene.

title: Bring coreEventToProto under a reflection parity guard (5th host→plugin event marshal site)
description:
Goal: extend the holomush-av954 reflection-parity guard pattern to coreEventToProto (internal/plugin/goplugin/host_service.go:701), the host→plugin event marshal site on the binary streaming-delivery/history-readback path that maps core.Event → pluginv1.Event.

Background: holomush-av954 guarded the four pluginsdk boundary structs (Event/EmitEvent/EmitIntent/SessionStreamsRequest). coreEventToProto is structurally the same 'marshal a host event onto the plugin proto' shape but maps the internal core.Event type, so it fell outside av954's enumerated scope and carries no reflection guard. No present defect: core.Event (internal/core/event.go) has no Sensitive and no Cursor field, so coreEventToProto already carries everything the source struct holds — this is a future-hazard guard, not a bug fix.

Files: internal/plugin/goplugin/host_service.go (coreEventToProto ~701, called ~494). Reference pattern: pkg/plugin/event_marshal.go + its round-trip guards.

Out of scope: the four structs already guarded in holomush-av954.
acceptance_criteria: coreEventToProto has a reflection-based exhaustiveness guard (round-trip or one-directional presence) that fails the build when a core.Event field is added without wiring it into the proto mapping, demonstrated by a temporary unwired field. Verify: task test -- ./internal/plugin/goplugin/...

title: Surface build version in UI + command palette + in-game command
description:
Goal: let players/operators see what build is running — closes the deployment-lag blind spot that made the 2026-05-29 scene-join triage slow (prod was pre-#4302; a visible version would have shown it instantly). Three surfaces requested by user:
  1. Web UI: a visible version (header help/about area — which is currently MISSING up top per user) — render the build version.
  2. Command-palette (⌘K) entry to show version (palette exists in SPA per terminal screenshot).
  3. (Perhaps) an in-game `version` command (telnet + web text command) for parity.

Existing plumbing (grounded):
  - Version is ldflag-stamped into the binary (.goreleaser.yaml: -X main.version).
  - Already exposed UNAUTHENTICATED via AdminService.Status -> StatusResponse{string version=1} (api/proto/holomush/admin/v1/admin.proto:113-114; doc comment explicitly: "Clients may render a [version]").
  - SvelteKit client already has generated stub (web/src/lib/connect/holomush/admin/v1/admin_connect.ts).
  - hostVersion is threaded into core.BootstrapVerbRegistry (internal/core/builtins.go:20) but only stamps host-<ver> on event registrations — no version verb/command exists.
  - No existing version/about command; `help` command exists.

So web display + palette = frontend-only (call AdminService.Status, render). In-game `version` command = new core/command-handler work; should share AdminService.Status as the single source rather than re-plumbing.

Candidate design questions for brainstorming (NOT decided here):
  - Does "about" become a new web surface (panel/modal) since help/about is missing, or just a status chip? Scope of about beyond version (commit/date/links)?
  - Do all three surfaces read AdminService.Status (single source), and does in-game `version` go through the same RPC or read host version directly server-side?
  - in-game command: host builtin vs a plugin command; capabilities.

Out of scope: the Sentry release-publishing CI work (holomush-da0r6, separate PR).
acceptance_criteria: Brainstorm -> spec -> plan before implementing (multi-surface feature). Acceptance to be refined in spec; minimally: build version visible in web UI, reachable via ⌘K palette, and (if in-scope) an in-game `version` command — all sourced from the build-stamped version, with tests + E2E for the web surface.

title: Audience-based IA restructure of site contributing/ docs (maintainer vs contributor)
description:
## Goal
Restructure the site contributing/ section so maintainer-workflow docs (jj, beads, pr-prep, sessions) are cleanly separated from contributor-facing docs throughout, rather than relying on the per-page audience banners added as interim mitigation.

## Background
Surfaced during holomush-ec22.17 brainstorm (2026-05-29). The CONTRIBUTING.md router (PR #4319) routes external contributors to published holomush.dev/contributing/ pages, but pr-prep.md (6 jj/beads refs) and sessions.md (25 refs) are maintainer-flavored. PR #4319 added :::note audience banners as a stopgap; this bead is the real IA fix.

## Reference
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-contributor-onboarding-surface-design.md §8.

## Out of scope
The onboarding entry points (README, CONTRIBUTING, CoC, .github templates) — done in ec22.17/ec22.21.

title: core-scenes: trim over-explained ULID-ordering comment in ReadSceneLogForSnapshot test
core-scenes: explicit id-ordering assertion in ReadSceneLogForSnapshot test
description:
Deferred suggestion from PR #4312 review (was holomush-n0e3x.1).

Goal: The ReadSceneLogForSnapshot integration test (plugins/core-scenes/store_integration_test.go:2406-2413) generates deterministic ULIDs via id.SetTime(seq) with zero entropy so ORDER BY id ASC is stable. The test infers chronological ordering only indirectly through the type-sequence assertion (logs[0..2].Type == pose/say/emit). Optionally add an explicit assertion that the three returned LogRow ids are strictly increasing (e.g. bytes.Compare on id bytes) to pin the ordering contract directly rather than via the type proxy.

Low value: the type sequence already fails if ordering breaks, and ids are not currently exposed on LogRow (would require exposing the id field).

Out of scope: changing production code.

title: Pin protoc-gen-doc version in Taskfile tools install (currently @latest)
description: docs:proto generation depends on protoc-gen-doc, installed via 'go install github.com/pseudomuto/protoc-gen-doc/cmd/protoc-gen-doc@latest' (Taskfile.yaml:978). @latest is non-reproducible: a future protoc-gen-doc release can change grpc-api.md output and spuriously break the SP4 coverage meta-test or produce noisy regen diffs. Pin to a specific tag. Pre-existing (predates SP4 okm59); surfaced by code-reviewer on the SP4 PR. Out of scope for okm59 itself.

title: docs(guide): rewrite building.md as a goal->steps->verify how-to once in-game building commands ship
description:
## Goal
guide/how-to/building.md scored P1 (9/16) in the SP5 audit for Mode mismatch — it's a how-to with no actual steps. The root cause is that in-game building commands do NOT exist yet (no handlers in internal/command/handlers; the page carries an 'Early Development' caution). During holomush-ivwij.9 the page was honestly improved (orientation, audience, cross-links, conceptual world-model retained) but the real how-to steps were NOT fabricated.

When the building-commands feature lands, rewrite building.md as a proper Diataxis how-to: goal -> prerequisites -> concrete command steps (dig/link/describe or whatever ships) -> a worked example -> verify. Move the conceptual world-model material to the world explanation page if it becomes redundant.

## Files
site/src/content/docs/guide/how-to/building.md

## Out of scope / blocked on
The in-game building-commands feature itself. Do NOT document commands until they exist.

Surfaced during holomush-ivwij.9 (Guide editorial pass).

title: docs(reference/events): make the generated events.md index orienting (generator change)
description:
## Goal
The generated reference/events.md scored P1 (8/16) in the SP5 audit: it's a stub index (three list items, no prose, no orientation, no links to explanation pages, no examples). It is auto-generated by scripts/gen-event-docs.sh, so it was OUT OF SCOPE for the SP5 editorial pass (holomush-ivwij.13, hand-written reference only). Improving it means enhancing the generator to emit an orientation paragraph + cross-links (to extending/reference/events conceptual page + event-sensitivity), or adding a hand-written intro the generator preserves.

## Files
scripts/gen-event-docs.sh (generator) and/or its output site/src/content/docs/reference/events.md

## Out of scope
Hand-editing the generated output directly (it would be overwritten). This is generator/SP4 work.

Surfaced during holomush-ivwij.13 (Reference editorial pass).

title: docs(reference/grpc-api): add orientation + api-guide link to generated grpc-api.md (generator/SP0-SP4)
description:
## Goal
The generated reference/grpc-api.md scored P2 (10/16) in the SP5 audit: its opener is a bare table of contents with no orientation sentence and no link back to extending/reference/api-guide. It is auto-generated (proto -> docs), so OUT OF SCOPE for the SP5 hand-written editorial pass (holomush-ivwij.13). Improving it means the proto-doc generator emitting an orientation + cross-link (relates to SP0 proto comments / SP4 gRPC coverage).

## Files
The gRPC-doc generator (docs:proto task / proto comments) and/or its output site/src/content/docs/reference/grpc-api.md

## Out of scope
Hand-editing the generated output directly. This is generator / SP0-SP4 work.

Surfaced during holomush-ivwij.13 (Reference editorial pass).

title: docs(extending): add grounded client-connection walkthrough to api-guide.md
description:
## Goal
Add a runnable client-connection walkthrough to site/src/content/docs/extending/reference/api-guide.md. During the SP5 editorial pass (holomush-ivwij.11) the page was reframed as 'Core gRPC API Reference' and a fabricated 'Supported commands' list was removed; a real runnable client example was intentionally NOT invented. Pull a grounded example from an integration test or the gateway client code (e.g. internal/grpc, internal/web gateway, or test/integration) so it stays accurate.

## Files
site/src/content/docs/extending/reference/api-guide.md

## Out of scope
Inventing API calls — the example MUST be grounded in real client/gateway/test code.

Surfaced during holomush-ivwij.11 (Extending editorial pass).

title: obs(sentry): wire CaptureException -> grouped Sentry Issues + broaden error capture
description:
## Goal
Wire the Sentry error-event capture path so ERROR-level events become grouped, deduplicated, alertable Sentry Issues (not just Logs-stream entries). These follow-ups were previously tracked informally in operating/how-to/sentry.md and were removed from operator-facing docs during the SP5 editorial pass (holomush-ivwij.10); capturing them here so they are not lost (holomush-0wun, the Sentry trial epic, is closed).

## Scope
- CaptureException -> grouped Issues via a pkg/errutil wrapper (per open decision holomush-1wbzn).
- Connect server interceptor capturing non-OK responses as Issues.
- Plugin runner panic capture.
- Optional Session Replay / RUM browser features (opt-in).

## Reference
Decision: holomush-1wbzn (confine the sentry-go CaptureException touchpoint to pkg/errutil).

Surfaced during holomush-ivwij.10 (Operating editorial pass).

title: docs(operating/crypto): write master-key bootstrap runbook in crypto-setup once Phase 8 lands
description:
## Goal
Write the master-key bootstrap runbook section in site/src/content/docs/operating/how-to/crypto/crypto-setup.md. During the SP5 editorial pass (holomush-ivwij.10) the fabricated/stub framing was made honest ('Not yet available'); the actual runbook content is deferred until the Phase 8 master-key bootstrap feature lands.

## Files
site/src/content/docs/operating/how-to/crypto/crypto-setup.md

## Out of scope
The Phase 8 feature implementation itself (this is docs-only, blocks on that feature).

Surfaced during holomush-ivwij.10 (Operating editorial pass).

title: Go comment on decodePolicyHashOrEmpty inaccurately cites 'genesis' empty case
description: The Go doc-comment on decodePolicyHashOrEmpty at internal/admin/readstream/handler.go:749-751 says it returns nil 'when the policy hash is empty (genesis) or malformed'. The '(genesis)' parenthetical is inaccurate: Config.Validate() (handler.go:135-136) rejects an empty PolicyHash with READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID, so the empty case is unreachable at runtime — only the malformed-decode guard applies. The proto comment that copied this framing was fixed in SP0 epic holomush-300ad (commit wk). Fix: drop '(genesis)' from the Go comment.

title: hostfunc-audit-table.md drifts from RegisteredFunctionsForAudit() — auto-sync or remove
description:
The hand-maintained reference table in site/src/content/docs/contributing/reference/hostfunc-audit-table.md must be manually kept in sync with internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions.go::RegisteredFunctionsForAudit(). It silently drifted: PR #4297 review found 3 registered functions (holomush.evaluate, holomush.decrypt_own_audit_rows, holomush.register_emit_type) missing from the table, and the existing context_audit meta-test only asserts code-internal coverage, not table↔doc parity, so CI never caught it.

Goal: eliminate the manual-sync burden. Decide between:
(A) Generate the table (or a parity meta-test) from RegisteredFunctionsForAudit() so the doc cannot drift — e.g. emit the rows at docs:build time, or add a meta-test asserting every registered fn appears in the table.
(B) Remove the per-function table and replace with a pointer to the code as the single source of truth, keeping only the invariant explanation.

Out of scope: the per-function classifications themselves (fixed in #4297).
Files: site/src/content/docs/contributing/reference/hostfunc-audit-table.md; internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions.go; internal/plugin/hostfunc/context_audit_test.go.

title: Wire publish/withdraw_publish per-action ABAC at the command leaf (or document inert policies)
description: Follow-up from holomush-5rh.20.44 (Phase 6 rebase + command-gating re-integration). Both abac-reviewer and code-reviewer flagged (Low, non-blocking) that the start-publish-as-participant (action 'publish') and withdraw-publish-as-owner (action 'withdraw_publish') policies in plugins/core-scenes/plugin.yaml are INERT at the command-dispatch path: the publish/log/vote/withdraw subcommands route directly (not via gated()) because they resolve scene via single-membership inference, which pkg/plugin GatedSubcommand.ResourceRef func(args string) cannot express (no ctx/characterID). Effective enforcement is the in-handler IsParticipant/owner predicate-mirroring gates (adjudicated CORRECT for B4) + service-layer gates — no fail-open. Resolution options: (a) extend GatedSubcommand with a ctx-aware ResourceRef so per-action policies are enforced at dispatch (follows handleEmit's resolve-then-Evaluate precedent), or (b) add a one-line comment on the two policies in plugin.yaml documenting they are enforced in-handler. Reviewer reports: .claude/agent-memory/abac-reviewer/reports/2026-05-25-2239-5rh-20-44.md and .claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/reports/2026-05-25-2239-holomush-5rh-20-44.md. Out of scope: any change to the closed Phase 6 handlers' gating behavior.

title: SDK DecryptOwnAuditRows helper panics on nil PluginHostServiceClient
description: pkg/plugin/audit.go DecryptOwnAuditRows SDK helper panics on a nil PluginHostServiceClient; early-return a PLUGIN_HOST_CLIENT_REQUIRED coded error instead. Public SDK entry point. Re-filed from holomush-m7pxs.18.

title: Ginkgo AfterEach nil-env teardown can mask setup failure (scene_history_readback_test)
description: test/integration/privacy/scene_history_readback_test.go AfterEach calls env.teardown() and cancel() unconditionally; nil-guard both so an early BeforeEach abort does not nil-panic and mask the original failure. Re-filed from holomush-m7pxs.19.

title: Lua decrypt_own_audit_rows flattens decrypted-to-empty plaintext to nil
description: internal/plugin/hostfunc/stdlib_audit.go renders an empty-but-decrypted RowResult plaintext as Lua nil, identical to a refusal, so the RowResult oneof decrypted-to-empty distinction does not reach Lua consumers. Fix: type-switch on res.GetOutcome() instead of len(GetPlaintext()). Re-filed from holomush-m7pxs.17. Unreachable in practice (audit payloads are ciphertext); low priority.

title: commit-lint PR-title check doesn't re-run on title edit (missing 'edited' trigger)
description:
Goal: make the Conventional Commit (PR title) check re-run when a PR title is edited.

Problem: .github/workflows/commit-lint.yaml uses `on: pull_request: branches:[main]` with NO `types:`. GitHub defaults to opened/synchronize/reopened — `edited` is absent. So when a PR title fails cog verify and the author FIXES the title, the check does NOT re-run; the stale failure stays pinned and blocks merge. `gh run rerun` does not help (it replays the frozen event payload with the OLD title). Current workaround: close + reopen the PR (fires reopened) — but that re-runs ALL default-triggered CI (E2E, integration), which is wasteful.

Discovered on PR #4265 (holomush-1eps2 epic): worker chose a non-conventional title; fixing it did not clear the check until a close+reopen.

Fix: add `types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, edited]` to commit-lint.yaml on: pull_request. Then a title edit alone re-runs the lightweight (~5s) check.

Files: .github/workflows/commit-lint.yaml.
acceptance_criteria: commit-lint.yaml pull_request trigger includes edited (and synchronize/reopened/opened); editing a PR title alone re-runs the Conventional Commit check without a close+reopen or new commit.

title: INV-TS lint guards fail-open when rg is missing/errors
description:
The INV-TS-1/2/3 lint guards in Taskfile.yaml use the shape:

  if rg -n '<pattern>' --type=go {{.ROOT_DIR}} | rg -v 'pgnanos-exempt:' ; then
    echo FAIL ...; exit 1
  fi

If rg is absent from PATH or errors (e.g. the unit-test job that shells out to these tasks via internal/store/lint_meta_test.go has no rg installed), the first rg fails inside the if-condition. set -euo pipefail does NOT abort on a failed if-condition, so the pipeline is simply falsy, the FAIL branch is skipped, and the guard exits 0 — a fail-OPEN no-op that masks real violations. Observed on PR #4264 CI: 'rg: executable file not found in $PATH' → guard passed vacuously.

FIX options: (a) precondition check 'command -v rg >/dev/null || { echo 'rg missing'; exit 1; }' at the top of each guard; (b) restructure to capture rg output and distinguish 'no match' (exit 1) from 'execution error' (exit 2/127) — rg exits 2 on error, 1 on no-match, 0 on match. Treat exit>=2 as guard failure.

Scope: lint:no-timestamptz (INV-TS-1), lint:no-unixnano-in-repos (INV-TS-2), lint:no-microsecond-truncate (INV-TS-3). Discovered while fixing holomush-vpvu5.

title: Signal-hygiene: expected/benign conditions should not masquerade as ERROR/WARN-with-stacktrace (design seed)
description:
SEED ONLY — this epic captures the NEED for a design pass, not the design itself. Next step is a dev-flow:brainstorming session that produces a spec; this bead is the entry point and context anchor. Do NOT start implementation against this epic directly.

## The need

Across a single dev boot on tip of main (2026-05-24) three independent findings shared one root smell: EXPECTED, benign conditions are surfaced as ERROR — or as WARN carrying a full multi-frame oops stacktrace. The net effect is eroded log signal, alarming-but-meaningless dev-boot logs, and false error spans in OTel/Sentry that can drive bogus alerts. We keep fixing these one at a time; the recurrence says we lack a shared POSTURE for log severity and error shape.

## Evidence (grounded, all observed 2026-05-24, tip of main)

- holomush-oeb4d (P1) — plugin loader logs WARN "DAG dependency resolution failed, falling back to priority sort" with an 8-frame oops stacktrace on EVERY boot, for an unsatisfiable (phantom) requires. (Functional bug underneath; the stacktrace-at-WARN is the signal-hygiene facet.)
- holomush-iw804 (P3) — admin KEK-unavailable logs WARN + 8-frame oops stacktrace on every dev boot, for an expected missing OPTIONAL env var (intentional fail-open).
- holomush-4g0gt (P3) — web unary RPC handlers log client context.Canceled at ERROR + oops stacktrace + errored OTel span, for an expected client disconnect (navigate-away mid history-load).
- Precedent already recognized: holomush-p0wc (P3) — Subscribe returns SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELLED instead of nil on clean context.Canceled (core/server side).

## The smell, stated plainly

1. oops errors ALWAYS capture a stacktrace; using them for expected sentinel conditions (missing-optional-config, unsatisfiable-but-tolerated, client-cancel) then logging the wrapped error means a stack rides along on a non-event.
2. context.Canceled from a client is treated as a server error in some paths but correctly downgraded in others (e.g. internal/web/handler.go streaming loop at 264/298/316 classifies it; the unary handlers do not). Inconsistency, not just noise.

## Questions for the brainstorm to resolve (NOT decided here — framings, not directives)

- A severity rubric: when is a condition ERROR vs WARN vs INFO vs DEBUG vs not-logged at all?
- When should an error carry a stacktrace (oops.Code/Wrap) vs be a plain sentinel / classified-and-swallowed?
- A SHARED classifier for "expected/benign" causes (context.Canceled / connect.CodeCanceled, missing-optional-config, anticipated graceful degradation) usable by BOTH the host (cmd/holomush, internal/plugin) and the gateway (internal/web, internal/grpc).
- Guardrail to prevent regression: a convention, a helper, or a lint that flags "logging an oops error at WARN for an expected branch" / "ERROR on context.Canceled".
- Scope boundary: which existing fail-open/WARN sites in cmd/holomush/core.go (KEK, crypto-operator lax+warn, dual-control, invalidation) are legitimately WARN vs over-logged.

## Explicitly out of scope for THIS seed

- The solution design (that is the brainstorm + spec).
- The individual remediations — those are the linked child beads (oeb4d / iw804 / 4g0gt / p0wc), which can land independently of this epic.
- Promotion to a theme:* label — single epic / ~4 beads today; revisit if it grows to a 5+ cluster or a 2nd epic.
acceptance_criteria:
A brainstorming session has run and produced a spec defining the log-severity + error-shape posture (severity rubric, stacktrace-vs-sentinel guidance, shared cancellation/expected classifier, regression guardrail).
The linked remediation beads are reconciled against the posture (kept as-is, re-scoped, or superseded).
Decision on whether a lint/convention guardrail is warranted is recorded (ADR-worthy if it shapes a codebase-wide rule).

title: Web unary RPC handlers log benign client cancellation (context.Canceled) at ERROR; streaming path already downgrades
description:
Goal: stop the web gateway unary RPC handlers from emitting ERROR logs (+ full oops stacktraces + errored OTel spans) when the browser client cancels a request (navigates away / closes tab / aborts fetch).

Symptom (tip of main, service=holomush-gateway, version=dev, 2026-05-24):
  ERROR "web: query stream history RPC failed"
  error.code=RPC_FAILED  error.err="rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"  method=QueryStreamHistory
  error.stacktrace=<8 frames: internal/grpc/client.go:251 Client.QueryStreamHistory -> internal/web/handler.go:431 WebQueryStreamHistory -> connect -> cookie.go:85 -> security_headers.go:71 -> otelhttp>
  (trace_id + span_id present -> this surfaces as an ERRORED span in traces/Sentry)

Why it is benign: the handler wraps ctx with context.WithTimeout(ctx, rpcTimeout=10s) at handler.go:27/428. A timeout would surface as DeadlineExceeded, NOT Canceled. The observed code=Canceled therefore comes from the PARENT (HTTP request) context being canceled = client disconnect. That is expected whenever a user navigates away mid history-load / backfill pagination (cf. the +page.svelte snapshot-apply-after-backfill race noted in holomush-eawl).

Root cause: internal/web/handler.go:431 WebQueryStreamHistory calls slog.ErrorContext on ANY error from h.client.QueryStreamHistory, including clean client context.Canceled. internal/grpc/client.go:248-253 wraps every RPC error in oops.Code("RPC_FAILED") (which captures the stacktrace), so the benign cancellation reaches the logger with a full stack.

Scope is a CLASS, not one site: the same unconditional ERROR-on-any-error shape exists in the sibling UNARY handlers:
  - WebQueryStreamHistory  (handler.go:440)
  - ListSessionStreams     (handler.go:483)
  - ListFocusPresence      (handler.go:510)
  - GetCommandHistory      (handler.go:376)
  - HandleCommand          (handler.go:132)
  - Disconnect             (handler.go:349)
All would log a client cancellation at ERROR.

Established pattern to mirror: the STREAMING Subscribe loop in the SAME file ALREADY classifies cancellation — handler.go:264 / 298 / 316 use errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || ... and treat it as clean. The unary handlers were never given the same treatment. Fix = extend that classification (ideally a shared helper) to the unary handlers: on clean context.Canceled / connect.CodeCanceled, log at Debug (or skip) and do not mark the span errored; reserve ERROR for genuine failures.

Files touched (candidates):
  - internal/web/handler.go (the 6 unary log sites; reuse the cancel check already at 264/298/316 — extract a shared helper)
  - internal/grpc/client.go:248-253 (consider NOT wrapping context.Canceled in RPC_FAILED so callers can classify it without unwrapping an oops stack)

Out of scope: holomush-p0wc covers the core/server Subscribe RPC returning nil on clean cancel — same class, different layer.
acceptance_criteria:
A browser client canceling a unary web RPC (e.g. navigate away mid QueryStreamHistory) produces NO ERROR log and NO errored OTel span — it logs at Debug/Info (or not at all).
Genuine failures (DeadlineExceeded, STREAM_ACCESS_DENIED, INVALID_ARGUMENT, internal errors) still log at ERROR with full context.
Cancellation classification is shared/consistent between the streaming and unary paths in handler.go (no duplicated inline checks).
task test -- ./internal/web/... passes.

title: Missing KEK env vars logs full oops stacktrace at WARN every dev boot; message lacks setup-doc reference
description:
Goal: stop the KEK-unavailable WARN from dumping a full multi-frame stacktrace on every dev boot for an EXPECTED condition, and make the message actionable by pointing operators at a setup doc.

Symptom (tip of main, version=dev, 2026-05-24): every dev boot (no HOLOMUSH_KEK_FILE set) emits
  WARN "admin handlers: KEK provider unavailable — TOTP-gated admin RPCs will fail at runtime"
  error.code=ADMIN_KEK_FILE_MISSING
  error.stacktrace=<8 frames: cmd_admin_totp_deps.go:107 buildKEKProviderFromConfig -> core.go:689 -> cobra -> main.go:37>

This is NOT a functional bug — it is intentional fail-open behavior (cmd/holomush/core.go:684-693: "On first boot without KEK env vars... the server continues to start"). task dev sets no KEK env vars, so the WARN fires every dev boot by design. This bead is purely about (a) log hygiene and (b) actionability.

Root cause: buildKEKProviderFromConfig (cmd/holomush/cmd_admin_totp_deps.go:102-108) returns a stacktrace-bearing oops.Code("ADMIN_KEK_FILE_MISSING") for the trivial "optional env var unset" case; core.go:691 logs that whole error at WARN. A captured stacktrace signals an unexpected fault — but a missing optional env var in dev is expected and benign. The stack erodes log signal (same class as the core-aliases phantom-requires WARN).

Two parts:
  (a) Hygiene: for the expected "env var unset" sentinel, log a concise stack-free message naming the missing vars. RESERVE full stack/diagnostic context for the genuine-failure codes ADMIN_KEK_FILE_SOURCE_FAILED / ADMIN_KEK_PROVIDER_FAILED / ADMIN_KEK_PASSPHRASE_MISSING (those SHOULD keep diagnostics).
  (b) Actionability: the message should reference the operator setup doc + how to enable admin TOTP. NOTE: site/docs/operating/crypto-setup.md currently documents ONLY the crypto.operator allow-list — HOLOMUSH_KEK_FILE / HOLOMUSH_KEK_PASSPHRASE are UNDOCUMENTED operator-facing. So this includes adding a KEK-setup section to crypto-setup.md (env vars + how to generate the key file) and pointing the WARN at it.

Files touched (candidates):
  - cmd/holomush/cmd_admin_totp_deps.go:102-108 (sentinel vs failure error shaping)
  - cmd/holomush/core.go:684-693 (the WARN log call + doc reference)
  - site/docs/operating/crypto-setup.md (new KEK-setup section to link)

Out of scope: whether production should FAIL-CLOSED when KEK is absent (separate design bead).
acceptance_criteria:
Dev boot without KEK env vars emits a single concise WARN (no multi-frame stacktrace) that names the missing env vars AND links the operator setup doc/section.
Genuine KEK construction failures (ADMIN_KEK_FILE_SOURCE_FAILED / ADMIN_KEK_PROVIDER_FAILED / ADMIN_KEK_PASSPHRASE_MISSING) still log with full diagnostic context.
site/docs/operating/crypto-setup.md documents HOLOMUSH_KEK_FILE + HOLOMUSH_KEK_PASSPHRASE and how to generate the key file.
task test and docs lint pass.

title: Capture attach_moment_ms inside Subscriber.OpenSession at actual consumer-attach commit (iu8j hygiene)
description:
Per CodeRabbit review on PR #4234 (https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/4234#discussion_r2566...), the attach-moment timestamp is currently captured in internal/grpc/server.go via time.Now().UTC().UnixMilli() AFTER subscriber.OpenSession returns. This introduces a small window between (consumer attached server-side inside OpenSession) and (the post-OpenSession time.Now() call) where an event can be:
- delivered live via the now-wired durable consumer
- AND fall under the captured attach_moment, satisfying backfill's not_after_ms filter

The duplicate-path consequence is handled correctly by the client-side dedup layer (backfill-wins, dimmed): events arriving via both paths render dimmed. This is functionally correct for events published in that window because they happened pre-attach from a strict-chronology view; the user perceives them as historical scrollback. User-issued commands cannot land in this window because the user can't dispatch commands until REPLAY_COMPLETE arrives, which is sent AFTER the post-OpenSession attach_moment capture.

So the race surface is theoretical (no observable wrong UX), but eliminating it is a hygiene improvement that prevents future regressions when the dedup story changes.

Recommended approach: change the eventbus.Subscriber interface to return (SessionStream, attachMomentMs int64, error), and stamp attachMomentMs inside JetStreamSubscriber.OpenSession at the exact CreateOrUpdateConsumer commit point in internal/eventbus/subscriber.go.

Scope:
1. Update eventbus.Subscriber interface (1 file)
2. Update JetStreamSubscriber.OpenSession impl (1 file)
3. Update all fake/stub Subscribers in tests (likely 5-10 files based on stubSubscriber + fakeBus + mock_Subscriber if mockery-generated)
4. Update the grpc.Subscribe call site to use the returned attachMomentMs directly (drop the time.Now() capture)
5. Verify integration tests still pass

Acceptance:
- Subscriber interface change ships with full test coverage (no behavior change in OpenSession's external contract beyond returning the timestamp)
- internal/grpc/server.go no longer captures time.Now() for attach_moment — uses the value returned by OpenSession
- Existing iu8j integration tests pass without modification
- A new boundary test asserting attach_moment_ms <= time.Now()-at-OpenSession-call-time (i.e. the stamped moment is bounded above by the call site's wall clock)

title: license:check (addlicense) scans local scripts/.venv — add .venv to -ignore
description:
**Goal:** task license:check (Taskfile license:run, addlicense) scans scripts/ and chokes on a local python virtualenv at scripts/.venv (thousands of third-party files without SPDX headers) → license:check exit 1, failing pr-prep LOCALLY for anyone who has created scripts/.venv (e.g. by running python tooling under scripts/). CI is unaffected (clean checkout, no .venv).

**Fix:** add `-ignore '**/.venv/**'` (and likely `-ignore '**/__pycache__/**'`) to the addlicense invocation in Taskfile.yaml license:run (currently ignores **/*.pb.go, vendor/**, internal/web/dist/**). Verify `task license:check` passes with a scripts/.venv present.

**Files touched:** Taskfile.yaml (license:run).

**Found during:** log-review-fixes pr-prep (epic holomush-hryj); pre-existing local-tooling gap, unrelated to that batch.

## Required Skills
jj:jujutsu

title: Add favicon.ico to web client (currently 404s)
description:
**Goal:** The SvelteKit web client ships no favicon, so every page load 404s on /favicon.ico (observed in browser console at https://game.holomush.dev on 2026-05-23 during sandbox go-live verification).

**Plan:** SvelteKit uses adapter-static (web/svelte.config.js). The source static dir (web/static/) does not exist — create it and add web/static/favicon.ico (a HoloMUSH-branded icon). SvelteKit serves files from the static dir at site root, so /favicon.ico resolves automatically once the file exists. Optionally add an explicit <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico"> (and a modern PNG/SVG variant + apple-touch-icon) in web/src/app.html <head> (currently has only %sveltekit.head%). Consider a 32x32 + SVG for crisp rendering.

**Files touched:** web/static/favicon.ico (create), web/src/app.html (optional link tags), possibly web/svelte.config.js (only if a non-default assets path is needed — default web/static/ should work).

**Out of scope:** Full PWA icon set / manifest theming (separate polish). This bead is just the favicon 404.

**Found during:** game.holomush.dev go-live (epic holomush-hryj).

## Required Skills
jj:jujutsu
acceptance_criteria: GET https://<host>/favicon.ico returns 200 with an icon (no 404 in browser console). Favicon shows in the browser tab. Verified by loading the web client and checking the network/console.

title: web: move restoreSession from +layout.svelte onMount to root +layout.ts load()
description:
Session restoration in web/src/routes/+layout.svelte runs inside
onMount(), which executes after the component has rendered. On full
page reload this causes a brief flash of unauthenticated state before
restoreSession reads sessionStorage and re-hydrates the auth store.

SvelteKit's pattern is to do session restoration in a root +layout.ts
load() so it runs before component render. The (authed) route group
already follows this pattern in web/src/routes/(authed)/+layout.ts;
the root layout has not been migrated.

Fix shape:
  - Add web/src/routes/+layout.ts with an async load() that calls
    restoreSession() guarded by 'typeof window !== "undefined"' so
    SSR (if ever enabled) doesn't blow up
  - Remove restoreSession() from the onMount block in +layout.svelte
  - Keep initTelemetry(), initSentry(), hydrateUiPrefs() in onMount —
    they are deliberately client-only

Source: CodeRabbit finding on PR #4197 thread 4. Flagged by proximity
to Sentry edits but the underlying issue predates the Sentry trial.
Deferred from #4197 to keep that PR single-purpose (Sentry trial).

title: cleanup: convert auto_focus_on_join_test.go TestAutoFocus* suite to table-driven
description:
CodeRabbit feedback on PR #4191 round 3:

- internal/grpc/focus/auto_focus_on_join_test.go ~line 290: 6 standalone TestAutoFocus* tests for the 6 scenarios (HappyPath_TerminalOnly, FiltersByClientType, SkipsAlreadyExplicitlyFocusedConn, FailsForMembershipAbsent, TotalConnectionCount, SessionNotFound). Convert to a table-driven suite per the **/*_test.go coding-guideline (table-driven for comprehensive coverage).

- internal/session/memstore_test.go ~line 1045: connection-update suite (multiple standalone Tests with repeated setup) similarly should be refactored to table-driven form.

These are 'heavy lift' refactors per CodeRabbit's own label. The current tests are correct and exhaustive; the refactor is purely style consolidation. Defer to a post-merge sweep that does both packages in one pass.
notes:
Add internal/grpc/focus/is_any_conn_focused_test.go (3 standalone TestIsAnyConnFocused* tests, ~line 90) to the table-driven sweep — same package as auto_focus_on_join_test.go, natural pairing.
Scope update (post PR #4314, 2026-05-29): Part B is now MOOT — internal/session/memstore_test.go no longer exists (session memstore removed; session.Store has a single PostgresSessionStore impl). Remaining valid scope = Part A only: convert the 6 standalone TestAutoFocus* funcs in internal/grpc/focus/auto_focus_on_join_test.go to a table-driven suite. (NB: focus_delta_test.go + focus_wiring_test.go were already converted to table-driven in PR #4314 — auto_focus_on_join_test.go was NOT touched and is the remaining target.)

title: cleanup: convert internal/store testing-style integration tests to Ginkgo
description: CodeRabbit feedback on PR #4191 (5rh.14 T7 thread): session_store_integration_test.go uses testing/testify but coding guideline (CLAUDE.md) says integration tests MUST use Ginkgo/Gomega. Mixed convention currently in internal/store/ — 4 files use Ginkgo (migrate_integration_test, role_store_integration_test, migrate_plugins_integration_test, migrations_audit_shape_integration_test), 4 use testing (session_store_integration_test, events_audit_test, plugin_repo_test, migrations_test). Convert all 4 testing-style integration test files to Ginkgo/Gomega in one pass to settle the convention. Out of scope on the 5rh.14 PR (heavy refactor).

title: Apply ADR ti1b omission pattern to remaining optional attrs (owner_id, held_by, contained_in, shadows_id, property value/owner)
description:
## Goal

Extend the ADR holomush-ti1b omission pattern to the remaining optional attributes across the four ULID-typed AttributeProviders. The 9gtl bead fixed only the location-related attrs (location/parent_location) — the architectural decision per ti1b says ALL optional attrs follow the same rule.

## Why P3 (not P2)

No current seed compares any of these attrs (owner_id, held_by, contained_in, shadows_id, value, owner) against another peer's same attr in a  shape. So the bug class doesn't manifest today. This is invariant-completion, not bug-fix.

If a future seed gates on e.g. `resource.object.owner_id == principal.character.id` and a character without an id (impossible today) is compared against an un-owned object (owner_id=''), the same fail-open would surface — but the precondition doesn't currently exist.

## Files touched

- internal/access/policy/attribute/{character,object,property,location}.go
- internal/access/policy/attribute/{character,object,property,location}_test.go

## Out of scope

- StreamProvider — already follows the pattern (stream.go:46-48)
- ExitProvider, CommandProvider, SceneProvider — non-ULID providers; reviewed for the pattern; do not emit optional attrs with the bug class

## Related

- holomush-ti1b: the ADR
- holomush-9gtl: parent bead (initial fix scoped to location)
- holomush-iv43: foundational DSL fail-safe semantics

## Required Skills
dev-flow:test-driven-development
acceptance_criteria:
1. CharacterProvider: no remaining empty-string sentinels (already only had location_id/location).
2. ObjectProvider: owner_id, held_by_character_id, contained_in_object_id keys OMITTED when their has_X witness is false.
3. PropertyProvider: value, owner keys OMITTED when has_value/has_owner are false.
4. LocationProvider: owner_id, shadows_id keys OMITTED when has_owner/is_shadow are false.
5. Tests updated: expectAttrs cases for un-populated attrs assert absent-keys (matching the pattern from holomush-9gtl test updates).
6. abac-reviewer + code-reviewer READY.
7. Schema() declarations unchanged (DSL compile-time still references the attrs).

title: Re-enable Benchmark Regression Check workflow with cost/scope fixes
description:
**Status:** the workflow at `.github/workflows/benchmark-check.yml` has been disabled (limited to `workflow_dispatch:` only) because it ran on every PR — including docs-only PRs — and took 1.5-5 min while contributing no signal for non-policy-engine changes.

**Goal:** re-enable benchmark regression detection in a form that's cheap on PRs and reliable as a signal.

## What the workflow does (for context)

Triggers on every PR to main. Runs on `namespace-profile-linux-amd64-4x8` (Namespace.so cloud, 4 vCPU / 8 GB). Setup: checkout → Go from go.mod → Go module/build cache (`nscloud-cache-action`) → install Task → `scripts/check-benchmark-regression.sh`.

The script loads 7 baseline values from `.benchmarks/baseline.txt` (all benchmarks in `internal/access/policy/` — single-policy eval, condition eval, 50-policy eval, attribute resolution, two worst-case scenarios, and end-to-end). Runs `go test -bench=. -benchmem -count=3 -benchtime=1s -run='^$' ./internal/access/policy/` via `task test:bench`. Averages each benchmark's `ns/op` across the 3 runs. **Fails if any benchmark's average exceeds 110% of its baseline** — hard 10% regression budget per metric.

## Why it's slow (cost breakdown)

| Cost | Approx |
|---|---|
| Hard floor: 7 benchmarks × 3 counts × 1s | ~21 s benchmark wall time |
| `BenchmarkWorstCase_NestedIf` alone (25 ms/op, fills the 1s budget every count) | ~3 s × 3 = 9 s |
| Runner queue + image pull | 10-60 s |
| Go setup + cache restore (cold cache especially) | 20-90 s |
| Test binary compile for `internal/access/policy/` | 10-30 s |

Best case ~1.5 min, ~3-5 min on cold cache. No parallelism — sequential execution.

## Cheap wins to consider before re-enabling

1. **Skip on docs-only PRs.** Add `paths-ignore:` matching `DOCS_ONLY_PATHS` from `Taskfile.yaml`, or wire into the existing `ci-docs-skip.yaml` mechanism that the main `ci.yaml` uses. Would have saved CI minutes on PRs #4114 and #4116.
2. **Scope to policy-engine changes only.** Add a `paths:` filter so the workflow only fires when `internal/access/policy/**`, `internal/access/**`, or `.benchmarks/baseline.txt` changes. Most PRs don't touch the policy engine — no benchmark regression risk.
3. **Drop `BENCH_TIME=1s` to `0.5s`.** Halves the benchmark floor to ~10s. Go still gets enough iterations on the cheap benchmarks (single eval = 200ns) for statistical sanity; the worst-case ones are dominated by per-op cost, not iteration count.
4. **Move to a smaller runner.** `namespace-profile-linux-amd64-4x8` (4 vCPU / 8 GB) is overprovisioned — benchmarks are single-threaded. A `1x2` profile would be cheaper and likely same wall time.
5. **Consider moving to `nightly-soak.yml`-style schedule.** If point-in-time PR signal isn't load-bearing, a nightly run against `main` would catch regressions without slowing every PR. Trade-off: regressions land before being caught.

## Recommendation order

Most impact first: (2) path scope + (1) docs-only skip — these eliminate the run for the majority of PRs entirely. Then (4) runner downsize. (3) and (5) are policy tweaks once it's running on the right PRs.

## Acceptance

- Workflow re-enabled with at minimum (1) docs-only skip + (2) policy-engine path scope.
- Verified on a test PR that touches `internal/access/policy/` (workflow runs) and on a docs-only PR (workflow skipped).
- Decision on (3), (4), (5) recorded in the bead or PR description.

## Motivating context

Filed during the holomush-lfri + holomush-f7t2 sequence (PRs #4114, #4116). Both were docs-only changes; both triggered the full benchmark suite. The latency was noticeable enough to ask "what does this even do?" — exactly the experience the path scope would prevent.
notes: Disable shipped 2026-05-19 via PR #4117 (workflow_dispatch only). Now waiting for someone to re-enable with the path-filter + paths-ignore shape described above.

title: Optimize filter-at-delivery: cache session floor in per-Subscribe goroutine
description:
Initial Subscribe dispatch (iwzt Task 14 / iwzt.15) does a per-event sessionStore.Get for the filter-at-delivery check in internal/grpc/server.go::dispatchDelivery. Latency-acceptable for v1 but expected to dominate hot-path for busy locations.

Optimization: snapshot relevant SessionInfo fields (LocationID, LocationArrivedAt, IsGuest, GuestCharacterCreatedAt, FocusMemberships) in a per-Subscribe goroutine local, refreshed by lifecycle hooks (BumpLocationArrivedAt, MovementHook fires, Subscribe.ReattachCAS). Requires extending the locationFollower ctrl channel or adding a sibling channel.

Acceptance: hot-path Get eliminated; floor snapshot rotates on reattach/move/scene-join; existing TestDispatchDelivery* coverage continues to pass.

title: Add SetGuestMetadata store method for safe guest-fields backfill on reattach
description:
## Context

iwzt.5 (PR #4043) added guest-resolution before the reattach branch in SelectCharacter, with the intent of backfilling `IsGuest` and `GuestCharacterCreatedAt` for sessions created pre-iwzt-T5. The naive backfill via `sessionStore.Set` was reverted because the in-memory `existingSession` carries stale `detached_at`/`expires_at` values that Set would overwrite, undoing the prior `UpdateStatus(active, nil, nil)` call's NULL-clearing.

## Fix

Add a targeted `SetGuestMetadata(ctx, sessionID, isGuest, guestCharacterCreatedAt) error` to `session.Store` (MemStore + PostgresSessionStore). The postgres impl is a tiny UPDATE:

```sql
UPDATE sessions
  SET is_guest = $1, guest_character_created_at = $2, updated_at = now()
  WHERE id = $3
```

Then in `internal/grpc/auth_handlers.go::SelectCharacter` reattach branch, replace the in-memory-only backfill with a call to this store method:
```go
if isGuest && existingSession.GuestCharacterCreatedAt.IsZero() {
    existingSession.IsGuest = true
    existingSession.GuestCharacterCreatedAt = guestCharCreatedAt
    if err := s.sessionStore.SetGuestMetadata(ctx, existingSession.ID, true, guestCharCreatedAt); err != nil {
        slog.WarnContext(ctx, "failed to backfill guest metadata on reattach", "error", err)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance

- `task test -- ./internal/session/ ./internal/store/` passes including a `TestSetGuestMetadata` test that verifies the backfill does not clobber detached_at/expires_at.
- `task test:int -- ./test/integration/auth/` E2E passes (the iwzt.5 autofix had failed 6 multi-tab/reattach tests before reverting the naive Set).

## Source

CodeRabbit thread on PR #4043: `internal/grpc/auth_handlers.go:331-360` — "reattach path can leave guest floor metadata unset" (🟠 Major). Initial autofix attempt used `sessionStore.Set` and broke 6 E2E tests; reverted to in-memory-only with this follow-up filed.

title: DSL ContainsCondition allows zero path segments before contains() method
description:
## Symptom

The policy DSL grammar allows expressions like `principal.containsAll([...])` where no attribute path is given between the root entity and the `containsAll`/`containsAny` method call. `HasCondition` and `AttrRef` correctly require at least one path segment; `ContainsCondition` is inconsistent.

## Where

- AST: `internal/access/policy/dsl/ast.go:176` — `Path []string \`parser:"(Dot @Ident)*"\`` should likely be `+` (one or more) to match HasCondition + AttrRef.
- Generated EBNF: `site/docs/reference/policy-dsl.ebnf:11` — reflects the AST.

## Source

CodeRabbit flagged this on PR #4039 (iwzt.22) as a minor issue. The PR introduced the generated EBNF file (first time `go generate` produced it), exposing a pre-existing AST inconsistency.

## Investigation

The runtime evaluator likely rejects `principal.containsAll(...)` since the root entity isn't a list. The grammar relaxation may be unreachable in practice. Verify by:
1. Reading the evaluator handler at `internal/access/policy/dsl/evaluator.go:228::evalContains`
2. Checking whether existing seeds use the zero-path form
3. Tightening the parser tag if no real usage depends on it

## Out of scope of original PR
This is a DSL grammar concern, not a manifest history_scope concern. Filed as separate bug.

title: Playwright E2E test: session TTL expiration behavior
description:

title: Building & objects plugin integration tests

No test/integration/building/building_test.go exists. Building plugin (plugins/core-building/ — dig, link) and objects plugin (plugins/core-objects/ — describe, create, set, examine) have unit-level coverage but no end-to-end integration tests exercising the full plugin → host → DB flow.

title: Reaper tests are time-dependent (2-second WithTimeout) — flaky under slow CI
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.32 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

reaper_test.go:37 uses context.WithTimeout(ctx, 2*time.Second) blocking on reaper.Run(). No clock injection or reapExpired() direct call.

Slow CI runners can hit the timeout before reapExpired completes its sweep.

Acceptance: inject a fake clock OR call reapExpired() directly without the Run() loop OR use a smaller deterministic interval with a guaranteed sweep.

title: Subscribe + replay subscribe tests use time.Sleep — flaky under slow CI
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.28 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

Subscribe tests in server_test.go use time.Sleep-based timing for async assertions. May flake on slow CI runners.

Acceptance: replace time.Sleep with Eventually/channels/clock injection. Cite specific test names and line numbers when fixing.

title: Subscribe: return nil for clean context.Canceled instead of SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELLED error
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.24 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

runSubscribeLoop:969 still returns `oops.Code("SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELLED").Wrap(ctx.Err())` on ctx.Done(). For client-initiated clean disconnect, returning nil (or a CANCELED gRPC status without error wrap) would more accurately signal intent.

Acceptance: distinguish context.Canceled (clean) from context.DeadlineExceeded (forced) and return appropriately. Log SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELLED at DEBUG only.

title: Four web/handler.go stub RPC methods repeat identical CodeUnimplemented error (verify still applies)

title: UpdateGridPresent SQL parameter order mismatch — maintenance hazard

Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.10 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

session_store.go:437-449: parameter order is `\$1=id` in WHERE, `\$2=present` in SET — reverse of conventional left-to-right reading order. Easy to break when adding columns or refactoring.

Acceptance: reorder bind params to match SET-then-WHERE column order.

title: session_connections.client_type is unsanitized user input stored in DB
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.9 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

server.go:733-748 accepts req.GetClientType() from the gRPC request with no allowlist validation before writing to the store.

Acceptance: validate client_type against {"terminal", "comms_hub", "telnet"} (the known set per session.go:192) before AddConnection.

title: Type-check entityPrefix in world.checkAccess — prevent silent unmapped-prefix bugs
description:
Re-filed from holomush-3cm7.6 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-3cm7 PR #88 ABAC bucket).

## Problem
checkAccess in world/service.go accepts a free-form `entityPrefix` string with no compile-time validation. The entityAccessDeniedCodes map in errors.go mitigates known prefixes, and TestEntityPrefixCoverage enforces map coverage for currently-declared prefixes. But a NEW call site introducing a typo prefix (e.g., "LOACTION") would produce an unmapped code that falls through to the default "Something went wrong" response with no failing test.

The guard is the test, not the type system.

## Acceptance
- Replace string parameter with typed enum (`EntityPrefix` int or named string type)
- Compile-time enforcement that only known prefixes can be passed
- TestEntityPrefixCoverage stays as belt-and-suspenders for the map → message mapping

title: echo-bot: design re-emit semantics for cross-plugin event types (crypto.emits interaction)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-k18g.3 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r).

echo-bot emits core-communication:say (re-emitting another plugin's event type) — not declared in echo-bot's own crypto.emits.

Phase 1 manifest contract assumes one plugin per event type. Phase 3+ should design how cross-plugin re-emit interacts with:
- sensitivity declarations
- ABAC grants
- audit trail attribution (who emitted vs who originated)

Refs: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-phase1-manifest-grammar.md (Task 7)

Verified 2026-05-16: plugins/echo-bot/plugin.yaml (origin/main) has no crypto.emits block; uses legacy `events: [say]`. No design doc exists for cross-plugin re-emit + crypto interaction.

title: Delete dead HandleSay/HandlePose methods in internal/core/engine.go
description:
Re-filed from holomush-k18g.1 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r).

During Task 5 of the event-payload-crypto Phase 1 migration (core-communication EventType constants moved to plugins/core-communication/), the implementer noted that internal/core/engine.go's HandleSay/HandlePose methods are dead production code (no non-test callers). They now use raw event-type string literals ("core-communication:say", "core-communication:pose") because package core cannot import plugins/core-communication without architectural inversion.

Two acceptable resolutions:
1. Delete the dead methods (cleanest — they have no live callers)
2. Move the canonical event-type catalogue into a shared internal/event-types or pkg/event-types package that both core and plugins can import

Option 1 is preferred per the plugin-boundary discipline (host MUST NOT own plugin-named event types). Verify by running 'rg HandleSay|HandlePose internal/' and ensuring no production caller exists.

Refs: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-phase1-manifest-grammar.md (Task 5 implementer report)

Verified 2026-05-16: HandleSay/HandlePose defined at internal/core/engine.go:78 with no production caller outside engine.go itself + test file.

Note: holomush-zfll.9 covers a different concern (telnet handler discarding resp.Success==false) on the related code path — not duplicate.

title: Production KeySelector: replace identityProductionKeySelector placeholder with KEK-backed impl
description:
Re-filed from holomush-1r0v.12 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r → 1r0v sub-epic).

## Goal

cmd/holomush/phase7_fence_wiring.go::buildKeySelector currently returns identityProductionKeySelector{} (a value-receiver zero-size struct placeholder). Replace with a real KEK-backed selector when production crypto wiring is ready. As long as it's a value-type, INV-P7-9's pointer-identity assertion in TestDispatcherAndHotTierShareSelector is degenerate (all instances are equal); when it becomes a non-trivial pointer-identity-sensitive type, the existing test will start gating production correctly.

## Design reference
Phase 7 spec §5.2 KeySelector wiring; INV-P7-9 final.

## TDD acceptance criteria
- buildKeySelector returns a KEK-backed implementation (e.g., wrapping crypto/dek manager).
- TestDispatcherAndHotTierShareSelector still PASSES (now with non-degenerate pointer identity).
- Optional: a wiring-shape assertion at construction confirming single-instance threading.

## Verification steps
- task test -- ./internal/eventbus/ ./cmd/holomush/ green.

## Files touched
- cmd/holomush/phase7_fence_wiring.go.
- Possibly internal/eventbus/codec/* for a new KeySelector adapter.

## Dependencies
Blocked by future KEK-backed crypto wiring work.

## Out of scope
- Vault provider integration (separate bead holomush-aub5 P4).

Verified 2026-05-16: cmd/holomush/phase7_fence_wiring.go::buildKeySelector returns identityProductionKeySelector{} (placeholder); codec.KeySelector interface comment says "no production implementation."

title: Catch-up replay protocol via last_invalidation_seq (deferred from ojw1.3.19, Decision 6)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-e49r.7 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r Event Payload Cryptography).

**Goal:** Implement permissive + reconciled cache-invalidation model (Option C) with catch-up replay on heartbeat-resume, so transient lapses don't trigger pills.

**Design reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §8 (cache invalidation), Phase 3c grounding doc Decision 6

**Discovered from:** holomush-ojw1.3.19 — deferred pending operational data on benign lapse frequency. The last_invalidation_seq field is already wired in heartbeat + probe-reply payloads (Decision 6), so no wire-format change needed.

**Scope:** ~150-250 LOC for replay subscription + window calculation + tombstone bridging during replay.

**Out of scope:** Wire format changes (already supported — verified at internal/cluster/payload.go:24,57).

Verified 2026-05-16: `git grep "catchup|catch_up_replay" origin/main` → 0 hits (subscription/window logic absent).

title: Pill message signing (deferred from ojw1.3.18, Decision 7)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-e49r.6 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r Event Payload Cryptography).

**Goal:** Add cryptographic signing to cache-invalidation pill messages to close the misconfig + cross-cluster gap.

**Design reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §8 (cache invalidation), Phase 3c grounding doc Decision 7

**Discovered from:** holomush-ojw1.3.18 — deferred per Decision 7. Cheaper protections already shipped: pill audit trail, pill source reporting, pill rate-limit, cluster ID in subject.

**Scope:** ~200-300 LOC including per-cluster signing key, distribution, and rotation substrate. Revisit if a real threat materializes (compromised plugin pills replicas; cross-cluster confusion under shared NATS).

**Out of scope:** Host-compromise threats (a compromised host can do far worse than pill peers — read DEK material, forge events).

Verified 2026-05-16: `git grep "pill.*sign|per.cluster.*sign" origin/main` → 0 hits.

title: CI hygiene: bead-existence smoke check for dek.Manager stubAllowSet
description:
Re-filed from holomush-e49r.3 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r Event Payload Cryptography).

Per code-reviewer finding #1 on Phase 2 substrate PR. The dek.Manager stub allow-set ({holomush-fi0n, holomush-jxo8}) is checked at Go test time but not against the live beads database — a closed/renamed bead would silently rot.

Acceptance: a CI job (or `task lint:beads` target) runs `bd show $ID` for every entry in stubAllowSet and fails if any returns 'no issue found'. Wire into pr-prep.

Reason for deferral: design notes Decision 1 explicitly scoped this out of Phase 2 ('Phase 4 and Phase 5 land before stubs are at risk of silent rot in production logs'). Filed as a hygiene followup.

Verified 2026-05-16: stubAllowSet exists at internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/api_test.go:119-127 as a Go map, NOT a CI job.

title: Plugin Phase 4 cleanup: auto-generate hostfunc bindings from proto + dynamic reload
description:
Re-filed from holomush-w77b.4 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-w77b).

Phase 4 from the plugin-architecture-rework spec was partially shipped: ServiceProxy domain methods removed (rg "ServiceProxy" internal/ → 0 hits), plugin admin commands shipped (internal/command/handlers/plugin_admin.go). What remains:

Replace hand-maintained Lua hostfunc bindings with auto-generated. internal/plugin/hostfunc/functions.go has no // Code generated header. internal/plugin/gen-schema/main.go exists but has not replaced the bindings file. Every new PluginHostService RPC currently requires hand-writing the Lua binding (parity rule in CLAUDE.md feedback_host_rpc_lua_parity).
