title: Backlog: Full ExitProvider and SceneProvider attributes (Phase 7.7+ expansion)
description:
## Goal

Expand the stub `ExitProvider` and `SceneProvider` attribute providers into full implementations with rich attributes. Currently both ship as stubs (return empty AttributeBags) to satisfy the ABAC engine's provider registry contract. Future ABAC policies referencing exit/scene attributes need real data.

**Status:** Re-filed 2026-05-15 as top-level standalone (consolidates `holomush-5k1.422` ExitProvider + `holomush-5k1.424` SceneProvider — both previously deferred children of now-closed Epic 7 epic `holomush-5k1`).

## Design reference

- Original beads: `holomush-5k1.422` (ExitProvider) + `holomush-5k1.424` (SceneProvider), filed 2026-02-09 under the Phase 7.3 plan.
- Stubs shipped: `internal/access/policy/attribute/exit.go` and `attribute/scene.go` per 2026-05-15 batch 14 audit.
- Future work: when ABAC policies need exit/scene attributes (e.g., `resource.exit.locked == true`, `resource.scene.participants ∋ subject.id`), expand both providers.

## Plan reference

### 1. ExitProvider expansion

Attributes to add (suggested):
- `resource.exit.from_location` (string — origin location ID)
- `resource.exit.to_location` (string — destination)
- `resource.exit.locked` (bool — has active lock)
- `resource.exit.visibility` (string — public/private/hidden)
- `resource.exit.owner` (string — character ID of owner, if any)

### 2. SceneProvider expansion

Attributes to add (suggested):
- `resource.scene.visibility` (string — public/private/unlisted)
- `resource.scene.participants` (list — character IDs in scene)
- `resource.scene.host` (string — character ID of scene host)
- `resource.scene.status` (string — active/paused/ended)
- `resource.scene.tags` (list — content warning tags)

Both providers register their attributes via `SchemaRegistry.Register()` and implement `ResolveResource()` with PostgreSQL queries.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- Each provider implements the full `AttributeProvider` contract.
- `*_test.go` covers happy path, error paths, and empty/nil resource cases.
- New seed policies (or test policies) demonstrate exit/scene attribute usage.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/policy/attribute/...` green.
2. `task test:int` green (integration tests if exit/scene policies are exercised).
3. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/attribute/exit.go` (expand stub)
- `internal/access/policy/attribute/scene.go` (expand stub)
- Corresponding `_test.go` files
- Possibly new seed policies in `internal/access/policy/seed.go`

## Dependencies

- None. Backlog work — needed when ABAC policies require exit/scene attributes.

## Out of scope

- Other provider expansions (env, command, stream already shipped as needed).
- Lock-related exit attributes (Phase 7.5 was deferred to Epic 8 per Decision #96).

title: AccessPolicyEngine call-site ergonomics: AccessRequest type encap + shared eval helper
description:
## Goal

Two related PR #88 review findings about cleaning up access-evaluation call sites. Both target the same ~29 production call sites that construct ABAC requests and handle decisions. Bundled because they share review context and would land in one or two coordinated PRs.

## Design reference

- Originally filed as 2 separate beads under `holomush-5k1` (Epic 7: Full ABAC): `holomush-2jpm` (AccessRequest type encapsulation) + `holomush-t70y` (shared access-eval helper).
- PR #88 comprehensive review surfaced both: https://github.com/holomush/holomush/pull/88
- Audit report: `.claude/agent-memory/bead-auditor/reports/2026-05-15-1319-5k1-batch20.md` (where t70y was flagged as deferred-work).

## Plan reference

Two scoped sub-improvements:

### 1. AccessRequest type encapsulation (from `2jpm`)

Goal: rated 3/10 for encapsulation. Security-critical format requirements (type:id subjects, known actions) are bare exported strings with no constructor.

- Define `Action*` constants (`ActionRead`, `ActionWrite`, `ActionExecute`, etc.) in `internal/access/policy/types/` or `internal/access/`.
- Add `NewAccessRequest(subject, action, resource string) (AccessRequest, error)` constructor with format validation (rejects unknown actions, malformed subject/resource prefixes).
- Define error-code constants for the hand-typed strings that appear in `world/service.go` and elsewhere.
- Migrate the ~29 call sites to use the constructor + constants.

### 2. Shared access-eval helper (from `t70y`)

Goal: ~60 lines of duplicated access-evaluation pattern across `world.Service.checkAccess`, `command.Dispatcher`, and `hostfunc.Commands`. Each has slightly different error handling but shares the core pattern: build request, evaluate, handle deny/error.

- Extract a shared helper that handles the common flow.
- Allow per-site customization of error codes (`ErrPermissionDenied` vs `ErrCapabilityCheckFailed` vs custom) and metrics labels.
- Migrate all three call sites.

## TDD acceptance criteria

- New tests for `NewAccessRequest` constructor: rejects malformed input, accepts valid input, returns correct error codes.
- New tests for the shared helper: covers happy-path, deny path, error path, with per-site customization knobs exercised.
- All existing tests still pass after call-site migration.

## Verification steps

1. `task test -- ./internal/access/... ./internal/command/... ./internal/world/...` green.
2. `rg "AccessRequest{" internal/` — no more struct-literal construction at call sites; all go through the constructor.
3. `task pr-prep` green.

## Files touched

- `internal/access/policy/types/types.go` (or new file in `internal/access/`) — Action constants + NewAccessRequest constructor
- `internal/access/policy/eval.go` (new) — shared helper
- `internal/world/service.go::checkAccess`
- `internal/command/dispatcher.go` + `internal/command/access.go`
- `internal/plugin/hostfunc/commands.go`
- Corresponding `_test.go` files

## Dependencies

- None. Independent ergonomic refactor.

## Out of scope

- Re-shaping the `Decision` type (separate concern — covered by gk8b.9 historical, now closed).
- Changing the AccessPolicyEngine interface itself.

title: Plugin manifest hot-reload callback infrastructure
description:
## Goal

Add a generic 'on manifest change' callback to internal/plugin/manager.go so consumers of manifest-derived state (e.g., the Phase 7 PluginDowngradeFence's always-sensitive event-type set) can subscribe to live updates.

## Why

Phase 7 design (holomush-1r0v) initially assumed this hook existed; design-reviewer confirmed it does NOT. Phase 7 v3 scoped down: the always-sensitive set is built atomically once at initial manifest load only. INV-P7-8 (hot-reload atomicity) was dropped.

When a plugin manifest is added/changed/removed at runtime, any consumer holding manifest-derived state has no way to learn about it today. Future consumers will hit the same gap.

## Acceptance criteria

- New callback registry on internal/plugin/Manager (mechanism TBD: channel, fn list, observer pattern)
- Hot-add / hot-change / hot-remove of plugin manifests triggers registered callbacks with the affected manifest
- Phase 7 PluginDowngradeFence subscribes; refreshes always-sensitive set atomically
- Invariant: callback invocation is serialized w.r.t. Manager's own manifest mutations
- Unit tests cover add/change/remove + concurrent subscribe/unsubscribe

## Out of scope

- Lua-side hot reload (separate concern; binary plugin process restart vs Lua VM reload have different semantics)
- Manifest-validation revalidation on reload (assume valid manifests stay valid; orthogonal)

## Files touched

- internal/plugin/manager.go (callback registry + invocation sites)
- internal/plugin/manager_test.go (callback coverage)
- internal/eventbus/audit/plugin_downgrade_fence.go (subscribe on Phase 7 fence)

## Dependencies

- Phase 7 (holomush-1r0v) ships first with initial-load-only set
- This bead activates AFTER Phase 7 lands

## History

Surfaced during Phase 7 brainstorming when design-reviewer (v2 review) flagged INV-P7-8 (atomic refresh on reload) as unverifiable absent this hook. User chose to drop the invariant and file this follow-up rather than block Phase 7 on substrate work.

title: Cache-resident plaintext DEK material: bound lifetime in dek.Cache LRU
description:
## Goal

Reduce the in-memory exposure window of plaintext DEK material that lives inside `dek.Material` instances cached by `internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/cache.go` (the LRU keyed by `CacheKey{KeyID, Version}`).

## Why this exists

PR #__ closed holomush-e49r.4 by zeroizing the *transient* plaintext byte slice that flows through `GetOrCreate` / `Rotate` / `unwrapAndCache` / `MintNewDEKForRekey`. Code-reviewer (2026-05-13) flagged a related but distinct gap that's out of scope for that fix:

`material := NewMaterial(dekBytes)` (material.go:30-32) makes a *defensive copy* of the plaintext into `Material.bytes`. That copy then lives inside the LRU cache for the configured `CacheConfig.TTL` (commonly 5 minutes in production wiring). `AsCodecKey` mints further independent copies into each emitted `codec.Key.Bytes` (material.go:44).

Net effect: even after the e49r.4 transient-buffer fix lands, plaintext DEK material is still resident in process memory for the cache TTL and across every codec.Key egress in flight.

## Design questions

- Bound the cache TTL more tightly? Current default is `5*time.Minute` per CryptoConfig.Defaults. A tighter window trades crypto-op throughput against exposure.
- Provide an explicit `Material.Zeroize()` method invoked by cache eviction so plaintext gets cleared the moment the cache decides the entry is gone?
- Track the lifetime of `codec.Key.Bytes` egresses similarly (each AsCodecKey call hands out a fresh slice that the codec consumer holds until it's done with the encrypt/decrypt op).

## TDD acceptance criteria

TBD by writing-plans. At minimum:
- Cache eviction zeroizes the evicted Material's plaintext before the entry is dropped.
- An integration test (similar in shape to TestE49R4_*) captures Material plaintext via a test seam, evicts the entry, and verifies the captured plaintext is zeroed.

## Dependencies

None (the cache + Material types are stable; this is additive).

## Out of scope

- Provider-side KEK material lifetime (separately bounded by provider implementation).
- Changing the cache's eviction policy (still LRU + TTL).
- Any change to `codec.Key` egress semantics — those are gated by the `codeckeybytesallowlist` gorules analyzer separately.

## History

Filed 2026-05-13 from code-reviewer non-blocking finding #2 during PR #__ (e49r.4 transient-buffer zeroize). Original finding: 'cache-resident plaintext is still long-lived' — out of scope for the e49r.4 commit but worth tracking.

title: @cluster status and @evict-member admin commands
description:
## Goal

Implement operator-facing admin commands consuming `cluster.Registry` LiveMembers + ProbeAndPill, on top of the localhost UDS admin-socket substrate.

## Design reference

- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §8 (cluster)
- Phase 5 sub-epic C: admin socket + OperatorAuthProvider (now landed)

## Discovered from

holomush-ojw1.3.20 — deferred pending admin-RPC framework adoption. `cluster.Registry.MemberObserver` is the substrate hook; `@cluster status` is a snapshot reader; `@evict-member` calls ProbeAndPill with `PillReasonOperatorEvict` (already an enum value in Phase 3c).

## Scope

Operator UX commands, not protocol substrate.

## Out of scope

Protocol-level cluster changes (already implemented in Phase 3c).

## Dependencies

None — the Phase 5 admin socket infrastructure (sub-epics C/D/E/F) is now merged. This is a standalone follow-up.

## History

Re-filed from holomush-jxo8.1 (closed) to detach from the jxo8.4 epic so the Phase 5 sub-epic C implementation can be considered complete. The work itself is unchanged P3 operator-UX debt.

title: Client-visible stale-DEK signaling via typed Event field
description:
## Goal

Distinguish stale-DEK from non-participant denial and AuthGuard infrastructure failure on the client wire surface so operators / clients can diagnose decryption failures without conflating three distinct cases.

## Why this exists

Phase 3d Decision 5 retired the `App-Warning: stale-dek` wire-header claim in favor of `metadata_only=true` + the `crypto.cold_dek_miss` metric. From the client's perspective, `metadata_only=true` currently conflates:

1. Non-participant denial (correct authz outcome)
2. AuthGuard infrastructure failure
3. Stale-DEK (cold-tier DEK missing or destroyed)

A typed Event proto field (e.g. `NoPlaintextReason` exposed on the wire response) or a distinct EventFrame status would distinguish these cases.

Acceptable for current state (no users, no production diagnostic surface yet). File as future-debt so the diagnostic gap is owned.

## Reference

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-event-payload-crypto-phase3d-grounding.md (Decision 5 + Bead updates §7)

Sub-epic F's `NoPlaintextReason` enum (4→7 expansion in `holomush-jxo8.8.2` / INV-F16) already carries the right reason taxonomy on the server side via `corev1.NoPlaintextReason`; this bead is about exposing it consistently on the non-AdminReadStream client surfaces too.

## TDD acceptance criteria

TBD by writing-plans. At minimum: client-visible decryption failures carry a typed reason field distinguishing stale-DEK, non-participant, and AuthGuard infra failure; existing AdminReadStream INV-F12 classifier matrix coverage is extended to the general client wire surface.

## Dependencies

None (sub-epic F's NoPlaintextReason expansion is the substrate).

## History

Re-filed from holomush-ojw1.6 (closed) to detach from the jxo8.7 epic so the Phase 5 sub-epic E implementation can be considered complete. The work itself is unchanged P3 diagnostic-debt.

title: In-game admin command to grant/revoke crypto.operator capability
description:
## Goal

Replace the config-file-only allow-list (shipped in sub-epic B / PR #3536) with an in-game admin UX so operators can be granted/revoked at runtime without editing YAML and restarting.

## Why this exists

Sub-epic B (`holomush-jxo8.5`) shipped the `crypto.operator` capability backed by a config-file allow-list with restart-only reload. The master spec §5.9.1 documents this as the v1 mechanism; an in-game grant UX was deferred to this follow-up.

## Design seam

Master spec §5.9.1 already documents the runtime-exposure shape. The capability constant (`access.CapabilityCryptoOperator`), facade (`access.HasPlayerGrant`), and `PlayerAttributeProvider` are all in place. This bead chooses where the grant state lives (DB table vs config-file mutation vs hybrid) and ships the admin command.

## TDD acceptance criteria

TBD by writing-plans. At minimum: `holomush admin grant crypto.operator <player>` and `revoke` commands; mutation is audited; `PlayerAttributeProvider` reflects the new state without restart (or documents restart-still-required if config-file mutation is chosen).

## Dependencies

None (sub-epic B's deliverables are merged).

## Out of scope

Granting other capabilities (this bead only handles `crypto.operator`).

## History

Re-filed from holomush-jxo8.5.5 (closed) to detach from the jxo8 epic so the Phase 5 sub-epic A-F implementation can be considered complete. The work itself is unchanged P3 feature debt.

title: events_audit composite index on (dek_ref, dek_version) — profile-then-decide
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.2 (detached from sub-epic E during epic wrap; trigger-deferred performance concern that doesn't require sub-epic E context — fires when real-world Rekey scan volume justifies the decision).

**Goal:** Add composite index on events_audit (dek_ref, dek_version) if profiling during Rekey shows the single-column dek_ref index is insufficient for scanning all events by (dek_ref, dek_version) pair.

**Design reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §5 (Rekey workflow)

**TDD acceptance criteria:**
- Profile Rekey scan on realistic event volume (100k+ rows) with single-column index
- If heap filter on dek_version exceeds 10ms per 10k rows: add composite index migration
- If single-column index is sufficient: close with rationale, no migration

**Verification steps:**
- task lint
- task test:int
- task pr-prep

**Files touched:**
- internal/store/migrations/ — new migration (if needed)

**Trigger:** profile observation in real-world Rekey scans. The single-column dek_ref index might suffice for typical deployments; this exists to capture the decision point if Rekey performance becomes an issue.

**Out of scope:**
- Performance optimization beyond this specific index
- Other events_audit indexes
- General Rekey profiling

title: INV-E meta-test enforcing INV-E[N] ↔ test-name binding (Phase 3c pattern)
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.49 (detached from sub-epic E during epic wrap; process/test-hygiene work standalone — INV-E table already shipped, this just enforces the binding going forward).

**Goal:** Add a meta-test that scans the codebase for test files and asserts every INV-E1..INV-E28 invariant has at least one named test binding via comment annotation '// Verifies: INV-EN'. Prevents future drift between invariants table (spec §8) and test files.

**Design reference:** crypto-reviewer + code-reviewer: 'No INV-E meta-test enforcing INV→test binding (Phase 3c had this)'.

**Background:** Phase 3c shipped TestEveryPhase3cInvariantHasAtLeastOneTestBinding which scans tests for '// Verifies: INV-N' annotations and proves each of INV-53..60 has at least one binding. Sub-epic E declares 28 invariants but ships TestSpecAmendmentsLandedSubEpicE (presence-only check on spec text), not the INV→test binding enforcement.

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestEveryPhase5EInvariantHasAtLeastOneTestBinding scans test files for INV-E1..INV-E28 annotations and asserts each has ≥1 binding. Annotate existing INV-E tests to satisfy the assertion.

**Verification steps:** task lint && task test -- ./test/meta/

**Files touched:** test/meta/inv_e_binding_test.go (new); annotations across existing INV-E test files

**Dependencies:** none

**Out of scope:** non-INV-E invariants

title: Verifier INV-E27 per-entry subject-derived scope cross-check (defensive)
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.47 (detached from sub-epic E during epic wrap; correctness-shaped but standalone — no longer requires sub-epic E context to address).

**Goal:** Strengthen the auditchain Verifier's INV-E27 scope cross-check from 'caller-supplied scope vs. payload-derived' to 'per-entry subject-derived vs. payload-derived' as the spec §3.6 text literally promises.

**Design reference:** crypto-reviewer R1 finding 'INV-E27 enforcement weakening' (caller-supplied scope vs. subject-derived). spec §3.6 INV-E27 description.

**Background:** verifier.go:169-189 compares caller-supplied scope against ScopeFromPayload. Via VerifyAll (boot path) this is transitively correct because VerifyAll derives scope from subject. Direct VerifyScope(scope) callers trust the caller. Repo's row-by-subject filter protects against misrouting in practice, but spec text promises entry-level independent extraction. Either amend spec to match impl OR add per-entry h.ScopeFromSubject(e.Subject) extraction in verifyEntries.

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestVerifier_ScopeMismatchBetweenSubjectAndPayload_RejectsRow covers the existing path. Add: TestVerifier_DirectCallerWithWrongScope_RejectsViaSubjectDerived if going the strict-impl route.

**Verification steps:** task lint && task test -- ./internal/eventbus/audit/chain/

**Files touched:** internal/eventbus/audit/chain/verifier.go

**Dependencies:** none

**Out of scope:** changes to Handler bundle shape or RecomputeSelfHash signature

title: Test gap: subscriber.go decodeAndAuthorize NoPlaintextReason stamps have no coverage
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-ojw1.6 code-reviewer Medium finding #1.

**Goal:** Add tests asserting that subscriber.go's decodeAndAuthorize (the live Subscribe decryption path) stamps NoPlaintextReason correctly at:
- subscriber.go:631 — AuthGuard deny → NoPlaintextReasonAuthGuardDeny
- subscriber.go:695 — plugin audit queue full → NoPlaintextReasonAuditQueueFull

The history path (dispatcher.go) has dedicated reason assertions in dispatcher_errors_test.go + decode_jetstream_test.go. The live Subscribe path got the stamps in ojw1.6 but no equivalent test coverage. The parity test (TestNoPlaintextReasonEnumParity) prevents enum drift but doesn't catch a regression where one stamp site forgets to set the reason.

**Background:** No existing test in internal/eventbus/*_test.go calls decodeAndAuthorize directly. Adding coverage requires new fakes for jetstream.Msg, SessionAuthGuard, SessionDEKManager, SessionAuditEmitter. Substantial scaffolding (~100-200 lines).

**TDD acceptance criteria:**
- TestSubscriber_DecodeAndAuthorize_AuthGuardDenyStampsAuthGuardReason
- TestSubscriber_DecodeAndAuthorize_PluginAuditQueueFullStampsAuditQueueFullReason

**Verification steps:** task test -- ./internal/eventbus/

**Files touched:** internal/eventbus/subscriber_test.go (extend) or new internal/eventbus/decode_and_authorize_test.go.

**Dependencies:** holomush-ojw1.6 (must land first).

**Out of scope:** changes to subscriber.go production code; tests for the dispatcher.go path (already covered).

title: Host-side guard: enforce MetadataOnly+NoPlaintextReason coherence at EventFrame conversion
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-ojw1.6 crypto-reviewer NIT #2.

**Goal:** The proto comment at api/proto/holomush/core/v1/core.proto:179-181 says `NoPlaintextReason UNSPECIFIED is the zero value and MUST hold when metadata_only=false`. The MUST is enforced by convention only. Go has two independent fields (eventbus.Event.MetadataOnly + eventbus.Event.NoPlaintextReason); nothing prevents a future emitter from setting one without the other.

**Fix options:**
- A) Collapse to a single typed field on eventbus.Event (e.g., `Delivery DeliveryStatus` carrying both bits as one enum: OK / AUTHGUARD_DENY / STALE_DEK / AUDIT_QUEUE_FULL). Cleaner but breaks Event struct shape and the wire-proto split.
- B) Add host-side assertion at the conversion sites (internal/grpc/query_stream_history.go:548 and internal/grpc/server.go:612): if !MetadataOnly && NoPlaintextReason != 0 → log/metric/force UNSPECIFIED; if MetadataOnly && reason == 0 → log/metric. Defense-in-depth.

**Background:** As a future-debt diagnostic this is acceptable. But the contract phrasing 'MUST hold' is strong, and a future regression could decouple the two without test coverage detecting it.

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestEventFrameConversion_StampsUnspecifiedWhenMetadataOnlyFalse + TestEventFrameConversion_LogsWhenMetadataOnlyTrueWithoutReason.

**Verification steps:** task test -- ./internal/grpc/

**Files touched:** internal/grpc/query_stream_history.go + internal/grpc/server.go (eventbusEventToEventFrame + toProtoSubscribeResponse).

**Dependencies:** holomush-ojw1.6 (must land first).

**Out of scope:** changes to the wire proto contract; changes to the existing stamp call sites.

title: Add MaxJustificationLength cap to RekeyRequest.Justification + admin handler validation
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.55 crypto-reviewer NIT #2.

**Goal:** Add a `MaxJustificationLength` constant (suggest 4000, matching internal/world/validation.go's MaxDescriptionLength) and validate `RekeyRequest.Justification` length in `internal/admin/socket/rekey_handler.go::buildRekeyRequest`. Today the field is unbounded operator-supplied free text that persists in:
- crypto_rekey_checkpoints.justification (PostgreSQL text, no size limit)
- events_audit.envelope (JCS-canonicalized audit payload)

**Background:** Pre-existing exposure, made newly load-bearing by holomush-jxo8.7.55 which persists Justification on the checkpoint row. The chain verifier canonicalizes every audit row on each verification pass; a megabyte-sized justification would inflate verification time. Trust boundary is the admin socket (crypto.operator capability), so risk is bounded — but defense-in-depth is cheap.

**Design reference:** Compare to internal/auth/registration.go:14-23 (MaxPasswordLength = 128 with explicit DoS rationale) and internal/world/validation.go:18-29 (MaxDescriptionLength = 4000).

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestBuildRekeyRequest_RejectsJustificationOverMax — submit a justification at MaxJustificationLength+1 bytes, assert a typed validation error code at the handler boundary.

**Verification steps:** task test -- ./internal/admin/socket/

**Files touched:** internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/rekey.go (add constant); internal/admin/socket/rekey_handler.go (validate in buildRekeyRequest); internal/admin/socket/rekey_handler_test.go (table-driven validation test).

**Out of scope:** changes to the on-disk column type (still text); changes to the audit payload schema.

title: admin_authenticate_e2e_test.go: refactor T25+T26+T27 to Ginkgo Ordered containers
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.46 code-review finding #1.

**Goal:** Refactor cmd/holomush/admin_authenticate_e2e_test.go so T25 / T26 / T27 each get their own It (or Describe) sharing the single runCoreWithDeps boot via Ginkgo's Ordered container + OncePerOrdered semantics. Currently all three scenarios pile into one ~230-line It block (line 499-727).

**Background:** The single-boot constraint is real (prometheus.DefaultRegisterer at cmd/holomush/core.go:428-432 + TOTP replay at internal/totp/service.go:309). Ginkgo Ordered containers + OncePerOrdered solve this idiomatically. A failure in T27 would then surface as a single-spec failure instead of buried mid-block, speeding post-mortem.

**Verification steps:** task test:int -- ./cmd/holomush/

**Files touched:** cmd/holomush/admin_authenticate_e2e_test.go (restructure to Ordered container); cmd/holomush/admin_rekey_e2e_test.go (extract T27 scenario into its own It if appropriate).

**Out of scope:** changes to runCoreWithDeps, prometheus registration, TOTP service.

title: crypto: background GC sweep for orphan DEK rows from aborted Rekey checkpoints
description:
**Goal:** When a Rekey is aborted (operator_abort, ttl_expired, or force_destroy_used) before Phase 6, the freshly-minted DEK_new row in crypto_keys becomes an orphan — referenced by no checkpoint (aborted ones don't count) and not the active version for its context. Orphans are not destructive (reads work, the active version is fine), but they accumulate over time and complicate forensic queries.

**Why deferred from sub-epic E:** Decision made during E plan stage 2026-05-10. Counting orphans is a single SQL query (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM crypto_keys WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT current_active OR successor) AND destroyed_at IS NULL`); deleting them is not destructive. Operator-driven sweep CLI (`holomush crypto orphan-sweep --dry-run/--apply`) is straightforward to add but deserves its own bead and operator UX considerations (force-vs-confirm gates, audit emission per sweep batch, threshold alerting).

**Design reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-10-event-payload-crypto-phase5-sub-epic-e-design.md §1.2 (Out of scope row 'Background GC for orphan DEK rows').

**Plan reference:** TBD when this is picked up.

**TDD acceptance criteria (preserved for future plan):**
- Synthesize 3 orphan crypto_keys rows + 1 active row across N contexts
- `holomush crypto orphan-sweep --dry-run` reports exact 3 orphans without modifying state
- `holomush crypto orphan-sweep --apply` UPDATEs destroyed_at on all 3 and emits an audit event with the count
- Active row untouched

**Verification steps:** task lint; task test; task test:int; task pr-prep.

**Files touched (estimated):**
- cmd/holomush/cmd_crypto_orphan_sweep.go — new CLI
- internal/admin/socket/orphan_sweep_handler.go — new RPC
- internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/orphan.go — query + sweep logic
- api/proto/holomush/admin/v1/orphan.proto — new RPC messages

**Dependencies:** holomush-jxo8.7 (E lands the orchestrator + sweep subsystem this builds on).

**Out of scope:**
- Automatic sweep (operator-driven only for v1)
- Threshold alerting (separate monitoring bead)
- Cross-tenant sweep (single-game scope)
notes:
**Model selection: sonnet**

Rationale: GC sweep for orphan DEK rows (P3 future-debt). Mechanical CLI + RPC + sweep logic; well-specified in the bead description. Not part of E execution but tagged for future executor.

Per the 2026-05-09 user policy: default sonnet; opus reserved for tasks
with genuine synthesis (state-machine design, cross-cutting refactors,
or high-risk integration). Subagent executing this bead MUST use the
model named above.

title: Centralize invariant capture, storage, and cross-reference across specs
description:
Multiple HoloMUSH specs declare numbered RFC2119 invariants (I-1, INV-21, INV-25, INV-39, INV-49, INV-50, etc.) as the load-bearing acceptance criteria for their feature. The pattern is good — see [feedback_invariants_and_docs_as_spec_acceptance] which mandates invariants + docs as PR-blocking spec acceptance criteria. The problem is that invariants are scattered across docs/superpowers/specs/*.md with no central index, no canonical declaration format, and no machine-readable link between an invariant declaration and the test(s) that enforce it.

Concrete pain points that surface this:

1. 'Which test enforces INV-39?' is currently grep-and-pray. holomush-ojw1.1.10 (Phase 3a T10) and holomush-71zq.14 (meta-test for invariant-test name alignment) are point-fixes for this exact issue, but each spec re-invents the wiring.
2. Renumbered/renamed invariants across spec revisions can silently de-link from their enforcing tests. Phase 3 had multiple grounding revisions where invariant numbers shifted.
3. New specs declare invariants in different formats (some I-1 prose blocks, some INV-XX bullet lists, some inline RFC2119 sentences without a label). No canonical schema.
4. Invariants frequently document scope decisions ('no production migration discipline') alongside runtime properties ('every Actor.id is a valid ULID'). These are different categories and benefit from being distinguished in any centralized registry.

Possible deliverables (epic-level scope; pick subset during planning):

- Convention: every spec MUST declare invariants under a canonical 'Invariants' section with consistent numbering scheme (e.g., INV-<spec-prefix>-<n>).
- Index: a generated docs/superpowers/specs/INVARIANTS.md (or site/docs/contributing/invariants.md) that aggregates every declared invariant with backlinks to declaring spec + enforcing test(s).
- Linkage: each invariant declaration MUST cite at least one named test (file:test_name) that enforces it. Specs without test linkage fail review.
- Tooling: a Go program or task to extract invariants from spec frontmatter or a fenced block, validate the test references resolve, and emit the index.
- Migration: backfill existing specs (Phase 3a-3d, Phase 1, Phase 2, etc.) to the canonical format.
- Meta-test: a regression test that fails if any declared invariant has zero enforcing tests, or if a renamed test breaks the linkage.

Discovered: 2026-05-04 during legacy_id elimination (holomush-w9ml) brainstorming — Section 1 of the design draft surfaced the question of how to phrase a 'scope decision' invariant (I-6) versus a 'runtime property' invariant, which lit up the broader pattern.

References:
- feedback_invariants_and_docs_as_spec_acceptance memory entry
- holomush-ojw1.1.10 (Phase 3a T10: wire invariants to tests)
- holomush-71zq.14 (Plan Task 12: meta-test for invariant-test name alignment)

Out of scope:
- Implementing invariant DSL / formal verification (this is documentation+tooling, not formal methods).
- Mandating invariants for all existing specs retroactively in one PR (backfill is its own task).

title: External NATS deploy: design + render deny rules + lifecycle pivot off embedded-only
description:
HoloMUSH external NATS deploy: server-account scoping + lifecycle pivot off embedded-only.

Phase 3d (holomush-ojw1.4) confirmed via empirical verification that game-topic NATS (events.>, audit.>, internal.>) is single-principal by architectural design — only the holomush server connects on these subjects. Per-principal NATS deny rules retired from Phase 3d (master spec §7.7 amended per Phase 3d grounding doc Appendix A).

Scope for this epic:

1. Embedded server lifecycle survey: pivot internal/eventbus/ from embedded-only to external dial mode.
2. Server-account scoping in external mode:
   Account "holomush-server":
     publish:   events.>, audit.>, internal.>
     subscribe: events.>, audit.>, internal.>
   Other accounts in the cluster have no permission on these subjects by default.
3. Optional: read-only operator account ("holomush-operator-read", subscribe events.> only) for monitoring/debugging.
4. Audit-table reads: localhost UNIX admin socket (Phase 5), not NATS subscribe.
5. CI: external-NATS test matrix; nats-server in test containers.
6. Operator runbook: external-NATS deploy guide.

Cross-references:
- holomush-ojw1.4 (Phase 3d) — surfaces the architectural realization
- Phase 6 (Vault provider) overlaps on multi-host crypto
- master spec §7.7 (amended) — architectural framing
- Phase 3d grounding doc Appendix A — verbatim §7.7 replacement applied in Phase 3d

Discovered: 2026-05-03 during Phase 3d brainstorming.

title: Assess pgxpool leakage across internal/world/postgres repos; centralize if warranted
description:
CodeRabbit's PR #3518 review on internal/world/postgres/binding_repo.go raised that the constructor accepts *pgxpool.Pool directly and does not reject nil. We declined that finding for binding_repo.go specifically because every other repo in internal/world/postgres/ (character_repo.go, exit_repo.go, location_repo.go, etc.) holds *pgxpool.Pool directly without a validated interface or nil-rejection. Refactoring just one creates inconsistency.

The broader question: should we (a) introduce a narrow DB interface (Exec/QueryRow) used by all repos, (b) centralize pool ownership behind a Datastore type that hands out repo-scoped views, or (c) accept the current pattern and document it as the project convention.

Scope this bead to:
1. Audit all *pgxpool.Pool callsites across internal/world/postgres/ and internal/store/.
2. Survey similar patterns in the Go ecosystem (pgx idioms; sqlc-style boundary).
3. Make a recommendation; if (a) or (b), file follow-up plan.

Origin: PR #3518 CodeRabbit comment on internal/world/postgres/binding_repo.go:49 (Phase 3b).

title: mergeCallerHeaders reserved-key check is case-sensitive; nats-msg-id bypasses guard
description: publisher.go:260: reservedHeaderKeys lookup and Nats- prefix check are case-sensitive. A caller writing nats-msg-id or App-codec bypasses the collision guard silently. Fix: lowercase k before checking, and add regression tests for lowercase variants.

title: RenderingPublisher.Publish rewraps inner error, masking EVENTBUS_PUBLISH_EXPIRED
description: rendering_publisher.go:96-100 wraps p.inner.Publish error with EMIT_PUBLISH_FAILED, which shadows the meaningful EVENTBUS_PUBLISH_EXPIRED code. errors.Is(err, ErrPublishExpired) still works through the chain, but callers reading the outermost oops code see the wrong value. Fix: either drop the rewrap or detect ErrPublishExpired and re-emit EMIT_PUBLISH_EXPIRED.

title: Optimize bats collision-test wall time (kill propagation through holder process tree)
description: Task 8's 3 collision tests take ~91s wall (3 × ~30s sleeps that survive end_collision's kill). Root cause: HOLDER_PID written to info is the sh -c subshell that flock owns; kill on that PID doesn't propagate SIGTERM to the descendant task pr-prep:run -> pr-prep:stub -> sleep 30 process chain. The wait HOLDER_BASH_PID then blocks until sleep exits naturally. Tests still pass (bats captures collision result before end_collision is called) but each test costs 30s of needless wait. Two viable fixes: (1) wrap the holder in setsid so the entire process group is reachable via 'kill -- -GROUP'; (2) install a SIGTERM trap in pr-prep:stub that exits early on signal. Either approach should bring the suite from 91s to <10s. Low priority cosmetic improvement; doesn't affect correctness. Useful when iterating on the test suite during development.

title: reorder Subscribe nil-subscriber guard vs ownership validation
description: Surfaced by holomush-9q8n.4 PR review. internal/grpc/server.go:657-659 returns oops.Code('NOT_CONFIGURED') before reaching auth.ValidateSessionOwnership at :663-676. In production this guard cannot fire (subscriber is wired in cmd/holomush/server.go), but a stale-token caller hitting a misconfigured server would receive NOT_CONFIGURED rather than the enumeration-safe SESSION_NOT_FOUND that every other Subscribe failure mode collapses to — a minor enumeration concern. Two acceptable fixes: (a) move the nil-guard after ValidateSessionOwnership, or (b) fold it into the same SESSION_NOT_FOUND return (the request is still 'failable' to a stale-token caller). The new test in test/integration/auth/multi_tab_test.go currently wires a stub eventbus.Subscriber so the validation path is reachable; if (a) or (b) lands, the unusedSubscriber stub becomes optional but still safe to keep as defense in depth.
notes: Test wiring (auth_suite_test.go unusedSubscriber + WithSubscriber) added in holomush-9q8n.4 mitigates the test-side risk of this ordering being silently changed — OpenSession returns a TEST_SUITE_BUG-coded error if reached, so a reorder regression surfaces distinctively rather than masquerading as SESSION_NOT_FOUND.

title: CI: nightly soak has no failure notification — failures invisible
description:
## Finding

`nightly-soak.yml` runs the soak test nightly but has no failure alerting. A failing soak is invisible until someone manually checks. Provides confidence only when monitored.

## Suggested direction

Add an `if: failure()` step that opens a GitHub issue (e.g. `peter-evans/create-issue-from-file`) or sends notification (e.g. `dawidd6/action-send-mail`). Auto-deduplicate or use the GitHub Issues API to update an existing "Nightly soak failing" issue rather than spamming.

## Severity

P3 (operational). Fix is cheap but prerequisite is deciding the notification channel.

title: Docs: archive ~30 stale plans + consolidate phase-7.x ABAC plan files
description:
## Finding

Plan/spec directory tree is overgrown.

### Counts
- docs/plans/ — 40 files
- docs/specs/ — 23 files
- docs/superpowers/plans/ — 61 files
- docs/superpowers/specs/ — 43 files
- Archived total — 10 files

163 active plan/spec files vs only 10 archived.

### Specific consolidations
- docs/plans/2026-01-18-holomush-roadmap-design.md and 2026-01-18-plugin-system-implementation.md sit in active plans/ when most have shipped.
- docs/plans/2026-02-06-full-abac-phase-7.1.md through 7.7.md — seven separate files for an arc that is complete or near-complete; consolidate into a single completion record.
- ~30 plans likely belong in archive/ based on their date and the work having shipped.

### The split is invisible to contributors
docs/plans/ vs docs/superpowers/plans/ split (AI-tooling-generated vs human-authored) is documented in CLAUDE.md but a contributor browsing the tree without that context cannot tell why two parallel hierarchies exist.

## Why it matters

A contributor opening docs/ today sees a wall of in-progress work that is mostly historical. Hard to find the live plans. Strong impression of a codebase that does not finish things — directly counter to the "not slop" goal.

## Severity

P3 (docs hygiene). Mechanical archival sweep.

title: Tests: weak/skeleton tests + zero-assertion compile-canaries + skip-with-unreachable-setup
description:
## Finding (consolidated low-rigor test cleanup)

### Single-assertion error tests
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:36 — TestMemStore_Get_NotFound has only `assert.Error(t, err)`. Passes for any error including a future refactor returning a "found but invalid" sentinel.
- internal/store/alias_test.go:588 — TestNewPostgresAliasRepository has only `require.NotNil(t, repo)` on a constructor. A constructor that returns nil is a compile error in idiomatic Go.

### Zero-assertion compile canaries (legitimate but lint-violating)
- internal/access/resolver_test.go:15 — TestLocationResolverSatisfiesInterface takes `_ *testing.T`, zero assertions. Same guarantee is better expressed as `var _ Interface = Type{}` outside a test function.
- internal/store/alias_test.go:579 — TestAliasRepositoryInterface, same pattern.
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:233 — TestMemStore_ConcurrentAccess takes `_ *testing.T` and relies on -race. The race-detector reasoning is sound but the test cannot register failures.

### Tests of language invariants
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:397 — TestFocusMutatorHasMutateField asserts that a zero-value struct's function field is nil. Tests Go language semantics, not behavior.

### Skip-with-unreachable-setup
- test/integration/eventbus_e2e/audit_drift_detector_test.go:36
- test/integration/eventbus_e2e/js_storage_corruption_test.go:38
- test/integration/eventbus_e2e/multi_protocol_fanout_test.go:36
- test/integration/eventbus_e2e/backfill_rebuild_test.go:28

All four have `t.Skip("TODO(bead-id)...")` as first line; the setup code below is unreachable but maintained. Either trim the body to just t.Skip with bead reference or guard with //nolint:unused.

## Severity

P3 (test rigor polish).

title: Tests: ~15 ACE-framework naming violations in older test files
description:
## Finding

CLAUDE.md mandates ACE-framework sentence test names (Action / Condition / Expectation). Newer test files comply (TestPublisherRejectsNilJetStream, TestAuthenticatePlayerEmitsSessionEndedForEvictedSessionChildren, TestOpenSessionIsIdempotentAcrossReopens). Older files predate the rule and have not been updated.

### Top-level violations
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:18 TestMemStore_GetSet (no expectation)
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:36 TestMemStore_Get_NotFound (double-underscore; not a sentence)
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:44 TestMemStore_Delete (action only)
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:110 TestMemStore_ConnectionTracking (topic, not sentence)
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:138 TestMemStore_AppendCommand
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:173 TestMemStore_UpdateGridPresent
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:338 TestMemStore_UpdateLastPaged
- internal/session/memstore_test.go:397 TestFocusMutatorHasMutateField (describes struct, not behavior)
- internal/store/alias_test.go:579 TestAliasRepositoryInterface
- internal/store/alias_test.go:588 TestNewPostgresAliasRepository
- internal/world/object_test.go:17, 53, 102, 125 — TestContainment_Constructors / Validate / Type / ID
- internal/session/session_test.go:32 TestStatus_String

### Subtest violations
- internal/store/player_session_store_test.go:64,135,276,331,447,766 — six "happy path" subtest names
- internal/plugin/manifest_test.go:326 — `{name: "simple", plugName: "echo"}`

## Severity

P3 (cosmetic but real). New contributors model naming from existing tests, so each violation propagates. Mechanical sweep.

title: Security polish: cookie/TLS coupling, dummy hash entropy, write timeout, addlicense pin
description:
## Finding (consolidated low-severity security polish)

### Cookie dev-mode is decoupled from listener TLS
- internal/web/cookie.go:55-58 — secure=false removes Secure attribute and downgrades SameSite from Strict to Lax. A misconfiguration where prod flips secure=false silently broadens cookie attack surface (plaintext + cross-site nav). Boot-time WARN exists per comment but does not gate.
- Suggested: refuse to start if listener has TLS but secure=false, or vice-versa.

### Dummy password hash uses zero salt/key
- internal/auth/auth_service.go:131 — argon2 timing-equality is preserved via 64 MiB memory cost on either path. Acceptable today. Defense-in-depth concern: a future argon2 short-circuit on all-zero salt or a future password-pepper scheme could reintroduce the timing leak.
- Suggested: generate a process-fixed random dummy hash at startup.

### No global http.Server.WriteTimeout
- internal/web/server.go:76-81 — acceptable since ConnectRPC streams are long-lived; HTTP/2 ping-pong covers liveness. Document the rationale inline so future contributors don't add a timeout that breaks streaming.

### addlicense unpinned
- Taskfile.yaml:456 (license:run) and lefthook.yaml:29 — addlicense@latest. Inconsistent with the rest of the project's SHA-pinning discipline. Minor risk of breaking change in license format.

## Severity

P3 (informational/hardening). None directly exploitable; collected here so they don't get lost.

title: Refactor QueryStreamHistory tests into table-driven cases
description: CodeRabbit PR #252 thread: internal/grpc/query_stream_history_test.go currently has 15+ near-identical TestQueryStreamHistory* functions. Consolidate into a single table-driven test with struct covering name, session setup, history reader, access engine, request, and expected outcome (error code or success + assertions on reader.gotQ/resp fields). Large churn so deferred — all current tests pass. Tracked from autofix round 2.

title: multi-protocol fan-out e2e: telnet + web see same pose
description: Full end-to-end test covering spec §8 'Multi-protocol fan-out' row. Stands up the telnet adapter + web adapter against a shared core, binds a character to a scene via telnet, connects the same character via web, publishes a pose via telnet, asserts both protocols see the same ULID/seq. Skeleton at test/integration/eventbus_e2e/multi_protocol_fanout_test.go currently skipped pending the multi-adapter harness; needs the telnet + web stack reachable from a Go test without full Docker compose.

title: JS storage rebuild from PG audit: restore tool
description: Build a tool that reconstructs JetStream EVENTS stream content from events_audit after embedded JS storage loss. Spec §8 'Embedded JS storage corruption -> Rebuild from PG audit; ULIDs stable'. Test skeleton at test/integration/eventbus_e2e/js_storage_corruption_test.go asserts ULIDs remain stable after rebuild. Contract: publishes each audit row back via the Publisher with the original ULID; dedup window on the stream ensures idempotency.

title: Memoize getBufferSize() in terminalStore.ts
description: In web/src/lib/stores/terminalStore.ts, getBufferSize() reads localStorage on EVERY appendLine call (high-frequency hot path). PR #235 reviewer flagged. Cache the value once per session (or invalidate when user changes the setting via a forthcoming UI). Trivial perf fix.

title: Centralize localStorage access via safeStorage helper
description: Multiple files (uiPrefsStore, themeStore, CommandInput, terminalStore getBufferSize, +page.svelte composer persist) wrap localStorage in try/catch for quota / privacy-mode safety with inconsistent logging. PR #235 review recommended a shared 'safeStorage' helper in web/src/lib/util/ or similar that wraps get/set/remove with dev-warn on failure and re-export. Refactor existing sites to use it. No functional change; improves diagnosability and reduces duplication.

title: Rail.svelte: expose class prop for shadcn-convention parity
description: Per web/CLAUDE.md, custom terminal chrome components MUST follow shadcn conventions including a 'class' prop for extension. Rail.svelte (added in PR #235) does not currently accept a class prop. Add it via $props() and merge with internal class list using cn() helper. Minor polish, not a user-visible change.

title: Extract detectMode to pure function for unit testing
description: In web/src/lib/components/terminal/CommandInput.svelte the mode detection logic (': ', 'pose ', 'say ', '"', 'ooc ' → say/pose/ooc chip) is a $derived inside the component. PR #235 reviewer flagged: covered by E2E for only 3 of 5 prefix paths. Extract to pure helper function (e.g. web/src/lib/terminal/detectMode.ts) with unit tests covering all 5 branches. Trivial refactor, improves regression safety.

title: ⌘R reverse-i-search overlay for command history
description: Terminal hi-fi revamp (PR #235) shipped with commandHistoryStore but does NOT include an interactive history search. Design: an inline overlay activated by ⌘R (or Ctrl+R on non-macOS) that lets users filter and jump to a prior command, similar to bash reverse-i-search. Lives on commandHistoryStore. Discovered during terminal hi-fi brainstorming 2026-04-18.

title: Server-side population of optional sidebar fields (mood, lastMode, isIdle)
description:
Terminal hi-fi revamp (PR forthcoming) ships client-side rendering for three optional sidebar fields that the server does not currently populate:

- RoomCard: location.mood — a short evocative description line under the room description (e.g., 'dust motes hang in slanted light'). Rendered only when present.
- PresenceCard: presence.lastMode — most recent message mode ('say' | 'pose' | 'ooc' | 'sys'). Drives avatar color in the presence list. Falls back to 'sys' styling when absent.
- PresenceCard: presence.isIdle — boolean. Drives grayscale + reduced-opacity treatment on idle characters.

This bead tracks server-side work to actually populate these fields:
- Data model: add mood to locations (schema + migration), add lastMode/isIdle to presence projection (likely derived at session/event layer, not stored).
- RPCs: extend location + presence responses on the web/terminal gRPC surface.
- Client mapper: populate the optional fields on the SvelteKit client's store types.

Non-goals: MUSH command to set mood (separate feature); idle timeout tuning (separate).

Discovered during terminal hi-fi design session on 2026-04-18.

title: Fix FindByCharacterName not-found semantics: MemStore returns SESSION_NOT_FOUND error, Postgres returns nil,nil — align to nil,nil and add interface doc
description: MemStore.FindByCharacterName returns (nil, SESSION_NOT_FOUND) but PostgresSessionStore returns (nil, nil). Interface doc is silent. Discovered during Task 7 review of 9es6. Production caller is already defensive but MemStore tests assert behavior that production never sees. Fix: align MemStore to return nil,nil and document the contract.

title: CoreClient vs GRPCClient dual-interface drift
description: internal/web CoreClient and cmd/holomush GRPCClient are 95% identical; each new RPC requires adding the method to both. Review whether to consolidate or accept the duplication and document the pattern (see B13 Task 2 code review).

title: localStorage scrollback persistence
description: Persist dimmed scrollback to localStorage keyed on sessionId. On reload, render from localStorage immediately then merge with QueryStreamHistory via event_id dedup. Prerequisite (event_id) landed in B13.

title: A11y: ARIA-live announcement for backfill loading
description: Announce 'Restoring scrollback…' via aria-live=polite while replayActive is true. B13 reused the existing --- REPLAY --- visual indicator without a screen-reader equivalent.

title: OTel tracing for backfill
description: Add backfill.* span with attributes: stream count, event count, failed streams, duration. Measures B13's SHOULD <1s goal.

title: Backfill scroll-up pagination (older history via before_id)
description: When user scrolls to top of terminal scrollback, fetch older events via WebQueryStreamHistory with before_id=oldestEventId. Extends B13's single-page fetch to full tmux-like scrollback.

title: Expand TypeScript unit test coverage for web client
description:
B13 introduced vitest to the web project for the first time, with unit tests limited to the new backfill helper + isUnimplementedError. Existing client code (stores, event router, theme system, auth flow, command input, markdown content) has zero unit test coverage — everything is tested via Playwright E2E only.

Add unit tests for:
- web/src/lib/stores/* (terminalStore, sidebarStore, authStore, themeStore, contentStore)
- web/src/lib/stores/eventRouter.ts (pure routing logic, easy to unit test)
- web/src/lib/theme/themeStore.ts (runtime var override, MUSH token detection)
- Any pure utility modules added since.

Goal: establish a reasonable baseline (e.g., 60%+ line coverage on src/lib/) so future changes are safer to make without full E2E runs. Also consider adding @testing-library/svelte for component-level coverage if that ROI holds up.

title: Plumb user_agent and ip_address through auth RPCs to ListPlayerSessions
description:
Discovered during PR #225 (bd-urbq) review. AuthenticatePlayer, CreatePlayer, and CreateGuest currently hardcode empty strings for userAgent and ipAddress when calling the auth service, even though PlayerSession persists these fields and ListPlayerSessions surfaces them as the 'this device' UX. Result: every session list shows blank UA/IP metadata.

Proper fix:
1. Add user_agent and ip_address fields to AuthenticatePlayerRequest, CreatePlayerRequest, CreateGuestRequest (proto changes)
2. Web gateway extracts from User-Agent header + RemoteAddr/X-Forwarded-For
3. Telnet gateway extracts from connection RemoteAddr; UA stays empty (telnet has no UA concept) or uses literal 'telnet'
4. Core handlers pass through to auth service

Alternatively, server-side extraction via metadata.FromIncomingContext() + peer.FromContext() can pull UA from gRPC headers and IP from the peer info — but this only works for web/ConnectRPC since telnet doesn't go through gRPC at the user boundary. Gateway-forwarded fields are more reliable and cleaner.

Not a security issue — UX/observability only.

title: RevokeOtherPlayerSessions: best-effort + per-ID failure reporting
description: Follow-up from session-identity implementation. internal/grpc/auth_handlers.go RevokeOtherPlayerSessions aborts on first delete error and returns a gRPC error, leaving the list partially revoked. Caller cannot tell which sessions succeeded and which failed. Switch to best-effort loop (continue on per-delete errors) and return per-ID failure details in the response alongside revoked_count. Refs: bd-urbq implementation.

title: Add composite index on player_sessions(player_id, expires_at)
description: Follow-up from PR addressing bd-urbq. CountActiveByPlayer, ListByPlayer, and DeleteOldestForPlayer subquery all filter player_id = $1 AND expires_at > now(). Under cap-of-10 per player the current indexes are fine, but as expired rows accumulate between DeleteExpired runs, a composite index (player_id, expires_at) avoids seq-scan risk. Add a migration: CREATE INDEX idx_player_sessions_player_expires ON player_sessions(player_id, expires_at).

title: Control plane shares CA with data plane
description: Admin gRPC uses same CA as core-gateway mTLS. Compromised gateway cert could trigger shutdown. Fix: Separate CA or add CN/SAN check.

title: Graceful degradation can silently mask ABAC policy failures
description: WithGracefulDegradation logs ABAC failures as warnings, server starts with broken policies. Fix: Distinguish soft vs hard failures.
notes: Investigation 2026-05-16 (status sync pass 4): bead was closed 2026-05-02T04:16:31Z then resurrected to status=open while closed_at preserved (sync regression). Re-close declined — dep on holomush-207m (Plugin host functions lack authorization) is genuinely open and never closed. The 2026-05-02 close was either pre-block or --force. Will close naturally when 207m resolves.
close_reason: Security finding verified fixed in code — degraded mode is loud and fail-closed (beads audit 2026-05-02)

title: Service registry lacks protected service name blocklist
description: No blocklist of reserved service names — ordering is the only defense. Fix: Add reserved prefixes.

title: chore(web): make SvelteKit dist regeneration deterministic or gitignore it
description:
The internal/web/dist/_app/immutable/*.js files are tracked in git
because they're embedded into the Go binary via go:embed. SvelteKit
regenerates them on every build with new hash filenames, causing
constant working-copy churn during development.

During Phase 3 execution (PR #202) this required restoring dist after
nearly every task build/lint invocation to keep commits clean. ~25
restore operations, multiple commits where dist churn rolled in
unintentionally.

**Options:**
1. Make SvelteKit build deterministic — set a stable hash seed,
   pin chunk splits, or use vite content-based hashing instead of
   build-time hashing.
2. Move dist to a build artifact directory excluded from git, and
   regenerate it deterministically in the production build pipeline
   only. The go:embed FS would point at a build-time-generated path.
3. Use git attributes to mark dist files as 'merge=ours' or
   similar so concurrent local builds don't cascade into commits.

**Why NOT in Phase 3:** Pure tooling friction, not a correctness issue.
Out of scope and would have eaten significant time during execution.

**Severity:** Low priority but high friction. Affects every PR that
rebuilds the web client.

Refs: holomush-5rh.12 PR #202

title: Flaky test: auth deletes expired sessions in bulk (clock-skew race on 1ms TTL)
description:
## Summary

The integration test `test/integration/auth/player_session_test.go:413` — `It("deletes expired sessions in bulk", ...)` inside the `Describe("DeleteExpired", ...)` block — is a timing-sensitive flake that passes 2 of 3 runs on local `task pr-prep`.

## Failure signature

```
Expected
    <int64>: 0
to be >=
    <int>: 2
```

The reaper ran but saw zero expired rows instead of the expected two.

## Root cause

Wall-clock resolution race, not a semantic bug:

1. `auth.NewPlayerSession(player.ID, tokenHash, "", "", 1*time.Millisecond)` computes `ExpiresAt = time.Now() + 1ms` against the test process's wall clock.
2. `PlayerSessionStore.DeleteExpired` filters via SQL `WHERE expires_at < NOW()` using Postgres's wall clock inside the container.
3. The test sleeps 10ms to let the 1ms TTLs cross, leaving only ~9ms of slack between the expiry time and the reaper query.
4. Under load (`-race`, Docker I/O, CI scheduling noise, container clock drift), that slack can evaporate — the reaper sees `expires_at > NOW()` and deletes nothing.

## Why it's not holomush-43nd

This test touches `auth.PlayerSessionStore.DeleteExpired` (player auth sessions, `internal/auth/player_session.go`). PR #198 only modified:

- `internal/store/session_store.go` — game sessions, a different table and store
- `internal/session/memstore.go` — in-memory game session store
- `internal/grpc/server.go` — game session cursor persistence

Completely separate code paths. The flake was observed on both the pre-fix baseline and the fixed stack, confirming pre-existing.

## Affected code

- `test/integration/auth/player_session_test.go:413` — the test itself
- `internal/auth/player_session.go` — `NewPlayerSession` (TTL computation)
- `internal/auth/player_session_store.go` (or similar) — `DeleteExpired` (the reaper)

## Proposed fixes (in order of preference)

1. **Inject a clock.** Add an injectable `Clock` interface to `NewPlayerSession` so tests can set `ExpiresAt` to a deterministic past time (e.g., `time.Unix(0, 0)`). Production wiring uses the real clock; tests use a fake. Zero race surface.

2. **Absurdly-past expiry via direct insertion.** Skip `NewPlayerSession` + sleep entirely. Insert rows via the store's raw SQL path with `ExpiresAt = time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour)`. Clean but requires exposing a test-only insertion hook.

3. **Bump the sleep buffer.** Cheapest fix: change `1*time.Millisecond` TTL + `10ms` sleep to something like `10*time.Millisecond` TTL + `100ms` sleep. Reduces flake frequency but doesn't eliminate the race. Not recommended.

## Impact

- Adds noise to CI — a re-run clears the failure, but it wastes runner minutes and developer attention.
- Reduces signal quality: a real regression in `DeleteExpired` might be dismissed as "just the flake."
- Low priority because re-runs fix it and no user-visible functionality is affected.

title: Add pprof HTTP endpoints to server for runtime profiling
description:
Expose Go's net/http/pprof endpoints on the holomush server so operators can attach for on-demand CPU, heap, goroutine, block, and mutex profiling.

## Scope

- Register pprof handlers (`net/http/pprof`) on a dedicated listener, NOT the main gRPC/HTTP listener (separation prevents accidental exposure through the public surface)
- Gate behind a config flag (default off in production, on in dev)
- Bind to localhost by default (no external exposure)
- Document in site/docs/operating/

## Rationale

pprof is the standard Go observability primitive for diagnosing CPU hotspots, memory leaks, goroutine leaks, and lock contention in a running process. Currently no production profiling story exists for the holomush server — if something goes sideways in prod we have metrics and logs but no way to introspect runtime behavior.

## Considerations

- **Security:** pprof endpoints leak sensitive runtime state (goroutine stacks may contain request data; heap profiles may contain user data). MUST NOT be exposed externally. Localhost-only bind + auth-gated access only.
- **Performance overhead:** pprof CPU/heap profiling has measurable runtime cost. Should be off by default; operators enable on demand.
- **Alignment with observability stack:** Complements existing OTel tracing and structured logging. pprof fills the gap where traces + logs aren't enough (e.g. "why is this process using 8GB RAM").

## Acceptance

- [ ] pprof endpoints available via opt-in config (e.g. `debug.pprof.enabled: true`, `debug.pprof.bind: 127.0.0.1:6060`)
- [ ] Default off in production builds
- [ ] Ops documentation covers: how to enable, how to collect profiles (`go tool pprof`), common investigation recipes
- [ ] Listener is a separate `http.Server` — never muxed onto the main server

## Out of scope

- Continuous profiling (Pyroscope, Parca, Grafana Cloud Profiles) — that's a bigger infrastructure bet
- Automatic profile collection on alert triggers — future enhancement
- Plugin-side pprof — plugins run as separate processes and have their own lifecycle

title: Consider scenes.updated_at column for sort/display in Phase 8/9
description:
**Context**

Phase 2 of the scenes rework adds the lifecycle state machine and partial-update RPC. The scenes table currently has created_at, ended_at, and archived_at, but no updated_at.

**The gap**

Phase 8 (scene board) and Phase 9 (web client chat view) are likely to want "sort by recently updated" or "show last edited X ago" UX. Without an updated_at column, neither feature has a clean source of truth for "when did the metadata of this scene last change?"

Options when this becomes relevant:

1. Add updated_at as a new migration in whichever phase first needs it. The migration would need to backfill existing rows with a sensible default (probably created_at). Cheap on a small table but the backfill is operator-visible.

2. Derive last-update time from the event stream once Phase 4 lands. Free in storage, but requires a query against the event store every time the board renders. Probably too expensive for the board's main listing.

3. Add updated_at proactively in some earlier phase as a low-risk migration. Cheaper than retrofitting later because the table is small and the backfill is easy.

**When this becomes urgent**

Phase 8 (board) and Phase 9 (web client) both depend on the answer. Whoever picks up the first of those two should decide whether to add the column as part of that phase's work, or to settle for a derived approach.

**Constraints / things to consider**

- Adding the column means every UPDATE statement in the store needs to set it. That's tedious but mechanical.
- Phase 2 uses RETURNING * which means adding a new column requires updating the sceneSelectColumns constant in store.go and the Scan target in scanSceneRow.
- The CHECK constraint pattern from Phase 2 (Task 7.5) is the precedent for how column-level constraints are added via plugin migrations.

**Out of scope for this bead**

This bead is the *consideration*. Whoever picks it up should decide whether to add the column, and if so when, as part of Phase 8 or Phase 9 design.

**Discovered from**

Scenes Phase 2 plan critical self-review, 2026-04-07. Surfaced as a forward-looking concern but deferred because Phase 2 has no direct need for updated_at.

title: Struct parameter for SetSystemAlias to prevent positional confusion
description: SetSystemAlias takes 4 positional strings (alias, cmd, createdBy, source) with implicit empty-string-means-NULL convention. CWE-683 risk. Consider converting to struct param SystemAliasUpsert{Alias, Command, CreatedBy, Source} for compile-time safety. Minimum fix: add AliasSourceSysalias constant to replace magic strings. From PR #194 review (HIGH-2).

title: Task test:int should use glob-based discovery
description: Taskfile.yaml test:int hardcodes a package list (./internal/content/ ./internal/store/ ./plugins/core-scenes/ ./test/integration/...) instead of using ./... with build tags. This leads to orphaned integration test files that never run. Switch to ./... once orphans in internal/plugin/ are resolved (see related bead). Discovered during PR #194 review.

title: Orphaned internal/plugin integration tests
description: internal/plugin/ contains 5 integration test files (communication, building, help, objects, aliases) with 27 pre-existing failures. These were orphaned when plugin integration testing moved to test/integration/plugin/. Either fix or delete these tests so internal/plugin/... can be added to task test:int. Discovered during PR #194 review.

title: Optimize plugin build: dynamic Taskfile tasks, caching, parallel builds
description: Current build-plugins.sh is a shell script that builds all platforms sequentially. Opportunities: (1) Dynamic Taskfile tasks — Taskfile v3 supports dynamic task generation; could auto-discover binary plugins and create per-plugin tasks with proper sources/generates for incremental builds. (2) Parallel builds — bash script builds 3 platforms sequentially; could parallelize with go build -p or GNU parallel. (3) Build caching — go build already caches, but the script always rebuilds; Taskfile sources/generates directives would skip unchanged plugins. (4) Plugin binary versioning — embed version/commit info via ldflags. (5) Plugin binary size — current core-scenes is 18MB stripped; consider UPX or further ldflags optimization.
notes: Deferred to post-PR-192. Build optimization is nice-to-have, not a blocker for plugin architecture landing.

title: Implement scope-aware CanPerformAction evaluation
description: CanPerformAction accepts scope but ignores it (param named _). The spec says scope enforcement is deferred: self checks own character, local checks current location context, global has no spatial constraint. Currently all scopes are treated identically. Implement the scope-aware evaluation when the attribute resolution supports location context.

title: Channel message search (full-text)
description:
Full-text search across channel message history. Separate from basic channel history replay (holomush-0sc.7) which is count-based tail reads.

## Scope
- Full-text search within a single channel's history
- Cross-channel search across all channels a player is a member of
- Filter by author, date range
- Requires indexing — likely PostgreSQL tsvector/tsquery on event payloads
- May benefit all message types (location say/pose), not just channels

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] @channel search <name> <query> returns matching messages
- [ ] Cross-channel search available (search all my channels)
- [ ] Author filter (@channel search <name> --from=<character> <query>)
- [ ] Date range filter
- [ ] Results respect membership (can only search channels you belong to)
- [ ] ABAC check on search capability
- [ ] PostgreSQL full-text index on message payloads
- [ ] Performance acceptable for large history (pagination)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests
- [ ] >80% test coverage

title: Server-side player preferences
description: gRPC PreferencesService for get/set/defaults, database schema for player preferences, client-side sync (localStorage as cache, server as source of truth). Follows from web client theme redesign spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-02-web-client-theme-redesign.md) which designs ThemePreferences as a clean serializable object ready for server persistence.

comments.author: Sean Brandt
comments.text: theme:web-portals note (2026-06-19): this is the PLAYER-level preferences BACKEND (PreferencesService + schema + sync). Its WEB control expression — a player settings UI — is the web-portals slice (partially present today as theme settings in the web client; full settings portal not built). Character-LEVEL settings live separately under holomush-qve.15. Labeled theme:web-portals to track the web settings surface.

title: Sidebar collapsed icons: add tooltips and hover highlight
description: When sidebar is collapsed, the icon-strip icons (location pin, door, people) need browser-native tooltips on hover and should highlight on hover like the logout/switch icons do. Currently they have title attributes but no visual hover feedback.

title: Control plane RPC: AdminResetPassword
description: Add AdminResetPassword RPC to the Control service for CLI/API-driven password resets without being in-game. Deferred from holomush-us6j (admin password reset command) to avoid expanding the control plane scope in this iteration.

title: Investigate moving guest authentication into a plugin
description:
The GuestAuthenticator currently lives in internal/telnet/ and is tightly coupled to core.go startup ordering (must be created after bootstrap resolves startLocationID). Guest auth is also the only Authenticator implementation — registered players use the two-phase login flow (AuthenticatePlayer → SelectCharacter) which bypasses Authenticator entirely.

This coupling caused bugs: GuestAuthenticator captured startLocationID by value before bootstrap resolved it, producing zero location IDs for all guest sessions.

Investigate whether guest support should be a setting plugin capability:
- Setting manifest declares guest support (enabled/disabled, naming theme, start location)
- SettingBootstrapper registers the guest authenticator with resolved config
- Removes startup ordering dependency from core.go
- Different settings could have different guest policies (e.g., skeleton disables guests)

Consider: does this belong in the plugin system, or is it a core server concern that just needs better initialization ordering?

title: Integration test: alias commands through plugin dispatch
description: The old alias integration test was removed because it referenced deleted handler functions. Need a proper integration test that exercises alias commands through the plugin dispatch path with a real database (testcontainers).

title: Design: admin web UI & config system
description: Research and design the admin experience for server operators. Determine which traditional MU* admin commands become web UI, which become config yaml, and which (if any) remain in-game commands. Cover server stats, player management, site bans, MOTD, DB search, and runtime configuration.

title: Design: inventory & object interaction model
description: Research and design the object model for inventory, containment, and interaction. Cover get/take/drop/give, containers, enter/leave, carrying capacity, and how objects relate to the existing world model. Survey how Ares, Penn, and Evennia handle object manipulation.

title: Admin Commands via Web UI & Config
description: Replace traditional MU* admin commands (@config, @motd, @stats, @version, @uptime, @pcreate, @newpassword, @ban, @sitelock, @toad, @dump, @search, @find) with web-based administration UI and/or config yaml. Design the admin UX for server operators.

title: Inventory & Object Manipulation
description: Design and implement inventory system and object manipulation commands (get/take, drop, give, containers, enter/leave). Deferred from system commands phase 2 — needs its own design pass covering object model, containment, and interaction patterns.

title: Make disconnect connection-remove + count atomic
description: CodeRabbit finding: Disconnect flow has a race between RemoveConnection and CountConnections/CountConnectionsByType (separate DB calls). Add a transactional RemoveConnectionAndCount method to session.Store that performs remove + count in a single DB transaction. Currently benign (UpdateStatus is idempotent) but should be fixed for correctness.

title: Add ErrAccessEvaluationFailed tests for remaining service methods
description: Service methods missing engine-error/infra-failure tests: GetExit, CreateExit, UpdateExit, DeleteExit, GetExitsByLocation, CreateObject, UpdateObject, MoveObject, AddSceneParticipant, RemoveSceneParticipant, ListSceneParticipants, FindLocationByName, MoveCharacter. Pattern established in GetLocation and Examine tests.

title: Add integration test for resolver fail-closed behavior end-to-end
description: Engine resolver changed from fail-open to fail-closed. Unit tests cover this but no integration test verifies resolver failure propagates as ErrAccessEvaluationFailed through the full call stack (engine → world.Service.checkAccess → world operation). See PR #88 finding holomush-eulm.10.

title: Add integration test for S1 system-subject injection via external ingress
description: Engine unit test covers S1 defense, but no integration test simulates external ingress with Subject='system'. Belt-and-suspenders for defense-in-depth. Discovered during PR #88 review.

title: ABAC: distinguish critical vs non-critical attribute providers for fail-closed behavior
description: resolver.Resolve fails closed on ANY provider error. Currently correct for security, but a future enhancement could distinguish critical providers (whose failures should fail-closed) from non-critical ones (time-of-day, etc) that degrade gracefully. Discovered during PR #88 review.

title: Deferred review findings from PR #88 (ABAC Phase 7.6 migration)
description:
Umbrella bead tracking 21 deferred review findings from PR #88 review (holomush-4vrc). Triaged during address-review-findings.

Type safety: .7, .11, .30, .46
Error handling: .2, .13, .18, .32
Code simplification: .8, .15, .17, .27, .50, .54, .68
Test coverage: .12, .20
Documentation: .51, .56, .63
Performance: .81

title: [E7] CI lint rule for char: prefix elimination
description: Add CI lint check to verify no char: prefix usage remains in production code (only character: prefix should be used). Currently CI checks for AccessControl.Check() calls but does NOT verify prefix migration. Discovered from PR #88 review finding #10.

title: Add role column to characters table
description: CharacterProvider currently hardcodes role='player'. Need to add a role column to the characters table and update the provider to read from DB. Discovered during T23b seed policy smoke testing — seed policies reference principal.character.role for builder/admin checks.

title: [BACKLOG] Command events without character_id should error
description:
**Context:** In communication plugin main.lua line 43:
```lua
character_name = character_name or "Someone"
```

A command event without character context indicates a bug in the dispatcher, not a valid state. Falling back to 'Someone' masks the real issue.

**Proposal:** 
1. Dispatcher MUST include character_id and character_name in command payloads
2. Plugins SHOULD return an error if these are missing rather than silently degrading
3. Consider making this a validation in the Lua host before delivery

title: Make migrations idempotent for concurrent execution
description:
**Source:** Discovered during holomush-5viy concurrent seed test

**Problem:**
When multiple processes run `seed` concurrently, each tries to run migrations. The migration system fails with:
```
ERROR: constraint "chk_exactly_one_containment" for relation "objects" already exists (SQLSTATE 42710)
```

The migration system is not idempotent - it doesn't use CREATE CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS or equivalent.

**Impact:**
- Concurrent seed commands may fail due to migration races
- Any scenario where multiple processes run migrations simultaneously

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Migrations use idempotent operations (IF NOT EXISTS patterns)
- [ ] Concurrent migration execution doesn't fail
- [ ] Test verifies concurrent seed commands all succeed

title: Add comprehensive Prometheus metrics across system
description:
Extend the existing observability infrastructure to add comprehensive Prometheus metrics across the HoloMUSH system.

## Background

We have basic metrics infrastructure in `internal/observability/server.go`:
- `holomush_connections_total` (by type)
- `holomush_requests_total` (by type, status)
- Standard Go runtime metrics

However, critical operational metrics are missing, particularly around retry behavior and event emission.

## Specific Concerns

### Event Emission Retries (from PR review x2t.75)
The `emitWithRetry` function in `internal/world/events.go` uses exponential backoff with up to 3 retries. Currently:
- Individual retry attempts log at DEBUG level (invisible in production)
- Only complete failures log at ERROR level

**Metrics needed:**
- `holomush_event_emission_retries_total` - Counter with labels: stream, event_type, outcome (success/failure)
- `holomush_event_emission_duration_seconds` - Histogram for emission latency

The go-retry library (`github.com/sethvargo/go-retry`) doesn't have built-in callback hooks. Metrics must be emitted within the retry function body where we currently increment `attempt`.

### Implementation Approach

1. Add new counters/histograms to `observability.Metrics` struct
2. Pass `*Metrics` through `world.ServiceConfig` to Service
3. Pass metrics to `emitWithRetry` and other functions that need instrumentation
4. Consider package-level metrics registration as alternative (simpler but less testable)

### Other Metrics to Consider

- Plugin execution duration and errors
- ABAC policy evaluation counts
- Repository operation latency
- WebSocket/Telnet connection lifecycle
- Session counts by state

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Event emission retry counter added
- [ ] Metrics plumbing through Service layer
- [ ] Documentation of available metrics
- [ ] Grafana dashboard template (optional)

title: [E11] Implementation Plan: Forums
description:
Create detailed implementation plan for Forums based on approved design.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Break down work into implementable tasks
- [ ] Define database migration strategy

- [ ] Document rollout/migration plan

Create detailed implementation plan for Discord integration based on the architecture design.

description:
Design the architecture for Discord integration with HoloMUSH.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document Discord bot architecture (go-discordgo or similar)
- [ ] Define channel bridging protocol and message format

- [ ] Specify notification routing (mentions, DMs)

description:
Design the forums architecture for HoloMUSH web client integration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document forum data model (boards, threads, posts, permissions)
- [ ] Define relationship between forum users and game characters
- [ ] Specify moderation workflow and permission levels
- [ ] Design notification system for replies
- [ ] Plan in-game command integration points
