title: Epic 12: Discord Integration
description:
Bridge Discord and HoloMUSH for seamless community interaction.

**Goal:** Unified community experience across Discord and game.

**Phases:**
- 12.1: Discord bot
- 12.2: Channel bridge
- 12.3: OAuth linking
- 12.4: Notifications
- 12.5: Status sync

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] go-plugin Discord bot implementation works
- [ ] Discord ↔ game channel sync works
- [ ] Discord account to player account linking works
- [ ] Mentions and DMs forwarded
- [ ] Online/offline presence synced
notes: Substrate-contract spec landed. Discord is a bridge plugin: groupkit forbidden (INV-S10); eventkit/replay permitted conditionally.

title: Epic 11: Forums
description:
Web-based discussion forums integrated with the game.

**Goal:** Community discussion space accessible from web and in-game.

**Phases:**
- 11.1: Forum schema
- 11.2: Web UI
- 11.3: Moderation tools
- 11.4: Notifications
- 11.5: In-game integration

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Boards, threads, posts schema works
- [ ] Web browsing and posting works
- [ ] Edit, delete, lock, move moderation works
- [ ] New reply notifications work
- [ ] forum recent command works in-game
notes: Substrate-contract spec landed. Forums uses eventkit ONLY (NOT groupkit) per spec §4.3 + INV-S10.

title: core-channels: admin create rate-limit bypass keys on exact matched-policy id (availability-only)
description: abac-reviewer Low finding (non-blocking, availability-only, NO fail-open direction). plugins/core-channels/service.go:359 bypasses the create rate limit only when dec.MatchedPolicy == adminCreatePolicyID. In an OR-of-permits engine an admin create could instead match the host admin-wildcard seed (seed.go:104), yielding a different matched-policy id, so an admin could be harmlessly rate-limited. A non-admin can never match adminCreatePolicyID (requires admin role), so there is no security fail-open. Fix (optional): bypass on an admin-role signal rather than a specific policy id, or document the coupling.

title: Scenes Phase 7: Templates
description:
Originally Phase 7 of the Scenes & RP epic (holomush-5rh); lifted out as a standalone backlog item 2026-07-03 — no longer part of an active phase sequence, priority P4.

Adds scene templates for reusable scene configurations.

**Spec reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-06-scenes-and-rp-design-v2.md (sections 1.4, 6.5, 7.2)

**Scope:**
- scene_templates table (migration 000004)
- 'scene save-template', 'scene templates', 'scene create --template' command handlers
- ListTemplates, CreateTemplate, DeleteTemplate gRPC RPCs
- Template-based scene creation: title/description/location/pose-order/warnings/tags inherited from template
- Templates are owned by the character that created them

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Templates table and store methods
- [ ] save-template captures current scene config
- [ ] create --template <name> creates from template
- [ ] templates lists user's templates only
- [ ] task pr-prep passes
notes: Recreated via create/delete from holomush-5rh.16 (deleted 2026-07-03) to fully detach from epic holomush-5rh's ID namespace — bd list --parent matches by ID prefix as well as the parent field, so clearing parent alone still left it appearing as a false child in prefix-based queries. Historical deps (both closed, not recreated as live edges): depended on holomush-5rh.15; blocked holomush-5rh.17.

title: Add forced-tie regression lock for SceneStore ListParticipants / pose-order determinism
description:
**Goal:** Convert the happy-path ListParticipants ordering test into a deterministic regression lock that fails on every run if the ORDER BY tiebreakers (gfo6.28, merged via PR #4198) are removed.

**Why:** gfo6.28 fixed flaky ordering via clock-domain harmonization + three ORDER BY tiebreakers, verified by 3x clean `task test:int` rather than a forced-tie test. The existing test at `plugins/core-scenes/store_integration_test.go:1184` only exercises the tie path by luck (joined_at usually differs across `CreateWithOwner`/`AddParticipant`), so a future regression that drops a tiebreaker would re-introduce intermittent flake rather than a deterministic CI failure.

**Plan:** After setup, force the tie — `UPDATE scene_participants SET joined_at = <const> WHERE scene_id = $1` for both rows — then assert owner-first (`[alice, bob]`). Add an equivalent forced-tie assertion for pose-order determinism (`ListParticipantsWithPoseMeta` + `GetPoseOrder`) tiebroken by `character_id`.

**Files touched:** `plugins/core-scenes/store_integration_test.go` (+ pose-order test if separate).

**Out of scope:** Production query/ORDER BY changes (already landed via PR #4198). World-side scene_repo mirror (gfo6.31 — also landed). This is test-only hardening.

**Origin:** Re-filed from `holomush-gfo6.35` (P3) as a standalone top-level P4 bead post-PR-#4198 merge. The gfo6 epic is otherwise complete; this is hygiene work that doesn't need to block the epic from closing.

## Required Skills
jj:jujutsu

title: Cleanup: drop unused oops.Code('SESSION_ENDED') wrap in quit.go and dispatcher.go
description:
Re-filed from holomush-5ayg.6 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-5ayg PR #139).

Verified 2026-05-16:
- internal/command/handlers/quit.go:19 — `return oops.Code("SESSION_ENDED").Wrap(command.ErrSessionEnded)`
- internal/command/dispatcher.go:420 — `return oops.Code("SESSION_ENDED").Wrap(ErrSessionEnded)`

No caller checks `case "SESSION_ENDED"` or `.Code() == "SESSION_ENDED"`. Detection relies solely on `errors.Is` at server.go:427. The `.Code("SESSION_ENDED")` is unused noise.

Acceptance: replace both call sites with `oops.Wrap(command.ErrSessionEnded)` (no .Code). Two-line change.

title: Document Lua state limitations for multi-turn commands
description:
Re-filed from holomush-g2tyt (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-wr9).

No doc found for Lua state limitations on multi-turn commands. Plugin authors may attempt to maintain state across command invocations without understanding the per-invocation state lifecycle.

Acceptance:
- Add section to site/docs/extending/ (plugin authoring guide) covering:
  - Lua state lifecycle per command invocation
  - How to persist state across commands (host KV functions, world properties)
  - Anti-patterns (module-level vars, expecting state retention)
- Cross-link from plugin authoring tutorial

Note: scope decision — if multi-turn commands are explicitly unsupported, doc should say so; if planned, separate design bead is needed.

title: [Needs human review] Investigate orphan wr9.127 with blank title
description:
Re-filed from holomush-wr9.127 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-wr9).

Original bead has blank title and no actionable description. Auditor flagged as KEEP-needs-human.

Acceptance: human reviewer determines intent from bead history (created_at, audit log, related commits) and either re-files with proper content or closes as obsolete.

title: Web StreamEvents sets ClientType='terminal' — should it be 'comms_hub'? Design clarification needed
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.27 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

handler.go:186 sends ClientType: "terminal" intentionally per the comment at line 154. Design choice but the bead's correctness argument about connection counting remains open: should a web-streaming connection count as a terminal or as a comms_hub for CountConnectionsByType purposes?

Acceptance: confirm or update the connection type taxonomy in session.go:192 docs; align web handler with the decision.

title: NewPlayerToken returns error that can never fail — misleading API (verify if signature still exists)
description:
Re-filed from holomush-mq5o.12 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-mq5o).

**Needs verification**: original auditor could not confirm internal/auth/player_token.go + NewPlayerToken still exist with the infallible signature. The file may have been renamed to player_session.go in the post-PR-#123 refactor.

If still present, change signature to `func NewPlayerToken(...) *PlayerToken` (no error return) since the operation cannot fail.

Acceptance:
- Verify if NewPlayerToken still exists
- If yes: remove the error return; update callers
- If no: close as superseded by the rename

title: Cluster operations documentation — write site/docs/operating/cluster.md
description:
Re-filed from holomush-hd8h.1 (2026-05-16 epic audit of holomush-e49r → hd8h sub-epic).

**Goal:** Write site/docs/operating/cluster.md covering deployment requirements, NTP requirement, @cluster status interpretation, and multi-tenancy guidance.

**Design reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §8 (cluster operations)

**Discovered from:** holomush-ojw1.3.21 — Phase 8 ops-docs sweep.

**Scope:** Supervisor requirement (systemd Restart=on-failure, k8s restartPolicy=Always, docker restart=on-failure since pill triggers os.Exit(125)); NTP requirement rationale; @cluster status field interpretation; INV-54 multi-tenancy guidance for shared NATS deployments.

**Out of scope:** Non-cluster crypto docs (covered by other Phase 8 beads).

Verified 2026-05-16: site/docs/operating/cluster.md absent from origin/main.

title: Remote KMS substrate: VaultTransitProvider + provider-migrate CLI + KEK rotation CLI
description:
## Goal

Add HashiCorp Vault Transit support to the existing crypto provider stack so deployments that require centralized key custody / HSM-backed roots / external audit of every KEK op can swap LocalAEADProvider for VaultTransitProvider without re-encrypting payloads, plus ship the two operational CLIs needed to migrate between providers and rotate the KEK in place.

## Why this exists (and why it's standalone)

Phase 2-5 shipped on `LocalAEADProvider` (XChaCha20-Poly1305 wrap with a file/env-sourced master key). That's sufficient for the vast majority of HoloMUSH deployments — single-node game servers managed by the operator who runs them. **Vault integration is genuinely nice-to-have, not core**: it serves orgs with corporate KMS policy or regulatory key-custody requirements, neither of which is typical for the HoloMUSH user base. Re-filed from holomush-ljj9 (closed) to detach from the core e49r epic line so the master crypto epic can converge cleanly without waiting on optional ops tooling.

## Scope (per spec §5.2 / §11.1)

- **VaultTransitProvider** — HashiCorp Vault Transit secret-engine adapter implementing the existing `kek.Provider` interface:
  - Auth via AppRole, Kubernetes service account, or static token
  - `Wrap` → `transit/encrypt/<key>`, `Unwrap` → `transit/decrypt/<key>`
  - `kekKeyID` = Vault-issued ciphertext version prefix (`vault:v3:...`)
  - KEK rotation = `transit/keys/<key>/rotate`; Vault retains all versions natively
- **Provider-migrate CLI** — `holomush crypto provider-migrate --to vault-transit` switches a deployment from one provider to another without downtime. Walks every `crypto_keys` row, unwraps with the old provider, re-wraps with the new, updates `wrap_provider` + `wrap_key_id`. **No payload re-encryption** — DEKs change wrapper, payload cryptograms unchanged.
- **KEK rotation CLI** — rotates the long-term root key without re-encrypting payloads. Existing DEKs are re-wrapped under the new KEK version, payload cryptograms unchanged.

## Substrate already in place

The `crypto_keys` table is already provider-agnostic: `wrap_provider TEXT` and `wrap_key_id TEXT` columns are part of every row (migration 000013). Phase 2's substrate was designed to make this drop-in. The `kek.Provider` interface (internal/eventbus/crypto/kek/provider.go) is stable.

## Related but separate beads

- **holomush-e49r.1** (P2) — keyring KEKSource alternative for the existing LocalAEADProvider. Useful independent of Vault.
- **holomush-e49r.2** (P2) — systemd-credential KEKSource. Same: useful independent of Vault.

Both will likely land before this if anyone takes a swing at the local-but-more-secure KEK source path.

## Spec references

- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §5.2 (two provider topologies — Topology 1: True remote KMS)
- §5.4 (VaultTransitProvider v1)
- §5.7 (Provider migration CLI shape)
- Provider invariants in §2

## TDD acceptance criteria

TBD by writing-plans. At minimum:
- VaultTransitProvider passes the same kek.Provider interface tests as LocalAEADProvider.
- Integration test with a testcontainer-based Vault (`hashicorp/vault`) exercises Wrap/Unwrap round-trip plus KEK rotation.
- Provider-migrate CLI walks a seeded crypto_keys table end-to-end with both directions (local→vault, vault→local) and verifies all wrapped DEKs unwrap cleanly post-migration.
- KEK rotation CLI does the same in-place under one provider.

## Dependencies

None. Phase 3 (the original prerequisite for the e49r tree) is now closed.

## Out of scope

- AWS KMS / GCP KMS / Azure Key Vault adapters (filed separately in spec §5.2 as future v2 work).
- Multi-region key replication.
- Hot-reload of provider config (provider-migrate is the migration path; restart with new config is acceptable for v1).

## History

Re-filed from holomush-ljj9 (closed) on 2026-05-13 per operator decision: 'nice to have but probably over-engineered for most HoloMUSH users.' Priority bumped P2→P3 to reflect the actual user demand profile (single-node deployments dominate).
notes: Priority dropped P3→P4 (2026-05-13) to reflect true-backlog status. No HoloMUSH deployment currently demands remote KMS; this stays parked until a deployment with corporate-KMS / HSM-custody requirements creates concrete demand.

title: RekeyAbort audit payload: carry operator-supplied reason in Justification
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.52 (detached from sub-epic E during epic wrap; correctness-shaped audit-attribution gap parallel to .55's justification persistence work, but standalone — no longer requires sub-epic E context).

**Goal:** Replace hardcoded 'aborted by operator' Justification on the RekeyAbort path with the operator-supplied reason. Today the audit row carries no operator context for aborts beyond PrimaryOperator.PlayerID, requiring a join to crypto_rekey_checkpoints.aborted_reason to reconstruct.

**Design reference:** abac-reviewer R1 non-blocking finding #2. cmd/holomush/crypto_rekey_wiring.go:326 + internal/testsupport/holomushtest/server.go:678.

**Background:** Wire proto RekeyAbortRequest doesn't carry a justification field today. Either populate Justification with req.PlayerID (already in scope at call site) or carry the player id explicitly in a new payload field.

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestRekeyAbort_AuditPayloadCarriesOperatorContext

**Verification steps:** task test:int -- ./test/integration/crypto/

**Files touched:** cmd/holomush/crypto_rekey_wiring.go; internal/testsupport/holomushtest/server.go; possibly api/proto/holomush/admin/v1/rekey.proto if a new field is added (regen .pb.go).

**Dependencies:** none

**Out of scope:** changes to abort dispatch (INV-E17 single-control)

title: Pre-production: Migration 30 bootstrap_metadata backfill (trigger-deferred)
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.56 (moved out of sub-epic E during E wrap — trigger-deferred standing concern that won't be actionable in E's lifetime).

**Goal:** Track that CodeRabbit PR #3670 review #16 flagged migration 000030's DROP+CREATE pattern as lossy. Currently defended by project rule 'no-prod-shape-for-undeployed' (spec §3.6) and inline migration comment. This bead exists to remind future maintainers: BEFORE the first production deployment of this codebase, replace migration 30's DROP+CREATE with a proper backfill (rename old → backfill into new → drop renamed) so existing initialization markers survive the schema transition.

**Design reference:** spec §3.6 'no-prod-shape discipline for undeployed codebases'.

**Plan reference:** master spec §A4 (subject prefix amendment + auditchain primitive introduction).

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestMigration_000030_PreservesLegacyRows — pre-30 schema, seed (key, value) rows, run migration up, assert rows survived as (chain_name, scope_key) tuples per backfill mapping.

**Verification steps:** task test:int -- -run TestMigration_000030 ./internal/store/

**Files touched:** internal/store/migrations/000030_replace_bootstrap_metadata.up.sql (replace DROP with RENAME+backfill); .down.sql (mirror); migrations_test.go (add backfill scenario).

**Dependencies:** TRIGGER — only execute when project policy moves off 'no-prod-shape-for-undeployed'.

**Out of scope:** changes to setting_bootstrap_state (migration 32 already separates concerns).

title: Force-destroy slog severity: Warn → Error for monitoring escalation
description:
**Discovered from:** holomush-jxo8.7.51 (moved out of sub-epic E during E wrap to keep epic scoped to correctness-shaped work).

**Goal:** Bump the force-destroy slog severity from Warn to Error so log-based alerts that filter on Error catch split-brain bypass events. Audit chain captures the canonical record (force_destroy=true + missing_members per INV-E11); the slog line is operational visibility only.

**Design reference:** abac-reviewer R1 non-blocking finding #1. rekey_phase5.go:282-289.

**TDD acceptance criteria:** TestForceDestroy_LogsAtErrorSeverity — assert slog.Handler captures with Level >= slog.LevelError.

**Verification steps:** task test -- ./internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/

**Files touched:** internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/rekey_phase5.go

**Dependencies:** none. Document in runbook either way (current Warn behavior or post-change Error).

**Out of scope:** changes to audit payload structure

title: crypto rekey: --purge-hot flag (master spec §6.3 Phase 4.4)
description:
**Goal:** Implement the optional --purge-hot flag for `holomush crypto rekey` per master spec §6.3 Phase 4.4. The flag enumerates JetStream messages with dek_ref pointing at the destroyed DEK and deletes them, rather than waiting for natural retention rolloff.

**Why deferred from sub-epic E:** Decision made during E's design brainstorm 2026-05-10. Cost/benefit didn't warrant shipping in E:

- Hot tier is volatile by design; default JS retention (7d) sweeps the same messages naturally
- Cold tier holds the canonical re-encrypted copies after Rekey Phase 3 completes
- INV-39 (sub-epic E) provides hot→cold-tier fallback for reads during the retention window, so steady-state read correctness is preserved without --purge-hot
- Realistic operator demand is low; compliance/forensic value is marginal because old DEK material is destroyed in Phase 6 regardless of whether stale hot-tier messages remain on disk
- Implementation cost: ~250 LoC orchestrator + tests, new Phase 4.5 with checkpoint state for mid-purge resume, JS subject enumeration, deletion error handling

**When to lift:** A real operator surfaces a strong no-old-DEK-message-on-disk requirement that retention-tuning (NATS `max_age` per stream) cannot satisfy. Current operator workaround documented in the sub-epic E spec: shorten JS retention via NATS config.

**Design reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-event-payload-crypto-design.md §6.3 Phase 4.4. See also INV-E* invariant in sub-epic E spec naming this as deferred.

**Plan reference:** TBD when this is picked up.

**TDD acceptance criteria (preserved for future plan):**
- Phase 4.5 (purge hot) iterates JS messages on the affected subjects, deletes those with matching dek_ref
- Mid-purge crash → checkpoint state captures last-deleted seqnum per subject; resume picks up cleanly
- Phase 4.5 only runs when --purge-hot was set on initial invocation; resume of an in-flight Rekey honors the original flag
- E2E: full-boot test verifies post-purge stream has no messages with dek_ref=destroyed
- Audit event captures count of purged messages

**Verification steps:** task lint; task test; task test:int; task pr-prep.

**Files touched (estimated):**
- internal/eventbus/crypto/dek/manager.go (Phase 4.5 in Rekey orchestrator)
- internal/store/migrations/ (extend crypto_rekey_checkpoints if needed for purge progress)
- cmd/holomush/cmd_crypto_rekey.go (add --purge-hot flag)

**Dependencies:** holomush-jxo8.7 (sub-epic E lands the orchestrator and checkpoint table this extends).

**Out of scope:**
- Changing default JS retention (operator config, not E or this bead)
- Rewriting hot-tier messages under new DEK (master spec explicitly rejects)

title: holomushlint: forbid errors.Is(err, oopsSentinel) — tautological under samber/oops
description:
**Goal:** Add a `holomushlint` analyzer rule that flags `errors.Is(err, X)` (and `errors.As` / testify `assert.ErrorIs` / gomega `MatchError`) when X is declared via `oops.Code(...).Errorf(...)` or `oops.Errorf(...)`.

**Why:** `samber/oops` v1.21.0 `OopsError.Is(err)` returns `true` for any target that is itself an `OopsError`, regardless of code. So `errors.Is(repoErr, ErrNotEnrolled)` returns `true` for ALL oops-wrapped DB errors, not just the named sentinel. This is a silent correctness footgun — caller branches incorrectly without any signal.

**Real incident:** Phase 5 sub-epic A T9 `Service.Verify` (caught at code-reviewer pre-push gate, fixed in commit `pll`). Without the lint rule, the pattern can recur in any package that mixes `oops.Code(...)` sentinels with `errors.Is`.

**Canonical fix pattern:** code comparison via `oops.AsOops(err)` then `.Code() == "..."`. See:
- `internal/auth/session_ownership.go::isSessionNotFound` (precedent)
- `internal/totp/service.go::isNotEnrolledErr` (Phase 5 fix)
- `pkg/errutil/testing.go::AssertErrorCode` (test-side helper)

**Detection:**
1. Identify package-level `var X = oops.Code(...).Errorf(...)` or `oops.Errorf(...)` declarations across the codebase.
2. Flag any call site of `errors.Is(*, X)`, `errors.As(*, &X)`, `assert.ErrorIs(*, *, X)`, `require.ErrorIs(*, *, X)`, `gomega.MatchError(X)` where X is a known oops sentinel.
3. Emit suggestion: `use errutil.AssertErrorCode for tests or oops.AsOops(err).Code() == CODE for production discrimination`.

**Files:**
- `gorules/analyzers/oops_errors_is_forbidden/` (new analyzer package)
- `gorules/analyzers/oops_errors_is_forbidden/oops_errors_is_forbidden_test.go` (testdata-driven)
- `gorules/cmd/custom-gcl/main.go` (wire into the custom golangci-lint binary)
- Possibly an allowlist for legitimate cross-domain comparisons (e.g., `errors.Is(err, context.Canceled)` where context is stdlib, not oops).

**Verification:**
- Test against the pre-fix Phase 5 T9 code (`internal/totp/service.go` at the bug-introducing commit `pv`) — must flag.
- Test against the post-fix code (`pll`) — must NOT flag.
- Run across the whole codebase: any pre-existing offenders should be filed as their own beads.

**Out of scope:**
- Auto-fixer; just diagnostics.
- Fixing pre-existing offenders (file separate beads per occurrence).
- Detecting tautological `errors.Is` against non-oops sentinels (different problem).

title: Audit zero CharacterID handling in Subscribe
description: Subscribe's ambient-stream fallback (server.go:789-791) emits world.CharacterStream(info.CharacterID) without an IsZero guard. If CharacterID is ever zero (e.g., malformed fixture, plugin injection), this emits a malformed 'character:00000000000000000000000000' stream name. B13's ListSessionStreams fallback matches Subscribe byte-for-byte; if we want to harden, we should fix both sites at once.

title: Remove transitional connection_id != '' gate in Subscribe handler
description: Follow-up from session-identity implementation. internal/grpc/server.go Subscribe handler has an 'if req.GetConnectionId() != ""' gate to preserve legacy-caller behavior. After Task 14, both production gateways (web + telnet) always pass connection_id, so the else-branch is dead in production but pre-existing gRPC unit tests still construct SubscribeRequest without it. Remove the gate and update the unit tests to always pass connection_id. Refs: bd-j2xj completion.

title: chore(dev): fix gopls workspace path confusion in jj worktree symlinks
description:
Throughout the Phase 3 execution, gopls (Go LSP) showed persistent
'undefined: SceneRow / startSpan / etc.' errors and 'this file is
within module ../../../../../../Users/sean/Code/...' workspace
warnings on every file in plugins/core-scenes/.

Root cause: the jj worktree at /Users/sean/Code/github.com/holomush/.worktrees/scene-rewrite
is accessible via TWO paths because of a Volume symlink:
- /Users/sean/Code/github.com/holomush/.worktrees/scene-rewrite
- /Volumes/Code/github.com/holomush/.worktrees/scene-rewrite

gopls opens files via one path but the go.mod resolution lands on
the other, so it sees the file as 'outside the workspace' and reports
every same-package symbol as undefined.

These were FALSE errors — task test, task test:int, and task lint all
exited 0 throughout. They cluttered the diagnostics for the entire
session and required ~25 'ignore stale gopls warning' confirmations
when reviewing implementer reports.

**Options:**
1. Add a go.work file that includes both paths.
2. Standardize on one path (either /Users or /Volumes) for all dev
   workspaces and update editor workspace settings to match.
3. Configure gopls 'directoryFilters' to handle the symlink.
4. Document in CLAUDE.md so future agents know to ignore these
   specific diagnostics during scene-rewrite work.

**Severity:** Cosmetic but high friction. Doesn't affect builds.
Affects every editor session in this jj worktree.

Refs: holomush-5rh.12 PR #202

title: MOTD on session start with appropriate commands
description: Display a message of the day (MOTD) when a session starts, along with a summary of available commands. Should be shown on initial connect and potentially on reconnect.

title: Add concurrent dispatch engine failure test for metric consistency
description: Dispatcher records StatusEngineFailure metrics but no concurrent test exercises this under the race detector. Low priority since Prometheus counters are goroutine-safe. See PR #88 finding holomush-eulm.11.

title: Follow-up: separate unrelated subscriber.go and codes.go changes from ABAC PR
description: subscriber.go context leak fix and codes.go fmt.Fprintf optimization were included in the ABAC Phase 7.6 PR but are unrelated. Future PRs should separate unrelated fixes into their own commits. Low priority process note.

title: [E7] Performance benchmark for AccessPolicyEngine vs AccessControl
description: Add benchmark comparing p50/p99 latency of AccessPolicyEngine.Evaluate vs AccessControl.Check to detect performance regressions from the migration. Discovered from PR #88 review finding #18.

title: [E7] Metrics query and alerting documentation
description: Add query examples and alerting documentation for the 4 ABAC metrics (engine failures, policy evaluations, etc.) to internal/observability/. Discovered from PR #88 review finding #17.

title: Fix broken relative links in markdown docs (MD057)
description: rumdl MD057 rule found ~60 broken relative links across README.md, docs/, and site/docs/. These point to pages not yet created. Re-enable MD057 in .rumdl.toml and site/.rumdl.toml after fixing.

title: Plugin self-describing manifests for dynamic loading
description:
## Summary
Allow plugins to declare their own metadata and subscriptions, enabling dynamic loading and reconfiguration without host code changes.

## Motivation
Currently (Phase 1), plugin subscriptions are configured by the host at load time. This works but requires host code changes for each new plugin.

## Future Capabilities
- Plugins export get_manifest function returning JSON with:
  - name, version, description, author
  - subscriptions (stream patterns)
  - required capabilities/permissions
- Host discovers and loads plugins from a directory
- Hot-reload plugins without restart
- Admin API to load/unload/reconfigure plugins at runtime

## Decision History
- Phase 1 decision: Host config only (Option 4)
- Documented in: docs/reference/plugin-authoring.md
- Predecessor issue: holomush-nvm

title: Gateway <-> Core ping/pong health checking
description:
Implement health check ping/pong mechanism between gateway and core processes.

**Requirements:**
- Gateway periodically pings core over gRPC
- Core responds with pong including health status
- Gateway tracks connection health and can report unhealthy state
- Metrics exposed for monitoring

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] gRPC health check service implemented in core
- [ ] Gateway sends periodic pings (configurable interval)
- [ ] Gateway exposes health status reflecting core connectivity
- [ ] Metrics for ping latency and failure counts

title: Control gRPC authentication with special client cert or admin privileges
description:
The control gRPC interface needs proper authentication. Options:
1. Special client certificate issued only for control operations
2. User authentication with system admin privileges

Currently the control interface uses the same mTLS certs as the main gRPC communication, which means any component with valid certs can control (shutdown) any process.
